text.NRSV: In the beginning when God created the heavens and the earth,
text.Rotherham: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.
text.KJV: In the beginning God created the Heauen, and the Earth.
text.NABRE: In the beginning, when God created the heavens and the earth—
text.DRB: In the beginning God created heaven and earth.

text.NRSV: the earth was a formless void and darkness covered the face of the deep, while a wind from God swept over the face of the waters.
text.Rotherham: Now, the earth, had become waste and wild, and darkness, was on the face of the roaring deep,—but, the Spirit of God, was brooding on the face of the waters,
text.KJV: And the earth was without forme, and voyd, and darkenesse was vpon the face of the deepe: and the Spirit of God mooued vpon the face of the waters.
text.NABRE: and the earth was without form or shape, with darkness over the abyss and a mighty wind sweeping over the waters—
text.DRB: And the earth was void and vacant, and darkness was upon the face of the depth: and the Spirit of God moved over the waters.

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Light, be, And light was.
text.KJV: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let there be light, and there was light.
text.DRB: And God said: Be light made. And light was made.

text.NRSV: And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
text.Rotherham: And God saw the light, that it was, good, and God divided the light, from the, darkness;
text.KJV: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God diuided the light from the darkenesse.
text.NABRE: God saw that the light was good. God then separated the light from the darkness.
text.DRB: And God saw the light that it was good: and he divided the light from the darkness.

text.NRSV: God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, the first day.
text.Rotherham: and God called the light, day, but the darkness, called he, night. So it was evening—and it was morning, one day.
text.KJV: And God called the light, Day, and the darknesse he called Night: and the euening and the morning were the first day.
text.NABRE: God called the light “day,” and the darkness he called “night.” Evening came, and morning followed—the first day.
text.DRB: And he called the light, Day, and the darkness, Night: and there was evening & morning, that made one day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let there be a dome in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters.”
text.Rotherham: And God said, Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it be a means of dividing, between waters and waters,
text.KJV: ¶ And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters: and let it diuide the waters from the waters.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let there be a dome in the middle of the waters, to separate one body of water from the other.
text.DRB: God also said: Be a firmament made amidst the waters: and let it divide between waters and waters.

text.NRSV: So God made the dome and separated the waters that were under the dome from the waters that were above the dome. And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God made the expanse, and it divided between the waters that were under the expanse and the waters that were above the expanse. And it was so.
text.KJV: And God made the firmament; and diuided the waters, which were vnder the firmament, from the waters, which were aboue the firmament: and it was so.
text.NABRE: God made the dome, and it separated the water below the dome from the water above the dome. And so it happened.
text.DRB: And God made a firmament, and divided the waters that were under the firmament, from those that were above the firmament. And it was so done.

text.NRSV: God called the dome Sky. And there was evening and there was morning, the second day.
text.Rotherham: And God called the expanse, heavens. So it was evening—and it was morning, a, second day.
text.KJV: And God called the firmament, Heauen: and the euening and the morning were the second day.
text.NABRE: God called the dome “sky.” Evening came, and morning followed—the second day.
text.DRB: And God called the firmament, Heaven: and there was evening & morning that made the second day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let the waters under the sky be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together, into one place, and let the dry—ground appear. And it was so.
text.KJV: ¶ And God said, Let the waters vnder the heauen be gathered together vnto one place, and let the dry land appeare: and it was so.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let the water under the sky be gathered into a single basin, so that the dry land may appear. And so it happened: the water under the sky was gathered into its basin, and the dry land appeared.
text.DRB: God also said: let the waters that are under heaven be gathered together into one place: and let the dry land appear. And it was so done.

text.NRSV: God called the dry land Earth, and the waters that were gathered together he called Seas. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And God called, the dry-ground, land, but the gathering together of the waters, called he seas. And God saw that it was good.
text.KJV: And God called the drie land, Earth, and the gathering together of the waters called hee, Seas: and God saw that it was good.
text.NABRE: God called the dry land “earth,” and the basin of water he called “sea.” God saw that it was good.
text.DRB: And God called the dry land, Earth, and the gathering of waters together, he called Seas. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Let the earth put forth vegetation: plants yielding seed, and fruit trees of every kind on earth that bear fruit with the seed in it.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the land put-forth vegetation—herb yielding seed, fruit-tree, bearing fruit after its kind, whose seed is within it on the land. And it was so,
text.KJV: And God said, Let the Earth bring foorth grasse, the herbe yeelding seed, and the fruit tree, yeelding fruit after his kinde, whose seed is in it selfe, vpon the earth: and it was so.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let the earth bring forth vegetation: every kind of plant that bears seed and every kind of fruit tree on earth that bears fruit with its seed in it. And so it happened:
text.DRB: And said: Let the earth shoot forth green herbs, and such as may seed, & fruit-trees yielding fruit after his kind, such as may have seed in itself upon the earth. And it was so done.

text.NRSV: The earth brought forth vegetation: plants yielding seed of every kind, and trees of every kind bearing fruit with the seed in it. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And the land brought-forth vegetation—herb yielding seed after its kind, and tree bearing fruit, whose seed is within it, after its kind, And God saw that it was good.
text.KJV: And the earth brought foorth grasse, and herbe yeelding seed after his kinde, and the tree yeelding fruit, whose seed was in it selfe, after his kinde: and God saw that it was good.
text.NABRE: the earth brought forth vegetation: every kind of plant that bears seed and every kind of fruit tree that bears fruit with its seed in it. God saw that it was good.
text.DRB: And the earth brought forth green herb, such as seedeth according to his kind, and tree that beareth fruit, having seed each one according to his kind. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: And there was evening and there was morning, the third day.
text.Rotherham: So it was evening—and it was morning, a third day.
text.KJV: And the euening and the morning were the third day.
text.NABRE: Evening came, and morning followed—the third day.
text.DRB: And there was evening and morning that made the third day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let there be lights in the dome of the sky to separate the day from the night; and let them be for signs and for seasons and for days and years,
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let there be luminaries in the expanse of the heavens, to divide between the day and the night,—and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days and years;
text.KJV: ¶ And God said, Let there bee lights in the firmament of the heauen, to diuide the day from the night: and let them be for signes and for seasons, and for dayes and yeeres.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let there be lights in the dome of the sky, to separate day from night. Let them mark the seasons, the days and the years,
text.DRB: Again God said: Be there lights made in the firmament of heaven, to divide the day and the night, and let them be for signs & seasons, and days and years;

text.NRSV: and let them be lights in the dome of the sky to give light upon the earth.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: yea let them be for luminaries in the expanse of the heavens, to give light on the earth. And it was so.
text.KJV: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heauen, to giue light vpon the earth: and it was so.
text.NABRE: and serve as lights in the dome of the sky, to illuminate the earth. And so it happened:
text.DRB: to shine in the firmament of heaven, and to give light upon the earth. And it was so done.

text.NRSV: God made the two great lights—the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night—and the stars.
text.Rotherham: And God made the two great luminaries,—the greater luminary to rule the day, and the lesser luminary to rule the night, also the stars.
text.KJV: And God made two great lights: the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the starres also.
text.NABRE: God made the two great lights, the greater one to govern the day, and the lesser one to govern the night, and the stars.
text.DRB: And God made two great lights; a greater light to govern the day, and a lesser light to govern the night; and stars.

text.NRSV: God set them in the dome of the sky to give light upon the earth,
text.Rotherham: And God set them in the expanse of the heavens,—to give light on the earth;
text.KJV: And God set them in the firmament of the heauen, to giue light vpon the earth:
text.NABRE: God set them in the dome of the sky, to illuminate the earth,
text.DRB: And he set them in the firmament of heaven, to shine upon the earth,

text.NRSV: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: and to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
text.KJV: And to rule ouer the day, and ouer the night, and to diuide the light from the darkenesse: and God saw that it was good.
text.NABRE: to govern the day and the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw that it was good.
text.DRB: and to govern the day & the night, and to divide the light and the darkness. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: And there was evening and there was morning, the fourth day.
text.Rotherham: So it was evening—and it was morning, a fourth day.
text.KJV: And the euening and the morning were the fourth day.
text.NABRE: Evening came, and morning followed—the fourth day.
text.DRB: And there was evening and morning that made the fourth day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the dome of the sky.”
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the waters swarm with an abundance of living soul, and, birds, shall fly over the earth, over the face of the expanse of the heavens.
text.KJV: And God said, Let the waters bring foorth aboundantly the mouing creature that hath life, and foule that may flie aboue the earth in the open firmament of heauen.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let the water teem with an abundance of living creatures, and on the earth let birds fly beneath the dome of the sky.
text.DRB: God also said: Let the waters bring forth creeping creature having life, and flying fowl, over the earth under the firmament of heaven.

text.NRSV: So God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves, of every kind, with which the waters swarm, and every winged bird of every kind. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And God created the great sea-monsters,—and every living soul that moveth—[with] which the waters swarmed after their kind and every winged bird—after its kind. And God saw that it was good.
text.KJV: And God created great whales, and euery liuing creature that moueth, which the waters brought forth aboundantly after their kinde, and euery winged foule after his kinde: and God saw that it was good.
text.NABRE: God created the great sea monsters and all kinds of crawling living creatures with which the water teems, and all kinds of winged birds. God saw that it was good,
text.DRB: And God created huge whales, and all living and moving creature, that the waters brought forth according to each sort, and all fowl according to their kind. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: God blessed them, saying, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God blessed them, saying,—Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let, the birds, multiply in the land.
text.KJV: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitfull, and multiply, and fill the waters in the Seas, and let foule multiply in the earth.
text.NABRE: and God blessed them, saying: Be fertile, multiply, and fill the water of the seas; and let the birds multiply on the earth.
text.DRB: And he blessed them saying: Increase and multiply, and replenish the waters of the sea: and let the birds be multiplied upon the earth.

text.NRSV: And there was evening and there was morning, the fifth day.
text.Rotherham: So it was evening—and it was morning, a fifth day.
text.KJV: And the euening and the morning were the fift day.
text.NABRE: Evening came, and morning followed—the fifth day.
text.DRB: And there was evening and morning that made the fifth day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let the earth bring forth living creatures of every kind: cattle and creeping things and wild animals of the earth of every kind.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the land, bring forth, living soul, after its kind, tame-beast and creeping thing and wild-beast, of the land, after its kind. And it was so.
text.KJV: ¶ And God said, Let the earth bring forth the liuing creature after his kinde, cattell, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kinde: and it was so.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let the earth bring forth every kind of living creature: tame animals, crawling things, and every kind of wild animal. And so it happened:
text.DRB: God said moreover: Let the earth bring forth living creature in his kind, cattle, and such as creep, and beasts of the earth according to their kinds: and it was so done.

text.NRSV: God made the wild animals of the earth of every kind, and the cattle of every kind, and everything that creeps upon the ground of every kind. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And God made the wild-beast of the land after its kind, and the tame-beast after its kind, and every creeping thing of the ground, after its kind. And God saw that it was good.
text.KJV: And God made the beast of the earth after his kinde, and cattell after their kinde, and euery thing that creepeth vpon the earth, after his kinde: and God saw that it was good.
text.NABRE: God made every kind of wild animal, every kind of tame animal, and every kind of thing that crawls on the ground. God saw that it was good.
text.DRB: And God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds, and cattle, and all that creepeth on the earth in his kind. And God saw that it was good,

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Let us make humankind in our image, according to our likeness; and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the wild animals of the earth, and over every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let us make man in our image after our likeness—and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the bird of the heavens and over the tame-beast—and over all the land, and over every creeping thing, that creepeth on the land.
text.KJV: ¶ And God said, Let vs make man in our Image, after our likenesse: and let them haue dominion ouer the fish of the sea, and ouer the foule of the aire, and ouer the cattell, and ouer all the earth, and ouer euery creeping thing that creepeth vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Let us make human beings in our image, after our likeness. Let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, the birds of the air, the tame animals, all the wild animals, and all the creatures that crawl on the earth.
text.DRB: and he said Let us make Man to our image & likeness: and let him have dominion over the fishes of the sea, and the fowls of the air, and the beasts, and the whole earth, and all creeping creature that moveth upon the earth.

text.NRSV: So God created humankind in his image, in the image of God he created them; male and female he created them.
text.Rotherham: And God created the man in his own image, In the image of God, created he him,—Male and female, created he, them.
text.KJV: So God created man in his owne Image, in the Image of God created hee him; male and female created hee them.
text.NABRE: God created mankind in his image; in the image of God he created them; male and female he created them.
text.DRB: And God created man to his own image: to the image of God he created him, male and female he created them.

text.NRSV: God blessed them, and God said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth and subdue it; and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the air and over every living thing that moves upon the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God blessed them, and God said to them Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth and subdue it,—and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the bird of the heavens, and over every living thing that moveth on the land.
text.KJV: And God blessed them, and God said vnto them, Be fruitfull, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it, and haue dominion ouer the fish of the sea, and ouer the foule of the aire, and ouer euery liuing thing that mooueth vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: God blessed them and God said to them: Be fertile and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it. Have dominion over the fish of the sea, the birds of the air, and all the living things that crawl on the earth.
text.DRB: And God blessed them, and saith: Increase and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it, and rule over the fishes and the sea, and fowls of the air & all living creatures that move upon the earth.

text.NRSV: God said, “See, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree with seed in its fruit; you shall have them for food.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Lo! I have given to you—every herb yielding seed which is on the face of all the land, and every tree wherein is the fruit of a tree yielding seed,—to you, shall it be, for food;
text.KJV: ¶ And God said, Behold, I haue giuen you euery herbe bearing seede, which is vpon the face of all the earth, and euery tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yeelding seed, to you it shall be for meat:
text.NABRE: God also said: See, I give you every seed-bearing plant on all the earth and every tree that has seed-bearing fruit on it to be your food;
text.DRB: And God said: Behold I have given you all manner of herb that seedeth upon the earth, and all trees that have in themselves seed of their own kind, to be your meat:

text.NRSV: And to every beast of the earth, and to every bird of the air, and to everything that creeps on the earth, everything that has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: and to every living thing of the land—and to every bird of the heavens, and to every thing that moveth on the land, wherein is a living soul, every green herb for food. And it was so.
text.KJV: And to euery beast of the earth, and to euery foule of the aire, and to euery thing that creepeth vpon the earth, wherein there is life, I haue giuen euery greene herbe for meat: and it was so.
text.NABRE: and to all the wild animals, all the birds of the air, and all the living creatures that crawl on the earth, I give all the green plants for food. And so it happened.
text.DRB: and to all beasts of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to all that move upon the earth, and wherein there is life, that they may have to feed upon. And it was so done.

text.NRSV: God saw everything that he had made, and indeed, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
text.Rotherham: And God saw every thing which he had made, and lo! it was very good. So it was evening—and it was morning, the sixth day.
text.KJV: And God saw euery thing that hee had made: and behold, it was very good. And the euening and the morning were the sixth day.
text.NABRE: God looked at everything he had made, and found it very good. Evening came, and morning followed—the sixth day.
text.DRB: And God saw all things that he had made, and they were very good. And there was evening and morning that made the sixth day.

text.NRSV: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all their multitude.
text.Rotherham: Thus were finished the heavens and the earth and all their host.
text.KJV: Thus the heauens and the earth were finished, and all the hoste of them.
text.NABRE: Thus the heavens and the earth and all their array were completed.
text.DRB: The heavens therefore & the earth were fully finished, and all the furniture of them.

text.NRSV: And on the seventh day God finished the work that he had done, and he rested on the seventh day from all the work that he had done.
text.Rotherham: Thus God finished, on the seventh day his work which he had made, and rested, on the seventh day, from all his work which he had made.
text.KJV: And on the seuenth day God ended his worke, which hee had made: And he rested on the seuenth day from all his worke, which he had made.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day God completed the work he had been doing; he rested on the seventh day from all the work he had undertaken.
text.DRB: And the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and rested the seventh day, from all work that he had done.

text.NRSV: So God blessed the seventh day and hallowed it, because on it God rested from all the work that he had done in creation.
text.Rotherham: And God blessed the seventh day, and hallowed it,—because therein, rested he from all his work which God, by creating, had made.
text.KJV: And God blessed the seuenth day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his worke, which God created and made.
text.NABRE: God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because on it he rested from all the work he had done in creation.
text.DRB: And he blessed the seventh day & sanctified it: because in it he had ceased from all his work which God created to make.

text.NRSV: These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created. In the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens,
text.Rotherham: These are the geneses of the heavens and the earth when they were created,—in the day when Yahweh God made earth and heavens.
text.KJV: ¶ These are the generations of the heauens, & of the earth, when they were created; in the day that the LORD God made the earth, and the heauens,
text.NABRE: This is the story of the heavens and the earth at their creation. When the Lord God made the earth and the heavens—
text.DRB: These are the generations of heaven & earth, when they were created in the day, when our Lord God made the heaven, and the earth,

text.NRSV: when no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up—for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was no one to till the ground;
text.Rotherham: Now, no bush of the field, as yet—was in the earth, and, no herb of the field, as yet had sprung up,—because Yahweh God had not sent rain on the earth, and, man, was there none to till the ground;
text.KJV: And euery plant of the field, before it was in the earth, and euery herbe of the field, before it grew: for the LORD God had not caused it to raine vpon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground.
text.NABRE: there was no field shrub on earth and no grass of the field had sprouted, for the Lord God had sent no rain upon the earth and there was no man to till the ground,
text.DRB: and every plant of the field, before it shot up in the earth, and every herb of the ground before it sprang. For our Lord God had not rained upon the earth; and man was not to till the earth:

text.NRSV: but a stream would rise from the earth, and water the whole face of the ground—
text.Rotherham: but, a vapour, went up from the earth,—and watered all the face of the ground.
text.KJV: But there went vp a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.
text.NABRE: but a stream was welling up out of the earth and watering all the surface of the ground—
text.DRB: But a spring rose out of the earth, watering all the overmost part of the earth.

text.NRSV: then the Lord God formed man from the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and the man became a living being.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed in his nostrils the breath of life—and man became a living soul.
text.KJV: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, & breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a liuing soule.
text.NABRE: then the Lord God formed the man out of the dust of the ground and blew into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living being.
text.DRB: Our Lord God therefore formed man of the slime of the earth: and breathed into his face the breath of life, & man became a living soul.

text.NRSV: And the Lord God planted a garden in Eden, in the east; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God planted a garden in Eden, on the east,—and put there the man whom he had formed.
text.KJV: ¶ And the LORD God planted a garden Eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
text.NABRE: The Lord God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and placed there the man whom he had formed.
text.DRB: And our Lord God had planted Paradise of pleasure from the beginning: wherein he placed man whom he had formed.

text.NRSV: Out of the ground the Lord God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food, the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God, caused to spring up, out of the ground, every tree pleasant to the sight and good for food,—and the tree of life, in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
text.KJV: And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow euery tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food: the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and euill.
text.NABRE: Out of the ground the Lord God made grow every tree that was delightful to look at and good for food, with the tree of life in the middle of the garden and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
text.DRB: And our Lord God brought forth of the ground all manner of trees, fair to behold, and pleasant to eat of: the tree of life also in the middle of Paradise: and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.

text.NRSV: A river flows out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides and becomes four branches.
text.Rotherham: Now, a river, was coming forth out of Eden, to water the garden,—and, from thence, it parted, and became four heads,
text.KJV: And a riuer went out of Eden to water the garden, and from thence it was parted, and became into foure heads.
text.NABRE: A river rises in Eden to water the garden; beyond there it divides and becomes four branches.
text.DRB: And a river issued out of the place of pleasure to water Paradise, which from thence is divided into four heads.

text.NRSV: The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
text.Rotherham: The name of the one, is Pishon,—the same, is that which surroundeth all the land of Havilah, where is gold;
text.KJV: The name of the first is Pison: that is it which compasseth the whole land of Hauilah, where there is gold.
text.NABRE: The name of the first is the Pishon; it is the one that winds through the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
text.DRB: The name of one is Phison: that is it which compasseth all the land of Hevilath, where gold groweth.

text.NRSV: and the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
text.Rotherham: moreover the gold of that land, is good,—there, is the bdellium, and the beryl stone,
text.KJV: And the gold of that land is good: There is Bdellium and the Onix stone.
text.NABRE: The gold of that land is good; bdellium and lapis lazuli are also there.
text.DRB: And the gold of that land is very good: there is found bdellium, and the stone onyx.

text.NRSV: The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Cush.
text.Rotherham: And the name of the second river, is Gihon,—the same, is that which surroundeth all the land of Cush,
text.KJV: And the name of the second riuer is Gihon: the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.
text.NABRE: The name of the second river is the Gihon; it is the one that winds all through the land of Cush.
text.DRB: And the name of the second river is Gehon: that is it which compasseth all the land of Ethiopia.

text.NRSV: The name of the third river is Tigris, which flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: And, the name of the third river, is Hiddekel, the same, is that which goeth in front of Assyria; and the fourth river, is Euphrates.
text.KJV: And the name of the third riuer is Hiddekel: that is it which goeth toward the East of Assyria: and the fourth riuer is Euphrates.
text.NABRE: The name of the third river is the Tigris; it is the one that flows east of Asshur. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
text.DRB: And the name of the third river is Tigris: that same passeth along by the Assyrians. And the fourth river, the same is Euphrates.

text.NRSV: The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and keep it.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh God took the man,—and placed him in the garden of Eden, to till it and to keep it.
text.KJV: And the LORD God tooke the man, and put him into the garden of Eden, to dresse it, and to keepe it.
text.NABRE: The Lord God then took the man and settled him in the garden of Eden, to cultivate and care for it.
text.DRB: Our Lord God therefore took man, & put him in the Paradise of pleasure, to work, & keep it.

text.NRSV: And the Lord God commanded the man, “You may freely eat of every tree of the garden;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God laid command on the man, saying,—Of every tree of the garden, thou mayest eat;
text.KJV: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, Of euery tree of the garden thou mayest freely eate.
text.NABRE: The Lord God gave the man this order: You are free to eat from any of the trees of the garden
text.DRB: And he commanded him saying: Of every tree of Paradise eat thou:

text.NRSV: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall die.”
text.Rotherham: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it,—for in the day thou eatest thereof, thou shalt die.
text.KJV: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and euill, thou shalt not eate of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof, thou shalt surely die.
text.NABRE: except the tree of knowledge of good and evil. From that tree you shall not eat; when you eat from it you shall die.
text.DRB: But of the tree of knowledge of good & evil eat thou not. For in what day soever thou shalt eat of it, thou shalt die the death.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God said, “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper as his partner.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God said, It is, not good, that the man should remain alone,—I will make for him a helper as his counterpart.
text.KJV: ¶ And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone: I will make him an helpe meet for him.
text.NABRE: The Lord God said: It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suited to him.
text.DRB: Our Lord God also said: It is not good for man to be alone: let us make him a help like unto himself.

text.NRSV: So out of the ground the Lord God formed every animal of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to the man to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called every living creature, that was its name.
text.Rotherham: Now Yahweh God had formed from the ground every living thing of the field and every bird of the heavens, which he brought in unto the man, that he might see what he should call it,—and, whatsoever the man should call it—any living soul, that, should be the name thereof.
text.KJV: And out of þ ground the LORD God formed euery beast of the field, and euery foule of the aire, and brought them vnto Adam, to see what he would call them: and whatsoeuer Adam called euery liuing creature, that was the name thereof.
text.NABRE: So the Lord God formed out of the ground all the wild animals and all the birds of the air, and he brought them to the man to see what he would call them; whatever the man called each living creature was then its name.
text.DRB: Our Lord God therefore having formed of clay all beasts of the earth, and fowls of the air; brought them to Adam, that he might see what to call them: for all that Adam called any living creature, the same is his name.

text.NRSV: The man gave names to all cattle, and to the birds of the air, and to every animal of the field; but for the man there was not found a helper as his partner.
text.Rotherham: So the man gave names to all the tame-beasts, and to the birds of the heavens, and to all the wild-beasts of the field,—but, for man, had there not been found a helper as his counterpart.
text.KJV: And Adam gaue names to all cattell, and to the foule of the aire, and to euery beast of the fielde: but for Adam there was not found an helpe meete for him.
text.NABRE: The man gave names to all the tame animals, all the birds of the air, and all the wild animals; but none proved to be a helper suited to the man.
text.DRB: And Adam called all beasts by their names, and all fowls of the air, and all cattle of the field: but unto Adam there was not found an helper like himself.

text.NRSV: So the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; then he took one of his ribs and closed up its place with flesh.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh God caused a deep sleep to fall on the man, and he slept,—and he took one of his ribs, and closed up flesh instead thereof.
text.KJV: And the LORD God caused a deepe sleepe to fall vpon Adam, and hee slept; and he tooke one of his ribs, and closed vp the flesh in stead thereof.
text.NABRE: So the Lord God cast a deep sleep on the man, and while he was asleep, he took out one of his ribs and closed up its place with flesh.
text.DRB: Our Lord God therefore cast a deep sleep upon Adam: and when he was fast asleep, he took one of his ribs, & filled up flesh for it.

text.NRSV: And the rib that the Lord God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God builded the rib which he had taken from the man, into a woman, and brought her in unto the man.
text.KJV: And the rib which the LORD God had taken from man, made hee a woman, & brought her vnto the man.
text.NABRE: The Lord God then built the rib that he had taken from the man into a woman. When he brought her to the man,
text.DRB: And our Lord God built the rib which he took of Adam into a woman, & brought her to Adam.

text.NRSV: Then the man said, “This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; this one shall be called Woman, for out of Man this one was taken.”
text.Rotherham: And the man said, This, one, now, is bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh! This, one shall be called Woman, for, out of a man, hath this one been taken.
text.KJV: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shalbe called woman, because shee was taken out of man.
text.NABRE: the man said: “This one, at last, is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; This one shall be called ‘woman,’ for out of man this one has been taken.”
text.DRB: And Adam said: This now is bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called woman, because she was taken out of man.

text.NRSV: Therefore a man leaves his father and his mother and clings to his wife, and they become one flesh.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, will a man leave his father and his mother, and cleave unto his wife, and they shall become one flesh.
text.KJV: Therefore shall a man leaue his father and his mother, and shall cleaue vnto his wife: and they shalbe one flesh.
text.NABRE: That is why a man leaves his father and mother and clings to his wife, and the two of them become one body.
text.DRB: Wherefore man shall leave his father & mother, & shall cleave to his wife, & they shall be two in one flesh.

text.NRSV: And the man and his wife were both naked, and were not ashamed.
text.Rotherham: And they were both of them naked, the man and his wife,—and put not each other to shame.
text.KJV: And they were both naked, the man & his wife, and were not ashamed.
text.NABRE: The man and his wife were both naked, yet they felt no shame.
text.DRB: And they were both naked, to wit Adam and his wife; and were not ashamed.

text.NRSV: Now the serpent was more crafty than any other wild animal that the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, “Did God say, ‘You shall not eat from any tree in the garden’?”
text.Rotherham: Now, the serpent, was more crafty than any living thing of the field which Yahweh God had made, so he said unto the woman, Can it really be that God hath said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?
text.KJV: Now the serpent was more subtill then any beast of the field, which the LORD God had made, and he said vnto the woman, Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of euery tree of the garden?
text.NABRE: Now the snake was the most cunning of all the wild animals that the Lord God had made. He asked the woman, “Did God really say, ‘You shall not eat from any of the trees in the garden’?”
text.DRB: But the serpent also was more subtile than all the beasts of the earth, which our Lord God had made. Which said to the woman: Why hath God commanded you, that you should not eat of every tree of Paradise?

text.NRSV: The woman said to the serpent, “We may eat of the fruit of the trees in the garden;
text.Rotherham: And the woman said unto the serpent,—Of the fruit of the trees of the garden, we may eat;
text.KJV: And the woman said vnto the serpent, Wee may eate of the fruite of the trees of the garden:
text.NABRE: The woman answered the snake: “We may eat of the fruit of the trees in the garden;
text.DRB: To whom the woman answered: Of the fruit of the trees that are in Paradise we do eat:

text.NRSV: but God said, ‘You shall not eat of the fruit of the tree that is in the middle of the garden, nor shall you touch it, or you shall die.’ ”
text.Rotherham: but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said Ye shall not eat of it neither shall ye touch it,—lest ye die.
text.KJV: But of the fruit of the tree, which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shal not eate of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.
text.NABRE: it is only about the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden that God said, ‘You shall not eat it or even touch it, or else you will die.’”
text.DRB: but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of Paradise, God hath commanded us that we should not eat: and that we should not touch it, lest perhaps we die.

text.NRSV: But the serpent said to the woman, “You will not die;
text.Rotherham: And the serpent said unto the woman,—Ye shall not die,
text.KJV: And the Serpent said vnto the woman, Ye shall not surely die.
text.NABRE: But the snake said to the woman: “You certainly will not die!
text.DRB: And the serpent said to the woman: No, you shall not die the death.

text.NRSV: for God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.”
text.Rotherham: For God doth know, that in the day ye eat thereof, then shall your eyes be opened,—and ye shall become like God, knowing good and evil.
text.KJV: For God doeth know, that in the day ye eate thereof, then your eyes shalbee opened: and yee shall bee as Gods, knowing good and euill.
text.NABRE: God knows well that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened and you will be like gods, who know good and evil.”
text.DRB: For God doth know that in what day soever you shall eat thereof, your eyes shall be opened: and you shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.

text.NRSV: So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was to be desired to make one wise, she took of its fruit and ate; and she also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate.
text.Rotherham: And, when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was desirable to the eyes and the tree was pleasant to make one knowing, then took she of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and she gave to her husband also, along with her, and he did eat.
text.KJV: And when the woman saw, that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, she tooke of the fruit thereof, and did eate, and gaue also vnto her husband with her, and hee did eate.
text.NABRE: The woman saw that the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eyes, and the tree was desirable for gaining wisdom. So she took some of its fruit and ate it; and she also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.
text.DRB: The woman therefore saw that the tree was good to eat, and fair to the eyes, and delectable to behold: and she took of the fruit thereof, & did eat, & gave to her husband, who did eat.

text.NRSV: Then the eyes of both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together and made loincloths for themselves.
text.Rotherham: Then were opened the eyes of them both, and they knew that, naked, they were,—so they tacked together fig-leaves, and made for themselves girdles,
text.KJV: And the eyes of them both were opened, & they knew that they were naked, and they sewed figge leaues together, and made themselues aprons.
text.NABRE: Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they knew that they were naked; so they sewed fig leaves together and made loincloths for themselves.
text.DRB: And the eyes of them both were opened: and when they perceived themselves to be naked, they sowed together leaves of a fig tree, and made themselves aprons.

text.NRSV: They heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden at the time of the evening breeze, and the man and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the Lord God among the trees of the garden.
text.Rotherham: Then heard they the sound of Yahweh God, walking to and fro in the garden at the breeze of the day,—so he hid himself—the man with his wife, from the face of Yahweh God, amid the trees of the garden.
text.KJV: And they heard the voyce of the LORD God, walking in the garden in the coole of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselues from the presence of the LORD God, amongst the trees of the garden.
text.NABRE: When they heard the sound of the Lord God walking about in the garden at the breezy time of the day, the man and his wife hid themselves from the Lord God among the trees of the garden.
text.DRB: And hearing the voice of our Lord God walking in Paradise at the afternoon air, Adam hid himself, and so did his wife from the face of our Lord God, amidst the trees of Paradise.

text.NRSV: But the Lord God called to the man, and said to him, “Where are you?”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God called unto the man,—and said to him, Where art thou?
text.KJV: And the LORD God called vnto Adam, and said vnto him, Where art thou?
text.NABRE: The Lord God then called to the man and asked him: Where are you?
text.DRB: And our Lord God called Adam, and said to him: Where art thou?

text.NRSV: He said, “I heard the sound of you in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, The sound of thee, heard I in the garden, and I was afraid, for, naked, was I, so I hid myself.
text.KJV: And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden: and I was afraid, because I was naked, and I hid my selfe.
text.NABRE: He answered, “I heard you in the garden; but I was afraid, because I was naked, so I hid.”
text.DRB: Who said: I heard thy voice in Paradise: and I feared, because I was naked, and I hid me.

text.NRSV: He said, “Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree of which I commanded you not to eat?”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Who told thee that, naked, thou wast? Of the tree whereof I commanded thee not to eat, hast thou eaten?
text.KJV: And he said, Who told thee, that thou wast naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee, that thou shouldest not eate?
text.NABRE: Then God asked: Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree of which I had forbidden you to eat?
text.DRB: To whom he said: And who hath told thee that thou wast naked, but that thou hast eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldst not eat?

text.NRSV: The man said, “The woman whom you gave to be with me, she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate.”
text.Rotherham: And the man said,—The woman whom thou didst put with me, she, gave me of the tree, so I did eat.
text.KJV: And the man said, The woman whom thou gauest to be with mee, shee gaue me of the tree, and I did eate.
text.NABRE: The man replied, “The woman whom you put here with me—she gave me fruit from the tree, so I ate it.”
text.DRB: And Adam said: The woman which thou gavest me to be my fellow-companion, gave me of the tree, & I did eat.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “What is this that you have done?” The woman said, “The serpent tricked me, and I ate.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh God to the woman, What is this that thou hast done? And the woman said, the serpent, deceived me, so I did eat.
text.KJV: And the LORD God said vnto the woman, What is this that thou hast done? And the woman said, The Serpent beguiled me, and I did eate.
text.NABRE: The Lord God then asked the woman: What is this you have done? The woman answered, “The snake tricked me, so I ate it.”
text.DRB: And our Lord God said to the woman: Why hast thou done this? Who answered: The serpent deceived me, and I did eat.

text.NRSV: The Lord God said to the serpent, “Because you have done this, cursed are you among all animals and among all wild creatures; upon your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh God unto the serpent—Because thou hast done this, Accursed, art thou above every tame-beast, and above every wild-beast of the field,—on thy belly, shalt thou go, and dust, shalt thou eat all the days of thy life.
text.KJV: And the LORD God said vnto the Serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou art cursed aboue all cattel, and aboue euery beast of the field: vpon thy belly shalt thou goe, and dust shalt thou eate, all the dayes of thy life.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord God said to the snake: Because you have done this, cursed are you among all the animals, tame or wild; On your belly you shall crawl, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life.
text.DRB: And our Lord God said to the serpent: Because thou hast done this thing, accursed art thou among all cattle, and beasts of the earth: upon thy breast shalt thou go, & earth shalt thou eat all the days of thy life.

text.NRSV: I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and hers; he will strike your head, and you will strike his heel.”
text.Rotherham: And enmity, will I put between thee, and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed,—He shall crush thy head, but, thou, shalt crush his heel.
text.KJV: And I will put enmitie betweene thee and the woman, and betweene thy seed and her seed: it shal bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heele.
text.NABRE: I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and hers; They will strike at your head, while you strike at their heel.
text.DRB: I will put enmity between thee & the woman, & thy seed and the seed of her: she shall bruise thy head in pieces, and thou shalt lie in wait of her heel.

text.NRSV: To the woman he said, “I will greatly increase your pangs in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children, yet your desire shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you.”
text.Rotherham: Unto the woman, he said, I will, increase, thy pain of pregnancy, In pain, shalt thou bear children,—Yet, unto thy husband, shall be thy longing, Though, he, rule over thee.
text.KJV: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorowe and thy conception. In sorow thou shalt bring forth children: and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and hee shall rule ouer thee.
text.NABRE: To the woman he said: I will intensify your toil in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children. Yet your urge shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you.
text.DRB: To the woman also he said: I will multiply thy travails and thy child-bearings: in travail shalt thou bring forth children, and thou shalt be under thy husband's power, and he shall have dominion over thee.

text.NRSV: And to the man he said, “Because you have listened to the voice of your wife, and have eaten of the tree about which I commanded you, ‘You shall not eat of it,’ cursed is the ground because of you; in toil you shall eat of it all the days of your life;
text.Rotherham: And, to the man, he said, Because thou didst hearken to the voice of thy wife, and so didst eat of the tree as to which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it, Accursed be the ground for thy sake, In pain, shalt thou eat of it, all the days of thy life;
text.KJV: And vnto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened vnto the voyce of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commaunded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eate of it: cursed is the ground for thy sake: in sorow shalt thou eate of it all the dayes of thy life.
text.NABRE: To the man he said: Because you listened to your wife and ate from the tree about which I commanded you, You shall not eat from it, Cursed is the ground because of you! In toil you shall eat its yield all the days of your life.
text.DRB: And to Adam he said: Because thou hast heard the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree wherefore I commanded thee that thou shouldst not eat, cursed is the earth in thy work: with much toiling shalt thou eat thereof all the days of thy life.

text.NRSV: thorns and thistles it shall bring forth for you; and you shall eat the plants of the field.
text.Rotherham: Thorn also and thistle, shall it shoot forth to thee,—when thou hast come to eat of the herb of the field:
text.KJV: Thornes also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee: and thou shalt eate the herbe of the field.
text.NABRE: Thorns and thistles it shall bear for you, and you shall eat the grass of the field.
text.DRB: Thorns and thistles shall it bring forth to thee, and thou shalt eat the herbs of the earth.

text.NRSV: By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread until you return to the ground, for out of it you were taken; you are dust, and to dust you shall return.”
text.Rotherham: In the sweat of thy face, shalt thou eat bread, until thou return to the ground, because therefrom, wast thou taken,—For, dust, thou art, And, unto dust, shalt thou return.
text.KJV: In the sweate of thy face shalt thou eate bread, till thou returne vnto the ground: for out of it wast thou taken, for dust thou art, and vnto dust shalt thou returne.
text.NABRE: By the sweat of your brow you shall eat bread, Until you return to the ground, from which you were taken; For you are dust, and to dust you shall return.
text.DRB: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return to earth, of which thou wast taken: because dust thou art, and into dust thou shalt return.

text.NRSV: The man named his wife Eve, because she was the mother of all living.
text.Rotherham: So the man called the name of his wife, Eve,—in that, she, was made mother of every one living.
text.KJV: And Adam called his wiues name Eue, because she was the mother of all liuing.
text.NABRE: The man gave his wife the name “Eve,” because she was the mother of all the living.
text.DRB: And Adam called the name of his wife, Eve: for because she was mother of all the living.

text.NRSV: And the Lord God made garments of skins for the man and for his wife, and clothed them.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God made for the man—and for his wife—tunics of skin and clothed them.
text.KJV: Unto Adam also, and to his wife, did the LORD God make coates of skinnes, and cloathed them.
text.NABRE: The Lord God made for the man and his wife garments of skin, with which he clothed them.
text.DRB: Our Lord God also made for Adam and his wife garments of skins, and clothed them:

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God said, “See, the man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil; and now, he might reach out his hand and take also from the tree of life, and eat, and live forever”—
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh God—Lo! man, hath become like one of us, in respect of knowing good and evil,—Now, therefore, lest he thrust forth his hand, and take even of the tree of life, and eat, and live to times age-abiding,—
text.KJV: ¶ And the LORD God said, Behold, the man is become as one of vs, to know good & euill. And now lest hee put foorth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eate and liue for euer:
text.NABRE: Then the Lord God said: See! The man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil! Now, what if he also reaches out his hand to take fruit from the tree of life, and eats of it and lives forever?
text.DRB: And said: Lo Adam is become as it were one of us, knowing good & evil: now therefore lest perhaps he reach forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever.

text.NRSV: therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from which he was taken.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh God put him forth from the garden of Eden,—to till the ground wherefrom he had been taken.
text.KJV: Therefore the LORD God sent him foorth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground, from whence he was taken.
text.NABRE: The Lord God therefore banished him from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from which he had been taken.
text.DRB: And our Lord God sent him out of the Paradise of pleasure, to work the earth of which he was taken.

text.NRSV: He drove out the man; and at the east of the garden of Eden he placed the cherubim, and a sword flaming and turning to guard the way to the tree of life.
text.Rotherham: So he expelled the man,—and caused to dwell—in front of the garden of Eden—cherubim and a brandishing sword—flame, to keep the way to the tree of life.
text.KJV: So he droue out the man: and he placed at the East of the garden of Eden, Cherubims, and a flaming sword, which turned euery way, to keepe the way of the tree of life.
text.NABRE: He expelled the man, stationing the cherubim and the fiery revolving sword east of the garden of Eden, to guard the way to the tree of life.
text.DRB: And he cast out Adam, and placed before the Paradise of pleasure Cherubins, and a flaming and a turning sword, for to keep the way of the tree of life.

text.NRSV: Now the man knew his wife Eve, and she conceived and bore Cain, saying, “I have produced a man with the help of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the man, having come to know Eve his wife,—she conceived and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a Man, even Yahweh!
text.KJV: And Adam knew Eue his wife, and shee conceiued, and bare Cain, and said, I haue gotten a man from the LORD.
text.NABRE: The man had intercourse with his wife Eve, and she conceived and gave birth to Cain, saying, “I have produced a male child with the help of the Lord.”
text.DRB: And Adam knew Eve his wife, who conceived and brought forth Cain, saying: I have gotten a man through God.

text.NRSV: Next she bore his brother Abel. Now Abel was a keeper of sheep, and Cain a tiller of the ground.
text.Rotherham: And she went on to bear his brother, Abel,—and Abel became a feeder of sheep, whereas Cain, was a tiller of the round.
text.KJV: And she againe bare his brother Abel, and Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.
text.NABRE: Next she gave birth to his brother Abel. Abel became a herder of flocks, and Cain a tiller of the ground.
text.DRB: And again she brought forth his brother Abel. And Abel was a shepherd, & Cain a husbandman.

text.NRSV: In the course of time Cain brought to the Lord an offering of the fruit of the ground,
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass, after certain days, that Cain brought in of the fruit of the ground, a present to Yahweh:
text.KJV: And in processe of time it came to passe, that Cain brought of the fruite of the ground, an offering vnto the LORD.
text.NABRE: In the course of time Cain brought an offering to the Lord from the fruit of the ground,
text.DRB: And it befell after many days that Cain offered of the fruits of the earth gifts to our Lord.

text.NRSV: and Abel for his part brought of the firstlings of his flock, their fat portions. And the Lord had regard for Abel and his offering,
text.Rotherham: Abel, also, even, he, brought in of the firstlings of his sheep, and of their fat, and Yahweh approved of Abel and of his present;
text.KJV: And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flocke, and of the fat thereof: and the LORD had respect vnto Abel, and to his offering.
text.NABRE: while Abel, for his part, brought the fatty portion of the firstlings of his flock. The Lord looked with favor on Abel and his offering,
text.DRB: Abel also offered of the first-begotten of his flock, and of their fat: and our Lord had respect of Abel, & to his gifts.

text.NRSV: but for Cain and his offering he had no regard. So Cain was very angry, and his countenance fell.
text.Rotherham: but of Cain and his present, he approved not,—and it angered Cain greatly, and his countenance fell.
text.KJV: But vnto Cain, and to his offring he had not respect: and Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.
text.NABRE: but on Cain and his offering he did not look with favor. So Cain was very angry and dejected.
text.DRB: But to Cain, and to his gifts he had not respect: And Cain was exceeding angry, and his countenance abated.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Cain, “Why are you angry, and why has your countenance fallen?
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh said unto Cain,—Wherefore hath it angered thee, and wherefore hath thy countenance fallen?
text.KJV: And the LORD said vnto Cain, Why art thou wroth? And why is thy countenance fallen?
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Cain: Why are you angry? Why are you dejected?
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Why art thou angry? and why is thy countenance fallen?

text.NRSV: If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is lurking at the door; its desire is for you, but you must master it.”
text.Rotherham: Shall it not, if thou do right, be lifted up? But if thou do not right, at the entrance a sin-bearer is lying,—Unto thee, moreover, shall be his longing, though, thou, rule over him.
text.KJV: If thou doe well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sinne lieth at the doore: And vnto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule ouer him.
text.NABRE: If you act rightly, you will be accepted; but if not, sin lies in wait at the door: its urge is for you, yet you can rule over it.
text.DRB: If thou do well, shall thou not receive again: but if thou doest ill, shall not thy sin forthwith be present at the door? But the lust thereof shall be under thee, and thou shalt have dominion over it.

text.NRSV: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let us go out to the field.” And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel, and killed him.
text.Rotherham: And Cain said unto Abel his brother Let us go into the field And it came to pass when they were in the field that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.
text.KJV: And Cain talked with Abel his brother: and it came to passe when they were in the field, that Cain rose vp against Abel his brother, and slew him.
text.NABRE: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let us go out in the field.” When they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.
text.DRB: And Cain said to Abel his brother: Let us go forth abroad. And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel, and slew him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?” He said, “I do not know; am I my brother’s keeper?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And he said I know not, the keeper of my brother, am, I?
text.KJV: ¶ And the LORD said vnto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And hee said, I know not: Am I my brothers keeper?
text.NABRE: Then the Lord asked Cain, Where is your brother Abel? He answered, “I do not know. Am I my brother’s keeper?”
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Cain: Where is Abel thy brother? Who answered: I know not, am I my brother's keeper?

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “What have you done? Listen; your brother’s blood is crying out to me from the ground!
text.Rotherham: And he said, What hast thou done? With a voice, the shed-blood of thy brother is crying out to me from the ground,
text.KJV: And he said, What hast thou done? the voyce of thy brothers blood cryeth vnto me, from the ground.
text.NABRE: God then said: What have you done? Your brother’s blood cries out to me from the ground!
text.DRB: And he said to him: what hast thou done? The voice of thy brother's blood cryeth to me out of the earth.

text.NRSV: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
text.Rotherham: Now therefore, accursed, art thou,—from the ground which hath opened her mouth, to receive the shed-blood of thy brother at thy hand.
text.KJV: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receiue thy brothers blood from thy hand.
text.NABRE: Now you are banned from the ground that opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
text.DRB: Now therefore cursed shalt thou be upon the earth, which hath opened her mouth, & received the blood of thy brother at thy hand.

text.NRSV: When you till the ground, it will no longer yield to you its strength; you will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Though thou till the ground, it shall not go on to give its vigour to thee. A wanderer and a fugitive, shalt thou be in the earth.
text.KJV: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yeeld vnto thee her strength: A fugitiue and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
text.NABRE: If you till the ground, it shall no longer give you its produce. You shall become a constant wanderer on the earth.
text.DRB: When thou shalt till it, it shall not yield to thee her fruit: a rogue and vagabond shalt thou be upon the earth.

text.NRSV: Cain said to the Lord, “My punishment is greater than I can bear!
text.Rotherham: And Cain said unto Yahweh—Greater is my punishment than I can bear.
text.KJV: And Cain said vnto the LORD, My punishment is greater, then I can beare.
text.NABRE: Cain said to the Lord: “My punishment is too great to bear.
text.DRB: And Cain said to our Lord: Mine iniquity is greater than that I may deserve pardon.

text.NRSV: Today you have driven me away from the soil, and I shall be hidden from your face; I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and anyone who meets me may kill me.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! thou has driven me out, this day, from off the face of the ground And from thy face, shall I be hid, So shall I become a wanderer and a fugitive in the earth. And it shall come to pass, whosoever findeth me, will slay me.
text.KJV: Behold, thou hast driuen me out this day from the face of the earth, and from thy face shall I be hid, and I shall be a fugitiue, and a vagabond in the earth: and it shall come to passe, that euery one that findeth me, shall slay me.
text.NABRE: Look, you have now banished me from the ground. I must avoid you and be a constant wanderer on the earth. Anyone may kill me at sight.”
text.DRB: Lo thou dost cast me out this day from the face of the earth, and from thy face shall I be hid, and I shall be a vagabond and fugitive on the earth: every one therefore that findeth me shall kill me.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, “Not so! Whoever kills Cain will suffer a sevenfold vengeance.” And the Lord put a mark on Cain, so that no one who came upon him would kill him.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said to him—Not so, whosoever slayeth Cain seven—fold, shall it be avenged. So Yahweh set, for Cain, a sign, that none finding him should smite him.
text.KJV: And the LORD said vnto him, Therefore whosoeuer slayeth Cain, vengeance shalbe taken on him seuen fold. And the LORD set a marke vpon Cain, lest any finding him, should kill him.
text.NABRE: Not so! the Lord said to him. If anyone kills Cain, Cain shall be avenged seven times. So the Lord put a mark on Cain, so that no one would kill him at sight.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: No, it shall not so be: but whosoever shall kill Cain, shall be punished seven-fold. And our Lord put a mark on Cain, that whosoever found him should not kill him.

text.NRSV: Then Cain went away from the presence of the Lord, and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
text.Rotherham: So Cain went forth from the presence of Yahweh,—and dwelt in the land of Nod, eastward of Eden.
text.KJV: ¶ And Cain went out from the presence of the LORD, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the East of Eden.
text.NABRE: Cain then left the Lord’s presence and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
text.DRB: And Cain went forth from the face of our Lord, and dwelt as a fugitive on the earth at the east side of Eden.

text.NRSV: Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch; and he built a city, and named it Enoch after his son Enoch.
text.Rotherham: And Cain knew his wife, and she conceived, and bare Enoch,—Now it happened that he was building a city, so he called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.
text.KJV: And Cain knew his wife, and she conceiued and bare Enoch, and hee builded a City, and called the name of the City, after the name of his sonne, Enoch.
text.NABRE: Cain had intercourse with his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch. Cain also became the founder of a city, which he named after his son Enoch.
text.DRB: And Cain knew his wife, who conceived and brought forth Enoch: And he built a city, and called the name thereof by the name of his son, Enoch.

text.NRSV: To Enoch was born Irad; and Irad was the father of Mehujael, and Mehujael the father of Methushael, and Methushael the father of Lamech.
text.Rotherham: And there was born to Enoch Irad, and, Irad, begat Mehujael,—and Mehujael, begat Methusael,—and Methusael, begat Lamech.
text.KJV: And vnto Enoch was borne Irad: and Irad begate Mehuiael, and Mehuiael begate Methusael, and Methusael begate Lamech.
text.NABRE: To Enoch was born Irad, and Irad became the father of Mehujael; Mehujael became the father of Methusael, and Methusael became the father of Lamech.
text.DRB: Moreover Enoch begat Irad, and Irad begat Maviael, and Maviael begat Mathusael, and Mathusael begat Lamech.

text.NRSV: Lamech took two wives; the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
text.Rotherham: And Lamech took to himself two wives,—the name of the one, was Adah, and, the name of the second Zillah.
text.KJV: ¶ And Lamech tooke vnto him two wiues: the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
text.NABRE: Lamech took two wives; the name of the first was Adah, and the name of the second Zillah.
text.DRB: Who took two wives, the name of the one was Ada, and the name of the other Sella.

text.NRSV: Adah bore Jabal; he was the ancestor of those who live in tents and have livestock.
text.Rotherham: And Adah bare Jabal,—he, was father of such as dwell in tents, and have cattle;
text.KJV: And Adah bare Iabal: he was the father of such as dwell in tents, and of such as haue cattell.
text.NABRE: Adah gave birth to Jabal, who became the ancestor of those who dwell in tents and keep livestock.
text.DRB: And Ada brought forth Iabel, who was the father of them that dwell in tents, and of herdsmen.

text.NRSV: His brother’s name was Jubal; he was the ancestor of all those who play the lyre and pipe.
text.Rotherham: and, the name of his brother, was Jubal,—he, was father of everyone handling lyre and flute,
text.KJV: And his brothers name was Iubal: hee was the father of all such as handle the harpe and organ.
text.NABRE: His brother’s name was Jubal, who became the ancestor of all who play the lyre and the reed pipe.
text.DRB: And his brother's name was Jubal: he was the father of them that sing on harp & organs.

text.NRSV: Zillah bore Tubal-cain, who made all kinds of bronze and iron tools. The sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
text.Rotherham: And, as for Zillah, she also, bare Tubal-Cain, a sharpener of every cutting instrument of bronze and iron,—and, the sister of Tubal-Cain, was Naamah.
text.KJV: And Zillah, she also bare Tubal-Cain, an instructer of euery artificer in brasse and iron: and the sister of Tubal-Cain was Naamah.
text.NABRE: Zillah, on her part, gave birth to Tubalcain, the ancestor of all who forge instruments of bronze and iron. The sister of Tubalcain was Naamah.
text.DRB: Sella also brought forth Tubalcain, who was a hammerer and worker in all work of brass and iron. And the sister of Tubalcain was Noema.

text.NRSV: Lamech said to his wives: “Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; you wives of Lamech, listen to what I say: I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for striking me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Lamech to his wives, Adah and Zillah! hear ye my voice, Ye wives of Lamech! give ear to my tale, For a man, have I slain in dealing my wounds, Yea, a youth, in smiting my blows:
text.KJV: And Lamech sayd vnto his wiues, Adah and Zillah, Heare my voyce, yee wiues of Lamech, hearken vnto my speech: for I haue slaine a man to my wounding, and a yong man to my hurt.
text.NABRE: Lamech said to his wives: “Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; wives of Lamech, listen to my utterance: I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for bruising me.
text.DRB: And Lamech said to his wives Ada and Sella: Hear my voice ye wives of Lamech, harken to my talk: for I have slain a man to the wounding of myself, and a stripling to mine own dry blow bruising.

text.NRSV: If Cain is avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy-sevenfold.”
text.Rotherham: If, sevenfold, be, the avenging of Cain, Then, of Lamech, seventy and seven.
text.KJV: If Cain shall bee auenged seuen fold, truely Lamech seuenty and seuen folde.
text.NABRE: If Cain is avenged seven times, then Lamech seventy-seven times.”
text.DRB: Seven-fold vengeance shall be taken of Cain: but of Lamech seventy times seven-fold.

text.NRSV: Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, for she said, “God has appointed for me another child instead of Abel, because Cain killed him.”
text.Rotherham: And Adam again knew his wife, and she bare a son, and called his name Seth,—For God hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, because Cain slew him!
text.KJV: ¶ And Adam knew his wife againe, and she bare a sonne, & called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed mee another seed in stead of Abel, whom Cain slew.
text.NABRE: Adam again had intercourse with his wife, and she gave birth to a son whom she called Seth. “God has granted me another offspring in place of Abel,” she said, “because Cain killed him.”
text.DRB: Adam also knew his wife again: and she brought forth a son, & called his name Seth, saying: God hath given me other seed for Abel, whom Cain slew.

text.NRSV: To Seth also a son was born, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to invoke the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And to Seth—to him also, was born a son, and he called his name Enosh,—then, was a beginning made, to call on the name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And to Seth, to him also there was borne a sonne, and he called his name Enos: then began men to call vpon the Name of the LORD.
text.NABRE: To Seth, in turn, a son was born, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to invoke the Lord by name.
text.DRB: But to Seth also was born a son, whom he called Enos, this man began to invocate the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: This is the list of the descendants of Adam. When God created humankind, he made them in the likeness of God.
text.Rotherham: This, is the record of the generations of Adam. In the day when God created man, In the likeness of God, made he him:
text.KJV: This is the booke of the generations of Adam: In the day that God created man, in the likenes of God made he him.
text.NABRE: This is the record of the descendants of Adam. When God created human beings, he made them in the likeness of God;
text.DRB: This is the book of the generation of Adam. In the day, when God created man, to the likeness of God made he him.

text.NRSV: Male and female he created them, and he blessed them and named them “Humankind” when they were created.
text.Rotherham: Male and female, created he them,—and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day they were created.
text.KJV: Male and female created hee them, and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.
text.NABRE: he created them male and female. When they were created, he blessed them and named them humankind.
text.DRB: Male and female created he them; and blessed them: and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.

text.NRSV: When Adam had lived one hundred thirty years, he became the father of a son in his likeness, according to his image, and named him Seth.
text.Rotherham: And Adam lived a hundred and thirty years, and begat [a son] in his likeness after his image,—and called his name Seth:
text.KJV: ¶ And Adam liued an hundred and thirtie yeeres, and begate a sonne in his owne likenesse, after his image; and called his name Seth.
text.NABRE: Adam was one hundred and thirty years old when he begot a son in his likeness, after his image; and he named him Seth.
text.DRB: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years; & begat to his own image and likeness, and called his name Seth.

text.NRSV: The days of Adam after he became the father of Seth were eight hundred years; and he had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and the days of Adam, after he begat Seth, were eight hundred years, and he begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And the dayes of Adam, after he had begotten Seth, were eight hundred yeeres: and he begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Adam lived eight hundred years after he begot Seth, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And the days of Adam, after he begat Seth, came to eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred thirty years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: So all the days of Adam which he lived, were nine hundred and thirty years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes that Adam liued, were nine hundred and thirtie yeeres: and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Adam was nine hundred and thirty years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the time that Adam lived, came to nine hundred & thirty years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Seth had lived one hundred five years, he became the father of Enosh.
text.Rotherham: And Seth lived a hundred and five years,—and begat Enosh:
text.KJV: And Seth liued an hundred and fiue yeeres: and begate Enos.
text.NABRE: When Seth was one hundred and five years old, he begot Enosh.
text.DRB: Set also lived an hundred five years, and begat Enos.

text.NRSV: Seth lived after the birth of Enosh eight hundred seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Seth lived, after he begat Enosh, eight hundred and seven years,—and begat sons and daughters:
text.KJV: And Seth liued, after he begate Enos, eight hundred and seuen yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Seth lived eight hundred and seven years after he begot Enosh, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Seth lived after he begat Enos, eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Seth were nine hundred twelve years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Seth were, nine hundred and I twelve years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Seth, were nine hundred and twelue yeeres, and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Seth was nine hundred and twelve years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Seth came to nine hundred & twelve years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Enosh had lived ninety years, he became the father of Kenan.
text.Rotherham: And Enosh lived ninety years,—and begat Kenan;
text.KJV: ¶ And Enos liued ninetie yeeres, and begate Cainan.
text.NABRE: When Enosh was ninety years old, he begot Kenan.
text.DRB: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan.

text.NRSV: Enosh lived after the birth of Kenan eight hundred fifteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Enosh lived after he begat Kenan, eight hundred and fifteen years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Enos liued after hee begate Cainan, eight hundred and fifteene yeeres, and begate sonnes & daughters.
text.NABRE: Enosh lived eight hundred and fifteen years after he begot Kenan, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: After whose birth he lived eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Enosh were nine hundred five years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Enos were nine hundred & fiue yeres; and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Enosh was nine hundred and five years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Enos came to nine hundred and five years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Kenan had lived seventy years, he became the father of Mahalalel.
