text.Rotherham: And Kenan, lived seventy years,—and begat Mahalalel;
text.KJV: ¶ And Cainan liued seuentie yeeres, and begate Mahalaleel.
text.NABRE: When Kenan was seventy years old, he begot Mahalalel.
text.DRB: Cainan also lived seventy years, & begat Malaleel.

text.NRSV: Kenan lived after the birth of Mahalalel eight hundred and forty years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Kenan lived after he begat Mahalalel, eight hundred and forty years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Cainan liued after he begate Mahalaleel, eight hundred and fourtie yeeres, & begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Kenan lived eight hundred and forty years after he begot Mahalalel, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Cainan lived after he begat Malaleel, eight hundred & forty years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Kenan were, nine hundred and ten years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And al the dayes of Cainan were nine hundred & ten yeres; and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Kenan was nine hundred and ten years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Cainan came to nine hundred and ten years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Mahalalel had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Jared.
text.Rotherham: And Mahalalel lived sixty-five years,—and begat Jared;
text.KJV: ¶ And Mahalaleel liued sixtie and fiue yeeres, and begat Iared.
text.NABRE: When Mahalalel was sixty-five years old, he begot Jared.
text.DRB: And Malaleel lived sixty five years and begat Jared.

text.NRSV: Mahalalel lived after the birth of Jared eight hundred thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Mahalalel lived after he begat Jared, eight hundred and thirty years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Mahalaleel liued after he begate Iared, eight hundred and thirtie yeeres, and begate sonnes & daughters.
text.NABRE: Mahalalel lived eight hundred and thirty years after he begot Jared, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Malaleel lived after he begat Jared, eight hundred & thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred ninety-five years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Mahalalel were, eight hundred and ninety-five years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Mahalaleel, were eight hundred ninetie and fiue yeeres, and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Mahalalel was eight hundred and ninety-five years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Malaleel came to eight hundred ninety five years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Jared had lived one hundred sixty-two years he became the father of Enoch.
text.Rotherham: And Jared lived, a hundred and sixty-two years, and begat Enoch;
text.KJV: ¶ And Iared liued an hundred sixtie and two yeeres, & he begat Enoch.
text.NABRE: When Jared was one hundred and sixty-two years old, he begot Enoch.
text.DRB: And Jared lived an hundred sixty two years, and begat Enoch.

text.NRSV: Jared lived after the birth of Enoch eight hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Jared lived after he begat Enoch, eight hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Iared liued after he begate Enoch, eight hundred yeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Jared lived eight hundred years after he begot Enoch, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty-two years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Jared were, nine hundred and sixty-two years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Iared were nine hundred sixtie and two yeeres, and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Jared was nine hundred and sixty-two years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Jared came to nine hundred sixty two years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Enoch had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Methuselah.
text.Rotherham: And Enoch lived sixty-five years,—and begat Methuselah;
text.KJV: ¶ And Enoch liued sixtie and fiue yeeres, and begate Methuselah.
text.NABRE: When Enoch was sixty-five years old, he begot Methuselah.
text.DRB: Moreover Enoch lived sixty five years, and begat Mathusala.

text.NRSV: Enoch walked with God after the birth of Methuselah three hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Enoch walked with God, after he begat Methuselah, three hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Enoch walked with God, after he begate Methuselah, three hundred yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Enoch walked with God after he begot Methuselah for three hundred years, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Enoch walked with God: and lived after he begat Mathusala, three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty-five years.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Enoch were, three hundred and sixty-five years;
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Enoch, were three hundred sixtie and fiue yeeres.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Enoch was three hundred and sixty-five years.
text.DRB: And all the days of Enoch came to three hundred sixty five years.

text.NRSV: Enoch walked with God; then he was no more, because God took him.
text.Rotherham: and Enoch walked with God,—and was not for God had taken him.
text.KJV: And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God tooke him.
text.NABRE: Enoch walked with God, and he was no longer here, for God took him.
text.DRB: And he walked with God, and was seen no more: because God took him.

text.NRSV: When Methuselah had lived one hundred eighty-seven years, he became the father of Lamech.
text.Rotherham: And Methuselah lived, a hundred and eighty-seven years,—and begat Lamech;
text.KJV: And Methuselah liued an hundred eightie and seuen yeeres, and begat Lamech.
text.NABRE: When Methuselah was one hundred and eighty-seven years old, he begot Lamech.
text.DRB: Mathusala also lived an hundred eighty seven years, and begat Lamech.

text.NRSV: Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech seven hundred eighty-two years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Methuselah lived, after he begat Lamech, seven hundred and eighty-two years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Methuselah liued, after hee begate Lamech, seuen hundred, eightie and two yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Methuselah lived seven hundred and eighty-two years after he begot Lamech, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Mathusala lived, after he begat Lamech, seven hundred eighty two years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty-nine years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Methuselah were, nine hundred and sixty-nine years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Methuselah were nine hundred, sixtie and nine yeeres, and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Methuselah was nine hundred and sixty-nine years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Mathusala came to nine hundred sixty nine years, and he died.

text.NRSV: When Lamech had lived one hundred eighty-two years, he became the father of a son;
text.Rotherham: And Lamech lived, a hundred and eighty-two years,—and begat a son;
text.KJV: ¶ And Lamech liued an hundred eightie and two yeeres: and begate a sonne.
text.NABRE: When Lamech was one hundred and eighty-two years old, he begot a son
text.DRB: And Lamech lived an hundred eighty two years, & begat a son:

text.NRSV: he named him Noah, saying, “Out of the ground that the Lord has cursed this one shall bring us relief from our work and from the toil of our hands.”
text.Rotherham: and he called his name Noah saying,—This, one shall give us rest from our work, And from the grievous toil of our hands, By reason of the ground which Yahweh hath cursed,
text.KJV: And he called his name Noah, saying; This same shall comfort vs, concerning our woorke and toyle of our hands, because of the ground, which the LORD hath cursed.
text.NABRE: and named him Noah, saying, “This one shall bring us relief from our work and the toil of our hands, out of the very ground that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text.DRB: and he called his name Noe, saying: This son shall comfort us from the works & labours of our hands on the earth, which our Lord cursed.

text.NRSV: Lamech lived after the birth of Noah five hundred ninety-five years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: And Lamech lived, after he begat Noah, five hundred and ninety-five years,—and begat sons and daughters;
text.KJV: And Lamech liued, after hee begate Noah, fiue hundred ninetie and fiue yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Lamech lived five hundred and ninety-five years after he begot Noah, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Lamech lived, after he begat Noe, five hundred ninety five years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy-seven years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Lamech were seuen hundred seuentie and seuen yeeres, and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Lamech was seven hundred and seventy-seven years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all the days of Lamech came to seven hundred seventy seven years, and he died. And Noe when he was five hundred years old, begat Sem, Cham, and Japhet.

text.NRSV: After Noah was five hundred years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text.Rotherham: And Noah was five hundred years old,—and Noah begat Shem, Ham and Japheth.
text.KJV: And Noah was fiue hundred yeeres olde: and Noah begate Sem, Ham, and Iapheth.
text.NABRE: When Noah was five hundred years old, he begot Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

text.NRSV: When people began to multiply on the face of the ground, and daughters were born to them,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when men had begun to multiply on the face of the ground and daughters had been born to them,
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were borne vnto them:
text.NABRE: When human beings began to grow numerous on the earth and daughters were born to them,
text.DRB: And after that men began to be multiplied upon the earth, and had procreation of daughters,

text.NRSV: the sons of God saw that they were fair; and they took wives for themselves of all that they chose.
text.Rotherham: that the sons of God saw the daughters of men! that they were, fair,—so they took to themselves wives of whomsoever they chose,
text.KJV: That the sonnes of God saw the daughters of men, that they were faire, and they took them wiues, of all which they chose.
text.NABRE: the sons of God saw how beautiful the daughters of human beings were, and so they took for their wives whomever they pleased.
text.DRB: the sons of God seeing the daughters of men that they were fair, took to themselves wives out of all which they had chosen.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “My spirit shall not abide in mortals forever, for they are flesh; their days shall be one hundred twenty years.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said—My spirit shall not rule in man to times age—abiding, for that, he also, is flesh,—Yet his days shall be a hundred and twenty years.
text.KJV: And the LORD said, My Spirit shall not alwayes striue with man; for that hee also is flesh: yet his dayes shalbe an hundred and twenty yeeres.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said: My spirit shall not remain in human beings forever, because they are only flesh. Their days shall comprise one hundred and twenty years.
text.DRB: And God said: My spirit shall not remain in man for ever, because he is flesh: and his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.

text.NRSV: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days—and also afterward—when the sons of God went in to the daughters of humans, who bore children to them. These were the heroes that were of old, warriors of renown.
text.Rotherham: The giants, were in the earth in those days, and also, after that, when the sons of God began to go in unto the daughters of men, and sons were born to them, the same, were the heroes that were from age-past times the men of renown.
text.KJV: There were Giants in the earth in those daies: and also after that, when the sonnes of God came in vnto the daughters of men, & they bare children to them; the same became mightie men, which were of old, men of renowme.
text.NABRE: The Nephilim appeared on earth in those days, as well as later, after the sons of God had intercourse with the daughters of human beings, who bore them sons. They were the heroes of old, the men of renown.
text.DRB: And Giants were upon the earth in those days. For after the sons of God did company with the daughters of men, & they brought forth children, these be the mighty of the old world, famous men.

text.NRSV: The Lord saw that the wickedness of humankind was great in the earth, and that every inclination of the thoughts of their hearts was only evil continually.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh saw that great, was the wickedness of man in the earth, and that, every purpose of the devices of his heart, was only wicked all the day;
text.KJV: ¶ And God saw, that the wickednes of man was great in the earth, and that euery imagination of the thoughts of his heart was onely euill continually.
text.NABRE: When the Lord saw how great the wickedness of human beings was on earth, and how every desire that their heart conceived was always nothing but evil,
text.DRB: And God seeing the malice of men was much on the earth, & that all the cogitation of their heart was bent to evil at all times,

text.NRSV: And the Lord was sorry that he had made humankind on the earth, and it grieved him to his heart.
text.Rotherham: and it grieved Yahweh, that he had made man in the earth,—and he took sorrow unto his heart.
text.KJV: And it repented the LORD that he had made man on the earth, and it grieued him at his heart.
text.NABRE: the Lord regretted making human beings on the earth, and his heart was grieved.
text.DRB: it repented him that he had made man on the earth. And touched inwardly with sorrow of heart,

text.NRSV: So the Lord said, “I will blot out from the earth the human beings I have created—people together with animals and creeping things and birds of the air, for I am sorry that I have made them.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said—I must wipe off man whom I created from off the face of the ground, from man unto beast unto creeping thing, and unto the bird of the heavens,—for I am grieved that I made them.
text.KJV: And the LORD said, I will destroy man, whom I haue created, from the face of the earth: both man and beast, and the creeping thing, and the foules of the aire: for it repenteth me that I haue made them.
text.NABRE: So the Lord said: I will wipe out from the earth the human beings I have created, and not only the human beings, but also the animals and the crawling things and the birds of the air, for I regret that I made them.
text.DRB: I will, saith he, clean take away man, whom I have created, from the face of the earth, from man even to beasts, from that which creepeth even unto the fowls of the air. For it repenteth me that I have made them.

text.NRSV: But Noah found favor in the sight of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, Noah, had found favour in the eyes, of Yahweh.
text.KJV: But Noah found grace in the eyes of the LORD.
text.NABRE: But Noah found favor with the Lord.
text.DRB: But Noe found grace before our Lord.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Noah. Noah was a righteous man, blameless in his generation; Noah walked with God.
text.Rotherham: These, are the generations of Noah, Noah, was, a righteous man blameless, in his generations,—with God, did Noah walk.
text.KJV: ¶ These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a iust man, and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Noah. Noah was a righteous man and blameless in his generation; Noah walked with God.
text.DRB: These are the generations of Noe: Noe was a just and perfect man in his generations, he did walk with God.

text.NRSV: And Noah had three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text.Rotherham: And Noah begat three sons,—Shem, Ham and Japheth.
text.KJV: And Noah begate three sonnes: Sem, Ham, and Iapheth.
text.NABRE: Noah begot three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text.DRB: And he begat three sons, Sem, Cham, and Japheth.

text.NRSV: Now the earth was corrupt in God’s sight, and the earth was filled with violence.
text.Rotherham: And the earth corrupted itself before God,—and the earth was filled with violence,
text.KJV: The earth also was corrupt before God; and the earth was filled with violence.
text.NABRE: But the earth was corrupt in the view of God and full of lawlessness.
text.DRB: And the earth was corrupted before God, & was replenished with iniquity.

text.NRSV: And God saw that the earth was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted its ways upon the earth.
text.Rotherham: And God beheld the earth, and lo! it had corrupted itself,—surely all flesh had corrupted its way, on the earth.
text.KJV: And God looked vpon the earth, and behold, it was corrupt: for all flesh had corrupted his way vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: When God saw how corrupt the earth had become, since all mortals had corrupted their ways on earth,
text.DRB: And when God had perceived that the earth was corrupted (for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth)

text.NRSV: And God said to Noah, “I have determined to make an end of all flesh, for the earth is filled with violence because of them; now I am going to destroy them along with the earth.
text.Rotherham: So God said unto Noah: the end of all flesh, hath come in before me, for, filled, is the earth with violence, because of them,—behold me, then, destroying them with the earth.
text.KJV: And God said vnto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before mee; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
text.NABRE: God said to Noah: I see that the end of all mortals has come, for the earth is full of lawlessness because of them. So I am going to destroy them with the earth.
text.DRB: He said to Noe: The end of all flesh is come before me, the earth is replenished with iniquity from the face of them, and I will destroy them with the earth.

text.NRSV: Make yourself an ark of cypress wood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and out with pitch.
text.Rotherham: Make for thee an ark of timbers of gopher, rooms, shalt thou make with the ark,—and thou shalt cover it within and without with pitch.
text.KJV: ¶ Make thee an Arke of Gopher-wood: roomes shalt thou make in the arke, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
text.NABRE: Make yourself an ark of gopherwood, equip the ark with various compartments, and cover it inside and out with pitch.
text.DRB: Make thee an ark of timber plank: cabinets shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with bitume.

text.NRSV: This is how you are to make it: the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is how thou shalt make it,—three hundred cubits, the length of the ark, fifty cubits, the breadth thereof, and thirty cubits the height thereof.
text.KJV: And this is the fashion, which thou shalt make it of: the length of the arke shalbe three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirtie cubits.
text.NABRE: This is how you shall build it: the length of the ark will be three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
text.DRB: And thus shalt thou make it. The length of the Ark shall be three hundred cubits: fifty cubits the breadth, and thirty cubits the height of it.

text.NRSV: Make a roof for the ark, and finish it to a cubit above; and put the door of the ark in its side; make it with lower, second, and third decks.
text.Rotherham: A place for light, shalt thou make to the ark and to a cubit, shalt thou finish it upwards, and the opening of the ark—in the side thereof, shalt thou put,—with lower, second and third stories, shalt thou make it.
text.KJV: A window shalt thou make to the arke, and in a cubite shalt thou finish it aboue; and the doore of the arke shalt thou set in the side thereof: With lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
text.NABRE: Make an opening for daylight and finish the ark a cubit above it. Put the ark’s entrance on its side; you will make it with bottom, second and third decks.
text.DRB: Thou shalt make a window in the ark, & in a cubit finish the top of it: & the door of the ark thou shalt set at the side below, middle chambers & third lofts shalt thou make in it.

text.NRSV: For my part, I am going to bring a flood of waters on the earth, to destroy from under heaven all flesh in which is the breath of life; everything that is on the earth shall die.
text.Rotherham: And, I, behold me! bringing in the flood—even waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh wherein is the spirit of life, from under the heavens,—everything that is in the earth, shall cease to breathe:
text.KJV: And behold, I, euen I doe bring a flood of waters vpon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life from vnder heauen, and euery thing that is in the earth shall die.
text.NABRE: I, on my part, am about to bring the flood waters on the earth, to destroy all creatures under the sky in which there is the breath of life; everything on earth shall perish.
text.DRB: Behold I will bring the waters of a great flood upon the earth, that I may destroy all flesh wherein there is breath of life under heaven. All things that are in the earth shall be consumed.

text.NRSV: But I will establish my covenant with you; and you shall come into the ark, you, your sons, your wife, and your sons’ wives with you.
text.Rotherham: therefore will I establish my covenant with thee,—and thou shalt enter into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives, with thee.
text.KJV: But with thee wil I establish my Couenant: and thou shalt come into the Arke, thou, and thy sonnes, and thy wife, and thy sonnes wiues with thee.
text.NABRE: I will establish my covenant with you. You shall go into the ark, you and your sons, your wife and your sons’ wives with you.
text.DRB: And I will establish my covenant with thee: and thou shalt enter into the ark, thou and thy sons, and thy wife, and the wives of thy sons with thee.

text.NRSV: And of every living thing, of all flesh, you shall bring two of every kind into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.
text.Rotherham: Moreover of all the living creatures of all flesh—two of each, shalt thou bring into the ark to keep alive with thee,—male and female, shall they be.
text.KJV: And of euery liuing thing of all flesh, two of euery sort shalt thou bring into the Arke, to keepe them aliue with thee: they shall be male and female.
text.NABRE: Of all living creatures you shall bring two of every kind into the ark, one male and one female, to keep them alive along with you.
text.DRB: And of all living creatures of all flesh, thou shalt bring pairs into the ark, that they may live with thee: of the male sex and the female.

text.NRSV: Of the birds according to their kinds, and of the animals according to their kinds, of every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of every kind shall come in to you, to keep them alive.
text.Rotherham: Of the bird after its kind and of the beast after its kind, [and] of every creeping thing of the ground after its kind, two of each, shall come in unto thee. for keeping alive.
text.KJV: Of fowles after their kinde, and of cattel after their kinde: of euery creeping thing of the earth after his kinde, two of euery sort shall come vnto thee, to keepe them aliue.
text.NABRE: Of every kind of bird, of every kind of animal, and of every kind of thing that crawls on the ground, two of each will come to you, that you may keep them alive.
text.DRB: Of fowls according to their kind, and beasts in their kind, and of all that creepeth on the earth, according to their kind: pairs of all sorts shall enter in with thee, that they may live.

text.NRSV: Also take with you every kind of food that is eaten, and store it up; and it shall serve as food for you and for them.”
text.Rotherham: But thou, take to thee of all food that is eaten, and gather it unto thee,—and it shall be for thee and for them for food.
text.KJV: And take thou vnto thee of all food that is eaten, and thou shalt gather it to thee; and it shall be for food, for thee, and for them.
text.NABRE: Moreover, you are to provide yourself with all the food that is to be eaten, and store it away, that it may serve as provisions for you and for them.
text.DRB: Thou shalt take therefore with thee of all meats, that may be eaten, and thou shalt lay them up with thee: & they shall be meat for thee and them.

text.NRSV: Noah did this; he did all that God commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Noah did [so],—according to all that God commanded him, so did he.
text.KJV: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.
text.NABRE: Noah complied; he did just as God had commanded him.
text.DRB: Noe therefore did all things which God commanded him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Noah, “Go into the ark, you and all your household, for I have seen that you alone are righteous before me in this generation.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said to Noah, Enter thou and all thy house, into the ark,—for, thee, have I seen righteous before me, in this generation.
text.KJV: And the LORD saide vnto Noah, Come thou and all thy house into the Arke: for thee haue I seene righteous before me, in this generation.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Noah: Go into the ark, you and all your household, for you alone in this generation have I found to be righteous before me.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Get thee in, thou and all thy house into the ark; for I have seen thee just in my sight in this generation.

text.NRSV: Take with you seven pairs of all clean animals, the male and its mate; and a pair of the animals that are not clean, the male and its mate;
text.Rotherham: Of all the clean beasts, shalt thou take to thee by sevens, a male and his female,—and, of the beasts that are not clean, shall be two a male and his female.
text.KJV: Of euery cleane beast thou shalt take to thee by seuens, the male and his female: and of beastes that are not cleane, by two, the male and his female.
text.NABRE: Of every clean animal, take with you seven pairs, a male and its mate; and of the unclean animals, one pair, a male and its mate;
text.DRB: Of all beasts that are clean, thou shalt take seven and seven, male and female:

text.NRSV: and seven pairs of the birds of the air also, male and female, to keep their kind alive on the face of all the earth.
text.Rotherham: Also of the bird of the heavens, by sevens, male and female,—to keep alive a seed on the face of all the earth.
text.KJV: Of fowles also of the aire, by seuens, the male & the female; to keepe seed aliue vpon the face of all the earth.
text.NABRE: likewise, of every bird of the air, seven pairs, a male and a female, to keep their progeny alive over all the earth.
text.DRB: but of the beasts that are unclean two and two, male and female. Yea and of the fowls also of the air seven and seven, male and female: that seed may be saved upon the face of the whole earth.

text.NRSV: For in seven days I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights; and every living thing that I have made I will blot out from the face of the ground.”
text.Rotherham: For in seven days more, I, am sending rain on the earth, forty days and forty nights,—so will I wipe out all the living things which I have made, from off the face of the ground.
text.KJV: For yet seuen dayes, and I will cause it to raine vpon the earth, fortie dayes, and forty nights: and euery liuing substance that I haue made, will I destroy, frō off the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: For seven days from now I will bring rain down on the earth for forty days and forty nights, and so I will wipe out from the face of the earth every being that I have made.
text.DRB: For yet a while, and after seven days I will rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights: and I will clean destroy all substance that I have made, from the face of the earth.

text.NRSV: And Noah did all that the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Noah did,—according to all that Yahweh commanded him.
text.KJV: And Noah did according vnto all that the LORD commanded him.
text.NABRE: Noah complied, just as the Lord had commanded.
text.DRB: Noe therefore did all things which our Lord had commanded him.

text.NRSV: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came on the earth.
text.Rotherham: Now, Noah, was six hundred years old,—when, the flood, came, even waters on the earth.
text.KJV: And Noah was sixe hundred yeeres old, when the flood of waters was vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood came upon the earth.
text.DRB: And he was six hundred years old, when the waters of the flood flowed over the earth.

text.NRSV: And Noah with his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives went into the ark to escape the waters of the flood.
text.Rotherham: So Noah entered, and his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives with him into the ark,—from before the waters of the flood,
text.KJV: ¶ And Noah went in, and his sonnes, and his wife, and his sonnes wiues with him, into the Arke, because of the waters of the Flood.
text.NABRE: Together with his sons, his wife, and his sons’ wives, Noah went into the ark because of the waters of the flood.
text.DRB: And Noe entered and his sons, his wife and the wives of his sons with him into the ark, because of the waters of the flood.

text.NRSV: Of clean animals, and of animals that are not clean, and of birds, and of everything that creeps on the ground,
text.Rotherham: Of the beasts that were clean, and of the beasts that were not clean,—and of the birds, and everything that creepeth on the ground,
text.KJV: Of cleane beasts, & of beasts that are not cleane, & of fowles, and of euery thing that creepeth vpon the earth,
text.NABRE: Of the clean animals and the unclean, of the birds, and of everything that crawls on the ground,
text.DRB: Of beasts also the clean and the unclean, and of fowls, and of all that moveth upon the earth.

text.NRSV: two and two, male and female, went into the ark with Noah, as God had commanded Noah.
text.Rotherham: two and two, went they in unto Noah, into the ark male and female,—as God commanded Noah.
text.KJV: There went in two and two vnto Noah into the Arke, the male & the female, as God had commanded Noah.
text.NABRE: two by two, male and female came to Noah into the ark, just as God had commanded him.
text.DRB: two and two went to Noe into the ark, male and female, as our Lord had commanded Noe.

text.NRSV: And after seven days the waters of the flood came on the earth.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the seven days,—that the waters of the flood, came on the earth.
text.KJV: And it came to passe after seuen dayes, that the waters of the Flood were vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: When the seven days were over, the waters of the flood came upon the earth.
text.DRB: And after the seven days were passed, the waters of the flood flowed over the earth.

text.NRSV: In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on that day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the windows of the heavens were opened.
text.Rotherham: In the six hundredth year, the year of the life of Noah. in the second month on the seventeenth day of the month on this day, were burst open all the fountains of the great roaring deep, and the windows of the heavens, were set open.
text.KJV: ¶ In the sixe hundredth yeere of Noahs life, in the second moneth, the seuenteenth day of the moneth, the same day, were al the fountaines of the great deepe broken vp, and the windowes of heauen were opened.
text.NABRE: In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month: on that day All the fountains of the great abyss burst forth, and the floodgates of the sky were opened.
text.DRB: In the six hundred year of the life of Noe, in the second month, in the seventeenth day of the month, all the fountains of the great depth were broken up, and the flood-gates of heaven were opened:

text.NRSV: The rain fell on the earth forty days and forty nights.
text.Rotherham: (And it came to pass that the heavy rain was on the earth,—forty days and forty nights.)
text.KJV: And the raine was vpon the earth, fortie dayes, and fortie nights.
text.NABRE: For forty days and forty nights heavy rain poured down on the earth.
text.DRB: and the rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights.

text.NRSV: On the very same day Noah with his sons, Shem and Ham and Japheth, and Noah’s wife and the three wives of his sons entered the ark,
text.Rotherham: On this selfsame day, entered Noah, and Shem and Ham and Japheth. Noah’s sons,—and Noah’s wife, and his sons’ three wives with them into the ark:
text.KJV: In the selfe same day entred Noah, and Sem, and Ham, and Iapheth, the sonnes of Noah, and Noahs wife, and the three wiues of his sonnes with them, into the Arke,
text.NABRE: On the very same day, Noah and his sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and Noah’s wife, and the three wives of Noah’s sons had entered the ark,
text.DRB: In the very point of that day entered Noe, and Sem, and Cham, and Japheth his sons; and his wife, and the three wives of his sons with them into the ark.

text.NRSV: they and every wild animal of every kind, and all domestic animals of every kind, and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth, and every bird of every kind—every bird, every winged creature.
text.Rotherham: they, and all the wild-beasts after their kind and all the tame-beasts after their kind, and all the creeping things that creep on the earth after their kind,—and all the birds after their kind, every bird of every wing.
text.KJV: They, and euery beast after his kinde, & all the cattell after their kinde: and euery creeping thing that creepeth vpon the earth after his kinde, and euery foule after his kinde, euery birde of euery sort.
text.NABRE: together with every kind of wild animal, every kind of tame animal, every kind of crawling thing that crawls on the earth, and every kind of bird.
text.DRB: they and every beast according to their kind, and all cattle in their kind, and all that moveth upon the earth according to their kind, and all fowl according to their kind, all birds, and all that fly.

text.NRSV: They went into the ark with Noah, two and two of all flesh in which there was the breath of life.
text.Rotherham: So they entered in unto Noah, into the ark,—two and two of all flesh, wherein was the spirit of life.
text.KJV: And they went in vnto Noah into the Arke, two and two of all flesh, wherein is the breath of life.
text.NABRE: Pairs of all creatures in which there was the breath of life came to Noah into the ark.
text.DRB: went to Noe into the ark, two and two of all flesh, wherein there was breath of life.

text.NRSV: And those that entered, male and female of all flesh, went in as God had commanded him; and the Lord shut him in.
text.Rotherham: And, they that entered, male and female of all flesh, entered, as God commanded him,—and Yahweh shut him in round about.
text.KJV: And they that went in, went in male and female of all flesh, as God had commaunded him: and the LORD shut him in.
text.NABRE: Those that entered were male and female; of all creatures they came, as God had commanded Noah. Then the Lord shut him in.
text.DRB: And such as entered in, male and female of all flesh did enter in, as God had commanded him: and our Lord shut him in on the out side.

text.NRSV: The flood continued forty days on the earth; and the waters increased, and bore up the ark, and it rose high above the earth.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that the flood was forty days on the earth,—and the waters increased and bare up the ark, and it was lifted high above the earth,
text.KJV: And the Flood was fortie dayes vpon the earth, and the waters increased, and bare vp the Arke, and it was lift vp aboue the earth.
text.NABRE: The flood continued upon the earth for forty days. As the waters increased, they lifted the ark, so that it rose above the earth.
text.DRB: And the flood grew forty days upon the earth: and the waters increased, and lifted up the ark on high from the earth.

text.NRSV: The waters swelled and increased greatly on the earth; and the ark floated on the face of the waters.
text.Rotherham: And the waters prevailed and increased greatly, on the earth,—and the ark went its way on the face of the waters.
text.KJV: And the waters preuailed, and were encreased greatly vpon the earth: and the Arke went vpon the face of the waters.
text.NABRE: The waters swelled and increased greatly on the earth, but the ark floated on the surface of the waters.
text.DRB: For they overflowed exceedingly; and filled all on the face of the earth: moreover the ark fleeted upon the waters.

text.NRSV: The waters swelled so mightily on the earth that all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered;
text.Rotherham: Yea the waters, prevailed very greatly, on the earth,—so that all the high mountains became covered, that were under all the heavens:
text.KJV: And the waters preuailed exceedingly vpon the earth, and all the high hils, that were vnder the whole heauen, were couered.
text.NABRE: Higher and higher on the earth the waters swelled, until all the highest mountains under the heavens were submerged.
text.DRB: And the waters prevailed out of measure upon the earth; and all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered.

text.NRSV: the waters swelled above the mountains, covering them fifteen cubits deep.
text.Rotherham: fifteen cubits upwards, prevailed the waters, so that the mountains became covered.
text.KJV: Fifteene cubits vpward, did the waters preuaile; and the mountaines were couered.
text.NABRE: The waters swelled fifteen cubits higher than the submerged mountains.
text.DRB: Fifteen cubits higher was the water above the mountains, which it covered.

text.NRSV: And all flesh died that moved on the earth, birds, domestic animals, wild animals, all swarming creatures that swarm on the earth, and all human beings;
text.Rotherham: And all flesh ceased to breathe that moved on the earth, of birds and of tame-beasts and of wild-beasts, and of all the swarming things that swarm on the earth,—and all mankind.
text.KJV: And all flesh died, that mooued vpon the earth, both of fowle, & of cattell, and of beast, and of euery creeping thing that creepeth vpon the earth, and euery man.
text.NABRE: All creatures that moved on earth perished: birds, tame animals, wild animals, and all that teemed on the earth, as well as all humankind.
text.DRB: And all flesh was consumed that moved upon the earth, of fowl, of cattle, of beasts, and of all creepers, that creep upon the earth: all men,

text.NRSV: everything on dry land in whose nostrils was the breath of life died.
text.Rotherham: All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life of all that were on the dry ground died.
text.KJV: All in whose nosethrils was the breath of life, of all that was in the dry land, died.
text.NABRE: Everything on dry land with the breath of life in its nostrils died.
text.DRB: and all things, wherein there is breath of life on the earth, died.

text.NRSV: He blotted out every living thing that was on the face of the ground, human beings and animals and creeping things and birds of the air; they were blotted out from the earth. Only Noah was left, and those that were with him in the ark.
text.Rotherham: Thus was wiped out all that existed on the face of the ground, from man unto beast unto creeping thing, and unto the bird of the heavens, thus were they wiped out from the earth,—so that there was left—only Noah and they that were with him in the ark.
text.KJV: And euery liuing substance was destroyed, which was vpon the face of the ground, both man and cattell, and the creeping things, and the foule of the heauen; and they were destroyed from the earth: and Noah onely remained aliue, and they that were with him in the Arke.
text.NABRE: The Lord wiped out every being on earth: human beings and animals, the crawling things and the birds of the air; all were wiped out from the earth. Only Noah and those with him in the ark were left.
text.DRB: And he clean destroyed all substance that was upon the earth, from man even to beast, as well it that creepeth, as the fowls of the air: and they were destroyed from off the earth: but only Noe remained, and they that were with him in the ark.

text.NRSV: And the waters swelled on the earth for one hundred fifty days.
text.Rotherham: Thus prevailed the waters on the earth,—a hundred and fifty days.
text.KJV: And the waters preuailed vpon the earth, an hundred and fifty dayes.
text.NABRE: And when the waters had swelled on the earth for one hundred and fifty days,
text.DRB: And the waters held on above the earth an hundred fifty days.

text.NRSV: But God remembered Noah and all the wild animals and all the domestic animals that were with him in the ark. And God made a wind blow over the earth, and the waters subsided;
text.Rotherham: And God remembered Noah, and all the wild—beasts and all the tame-beasts that were with him in the ark,—and God caused a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters subsided;
text.KJV: And God remembred Noah, and euery liuing thing, and all the cattell that was with him in the Arke: and God made a winde to passe ouer the earth, and the waters asswaged.
text.NABRE: God remembered Noah and all the animals, wild and tame, that were with him in the ark. So God made a wind sweep over the earth, and the waters began to subside.
text.DRB: And God remembered Noe, and all the beasts, and all the cattle which were with him in the ark, and brought a wind upon the earth, and the waters decreased.

text.NRSV: the fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed, the rain from the heavens was restrained,
text.Rotherham: and the fountains of the roaring-deep were shut, and the windows of the heavens,—and the heavy rain was restrained from the heavens;
text.KJV: The fountaines also of the deepe, and the windowes of heauen were stopped, and the raine from heauen was restrained.
text.NABRE: The fountains of the abyss and the floodgates of the sky were closed, and the downpour from the sky was held back.
text.DRB: And the fountains of the depth and the flood-gates of heaven were shut up: and the rain from heaven was stayed.

text.NRSV: and the waters gradually receded from the earth. At the end of one hundred fifty days the waters had abated;
text.Rotherham: and the waters returned from off the earth they went on returning,—and so the waters decreased at the end of a hundred and fifty days.
text.KJV: And the waters returned from off the earth, continually: and after the end of the hundred and fiftie dayes, the waters were abated.
text.NABRE: Gradually the waters receded from the earth. At the end of one hundred and fifty days, the waters had so diminished
text.DRB: And the waters returned from the earth going & coming: and they began to decrease after an hundred fifty days.

text.NRSV: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
text.Rotherham: And the ark rested, in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month,—on the mountains of Ararat.
text.KJV: And the Arke rested in the seuenth moneth, on the seuenteenth day of the moneth, vpon the mountaines of Ararat.
text.NABRE: that, in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
text.DRB: And the ark rested the seventh month, the seven and twentieth day of the month, upon the mountains of Armenia.

text.NRSV: The waters continued to abate until the tenth month; in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains appeared.
text.Rotherham: But, the waters went on decreasing, until the tenth month,—in the tenth [month], on the first of the month, were seen the tops of the mountains.
text.KJV: And the waters decreased continually vntill the tenth moneth: in the tenth moneth, on the first day of the moneth, were the tops of the mountaines seene.
text.NABRE: The waters continued to diminish until the tenth month, and on the first day of the tenth month the tops of the mountains appeared.
text.DRB: But the waters for all that were going and decreasing until the tenth month: for in the tenth month, the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains appeared.

text.NRSV: At the end of forty days Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at the end of forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made;
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe at the end of forty dayes, that Noah opened the window of the Arke which he had made.
text.NABRE: At the end of forty days Noah opened the hatch of the ark that he had made,
text.DRB: And after that forty days were passed, Noe opening the window of the ark, which he had made, let forth a crow:

text.NRSV: and sent out the raven; and it went to and fro until the waters were dried up from the earth.
text.Rotherham: and sent forth a raven,—and it kept going forth and returning, until the drying up of the waters from off the earth.
text.KJV: And he sent forth a Rauen, which went foorth to and fro, vntill the waters were dried vp from off the earth.
text.NABRE: and he released a raven. It flew back and forth until the waters dried off from the earth.
text.DRB: which went forth, and did not return, till the waters were dried upon the earth.

text.NRSV: Then he sent out the dove from him, to see if the waters had subsided from the face of the ground;
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth a dove from him,—to see whether the waters had abated, from off the face of the ground;
text.KJV: Also hee sent foorth a doue from him, to see if the waters were abated from off the face of the ground.
text.NABRE: Then he released a dove, to see if the waters had lessened on the earth.
text.DRB: He sent forth also a dove after him, to see if the waters were ceased yet upon the face of the earth.

text.NRSV: but the dove found no place to set its foot, and it returned to him to the ark, for the waters were still on the face of the whole earth. So he put out his hand and took it and brought it into the ark with him.
text.Rotherham: but the dove found no resting-place for the sole of her foot so she returned unto him into the ark, for, waters, were on the face of all the earth; and he put forth his hand and took her, and brought her in unto him, into the ark.
text.KJV: But the doue found no rest for the sole of her foote, and she returned vnto him into the Arke: for the waters were on the face of the whole earth. Then he put foorth his hand, and tooke her, and pulled her in vnto him, into the Arke.
text.NABRE: But the dove could find no place to perch, and it returned to him in the ark, for there was water over all the earth. Putting out his hand, he caught the dove and drew it back to him inside the ark.
text.DRB: Which finding not where her foot might rest, returned to him into the ark: for the waters were upon the whole earth: and he stretched forth his hand and caught her, and brought her into the ark.

text.NRSV: He waited another seven days, and again he sent out the dove from the ark;
text.Rotherham: Then stayed he yet seven days more,—and, again sent forth the dove out of the ark.
text.KJV: And hee stayed yet other seuen dayes; and againe hee sent foorth the doue out of the Arke.
text.NABRE: He waited yet seven days more and again released the dove from the ark.
text.DRB: And having expected yet seven more days again he let forth a dove out of the ark.

text.NRSV: and the dove came back to him in the evening, and there in its beak was a freshly plucked olive leaf; so Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth.
text.Rotherham: And the dove came in unto him at eventide, and lo! a newly sprouted olive-leaf, in her mouth,—so Noah knew that the waters had abated from off the earth.
text.KJV: And the doue came in to him in the euening, and loe, in her mouth was an Oliue leafe pluckt off: So Noah knew that the waters were abated from off the earth.
text.NABRE: In the evening the dove came back to him, and there in its bill was a plucked-off olive leaf! So Noah knew that the waters had diminished on the earth.
text.DRB: But she came to him at eventide, carrying a bough of an olive tree that had green leaves, in her mouth. Noe therefore understood that the waters were ceased upon the earth.

text.NRSV: Then he waited another seven days, and sent out the dove; and it did not return to him any more.
text.Rotherham: And he stayed yet seven days more,—and sent forth the dove, but she returned not again unto him any more.
text.KJV: And hee stayed yet other seuen dayes, and sent forth the doue, which returned not againe vnto him any more.
text.NABRE: He waited yet another seven days and then released the dove; but this time it did not come back.
text.DRB: And he expected yet nevertheless other seven days: and he sent forth a dove, which returned not any more unto him.

text.NRSV: In the six hundred first year, in the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were dried up from the earth; and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and saw that the face of the ground was drying.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass in the six hundred and first year at the beginning, on the first of the month, that the waters had dried up from off the earth,—and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked and lo! the face of the ground was dried.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe in the sixe hundredth and one yeere, in the first moneth, the first day of the moneth, the waters were dryed vp from off the earth: and Noah remooued the couering of the Arke, and looked, and behold, the face of the ground was drie.
text.NABRE: In the six hundred and first year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, the water began to dry up on the earth. Noah then removed the covering of the ark and saw that the surface of the ground had dried.
text.DRB: Therefore in the sixth hundred and one year, the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were clean diminished upon the earth: & Noe opening the roof of the ark, looked, and saw that the face of the earth was dried.

text.NRSV: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.
text.Rotherham: And in the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, was the earth dry.
text.KJV: And in the second moneth, on the seuen and twentieth day of the moneth, was the earth dried.
text.NABRE: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.
text.DRB: In the second month, the seven and twentieth day of the month the earth was dried.

text.NRSV: Then God said to Noah,
text.Rotherham: So then, God spake to Noah, saying:
text.KJV: ¶ And God spake vnto Noah, saying,
text.NABRE: Then God said to Noah:
text.DRB: And God spake to Noe, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go out of the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons’ wives with you.
text.Rotherham: Come forth out of the ark,—thou and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
text.KJV: Goe foorth of the Arke, thou, and thy wife, and thy sonnes, and thy sonnes wiues with thee:
text.NABRE: Go out of the ark, together with your wife and your sons and your sons’ wives.
text.DRB: Go forth of the ark, thou and thy wife, thy sons and the wives of thy sons with thee.

text.NRSV: Bring out with you every living thing that is with you of all flesh—birds and animals and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth—so that they may abound on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: All the living creatures that are with thee of all flesh among birds, and among beasts, and among all the creeping things that creep on the earth, bring forth with thee,—and they shall swarm in the earth, and be fruitful and multiply, on the earth.
text.KJV: Bring foorth with thee euery liuing thing that is with thee, of all flesh, both of fowle, and of cattell, and of euery creeping thing that creepeth vpon the earth, that they may breed abundantly in the earth, and be fruitfull, and multiply vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: Bring out with you every living thing that is with you—all creatures, be they birds or animals or crawling things that crawl on the earth—and let them abound on the earth, and be fertile and multiply on it.
text.DRB: All cattle that are with thee, of all flesh, as well in fowls, as in beasts, and all creepers that creep upon the earth, bring out with thee, and go ye upon the earth: increase and multiply upon it.

text.NRSV: So Noah went out with his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives.
text.Rotherham: So Noah came forth,—and his sons and his wife, and his sons’ wives with him:
text.KJV: And Noah went foorth, and his sonnes, and his wife, and his sonnes wiues with him:
text.NABRE: So Noah came out, together with his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives;
text.DRB: Noe therefore went forth, and his sons: his wife, and the wives of his sons with him.

text.NRSV: And every animal, every creeping thing, and every bird, everything that moves on the earth, went out of the ark by families.
text.Rotherham: All the living creatures, all that move along, and all birds, and all that moveth along over the earth—by their families, came forth out of the ark.
text.KJV: Euery beast, euery creeping thing, and euery fowle, and whatsoeuer creepeth vpon the earth, after their kinds, went foorth out of the Arke.
text.NABRE: and all the animals, all the birds, and all the crawling creatures that crawl on the earth went out of the ark by families.
text.DRB: Yea and all cattle, beasts, & creepers that creep upon the earth, according to their kind, went forth out of the ark.

text.NRSV: Then Noah built an altar to the Lord, and took of every clean animal and of every clean bird, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
text.Rotherham: And Noah builded an altar to Yahweh,—and took of all the clean beasts and of all the clean birds, and caused ascending Sacrifices to go up on the altar.
text.KJV: ¶ And Noah builded an Altar vnto the LORD, and tooke of euery cleane beast, and of euery cleane fowle, and offred burnt offrings on the Altar.
text.NABRE: Then Noah built an altar to the Lord, and choosing from every clean animal and every clean bird, he offered burnt offerings on the altar.
text.DRB: And Noe built an Altar to our Lord: and taking of all cattle and fowls that were clean, offered Holocausts upon the Altar.

text.NRSV: And when the Lord smelled the pleasing odor, the Lord said in his heart, “I will never again curse the ground because of humankind, for the inclination of the human heart is evil from youth; nor will I ever again destroy every living creature as I have done.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh smelled a satisfying odour, so Yahweh said to himself. I will not, again, curse any more the ground for man’s sake, although the device of the heart of man, be wicked from his youth,—neither will I again, any more smite every living thing, as I have done.
text.KJV: And the LORD smelled a sweete sauour, and the LORD said in his heart, I will not againe curse the ground any more for mans sake; for the imagination of mans heart is euil from his youth: neither will I againe smite any more euery thing liuing, as I haue done.
text.NABRE: When the Lord smelled the sweet odor, the Lord said to himself: Never again will I curse the ground because of human beings, since the desires of the human heart are evil from youth; nor will I ever again strike down every living being, as I have done.
text.DRB: And our Lord smelled a sweet savour, and said: I will no more curse the earth for men: for the sense and cogitation of man's heart are prone to evil from their youth: I will no more therefore strike every living soul as I have done.

text.NRSV: As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night, shall not cease.”
text.Rotherham: During all the days of the earth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat and summer and winter, and day and night, shall not cease.
text.KJV: While the earth remaineth, seed-time and haruest, and cold, and heat, and Summer, and Winter, and day and night, shall not cease.
text.NABRE: All the days of the earth, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, Summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.
text.DRB: All the days of the earth, seed-time and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, night and day shall not rest.

text.NRSV: God blessed Noah and his sons, and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth.
text.Rotherham: So God blessed Noah and his sons,—and said to them Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth;
text.KJV: And God blessed Noah, and his sonnes, and said vnto them, Bee fruitfull and multiply, and replenish the earth.
text.NABRE: God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them: Be fertile and multiply and fill the earth.
text.DRB: And God blessed Noe and his sons, And he said to them: Increase & multiply, and replenish the earth.

text.NRSV: The fear and dread of you shall rest on every animal of the earth, and on every bird of the air, on everything that creeps on the ground, and on all the fish of the sea; into your hand they are delivered.
text.Rotherham: and let the fear of you and the dread of you be upon every living creature of the earth, and upon every bird of the heavens,—over everything that moveth along on the ground, and over all the fishes of the sea,—into your hand, have they been given.
text.KJV: And the feare of you, & the dread of you shall be vpon euery beast of the earth, and vpon euery fowle of the aire, vpon all that mooueth vpon the earth, and vpon all the fishes of the sea; into your hand are they deliuered.
text.NABRE: Fear and dread of you shall come upon all the animals of the earth and all the birds of the air, upon all the creatures that move about on the ground and all the fishes of the sea; into your power they are delivered.
text.DRB: And your terror and dread be it upon all the beasts of the earth, and upon all the fowls of the air, with all that move upon the earth: all the fishes of the sea are delivered to your hand.

text.NRSV: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you; and just as I gave you the green plants, I give you everything.
text.Rotherham: As for every moving thing that hath life, yours, shall it be, for food,—Like the green herb, have I given you all things.
text.KJV: Euery mouing thing that liueth, shalbe meat for you; euen as the greene herbe haue I giuen you all things.
text.NABRE: Any living creature that moves about shall be yours to eat; I give them all to you as I did the green plants.
text.DRB: And all that moveth and liveth shall be yours for meat: even as the green herbs have I delivered all to you.

text.NRSV: Only, you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood.
text.Rotherham: Yet flesh with the life thereof, the blood thereof, shall ye not eat;
text.KJV: But flesh with the life thereof, which is the blood thereof, shall you not eate.
text.NABRE: Only meat with its lifeblood still in it you shall not eat.
text.DRB: Saving that flesh with blood you shall not eat.

text.NRSV: For your own lifeblood I will surely require a reckoning: from every animal I will require it and from human beings, each one for the blood of another, I will require a reckoning for human life.
text.Rotherham: And surely your blood, of your lives, will I require, From the hand of every living creature, will I require it,—and from the hand of man From the hand of each one’s brother, will I require the life of man:
text.KJV: And surely your blood of your liues will I require: at the hand of euery beast will I require it, & at the hand of man, at the hand of euery mans brother will I require the life of man.
text.NABRE: Indeed for your own lifeblood I will demand an accounting: from every animal I will demand it, and from a human being, each one for the blood of another, I will demand an accounting for human life.
text.DRB: For I will require the blood of your souls at the hands of all beasts: and at the hand of man, at the hand of each man, and of his brother, will I require the soul of man.

text.NRSV: Whoever sheds the blood of a human, by a human shall that person’s blood be shed; for in his own image God made humankind.
text.Rotherham: He that sheddeth man’s blood, by man, shall his blood be shed,—For, in the image of God, made he man.
text.KJV: Who so sheddeth mans blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.
text.NABRE: Anyone who sheds the blood of a human being, by a human being shall that one’s blood be shed; For in the image of God have human beings been made.
text.DRB: Whosoever shall shed man's blood, his blood shall be shed: for to the image of God man was made.

text.NRSV: And you, be fruitful and multiply, abound on the earth and multiply in it.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, be fruitful and multiply, swarm in the earth and multiply therein.
text.KJV: And you, be ye fruitfull, and multiply, bring foorth aboundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.
text.NABRE: Be fertile, then, and multiply; abound on earth and subdue it.
text.DRB: But increase you and multiply, and go upon the earth, and fill it.

text.NRSV: Then God said to Noah and to his sons with him,
text.Rotherham: And God spake unto Noah, and unto his sons with him saying:
text.KJV: ¶ And God spake vnto Noah, and to his sonnes with him, saying;
text.NABRE: God said to Noah and to his sons with him:
text.DRB: Thus also said God to Noe, and his sons with him:

text.NRSV: “As for me, I am establishing my covenant with you and your descendants after you,
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, behold me! establishing my covenant with you,—and with your seed after you;
text.KJV: And I, behold, I establish my couenant with you, and with your seede after you:
text.NABRE: See, I am now establishing my covenant with you and your descendants after you
text.DRB: Behold I will establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you:

text.NRSV: and with every living creature that is with you, the birds, the domestic animals, and every animal of the earth with you, as many as came out of the ark.
text.Rotherham: and with every living soul that is with you, of birds, of tame-beasts and of all wild-beasts of the earth that are with you,—of all coming forth out of the ark, even to all wild-beasts of the earth;
text.KJV: And with euery liuing creature that is with you, of the fowle, of the cattell, and of euery beast of the earth with you, from all that goe out of the Arke, to euery beast of the earth.
text.NABRE: and with every living creature that was with you: the birds, the tame animals, and all the wild animals that were with you—all that came out of the ark.
text.DRB: and with every living soul that is with you, as well in all fowls as in cattle & beasts of the earth that are come forth out of the ark, and in all beasts of the earth.

text.NRSV: I establish my covenant with you, that never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of a flood, and never again shall there be a flood to destroy the earth.”
text.Rotherham: yea, I will establish my covenant with you, so that all flesh shall not be cut off any more, by reason of the waters of a flood,—neither shall there be any more a flood to destroy the earth.
text.KJV: And I wil establish my couenant with you, neither shal all flesh be cut off any more, by the waters of a flood, neither shall there any more be a flood to destroy the earth.
text.NABRE: I will establish my covenant with you, that never again shall all creatures be destroyed by the waters of a flood; there shall not be another flood to devastate the earth.
text.DRB: I will establish my covenant with you, and all flesh shall be no more destroyed with the waters of a flood, neither shall there be from hence-forth a flood to waste the earth.

text.NRSV: God said, “This is the sign of the covenant that I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all future generations:
text.Rotherham: And God said, This, is the sign of the covenant which I am granting betwixt me and you, and every living soul that is with you,—to age-abiding generations:—
text.KJV: And God said, This is the token of the Couenant which I make betweene mee and you, and euery liuing creature that is with you, for perpetuall generations.
text.NABRE: God said: This is the sign of the covenant that I am making between me and you and every living creature with you for all ages to come:
text.DRB: And God said: This is the sign of the covenant which I give between me and you, and between every living soul, that is with you, for perpetual generations:

text.NRSV: I have set my bow in the clouds, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.
text.Rotherham: My bow, have I set in the cloud,—and it shall be for a sign of a covenant, betwixt me and the earth;
text.KJV: I doe set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a couenant, betweene me and the earth.
text.NABRE: I set my bow in the clouds to serve as a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.
text.DRB: my bow will I set in the clouds, and it shall be the sign of a covenant between me and between the earth.

text.NRSV: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow is seen in the clouds,
text.Rotherham: and it shall be when I draw a veil of cloud over the earth,—and the bow in the cloud appeareth,
text.KJV: And it shall come to passe, when I bring a cloud ouer the earth, that the bow shall be seene in the cloud.
text.NABRE: When I bring clouds over the earth, and the bow appears in the clouds,
text.DRB: And when I shall cover the element with clouds, my bow shall appear in the clouds:

text.NRSV: I will remember my covenant that is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall never again become a flood to destroy all flesh.
text.Rotherham: then will I remember my covenant which is betwixt me and you, and every living soul among all flesh,—that the waters may no more become a flood, to destroy all flesh:
text.KJV: And I will remember my couenant, which is betweene mee and you, and euery liuing creature of all flesh: and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.
text.NABRE: I will remember my covenant between me and you and every living creature—every mortal being—so that the waters will never again become a flood to destroy every mortal being.
text.DRB: and I shall remember my covenant with you, and with every living soul that beareth flesh: and there shall no more be waters of a flood, to destroy all flesh.

text.NRSV: When the bow is in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: so the bow shall be in the cloud,—and I will behold it, to remember an age-abiding covenant, between God, and every living soul among all flesh that is on the earth.
text.KJV: And the bow shalbe in the cloud; and I will looke vpon it, that I may remember the euerlasting couenant betweene God and euery liuing creature, of all flesh that is vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: When the bow appears in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature—every mortal being that is on earth.
text.DRB: And my bow shall be in the clouds, and I shall see it, and I shall remember the everlasting covenant, that was made between God and every living soul of all flesh which is upon the earth.

text.NRSV: God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant that I have established between me and all flesh that is on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Noah, This is the sign of the covenant which have established, between me and all flesh that is on the earth.
text.KJV: And God said vnto Noah, This is the token of the couenant, which I haue established betweene mee and all flesh, that is vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: God told Noah: This is the sign of the covenant I have established between me and every mortal being that is on earth.
text.DRB: And God said to Noe: This shall be the sign of the covenant, which I established between me & all flesh of the earth.

text.NRSV: The sons of Noah who went out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Noah who came forth out of the ark, were—Shem and Ham and Japheth,—now, Ham, was the father of Canaan.
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Noah that went forth of the Arke, were Shem, and Ham, and Iaphet: and Ham is the father of Canaan.
text.NABRE: The sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.
text.DRB: The sons therefore of Noe, that came out of the ark, were Sem, Cham, and Japheth: and Cham he is the father of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: These three were the sons of Noah; and from these the whole earth was peopled.
text.Rotherham: These three, were the sons of Noah,—and from these, was all the earth overspread.
text.KJV: These are the three sonnes of Noah: and of them was the whole earth ouerspread.
text.NABRE: These three were the sons of Noah, and from them the whole earth was populated.
text.DRB: These three are the sons of Noe: and of these was all mankind spread over the whole earth.

text.NRSV: Noah, a man of the soil, was the first to plant a vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And Noah began to be a husbandman,—so he planted a vineyard:
text.KJV: And Noah began to bee an husbandman, and he planted a vineyard.
text.NABRE: Noah, a man of the soil, was the first to plant a vineyard.
text.DRB: And Noe a husbandman began to till the ground, and planted a vineyard.

text.NRSV: He drank some of the wine and became drunk, and he lay uncovered in his tent.
text.Rotherham: and dank of the wine and became drunk, and uncovered himself in the midst of his tent.
text.KJV: And he dranke of the wine, and was drunken, and hee was vncouered within his tent.
text.NABRE: He drank some of the wine, became drunk, and lay naked inside his tent.
text.DRB: And drinking of the wine was made drunk, and naked in his tabernacle.

text.NRSV: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brothers outside.
text.Rotherham: And Ham the father of Canaan saw his father’s shame,—and told his two brethren outside.
text.KJV: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakednesse of his father, and told his two brethren without.
text.NABRE: Ham, the father of Canaan, saw his father’s nakedness, and he told his two brothers outside.
text.DRB: Which when Cham the father of Chanaan had seen, to wit that his father's privities were bare, he told it to his two brethren abroad.

text.NRSV: Then Shem and Japheth took a garment, laid it on both their shoulders, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of their father; their faces were turned away, and they did not see their father’s nakedness.
text.Rotherham: And Shem and Japheth took a mantle, and put it on the shoulder of them both and went backwards, and covered the shame of their father, but their faces were backwards, and the shame of their father, saw they not.
text.KJV: And Shem and Iaphet tooke a garment, and layed it vpon both their shoulders, and went backward, and couered the nakednesse of their father, and their faces were backward, and they saw not their fathers nakednesse.
text.NABRE: Shem and Japheth, however, took a robe, and holding it on their shoulders, they walked backward and covered their father’s nakedness; since their faces were turned the other way, they did not see their father’s nakedness.
text.DRB: But indeed Sem and Japhet put a cloak upon their shoulders, and going backward, covered the privities of their father: and their faces were turned away, and they saw not their father's privities.

text.NRSV: When Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him,
text.Rotherham: And Noah awoke from his wine,—and came to know what his youngest son had done to him.
text.KJV: And Noah awoke from his wine, and knew what his yonger sonne had done vnto him.
text.NABRE: When Noah woke up from his wine and learned what his youngest son had done to him,
text.DRB: And Noe awaking from the wine, when he had learned what his younger son had done to him,

text.NRSV: he said, “Cursed be Canaan; lowest of slaves shall he be to his brothers.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Accursed be Canaan,—a servant of servants, shall he be to his brethren!
text.KJV: And he said, Cursed bee Canaan: a seruant of seruants shall hee be vnto his brethren.
text.NABRE: he said: “Cursed be Caanan! The lowest of slaves shall he be to his brothers.”
text.DRB: he said: Cursed be Chanaan, a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.

text.NRSV: He also said, “Blessed by the Lord my God be Shem; and let Canaan be his slave.
text.Rotherham: And he said, Blessed be Yahweh, God of Shem,—And let Canaan be their servant:
text.KJV: And hee saide, Blessed bee the LORD God of Shem, and Canaan shalbe his seruant.
text.NABRE: He also said: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Shem! Let Canaan be his slave.
text.DRB: And he saith: Blessed be the Lord God of Sem, Chanaan be his servant.

text.NRSV: May God make space for Japheth, and let him live in the tents of Shem; and let Canaan be his slave.”
text.Rotherham: God give extension to Japheth, But make his habitation in the tents of Shem, And let Canaan be their servant.
text.KJV: God shall enlarge Iaphet, and he shal dwel in the tents of Shem, and Canaan shalbe his seruant.
text.NABRE: May God expand Japheth, and may he dwell among the tents of Shem; and let Canaan be his slave.”
text.DRB: God enlarge Japheth, and dwell he in the tabernacles of Sem, and Chanaan be his servant.

text.NRSV: After the flood Noah lived three hundred fifty years.
text.Rotherham: And Noah lived after the flood,—three hundred and fifty years:
text.KJV: ¶ And Noah liued after the flood, three hundred and fifty yeeres.
text.NABRE: Noah lived three hundred and fifty years after the flood.
text.DRB: And Noe lived after the flood three hundred fifty years.

text.NRSV: All the days of Noah were nine hundred fifty years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: so, all the days of Noah, were—nine hundred and fifty years,—and he died.
text.KJV: And all the dayes of Noah were nine hundred & fifty yeeres, and he died.
text.NABRE: The whole lifetime of Noah was nine hundred and fifty years; then he died.
text.DRB: And all his days were in the whole nine hundred fifty years: and he died.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Noah’s sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth; children were born to them after the flood.
text.Rotherham: Now, these are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and there were born to them sons after the flood.
text.KJV: Now these are the generations of the sonnes of Noah; Shem, Ham, and Iaphet: and vnto them were sonnes borne after the Flood.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Noah’s sons, Shem, Ham and Japheth, to whom children were born after the flood.
text.DRB: These are the generations of the sons of Noe, Sem, Cham, and Japheth: and children were born to them after the flood.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Japheth: Gomer and Magog, and Madai, and Javan and Tubal,—and Meshech, and Tiras.
text.KJV: The sonnes of Iaphet: Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Iauan, & Tubal, and Meshech, & Tiras.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech and Tiras.
text.DRB: The children of Japheth: Gomer, & Magog, & Madai, & Javan, & Tubal, and Mosoch, and Thiras.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz and Riphath, and Togarmah.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Gomer: Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Diphath and Togarmah.
text.DRB: Moreover the children of Gomer: Ascenez, and Riphath, and Thogorma.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Javan: Elishah, and Tarshish. Kittim, and Rodanim,—
text.KJV: And the sons of Iauan: Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittim and the Rodanim.
text.DRB: And the sons of Javan: Elisa, and Tharsis, the Cetims and the Dodanims.

text.NRSV: From these the coastland peoples spread. These are the descendants of Japheth in their lands, with their own language, by their families, in their nations.
text.Rotherham: From these, were dispersed [the inhabitants] of the coastlands of the nations, in their lands, each man by his tongue, by their families in their nations.
text.KJV: By these were the Iles of the Gentiles diuided in their lands, euery one after his tongue: after their families, in their nations.
text.NABRE: From these branched out the maritime nations. These are the descendants of Japheth by their lands, each with its own language, according to their clans, by their nations.
text.DRB: Of these were divided the Isles of Nations in their countries, each one according to his tongue, & their families in their nations.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ham: Cush and Mizraim and Phut and Canaan.
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Ham: Cush, and Mizraim, and Phut, and Canaan.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Put and Canaan.
text.DRB: And the sons of Cham: Chus, & Mesraim, & Phut, and Chanaan.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The descendants of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Cush: Seba and Havilah, and Sabtah and Raamah, and Sabtechah,—And the sons of Raamah, Sheba and Dedan.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Cush, Seba, and Hauilah, and Sabtah, and Raamah, and Sabtecha: and the sonnes of Raamah: Sheba, and Dedan.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah and Sabteca. The descendants of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
text.DRB: And the sons of Chus: Saba, Hevila, and Sabatha, & Regma, & Sabathaca. The sons of Regma: Saba, and Dadan.

text.NRSV: Cush became the father of Nimrod; he was the first on earth to become a mighty warrior.
text.Rotherham: And, Cush begat Nimrod.—he, became a hero in the earth;
text.KJV: And Cush begat Nimrod: he began to be a mighty one in the earth.
text.NABRE: Cush became the father of Nimrod, who was the first to become a mighty warrior on earth.
text.DRB: Moreover Chus begat Nemrod: he began to be mighty in the earth.
