text.NRSV: He was a mighty hunter before the Lord; therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: he, became a hero of hunting before Yahweh, for this cause, it is said. Like Nimrod, a hero of hunting before Yahweh.
text.KJV: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD: wherefore it is saide, Euen as Nimrod the mightie hunter before the LORD.
text.NABRE: He was a mighty hunter in the eyes of the Lord; hence the saying, “Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter in the eyes of the Lord.”
text.DRB: and he was a valiant hunter before our Lord. Thereof rose a proverb: As it were Nemrod the valiant hunter before our Lord.

text.NRSV: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, and Accad, all of them in the land of Shinar.
text.Rotherham: So the beginning of his kingdom came to be Babel, and Erech, and Accad and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
text.KJV: And the beginning of his kingdome was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
text.NABRE: His kingdom originated in Babylon, Erech and Accad, all of them in the land of Shinar.
text.DRB: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babylon, and Arach, and Achad, and Chalanne in the land of Sennaar.

text.NRSV: From that land he went into Assyria, and built Nineveh, Rehoboth-ir, Calah, and
text.Rotherham: From that land, went forth Asshur,—and he built Nineveh, and Rehoboth-ir, and Calah:
text.KJV: Out of that land went forth Asshur, and builded Nineueh, and the citie Rehoboth, and Calah,
text.NABRE: From that land he went forth to Assyria, where he built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir and Calah,
text.DRB: Out of that land came forth Assur, & builded Ninive, & the streets of the city, and Chale.

text.NRSV: Resen between Nineveh and Calah; that is the great city.
text.Rotherham: and Resen, between Niveveh and Calah,—the same is the great city.
text.KJV: And Resen betweene Nineueh and Calah: the same is a great citie.
text.NABRE: as well as Resen, between Nineveh and Calah, the latter being the principal city.
text.DRB: Resen also between Ninive, & Chale: this is the great city.

text.NRSV: Egypt became the father of Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
text.Rotherham: And, Mizraim, begat Ludim and Anamim and Lehabim, and Naphtuchim;
text.KJV: And Mizraim begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim, and Naphtuhim,
text.NABRE: Mizraim became the father of the Ludim, the Anamim, the Lehabim, the Naphtuhim,
text.DRB: But Mesraim also begat the Ludims, & the Anamims, & the Laabims, the Nephthuims,

text.NRSV: Pathrusim, Casluhim, and Caphtorim, from which the Philistines come.
text.Rotherham: and Pathrusim and Casluhim whence came forth Philistim and Caphtorim.
text.KJV: And Pathrusim, and Casluhim (out of whome came Philistiim) and Caphtorim.
text.NABRE: the Pathrusim, the Casluhim, and the Caphtorim from whom the Philistines came.
text.DRB: and the Phetrusims, & the Casluims: of whom came forth the Philistims & the Caphtorims.

text.NRSV: Canaan became the father of Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
text.Rotherham: And Canaan begat Zidon his firstborn, and Heth:
text.KJV: ¶ And Canaan begate Sidon his first borne, and Heth,
text.NABRE: Canaan became the father of Sidon, his firstborn, and of Heth;
text.DRB: And Chanaan begat Sidon his first-begotten, Hethæus,

text.NRSV: and the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
text.Rotherham: and the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgashite;
text.KJV: And the Iebusite, and the Emorite, and the Girgasite,
text.NABRE: also of the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
text.DRB: and Iebusæus, and Amorrhæus, and Gergesæus.

text.NRSV: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
text.Rotherham: and the Hivite and the Arkite, and the Sinite:
text.KJV: And the Hiuite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,
text.NABRE: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
text.DRB: Hevæus and Aracæus, Sinæus,

text.NRSV: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Afterward the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.
text.Rotherham: and the Arvadite and the Zemarite and the Hamathite,—and after-wards, were spread abroad the families of the Canaanite.
text.KJV: And the Aruadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite: and afterward were the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.
text.NABRE: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Afterward, the clans of the Canaanites spread out,
text.DRB: and Aradius, Samaræus, and Hamathæus: and afterwards were spread the people of the Chananites.

text.NRSV: And the territory of the Canaanites extended from Sidon, in the direction of Gerar, as far as Gaza, and in the direction of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the boundary of the Canaanite was from Zidon, as thou enterest in towards Gerar unto Gaza,—as thou enterest in towards Sodom and Gomorrah and Admah and Zeboim, as far as Lasha.
text.KJV: And the border of the Canaanites, was from Sidon, as thou commest to Gerar, vnto Gaza, as thou goest vnto Sodoma and Gomorah, and Admah, & Zeboim, euen vnto Lasha.
text.NABRE: so that the Canaanite borders extended from Sidon all the way to Gerar, near Gaza, and all the way to Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, near Lasha.
text.DRB: And the limits of Chanaan were from Sidon as we come to Gerara even to Gaza, until thou enter to Sodoma and Gomorrha, and Adama, and Seboim, even to Lesa.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Ham, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Ham, by their families by their tongues, in their lands in their nations.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Ham, after their families, after their tongues, in their countries, and in their nations.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Ham, according to their clans, according to their languages, by their lands, by their nations.
text.DRB: These are the children of Cham in their kindreds, and tongues, and generations, and lands, and nations.

text.NRSV: To Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the elder brother of Japheth, children were born.
text.Rotherham: Now, to Shem, father of all the sons of Eber, elder brother of Japheth, to him also, were children born.
text.KJV: ¶ Unto Shem also the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Iaphet the elder, euen to him were children borne.
text.NABRE: To Shem also, Japheth’s oldest brother and the ancestor of all the children of Eber, children were born.
text.DRB: Of Sem also father of all the children of Heber, the elder brother of Japheth, were born:

text.NRSV: The descendants of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Shem, Elam and Asshur,—and Arpachshad and Lud and Aram.
text.KJV: The children of Shem: Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud and Aram.
text.DRB: The children of Sem: Ælam and Assur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Aram: Uz and Hul, and Gether and Mash.
text.KJV: And the children of Aram: Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether and Mash.
text.DRB: The children of Aram: Us, and Hul, and Gether, and Mes.

text.NRSV: Arpachshad became the father of Shelah; and Shelah became the father of Eber.
text.Rotherham: Now Arpachshad, begat Shelah, and Shelah begat Eber.
text.KJV: And Arphaxad begate Salah, and Salah begate Eber.
text.NABRE: Arpachshad became the father of Shelah, and Shelah became the father of Eber.
text.DRB: And Arphaxad also begat Sale, of whom was born Heber.

text.NRSV: To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and his brother’s name was Joktan.
text.Rotherham: And to Eber, were born two sons,—the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days, was the earth divided, and the name of his brother was Joktan.
text.KJV: And vnto Eber were borne two sonnes: the name of one was Peleg, for in his dayes was the earth diuided, and his brothers name was Ioktan.
text.NABRE: To Eber two sons were born: the name of the first was Peleg, for in his time the world was divided; and the name of his brother was Joktan.
text.DRB: And to Heber were born two sons: the name of the one was Phaleg, because that in his days was the earth divided: and his brother's name was Jectan.

text.NRSV: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
text.Rotherham: Now, Joktan, begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah;
text.KJV: And Ioktan begate Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaueth, and Ierah,
text.NABRE: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
text.DRB: The which Jectan begat Elmodad, and Saleph, and Asarmoth, Jare;

text.NRSV: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
text.Rotherham: and Hadoram and Uzal, and Diklah:
text.KJV: And Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,
text.NABRE: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
text.DRB: and Aduram, and Uzal, and Decla,

text.NRSV: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
text.Rotherham: and Obal and Abimael, and Sheba:
text.KJV: And Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,
text.NABRE: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
text.DRB: and Ebal, and Abimael, Saba,

text.NRSV: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab; all these were the descendants of Joktan.
text.Rotherham: and Ophir and Havilah, and Jobab—all these were sons of Joktan.
text.KJV: And Ophir, and Hauilah, & Iobab: all these were the sonnes of Ioktan.
text.NABRE: Ophir, Havilah and Jobab. All these were descendants of Joktan.
text.DRB: and Ophir, and Hevila, and Jobab. All these were the children of Jectan.

text.NRSV: The territory in which they lived extended from Mesha in the direction of Sephar, the hill country of the east.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou enterest in towards Sephar, the mountain of the east.
text.KJV: And their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou goest vnto Sephar, a mount of the East.
text.NABRE: Their settlements extended all the way from Mesha to Sephar, the eastern hill country.
text.DRB: And their dwelling was from Messa as we go on as far as Sephar a mountain in the east.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Shem, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
text.Rotherham: These are the sons of Shem, by their families by their tongues, in their lands by their nations.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Shem, after their families, after their tongues, in their lands after their nations.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Shem, according to their clans, according to their languages, by their lands, by their nations.
text.DRB: These are the children of Sem according to their kindred, and tongues, and countries in their nations.

text.NRSV: These are the families of Noah’s sons, according to their genealogies, in their nations; and from these the nations spread abroad on the earth after the flood.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the sons of Noah, by their generations in their nations, and from these, were dispersed the nations in the earth after the flood.
text.KJV: These are the families of the sonnes of Noah after their generations, in their nations: and by these were the nations diuided in the earth after the Flood.
text.NABRE: These are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their origins and by their nations. From these the nations of the earth branched out after the flood.
text.DRB: These are the families of Noe, according to their peoples and nations. Of these were the nations divided on the earth after the flood.

text.NRSV: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass that, all the earth, was of one manner of speech,—and of one stock of words.
text.KJV: And the whole earth was of one language, and of one speach.
text.NABRE: The whole world had the same language and the same words.
text.DRB: And the earth was of one tongue, and all one speech.

text.NRSV: And as they migrated from the east, they came upon a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as they brake away onwards, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar, and dwelt there.
text.KJV: And it came to passe as they iourneyed from the East, that they found a plaine in the land of Shinar, and they dwelt there.
text.NABRE: When they were migrating from the east, they came to a valley in the land of Shinar and settled there.
text.DRB: And when they removed from the east, they found a plain in the land of Sennaar, and dwelt in it.

text.NRSV: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
text.Rotherham: Then said they, each man to his friend, Come on! let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly,—So the bricks, served them for stone, and bitumen, served them for mortar,
text.KJV: And they sayd one to another; Goe to, let vs make bricke, and burne them thorowly. And they had bricke for stone, and slime had they for morter.
text.NABRE: They said to one another, “Come, let us mold bricks and harden them with fire.” They used bricks for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
text.DRB: And each one said to his neighbour: Come, let us make brick, and bake them with fire. And they had brick instead of stone, and bitume instead of mortar:

text.NRSV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves; otherwise we shall be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.”
text.Rotherham: And they said Come on! let us build for ourselves a city, and a tower with its head in the heavens, so let us make for ourselves a name,—lest we be scattered abroad over the face of all the earth.
text.KJV: And they said; Goe to, let vs build vs a city and a tower, whose top may reach vnto heauen, and let vs make vs a name, lest we be scattered abroad vpon the face of the whole earth.
text.NABRE: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the sky, and so make a name for ourselves; otherwise we shall be scattered all over the earth.”
text.DRB: and they said, Come, let us make us a city and a tower, the top whereof may reach to heaven: and let us renown our name before we be dispersed into all lands.

text.NRSV: The Lord came down to see the city and the tower, which mortals had built.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh came down, to behold the city, and the tower,—which the sons of men had built.
text.KJV: And the LORD came downe to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.
text.NABRE: The Lord came down to see the city and the tower that the people had built.
text.DRB: And our Lord descended to see the city and the tower, which the children of Adam builded,

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “Look, they are one people, and they have all one language; and this is only the beginning of what they will do; nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh: Lo! one people and one manner of speech, have they all, this, then is what they have begun to do,—now, therefore, nothing will be withholden from them which they may plan to do.
text.KJV: And the LORD said; Behold, the people is one, and they haue all one language: and this they begin to doe: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they haue imagined to doe.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said: If now, while they are one people and all have the same language, they have started to do this, nothing they presume to do will be out of their reach.
text.DRB: and he said: Behold, it is one people, and one tongue is to all: and they have begun to do this, neither will they leave off from their determinations, till they accomplish them indeed.

text.NRSV: Come, let us go down, and confuse their language there, so that they will not understand one another’s speech.”
text.Rotherham: Come on! let us go down, and there confuse their speech,—that they may not understand, each man the speech of his friend.
text.KJV: Goe to, let vs go downe, and there cōfound their language, that they may not vnderstand one anothers speech.
text.NABRE: Come, let us go down and there confuse their language, so that no one will understand the speech of another.
text.DRB: Come ye therefore, let us go down, and there confound their tongue, that none may hear his neighbour's voice.

text.NRSV: So the Lord scattered them abroad from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh scattered them from thence over the face of all the earth,—and so they left off building the city.
text.KJV: So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence, vpon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the Citie.
text.NABRE: So the Lord scattered them from there over all the earth, and they stopped building the city.
text.DRB: And so our Lord dispersed them from that place into all lands, and they ceased to build the city.

text.NRSV: Therefore it was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth; and from there the Lord scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, was the name thereof called Babel [="Confusion"]; because there, did Yahweh confuse the speech of all the earth,—and, from thence, did Yahweh scatter them abroad, over the face of all the earth.
text.KJV: Therefore is the name of it called Babel, because the LORD did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the LORD scatter them abroad vpon the face of all the earth.
text.NABRE: That is why it was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the speech of all the world. From there the Lord scattered them over all the earth. Descendants from Shem to Abraham.
text.DRB: And therefore the name thereof was called Babel, because there the tongue of the whole earth was confounded: and from thence our Lord dispersed them upon the face of all countries.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Shem. When Shem was one hundred years old, he became the father of Arpachshad two years after the flood;
text.Rotherham: These, are the genealogies of Shem, Shem was a hundred years old when he begat Arpachshad, two years after the flood
text.KJV: ¶ These are the generations of Shem. Shem was an hundred yeres old, and begate Arphaxad two yeeres after the Flood.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Shem. When Shem was one hundred years old, he begot Arpachshad, two years after the flood.
text.DRB: These are the generations of Sem: Sem was an hundred years old when he begat Arphaxad, two years after the flood.

text.NRSV: and Shem lived after the birth of Arpachshad five hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Shem lived after he begat Arpachshad, five hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Shem liued, after he begate Arphaxad, fiue hundred yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Shem lived five hundred years after he begot Arpachshad, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Sem lived after he begat Arphaxad, five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Arpachshad had lived thirty-five years, he became the father of Shelah;
text.Rotherham: And, Arpachshad, lived thirty-five years,—and begat Shelah;
text.KJV: And Arphaxad liued fiue and thirtie yeeres, and begate Salah.
text.NABRE: When Arpachshad was thirty-five years old, he begot Shelah.
text.DRB: Moreover Arphaxad lived thirty five years, and begat Sale.

text.NRSV: and Arpachshad lived after the birth of Shelah four hundred three years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Arpachshad lived after he begat Shelah, four hundred and three years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Arphaxad liued, after he begate Salah, foure hundred and three yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Arpachshad lived four hundred and three years after he begot Shelah, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Sale, three hundred three years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Shelah had lived thirty years, he became the father of Eber;
text.Rotherham: And, Shelah, lived thirty years,—and begat Eber;
text.KJV: And Salah liued thirtie yeeres, and begate Eber.
text.NABRE: When Shelah was thirty years old, he begot Eber.
text.DRB: Sale also lived thirty years, and begat Heber.

text.NRSV: and Shelah lived after the birth of Eber four hundred three years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Shelah lived, after he begat Eber, four hundred and three years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Salah liued, after hee begate Eber, foure hundred and three yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Shelah lived four hundred and three years after he begot Eber, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Sale lived after he begat Heber, four hundred three years, & begat sons & daughters.

text.NRSV: When Eber had lived thirty-four years, he became the father of Peleg;
text.Rotherham: And Eber lived thirty-four years,—and begat Peleg;
text.KJV: And Eber liued foure and thirty yeeres, and begate Peleg.
text.NABRE: When Eber was thirty-four years old, he begot Peleg.
text.DRB: And Heber lived thirty four years, and begat Phaleg.

text.NRSV: and Eber lived after the birth of Peleg four hundred thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Eber lived after he begat Peleg, four hundred and thirty years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Eber liued, after hee begate Peleg, foure hundred and thirtie yeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Eber lived four hundred and thirty years after he begot Peleg, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Heber lived after he begat Phaleg, four hundred thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Peleg had lived thirty years, he became the father of Reu;
text.Rotherham: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:
text.KJV: And Peleg liued thirtie yeeres, and begate Reu.
text.NABRE: When Peleg was thirty years old, he begot Reu.
text.DRB: Phaleg also lived thirty years, and begat Reu.

text.NRSV: and Peleg lived after the birth of Reu two hundred nine years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Peleg lived after he begat Reu, two hundred and nine years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Peleg liued, after hee begate Reu, two hundred and nine yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Peleg lived two hundred and nine years after he begot Reu, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Phaleg lived after he begat Reu, two hundred nine years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Reu had lived thirty-two years, he became the father of Serug;
text.Rotherham: And Reu lived thirty-two years—and begat Serug;
text.KJV: And Reu liued two and thirtie yeeres, and begate Serug.
text.NABRE: When Reu was thirty-two years old, he begot Serug.
text.DRB: And Reu lived thirty two years, and begat Sarug.

text.NRSV: and Reu lived after the birth of Serug two hundred seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Reu lived after he begat Serug, two hundred and seven years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Reu liued, after hee begate Serug, two hundreth and seuen yeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Reu lived two hundred and seven years after he begot Serug, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: Reu lived also after he begat Sarug, two hundred seven years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Serug had lived thirty years, he became the father of Nahor;
text.Rotherham: And Serug lived thirty years,—and begat Nahor;
text.KJV: And Serug liued thirtie yeeres, and begate Nahor.
text.NABRE: When Serug was thirty years old, he begot Nahor.
text.DRB: And Sarug lived thirty years, and begat Nachor.

text.NRSV: and Serug lived after the birth of Nahor two hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Serug lived after he begat Nahor, two hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Serug liued, after he begate Nahor, two hundred yeeres, and begat sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Serug lived two hundred years after he begot Nahor, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Sarug lived after he begat Nachor, two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Nahor had lived twenty-nine years, he became the father of Terah;
text.Rotherham: And Nahor lived twenty-nine years,—and begat Terah;
text.KJV: And Nahor liued nine and twentie yeeres, and begate Terah.
text.NABRE: When Nahor was twenty-nine years old, he begot Terah.
text.DRB: And Nachor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Thare.

text.NRSV: and Nahor lived after the birth of Terah one hundred nineteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Nahor lived, after he begat Terah, a hundred and nineteen years,—and begat sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And Nahor liued, after he begate Terah, an hundred & nineteene yeeres, and begate sonnes and daughters.
text.NABRE: Nahor lived one hundred and nineteen years after he begot Terah, and he had other sons and daughters.
text.DRB: And Nachor lived after he begat Thare, and hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Terah had lived seventy years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
text.Rotherham: And Terah lived seventy years,—and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
text.KJV: And Terah liued seuenty yeeres, and begate Abram, Nahor, & Haran.
text.NABRE: When Terah was seventy years old, he begot Abram, Nahor and Haran.
text.DRB: And Thare lived seventy years, and begat Abram, and Nachor, and Aran.

text.NRSV: Now these are the descendants of Terah. Terah was the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran was the father of Lot.
text.Rotherham: These, then are the generations of Terah, Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran,—and, Haran, begat Lot;
text.KJV: ¶ Now these are the generations of Terah: Terah begate Abram, Nahor, and Haran: And Haran begate Lot.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Terah. Terah begot Abram, Nahor, and Haran, and Haran begot Lot.
text.DRB: And these are the generations of Thare: Thare begat Abram, Nachor, and Aran. Moreover Aran begat Lot.

text.NRSV: Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: and Haran died, in the presence of Terah his father,—in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldees.
text.KJV: And Haran died, before his father Terah in the land of his natiuity, in Ur of the Chaldees.
text.NABRE: Haran died before Terah his father, in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
text.DRB: And Aran died before Thare his father, in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.

text.NRSV: Abram and Nahor took wives; the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s wife was Milcah. She was the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
text.Rotherham: And Abram and Nahor took to themselves wives; the name of Abram’s wife, was Sarai; and, the name of Nahor’s wife, Milcah, daughter of Haran father of Milcah, and father of Iscah.
text.KJV: And Abram and Nahor tooke them wiues: the name of Abrams wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahors wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah, and the father of Iscah.
text.NABRE: Abram and Nahor took wives; the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s wife was Milcah, daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah and Iscah.
text.DRB: And Abram and Nachor married wives: the name of Abram his wife was Sarai: and the name of Nachor his wife, Melcha, the daughter of Aran the father of Melcha, and the father of Jescha.

text.NRSV: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.
text.Rotherham: And, Sarai, remained barren,—she had no child,
text.KJV: But Sarai was barren; she had no childe.
text.NABRE: Sarai was barren; she had no child.
text.DRB: And Sarai was barren, neither had she children.

text.NRSV: Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram’s wife, and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
text.Rotherham: And Terah took Abram his son and Lot son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, wife of Abram his son,—and came forth with them out of Ur of the Chaldees, that they might go their way towards the land of Canaan, and they came in as far as Haran and dwelt there.
text.KJV: And Terah tooke Abram his sonne, and Lot the sonne of Haran his sonnes sonne, and Sarai his daughter in lawe, his sonne Abrams wife, and they went foorth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to goe into the land of Canaan: and they came vnto Haran, and dwelt there.
text.NABRE: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot, son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, the wife of his son Abram, and brought them out of Ur of the Chaldeans, to go to the land of Canaan. But when they reached Haran, they settled there.
text.DRB: Thare therefore took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Aran, his son's son, and Sarai his daughter in law, the wife of Abram his son, and brought them out of Ur of the Chaldees, for to go into the land of Chanaan: and they came as far as Haran, and dwelled there.

text.NRSV: The days of Terah were two hundred five years; and Terah died in Haran.
text.Rotherham: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years,—and Terah died in Haran.
text.KJV: And the dayes of Terah, were two hundred and fiue yeres: and Terah died in Haran.
text.NABRE: The lifetime of Terah was two hundred and five years; then Terah died in Haran.
text.DRB: And the days of Thare came to two hundred five years, and he died in Haran.

text.NRSV: Now the Lord said to Abram, “Go from your country and your kindred and your father’s house to the land that I will show you.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Abram: Come thou on thy way, Out of thy land and out of the place of thy birth and out of the house of thy father,—Unto the land that I will show thee;
text.KJV: Now the LORD had said vnto Abram, Get thee out of thy countrey, and from thy kinred, and from thy fathers house, vnto a land that I will shew thee.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Abram: Go forth from your land, your relatives, and from your father’s house to a land that I will show you.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Abram: Go forth of thy country, and out of thy kindred, and out of thy father's house, and come into a land which I will shew thee.

text.NRSV: I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you, and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.
text.Rotherham: That I may make thee into a great nation, And bless thee and make great thy name, And become thou a blessing;
text.KJV: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I wil blesse thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt bee a blessing.
text.NABRE: I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you; I will make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.
text.DRB: And I will make thee into a great nation, and I will bless thee, and magnify thy name, and thou shalt be blessed.

text.NRSV: I will bless those who bless you, and the one who curses you I will curse; and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.”
text.Rotherham: That I may bless them who bless thee, But him who maketh light of thee, will I curse,—So shall be, blessed in thee, all the families of the ground.
text.KJV: And I will blesse them that blesse thee, and curse him, that curseth thee: and in thee shal all families of the earth be blessed.
text.NABRE: I will bless those who bless you and curse those who curse you. All the families of the earth will find blessing in you.
text.DRB: I will bless them that bless thee, and curse them that curse thee, and IN Thee shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.

text.NRSV: So Abram went, as the Lord had told him; and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.
text.Rotherham: And Abram came on his way, according to that which Yahweh had spoken unto him, and Lot came with him,—now, Abram, was seventy-five years old, when he came forth out of Haran.
text.KJV: So Abram departed, as the LORD had spoken vnto him, and Lot went with him: And Abram was seuentie and fiue yeeres old when he departed out of Haran.
text.NABRE: Abram went as the Lord directed him, and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he left Haran.
text.DRB: Abram therefore went out as our Lord had commanded him, and with him went Lot: seventy five years old was Abraham when he went forth out of Haran.

text.NRSV: Abram took his wife Sarai and his brother’s son Lot, and all the possessions that they had gathered, and the persons whom they had acquired in Haran; and they set forth to go to the land of Canaan. When they had come to the land of Canaan,
text.Rotherham: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother’s son, with all the goods that they had gathered and the souls that they had gained in Haran,—and they came forth to go towards the land of Canaan, and came in to the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: And Abram tooke Sarai his wife, and Lot his brothers sonne, and all their substance that they had gathered, and the soules that they had gotten in Haran, and they went foorth to goe into the land of Canaan: and into the land of Canaan they came.
text.NABRE: Abram took his wife Sarai, his brother’s son Lot, all the possessions that they had accumulated, and the persons they had acquired in Haran, and they set out for the land of Canaan. When they came to the land of Canaan,
text.DRB: And he took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all the substance which they had possessed, and the souls which they had gotten in Haran: and went forth to go into the land of Chanaan. And when they were come into it.

text.NRSV: Abram passed through the land to the place at Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time the Canaanites were in the land.
text.Rotherham: And Abram passed along throughout the land, as far as the place of Shechem as far as the Teacher’s Terebinth, the Canaanite being then in the land.
text.KJV: ¶ And Abram passed through the land, vnto the place of Sichem, vnto the plaine of Moreh. And the Canaanite was then in the land.
text.NABRE: Abram passed through the land as far as the sacred place at Shechem, by the oak of Moreh. The Canaanites were then in the land.
text.DRB: Abram passed through the country unto the place Sichem, as far as the noble vale: and the Chananite was at that time in the country.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord appeared to Abram, and said, “To your offspring I will give this land.” So he built there an altar to the Lord, who had appeared to him.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appeared unto Abram, and said: To thy seed, will I give this land,—And he built there an altar, unto Yahweh who appeared unto him.
text.KJV: And the LORD appeared vnto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed wil I giue this land: and there builded hee an altar vnto the LORD, who appeared vnto him.
text.NABRE: The Lord appeared to Abram and said: To your descendants I will give this land. So Abram built an altar there to the Lord who had appeared to him.
text.DRB: And our Lord appeared to Abram, and said to him: To thy seed will I give this land. Who builded there an altar to our Lord, that had appeared to him.

text.NRSV: From there he moved on to the hill country on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east; and there he built an altar to the Lord and invoked the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he moved on from thence towards the hill country, on the east of Bethel and pitched his tent,—with Bethel on the west, and Ai on the east, and built there an altar to Yahweh, and called on the name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he remoued from thence vnto a mountaine, on the East of Beth-el, and pitched his tent hauing Beth-el on the West, and Hai on the East: and there hee builded an altar vnto the LORD, and called vpon the Name of the LORD.
text.NABRE: From there he moved on to the hill country east of Bethel, pitching his tent with Bethel to the west and Ai to the east. He built an altar there to the Lord and invoked the Lord by name.
text.DRB: And marching on from thence to a mountain, that was on the east side of Bethel, there he pitched his tent, having Bethel on the west, and Hai on the east: he builded there also an altar to our Lord, and called upon his name.

text.NRSV: And Abram journeyed on by stages toward the Negeb.
text.Rotherham: Thus Abram brake up, again and again, towards the South.
text.KJV: And Abram iourneyed, going on still toward the South.
text.NABRE: Then Abram journeyed on by stages to the Negeb. Abram and Sarai in Egypt.
text.DRB: And Abram went forward going, and proceeding on to the south.

text.NRSV: Now there was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to reside there as an alien, for the famine was severe in the land.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that there was a famine, in the land,—so Abram went down towards Egypt, to sojourn there, because grievous, was the famine in the land.
text.KJV: ¶ And there was a famine in the land, and Abram went downe into Egypt, to soiourne there: for the famine was grieuous in the land.
text.NABRE: There was famine in the land; so Abram went down to Egypt to sojourn there, since the famine in the land was severe.
text.DRB: And there came a famine in the country: and Abram descended into Ægypt, to be as a pilgrim there: for the famine was very sore in the land.

text.NRSV: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, “I know well that you are a woman beautiful in appearance;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when he had come near to enter into Egypt, that he said unto Sarai his wife: Behold! I beseech thee I know that a woman fair to look on, thou art:
text.KJV: And it came to passe when he was come neere to enter into Egypt, that he said vnto Sarai his wife, Behold now, I know that thou art a faire woman to looke vpon.
text.NABRE: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai: “I know that you are a beautiful woman.
text.DRB: And when he was near to enter into Ægypt, he said to Sarai his wife: I know that thou art a fair woman,

text.NRSV: and when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife’; then they will kill me, but they will let you live.
text.Rotherham: so will it come to pass when the Egyptians behold thee, that they will say, His wife, this! and will slay me while thee, they preserve alive.
text.KJV: Therefore it shall come to passe, when the Egyptians shall see thee, that they shall say, This is his wife: and they will kill me, but they will saue thee aliue.
text.NABRE: When the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘She is his wife’; then they will kill me, but let you live.
text.DRB: and that when the Ægyptians shall see thee, they will say: She is his wife; and they will kill me, and reserve thee.

text.NRSV: Say you are my sister, so that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared on your account.”
text.Rotherham: Say, I beseech thee that my sister, art thou,—to the end it may be well with me for thy sake, so shall my soul be preserved alive because of thee.
text.KJV: Say, I pray thee, thou art my sister, that it may be wel with me, for thy sake; and my soule shall liue, because of thee.
text.NABRE: Please say, therefore, that you are my sister, so that I may fare well on your account and my life may be spared for your sake.”
text.DRB: Say therefore, I pray thee, that thou art my sister: that I may be well used for thee, and that my soul may live for thy sake.

text.NRSV: When Abram entered Egypt the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.
text.Rotherham: And so it was when Abram entered into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman, that fair, was she exceedingly.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, that when Abram was come into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman, that shee was very faire.
text.NABRE: When Abram arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.
text.DRB: When Abram therefore was entered into Ægypt, the Ægyptians, saw the woman that she was passing beautiful.

text.NRSV: When the officials of Pharaoh saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh. And the woman was taken into Pharaoh’s house.
text.Rotherham: And the princes of Pharaoh beheld her, and praised her unto Pharaoh,—so the woman was taken to the house of Pharaoh;
text.KJV: The Princes also of Pharaoh saw her, and commended her before Pharaoh: and the woman was taken into Pharaohs house.
text.NABRE: When Pharaoh’s officials saw her they praised her to Pharaoh, and the woman was taken into Pharaoh’s house.
text.DRB: And the Princes told Pharao, and praised her to him: and the woman was taken into the house of Pharao.

text.NRSV: And for her sake he dealt well with Abram; and he had sheep, oxen, male donkeys, male and female slaves, female donkeys, and camels.
text.Rotherham: and with Abram, dealt he well for her sake,—so that he came to have flocks and herds and he-asses, and men-servants, and maid-servants, and she-asses and camels.
text.KJV: And he entreated Abram well for her sake: and he had sheepe, and oxen, and hee asses, and men seruants, and maid seruants, and shee asses, and camels.
text.NABRE: Abram fared well on her account, and he acquired sheep, oxen, male and female servants, male and female donkeys, and camels.
text.DRB: And they used Abram well for her sake. And he had sheep and oxen and he asses, and men servants, and maid servants, and she asses, and Camels.

text.NRSV: But the Lord afflicted Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram’s wife.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh plagued Pharaoh with great plagues, also his house,—for the matter of Sarai, wife of Abram.
text.KJV: And the LORD plagued Pharaoh & his house with great plagues, because of Sarai Abrams wife.
text.NABRE: But the Lord struck Pharaoh and his household with severe plagues because of Sarai, Abram’s wife.
text.DRB: And our Lord scourged Pharao with very sore plagues, and his house, for Sarai Abram's wife.

text.NRSV: So Pharaoh called Abram, and said, “What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh called out to Abram, and said, What is this thou hast done to me? Wherefore didst thou not tell me, that she was, thy wife?
text.KJV: And Pharaoh called Abram, and said, What is this that thou hast done vnto me? Why diddest thou not tell me, that she was thy wife?
text.NABRE: Then Pharaoh summoned Abram and said to him: “How could you do this to me! Why did you not tell me she was your wife?
text.DRB: And Pharao called Abram, and said to him: What is this that thou hast done to me? Why didst thou not tell me that she was thy wife?

text.NRSV: Why did you say, ‘She is my sister,’ so that I took her for my wife? Now then, here is your wife, take her, and be gone.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore saidst thou My sister, she; and so I was about to take her to me, to wife? But now, lo! thy wife take her and go thy way.
text.KJV: Why saidest thou, Shee is my sister? so I might haue taken her to mee to wife: now therfore behold, thy wife, take her and goe thy way.
text.NABRE: Why did you say, ‘She is my sister,’ so that I took her for my wife? Now, here is your wife. Take her and leave!”
text.DRB: For what cause didst thou say she was thy sister, that I might take her to my wife? Now therefore there is thy wife, take her, and go thy ways.

text.NRSV: And Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning him; and they set him on the way, with his wife and all that he had.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh gave command concerning him unto certain men,—and sent him away, with his wife and all that he had.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh cōmanded his men concerning him: and they sent him away, and his wife, and all that he had.
text.NABRE: Then Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning Abram, and they sent him away, with his wife and all that belonged to him.
text.DRB: And Pharao gave certain men commandment in the behalf of Abram: and they conducted him, and his wife, and all that he had.

text.NRSV: So Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the Negeb.
text.Rotherham: So Abram came up out of Egypt he and his wife and all that he had and Lot with him, towards the South.
text.KJV: And Abram went vp out of Egypt, he and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the South.
text.NABRE: From Egypt Abram went up to the Negeb with his wife and all that belonged to him, and Lot went with him.
text.DRB: Abram therefore ascended out of Ægypt, he and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him to the south coast.

text.NRSV: Now Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.
text.Rotherham: Now, Abram, was very rich,—in cattle, in silver and in gold.
text.KJV: And Abram was very rich in cattell, in siluer, and in gold.
text.NABRE: Now Abram was very rich in livestock, silver, and gold.
text.DRB: And he was very rich in possession of gold and silver.

text.NRSV: He journeyed on by stages from the Negeb as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,
text.Rotherham: And he went his way, by his removals, from the South even as far as to Bethel,—as far as the place where his tent was at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai:
text.KJV: And hee went on his iourneyes from the South, euen to Beth-el, vnto the place where his tent had bene at the beginning, betweene Beth-el and Hai:
text.NABRE: From the Negeb he traveled by stages toward Bethel, to the place between Bethel and Ai where his tent had formerly stood,
text.DRB: And he returned by the way that he came, from the south unto Bethel, even to the place where before he had pitched a tabernacle between Bethel and Hai:

text.NRSV: to the place where he had made an altar at the first; and there Abram called on the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: unto the place of the altar, which he made there at first,—and Abram called there, on the name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Unto the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first: and there Abram called on the Name of the LORD.
text.NABRE: the site where he had first built the altar; and there Abram invoked the Lord by name.
text.DRB: in the place of the altar which he had made before, and there he called upon the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents,
text.Rotherham: Now, Lot also, who was going with Abram, had flocks and herds and tents.
text.KJV: ¶ And Lot also which went with Abram, had flocks and heards, & tents.
text.NABRE: Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents,
text.DRB: But Lot also that was with Abram had flocks of sheep, and herds of beasts, and tents.

text.NRSV: so that the land could not support both of them living together; for their possessions were so great that they could not live together,
text.Rotherham: And the land suffered them not to dwell together,—because their substance had become, great, so that they could not dwell together.
text.KJV: And the land was not able to beare them, that they might dwell together: for their substance was great, so that they could not dwell together.
text.NABRE: so that the land could not support them if they stayed together; their possessions were so great that they could not live together.
text.DRB: Neither was the land able to receive them for to dwell together: for their substance was much, and they could not dwell together.

text.NRSV: and there was strife between the herders of Abram’s livestock and the herders of Lot’s livestock. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites lived in the land.
text.Rotherham: And there arose a strife betwixt the herdmen of Abram, and the herdmen of Lot. Now the Canaanite and the Perizzite, were then dwelling in the land.
text.KJV: And there was a strife betweene the heardmen of Abrams cattell, and the heardmen of Lots cattell: And the Canaanite, and the Perizzite dwelled then in the land.
text.NABRE: There were quarrels between the herders of Abram’s livestock and the herders of Lot’s livestock. At this time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were living in the land.
text.DRB: Whereupon also there arose strife amongst the herdsmen of Abram and of Lot. And that time the Chananite and the Pherisite dwelled in that country.

text.NRSV: Then Abram said to Lot, “Let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herders and my herders; for we are kindred.
text.Rotherham: So then Abram said unto Lot Pray let not cause of strife arise betwixt me and thee, or betwixt my herdmen and thy herdmen; for brethren, are we.
text.KJV: And Abram said vnto Lot, Let there be no strife, I pray thee, betweene mee and thee, and betweene my heardmen and thy heardmen: for wee bee brethren.
text.NABRE: So Abram said to Lot: “Let there be no strife between you and me, or between your herders and my herders, for we are kindred.
text.DRB: Abram therefore said to Lot: Let there be no brawl, I beseech thee, between me and thee, and between my herdsmen, and thy herdsmen: for we be brethren.

text.NRSV: Is not the whole land before you? Separate yourself from me. If you take the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if you take the right hand, then I will go to the left.”
text.Rotherham: Is not, all the land, before thee? I pray thee, separate thyself from me,—if to the left hand, then I will go to the right, if to the right hand, then I will go to the left.
text.KJV: Is not the whole land before thee? Separate thy selfe, I pray thee, from mee: if thou wilt take the left hand, then I will goe to the right: or if thou depart to the right hand, then I will goe to the left.
text.NABRE: Is not the whole land available? Please separate from me. If you prefer the left, I will go to the right; if you prefer the right, I will go to the left.”
text.DRB: Behold the whole land is before thee: go apart from me, I pray thee: if thou wilt go to the left hand, I will take the right: if thou choose the right hand, I will pass to the left.

text.NRSV: Lot looked about him, and saw that the plain of the Jordan was well watered everywhere like the garden of the Lord, like the land of Egypt, in the direction of Zoar; this was before the Lord had destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.
text.Rotherham: So Lot lifted up his eyes and beheld all the circuit of the Jordan, that the whole of it, was well-watered,—before Yahweh destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, like the Garden of Yahweh, like the land of Egypt, as thou enterest into Zoar.
text.KJV: And Lot lifted vp his eyes, and beheld all the plaine of Iordane, that it was well watered euery where before the Lord destroyed Sodome and Gomorah, euen as the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, as thou commest vnto Zoar.
text.NABRE: Lot looked about and saw how abundantly watered the whole Jordan Plain was as far as Zoar, like the Lord’s own garden, or like Egypt. This was before the Lord had destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.
text.DRB: Lot therefore lifting up his eyes, saw all the country about Jordan, which was watered throughout before that our Lord subverted Sodome and Gomorre, as the Paradise of our Lord, and like as Ægypt as men come unto Segor.

text.NRSV: So Lot chose for himself all the plain of the Jordan, and Lot journeyed eastward; thus they separated from each other.
text.Rotherham: And Lot chose for himself all the circuit of the Jordan, so Lot brake up eastwards,—and they separated themselves, each man from his brother:
text.KJV: Then Lot chose him all the plaine of Iordane: and Lot iourneyed East; and they separated themselues the one from the other.
text.NABRE: Lot, therefore, chose for himself the whole Jordan Plain and set out eastward. Thus they separated from each other.
text.DRB: And Lot chose unto him the country about Jordan, and he departed from the East: and they were separated either brother from the other.

text.NRSV: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot settled among the cities of the Plain and moved his tent as far as Sodom.
text.Rotherham: Abram, fixed his dwelling in the land of Canaan,—but, Lot, fixed his dwelling among the cities, of the circuit, and moved his tent as far as Sodom.
text.KJV: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled in the cities of the plaine, and pitched his tent toward Sodome.
text.NABRE: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot settled among the cities of the Plain, pitching his tents near Sodom.
text.DRB: Abram dwelt in the land of Chanaan: and Lot abode in the towns that were about Jordan, and dwelt in Sodome.

text.NRSV: Now the people of Sodom were wicked, great sinners against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Now, the men of Sodom were base and sinful,—against Yahweh, exceedingly.
text.KJV: But the men of Sodome were wicked, and sinners before the LORD exceedingly.
text.NABRE: Now the inhabitants of Sodom were wicked, great sinners against the Lord.
text.DRB: And the men of Sodome were very wicked, and sinners before the face of our Lord out of measure.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, “Raise your eyes now, and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward;
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, said unto Abram after that Lot had separated himself from him, Lift up, I pray thee thine eyes and look, from the place where thou art,—northward and southward and eastward and westward;
text.KJV: ¶ And the LORD said vnto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift vp now thine eyes, and looke from the place where thou art, Northward, and Southward, and Eastward, and Westward.
text.NABRE: After Lot had parted from him, the Lord said to Abram: Look about you, and from where you are, gaze to the north and south, east and west;
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Abram, after that Lot was separated from him: Lift up thine eyes, and look from the place wherein thou now art, to the north and south, to the east and west:

text.NRSV: for all the land that you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever.
text.Rotherham: for all the land which thou art beholding—to thee, will I give it, and to thy seed unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: For all the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seede for euer.
text.NABRE: all the land that you see I will give to you and your descendants forever.
text.DRB: All the land which thou seest will I give to thee, and to thy seed for ever.

text.NRSV: I will make your offspring like the dust of the earth; so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your offspring also can be counted.
text.Rotherham: and I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth,—so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, thy seed also, may be numbered.
text.KJV: And I will make thy seede as the dust of the earth: so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, then shall thy seed also be numbred.
text.NABRE: I will make your descendants like the dust of the earth; if anyone could count the dust of the earth, your descendants too might be counted.
text.DRB: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth: if any man be able to number the dust of the earth, thy seed also shall he be able to number.

text.NRSV: Rise up, walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I will give it to you.”
text.Rotherham: Rise! go up and down in the land, to the length thereof and to the breadth thereof, for to thee, will I give it.
text.KJV: Arise, walke through the land, in the length of it, and in the breadth of it: for I will giue it vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Get up and walk through the land, across its length and breadth, for I give it to you.
text.DRB: Arise and walk through the land in the length, and in the breadth thereof: for I will give it to thee.

text.NRSV: So Abram moved his tent, and came and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron; and there he built an altar to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So Abram moved his tent and came in and dwelt among the oaks of Mamre, which were in Hebron,—and built there an altar to Yahweh.
text.KJV: Then Abram remoued his tent, and came and dwelt in the plaine of Mamre, which is in Hebron, and built there an altar vnto the LORD.
text.NABRE: Abram moved his tents and went on to settle near the oak of Mamre, which is at Hebron. There he built an altar to the Lord.
text.DRB: Abram therefore removing his tent, came, and dwelt beside the vale of Mambre, which is in Hebron: and he builded there an altar to our Lord.

text.NRSV: In the days of King Amraphel of Shinar, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the days of Amraphel, king of Shinar, Arioch, king of Ellasar,—Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goim,
text.KJV: And it came to passe in the dayes of Amraphel King of Shinar, Arioch King of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer King of Elam, and Tidal King of nations:
text.NABRE: When Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim
text.DRB: And it came to pass in that time, that Amraphel the king of Sennaar, and Arioch the king of Pontus, and Chodorlahomor king of the Elamites, and Thadal the king of Nations.

text.NRSV: these kings made war with King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).
text.Rotherham: that they made war, with Bera, king of Sodom, and with Birsha, king of Gomorrah,—Shinab, king of Admah, and Shemeber, king of Zeboim, and the king of Bela—the same, is Zoar.
text.KJV: That these made warre with Bera King of Sodome, and with Birsha King of Gomorrah, Shinab King of Admah, and Shemeber King of Zeboiim, and the King of Bela, which is Zoar.
text.NABRE: made war on Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar),
text.DRB: made war against Barra the king of Sodome, and against Bersa the king of Gomorra, and against Sennaab the king of Adama, and against Semebar the king of Seboim, and against the king of Bala, the same is Segor.

text.NRSV: All these joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Dead Sea).
text.Rotherham: All these, joined together in the valley of the open fields, the same, is the Salt Sea.
text.KJV: All these were ioyned together in the vale of Siddim; which is the salt Sea.
text.NABRE: all the latter kings joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Salt Sea).
text.DRB: All these came together into the wood-land vale, which now is the salt sea.

text.NRSV: Twelve years they had served Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
text.Rotherham: Twelve years, had they served Chedorlaomer,—but in the thirteenth year had they rebelled;
text.KJV: Twelue yeeres they serued Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth yeere they rebelled.
text.NABRE: For twelve years they had served Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
text.DRB: For they had served Chodorlahomor twelve years, & the thirteenth year they revolted from him.

text.NRSV: In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and subdued the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,
text.Rotherham: and in the fourteenth year, had Chedorlaomer come in and the kings who were with him, so they smote the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, and the Zuzim in Ham,—and the Emim, in Shaveh-kiriathaim;
text.KJV: And in the fourteenth yeere came Chedorlaomer, and the Kings that were with him, and smote the Rephaims, in Ashteroth Karnaim, & the Zuzims in Ham, and the Emims in Shaueh Kiriathaim;
text.NABRE: In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings allied with him came and defeated the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,
text.DRB: Therefore in the fourteenth year came Chodorlahomor, and the kings that were with him, and they struck Raphaim in Astarothcarnaim, and Susim with them, and Emim in Savee of Cariathaim,

text.NRSV: and the Horites in the hill country of Seir as far as El-paran on the edge of the wilderness;
text.Rotherham: and the Horites in their Mount Seir,—as far as El-paran, which is by the desert.
text.KJV: And the Horites in their mount Seir, vnto El-Paran, which is by the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: and the Horites in the hill country of Seir, as far as El-paran, close by the wilderness.
text.DRB: and the Corrheans in the mountains of Seir, even to the Champion country of Pharan, which is in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: then they turned back and came to En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and subdued all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.
text.Rotherham: So they returned and came in unto En-mishpat, the same, is Kadish, and smote all the field of the Amalekites,—and the Amorites also that dwelt in Hazazon-tamar,
text.KJV: And they returned, and came to En-mishpat, which is Kadesh, & smote all the countrey of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites, that dwelt in Hazezon-tamar.
text.NABRE: They then turned back and came to En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and they subdued the whole country of both the Amalekites and the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.
text.DRB: And they returned, and came as far as the fountain of Misphat, the same is Cades: and they struck all the country of the Amelechites, and of the Amorrheans, that dwell in Asasonthamar.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out, and they joined battle in the Valley of Siddim
text.Rotherham: Thus then went forth the King of Sodom and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah and the king of Zeboim, and the king of Bela the same, is Zoar—and set themselves in array against them for battle, in the valley of the open fields:
text.KJV: And there went out the King of Sodome, and the King of Gomorrah, and the King of Admah, and the King of Zeboiim, and the King of Bela, (the same is Zoar) and they ioyned battell with them, in the vale of Siddim,
text.NABRE: Thereupon the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) marched out, and in the Valley of Siddim they went into battle against them:
text.DRB: And they went forth the king of Sodome, and the king of Gomorra, and the king of Adama, and the king of Seboim, moreover also the king of Bala, which is Segor: and they set themselves against them in battle array in the wood-land vale:

text.NRSV: with King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Shinar, and King Arioch of Ellasar, four kings against five.
text.Rotherham: against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goim, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar,—four kings against five.
text.KJV: With Chedorlaomer the King of Elam, and with Tidal King of nations, and Amraphel King of Shinar, and Arioch King of Ellasar; foure Kings with fiue.
text.NABRE: against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar—four kings against five.
text.DRB: to wit against Chodorlahomor king of the Elamites, and Thadal king of Nations, and Amraphel king of Sennaar, and Arioch king of Pontus: four kings against five.

text.NRSV: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits; and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, and the rest fled to the hill country.
text.Rotherham: Now, the valley of the open fields, had many pits of bitumen, so the king of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there,—while, they who remained, towards a mountain, fled.
text.KJV: And the vale of Siddim was full of slime-pits: and the Kings of Sodome & Gomorrah fled, and fell there: and they that remained, fled to the mountaine.
text.NABRE: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits; and as the king of Sodom and the king of Gomorrah fled, they fell into these, while the rest fled to the mountains.
text.DRB: But the wood-land vale had many pits of bitume. Therefore the king of Sodome and of Gomorra turned their backs, and were overthrown there: and they that remained fled to the mountain.

text.NRSV: So the enemy took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their provisions, and went their way;
text.Rotherham: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food and went their way.
text.KJV: And they tooke all the goods of Sodome and Gomorrah, and all their victuals, and went their way.
text.NABRE: The victors seized all the possessions and food supplies of Sodom and Gomorrah and then went their way.
text.DRB: And they took all the substance of the Sodomites and Gomorrheans, and took all kind of victuals, and went their way:

text.NRSV: they also took Lot, the son of Abram’s brother, who lived in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.
text.Rotherham: And they took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son with his goods, and went their way,—he, being a dweller in Sodom.
text.KJV: And they tooke Lot, Abrams brothers sonne, (who dwelt in Sodome) and his goods, and departed.
text.NABRE: They took with them Abram’s nephew Lot, who had been living in Sodom, as well as his possessions, and departed.
text.DRB: and Lot also and his substance, the son of Abrams brother, who dwelled in Sodom.

text.NRSV: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and of Aner; these were allies of Abram.
text.Rotherham: Then came in a fugitive, and told Abram the Hebrew,—he, having his dwelling among the oaks of Mamre the Amorite brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner, they, also having a covenant with Abram.
text.KJV: ¶ And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew, for hee dwelt in the plaine of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner: and these were confederate with Abram.
text.NABRE: A survivor came and brought the news to Abram the Hebrew, who was camping at the oak of Mamre the Amorite, a kinsman of Eshcol and Aner; these were allies of Abram.
text.DRB: And behold one that had escaped told Abram the Hebrew, that dwelt in the vale of Mambre, the Amorrean, brother of Eschol, and the brother of Aner: for these made a league with Abram.

text.NRSV: When Abram heard that his nephew had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, three hundred eighteen of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
text.Rotherham: So Abram, hearing that his brother had been taken captive, drew forth his trained men born in his house three hundred and eighteen, and pursued as far as Dan.
text.KJV: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captiue, he armed his trained seruants borne in his owne house, three hundred and eighteene, and pursued them vnto Dan.
text.NABRE: When Abram heard that his kinsman had been captured, he mustered three hundred and eighteen of his retainers, born in his house, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
text.DRB: Which when Abram had heard, to wit, that his brother Lot was taken, he numbered of the servants born in his house, well appointed, three hundred and eighteen: and pursued them unto Dan.

text.NRSV: He divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and routed them and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
text.Rotherham: And he divided himself against them by night, he, and his servants, and smote them,—and pursued them as far as Hobah, which was on the left of Damascus.
text.KJV: And hee diuided himselfe against them, he and his seruants by night, and smote them, and pursued them vnto Hoba, which is on the left hand of Damascus:
text.NABRE: He and his servants deployed against them at night, defeated them, and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is north of Damascus.
text.DRB: And dividing his company, he ran upon them in the night, and struck them, and pursued them unto Hoba, which is on the left hand of Damascus.

text.NRSV: Then he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his nephew Lot with his goods, and the women and the people.
text.Rotherham: So he brought back all the goods,—yea Lot also his brother with his goods, did he bring back, and the women also, and the people.
text.KJV: And hee brought backe all the goods, and also brought againe his brother Lot, and his goods, and the women also, and the people.
text.NABRE: He recovered all the possessions. He also recovered his kinsman Lot and his possessions, along with the women and the other people.
text.DRB: And he brought back all the substance, and Lot his brother with his substance, the women also and the people.

text.NRSV: After his return from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King’s Valley).
text.Rotherham: Then came forth the king of Sodom to meet him, after his return from the smiting of Chedorlaomer, and the kings who were with him,—into the vale of Shaveh the same, was the vale of the king.
text.KJV: ¶ And the king of Sodome went out to meete him, (after his returne from the slaughter of Chedorlaomer, and of the Kings that were with him) at the valley of Saueh, which is the Kings dale.
text.NABRE: When Abram returned from his defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were allied with him, the king of Sodom went out to greet him in the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King’s Valley).
text.DRB: And the king of Sodom went forth to meet them, after he returned from the slaughter of Chodorlahomor, and of the kings that were with him in the vale Savee, which is the king's vale.

text.NRSV: And King Melchizedek of Salem brought out bread and wine; he was priest of God Most High.
text.Rotherham: Now Melchizedek king of Salem, had brought forth bread and wine,—he, being priest of GOD Most High.
text.KJV: And Melchizedek King of Salem brought foorth bread and wine: and he was the Priest of the most high God.
text.NABRE: Melchizedek, king of Salem, brought out bread and wine. He was a priest of God Most High.
text.DRB: But Melchisedech the king of Salem, bringing forth bread and wine, for he was the Priest of God most High,

text.NRSV: He blessed him and said, “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, maker of heaven and earth;
text.Rotherham: So he blessed him and said,—Blessed be Abram of GOD Most High, possessor of [the] heavens and earth;
text.KJV: And hee blessed him, and saide; Blessed bee Abram of the most high God, possessour of heauen and earth,
text.NABRE: He blessed Abram with these words: “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, the creator of heaven and earth;
text.DRB: blessed him, and said: Blessed be Abram to God the Highest, which created heaven and earth:

text.NRSV: and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand!” And Abram gave him one tenth of everything.
text.Rotherham: And blessed be GOD Most High, who hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. So he gave unto him a tenth of all.
text.KJV: And blessed bee the most high God, which hath deliuered thine enemies into thy hand: and hee gaue him tithes of all.
text.NABRE: And blessed be God Most High, who delivered your foes into your hand.” Then Abram gave him a tenth of everything.
text.DRB: and blessed be God the Highest, by whose protection the enemies are in thy hands. And he gave him the tithes of all.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the persons, but take the goods for yourself.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Sodom unto Abram,—Give unto me the persons, but the goods, take thou for thyself.
text.KJV: And the King of Sodome said vnto Abram, giue me the persons, and take the goods to thy selfe.
text.NABRE: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the captives; the goods you may keep.”
text.DRB: And the king of Sodom said to Abram: Give me the souls, and the rest take to thee.

text.NRSV: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, “I have sworn to the Lord, God Most High, maker of heaven and earth,
text.Rotherham: And Abram said unto the king of Sodom,—I have lifted up my hand unto Yahweh GOD Most High, possessor of [the] heavens and earth:
text.KJV: And Abram said to the King of Sodome, I haue lift vp my hand vnto the LORD, the most high God, the possessour of heauen and earth,
text.NABRE: But Abram replied to the king of Sodom: “I have sworn to the Lord, God Most High, the creator of heaven and earth,
text.DRB: Who answered him: I lift up my hand to my Lord God most High, possessor of heaven and earth,

text.NRSV: that I would not take a thread or a sandal-thong or anything that is yours, so that you might not say, ‘I have made Abram rich.’
text.Rotherham: That not from a thread even unto a sandal-thong,—will I take, anything, that is thine,—Lest thou shouldst say, I, enriched Abram!
text.KJV: That I wil not take from a threed euen to a shoe latchet, and that I will not take any thing that is thine, lest thou shouldest say, I haue made Abram rich:
text.NABRE: that I would not take so much as a thread or a sandal strap from anything that is yours, so that you cannot say, ‘I made Abram rich.’
text.DRB: that from the very woof-thread unto the shoe latchet, I will not take of all that are thine, lest thou say, I have enriched Abram:

text.NRSV: I will take nothing but what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre. Let them take their share.”
text.Rotherham: Save only what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me, Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre, they may take their share.
text.KJV: Saue onely that which the yong men haue eaten, and the portion of the men which went with mee, Aner, Eschol, and Mamre; let them take their portion.
text.NABRE: Nothing for me except what my servants have consumed and the share that is due to the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol and Mamre; let them take their share.”
text.DRB: except such things as the young men have eaten, and the shares of the men that came with me, Aner, Eschol, and Mambre: these shall take their shares.

text.NRSV: After these things the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision, “Do not be afraid, Abram, I am your shield; your reward shall be very great.”
text.Rotherham: After these things, came the word of Yahweh unto Abram, in a vision saying,—Do not fear Abram, I am a shield to thee, thine exceeding great reward.
text.KJV: After these things, the word of the LORD came vnto Abram in a vision, saying; Feare not, Abram: I am thy shield, and thy exceeding great reward.
text.NABRE: Some time afterward, the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision: Do not fear, Abram! I am your shield; I will make your reward very great.
text.DRB: When these things therefore were done, the word of our Lord was made to Abram by a vision saying: fear not Abram, I am thy Protector, and thy reward exceeding great.

text.NRSV: But Abram said, “O Lord GOD, what will you give me, for I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?”
text.Rotherham: And Abram said My Lord Yahweh, what canst thou give me, when, I, am going on childless,—and the heir of my house, is Eliezer, of Damascus,
text.KJV: And Abram said, Lord GOD, what wilt thou giue me, seeing I goe childlesse? and the steward of my house is this Eliezer of Damascus.
text.NABRE: But Abram said, “Lord God, what can you give me, if I die childless and have only a servant of my household, Eliezer of Damascus?”
text.DRB: And Abram said: Lord God, what wilt thou give me? I shall go without children: and the son of the steward of my house is this Damascus Eliezer.

text.NRSV: And Abram said, “You have given me no offspring, and so a slave born in my house is to be my heir.”
text.Rotherham: And Abram said—Lo, to me, hast thou not given seed,—And lo! a son of my household, is mine heir!
text.KJV: And Abram said; Behold, to mee thou hast given no seed: and loe, one borne in my house is mine heire.
text.NABRE: Abram continued, “Look, you have given me no offspring, so a servant of my household will be my heir.”
text.DRB: And Abram added: But to me thou hast not given seed: and Lo my servant born in my house shall be mine heir.

text.NRSV: But the word of the Lord came to him, “This man shall not be your heir; no one but your very own issue shall be your heir.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! the word of Yahweh [came] unto him, saying, This one, shall not be thine heir: But one who cometh forth of thy body—he shall be thine heir.
text.KJV: And behold, the word of the LORD came vnto him, saying; This shall not be thine heire: but he that shall come foorth out of thy owne bowels, shalbe thine heire.
text.NABRE: Then the word of the Lord came to him: No, that one will not be your heir; your own offspring will be your heir.
text.DRB: And immediately the word of our Lord came to him saying: He shall not be thine heir: but he that shall come out of thy womb him shalt thou have thine heir.

text.NRSV: He brought him outside and said, “Look toward heaven and count the stars, if you are able to count them.” Then he said to him, “So shall your descendants be.”
text.Rotherham: And he brought him forth abroad and said—Look steadfastly. I pray thee towards the heavens, and number the stars, if thou be able to number, them, And he said to him, Thus, shall be thy seed.
text.KJV: And he brought him forth abroad, and said, Looke now towards heauen, and tell the starres, if thou be able to number them. And hee said vnto him, So shall thy seed be.
text.NABRE: He took him outside and said: Look up at the sky and count the stars, if you can. Just so, he added, will your descendants be.
text.DRB: And he brought him forth abroad, and said to him: Look up to heaven, and number the stars, if thou canst. And he said to him: so shall thy seed be.

text.NRSV: And he believed the Lord; and the Lord reckoned it to him as righteousness.
text.Rotherham: And he had faith in Yahweh,—so he reckoned it to him as righteousness.
text.KJV: And he beleeued in the LORD; and hee counted it to him for righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: Abram put his faith in the Lord, who attributed it to him as an act of righteousness.
text.DRB: Abram believed God, and it was reputed to him unto justice.

text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “I am the Lord who brought you from Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him,—I, am Yahweh, who brought thee forth out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give thee this land to inherit it.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him; I am the LORD that brought thee out of Ur of the Caldees, to give thee this land, to inherit it.
text.NABRE: He then said to him: I am the Lord who brought you from Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land as a possession.
text.DRB: And he said to him: I am the Lord that brought thee out from Ur of the Chaldees for to give thee this land, and that thou mightest possess it.

text.NRSV: But he said, “O Lord GOD, how am I to know that I shall possess it?”
text.Rotherham: And he said, My Lord Yahweh, whereby, can I know that I shall inherit it?
text.KJV: And he said, Lord GOD, whereby shal I know that I shall inherit it?
text.NABRE: “Lord God,” he asked, “how will I know that I will possess it?”
text.DRB: But he said: Lord God, how may I know that I shall possess it?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Bring me a heifer three years old, a female goat three years old, a ram three years old, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.”
