text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, Take for me a heifer three years old, and a she-goat three years old, and a ram three years old, and a turtle-dove and a young pigeon.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Take me an heifer of three yeeres old, and a shee goat of three yeeres old, and a ramme of three yeeres old, and a turtle doue, and a yong pigeon.
text.NABRE: He answered him: Bring me a three-year-old heifer, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.
text.DRB: And our Lord answered, and said: Take me a cow of three years old, and a she goat of three years, and a ram of three years, a turtle also, and a pigeon.

text.NRSV: He brought him all these and cut them in two, laying each half over against the other; but he did not cut the birds in two.
text.Rotherham: So he took for him all these, and divided them, in the midst, and placed each piece over against its fellow, but the birds, divided he not.
text.KJV: And he tooke vnto him all these, and diuided them in the midst, and layd each peece one against another: but the birds diuided he not.
text.NABRE: He brought him all these, split them in two, and placed each half opposite the other; but the birds he did not cut up.
text.DRB: Who taking all these, divided them by the midst, and laid each two pieces arow one against the other: but the birds he divided not.

text.NRSV: And when birds of prey came down on the carcasses, Abram drove them away.
text.Rotherham: Then came down the birds of prey upon the carcass, and Abram drove them away.
text.KJV: And when the fowles came downe vpon the carcases, Abram droue them away.
text.NABRE: Birds of prey swooped down on the carcasses, but Abram scared them away.
text.DRB: And the fowls lighted upon the carcasses, and Abram drove them away.

text.NRSV: As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram, and a deep and terrifying darkness descended upon him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when, the sun, was about to go in, and, a deep sleep, had fallen upon Abram, lo! a terror a great darkness, was falling upon him.
text.KJV: And when the Sunne was going downe, a deepe sleepe fell vpon Abram: and loe, an horrour of great darkenesse fell vpon him.
text.NABRE: As the sun was about to set, a deep sleep fell upon Abram, and a great, dark dread descended upon him.
text.DRB: And when the sun was setting, a deep sleep fell upon Abram, and a great and darksome horror invaded him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Abram, “Know this for certain, that your offspring shall be aliens in a land that is not theirs, and shall be slaves there, and they shall be oppressed for four hundred years;
text.Rotherham: And he said to Abram—Thou must surely know, that, sojourners, will thy seed become in a land not theirs, and shall serve them and they will humble them, four hundred years;
text.KJV: And he said vnto Abram, Know of a surety, that thy seed shalbe a stranger, in a land that is not theirs, and shal serue them, and they shall afflict them foure hundred yeeres.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Abram: Know for certain that your descendants will reside as aliens in a land not their own, where they shall be enslaved and oppressed for four hundred years.
text.DRB: And it was said unto him: Know and foreknow that a pilgrim shall thy seed be in a land not their own (and they shall bring them under bondage, and afflict them) four hundred years.

text.NRSV: but I will bring judgment on the nation that they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions.
text.Rotherham: moreover also, the nation whom they will serve, I, am going to judge, and after that, shall they come forth with great substance.
text.KJV: And also that nation whom they shall serue, wil I iudge: and afterward shall they come out with great substance.
text.NABRE: But I will bring judgment on the nation they must serve, and after this they will go out with great wealth.
text.DRB: But the Nation, whom they shall serve, I will judge: and after this they shall go forth with great substance.

text.NRSV: As for yourself, you shall go to your ancestors in peace; you shall be buried in a good old age.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, shalt go in unto thy fathers in peace, thou shalt be buried in a good old age.
text.KJV: And thou shalt goe to thy fathers in peace; thou shalt be buried in a good old age.
text.NABRE: You, however, will go to your ancestors in peace; you will be buried at a ripe old age.
text.DRB: And thou shalt go to thy fathers in peace, buried in a good old age.

text.NRSV: And they shall come back here in the fourth generation; for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.”
text.Rotherham: And, in the fourth generation, shall they return hither,—for not complete, is the iniquity of the Amorites, as yet,
text.KJV: But in the fourth generation they shall come hither againe: for the iniquitie of the Amorites is not yet full.
text.NABRE: In the fourth generation your descendants will return here, for the wickedness of the Amorites is not yet complete.
text.DRB: But in the fourth generation they shall return hither: for as yet the iniquities of the Amorrheans are not at the full until this present time.

text.NRSV: When the sun had gone down and it was dark, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when, the sun, had gone in, and, a thick darkness, had come on, that lo there was a smoking hearth and torch of fire, which passed through between these pieces.
text.KJV: And it came to passe that when the Sunne went downe, and it was darke, behold, a smoking furnace, and a burning lampe that passed betweene those pieces.
text.NABRE: When the sun had set and it was dark, there appeared a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch, which passed between those pieces.
text.DRB: Therefore when the sun was set, there arose a dark mist, and there appeared a furnace smoking, and a flake of fire passing between those divisions.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram, saying, “To your descendants I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates,
text.Rotherham: In that day, did Yahweh solemnise with Abram a covenant, saying,—To thy seed, have I given this land, from the river of Egypt as far as the great river—the river Euphrates:
text.KJV: In that same day the LORD made a couenant with Abram, saying; Unto thy seed haue I giuen this land from the riuer of Egypt vnto the great riuer, the riuer Euphrates:
text.NABRE: On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram, saying: To your descendants I give this land, from the Wadi of Egypt to the Great River, the Euphrates,
text.DRB: That day God made a covenant with Abram, saying: To thy seed will I give this land from the river of Ægypt even to the great river Euphrates.

text.NRSV: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text.Rotherham: the Kenite, and the Kenizzite, and the Kadmonite;
text.KJV: The Kenites, and the Kenizites, and the Kadmonites:
text.NABRE: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text.DRB: the Cineans, and Cenezeites, the Cedmonites,

text.NRSV: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
text.Rotherham: and the Hittite and the Perizzite, and the Rephaim;
text.KJV: And the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Rephaims,
text.NABRE: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
text.DRB: and the Hethits, and the Pherezits, the Raphaims also,

text.NRSV: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.”
text.Rotherham: and the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Girgashite, and the Jebusite.
text.KJV: And the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Girgashites, and the Iebusites.
text.NABRE: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text.DRB: and the Amorreans and the Chananites, and Gargasites, and the Jebusites.

text.NRSV: Now Sarai, Abram’s wife, bore him no children. She had an Egyptian slave-girl whose name was Hagar,
text.Rotherham: But Sarai, Abram’s wife, had borne him no children,—she had, however, an Egyptian handmaid, whose name was Hagar.
text.KJV: Now Sarai Abrams wife bare him no children: and she had an handmaide, an Egyptian, whose name was Hagar.
text.NABRE: Abram’s wife Sarai had borne him no children. Now she had an Egyptian maidservant named Hagar.
text.DRB: Sarai therefore the wife of Abram had brought forth no children; but having an handmaid, an Ægyptian named Agar,

text.NRSV: and Sarai said to Abram, “You see that the Lord has prevented me from bearing children; go in to my slave-girl; it may be that I shall obtain children by her.” And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.
text.Rotherham: So then Sarai said unto Abram Behold, I pray thee, Yahweh hath restrained me, from bearing, go in I pray thee unto, my handmaid, peradventure I may be built up from, her, And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.
text.KJV: And Sarai said vnto Abram, Behold now, the LORD hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee go in vnto my maid: it may bee that I may obtaine children by her: and Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.
text.NABRE: Sarai said to Abram: “The Lord has kept me from bearing children. Have intercourse with my maid; perhaps I will have sons through her.” Abram obeyed Sarai.
text.DRB: she said to her husband: Behold, our Lord hath closed me, that I might not bear; Go in unto my handmaid, if happily of her at the least I may have children. And when he agreed to her in this request,

text.NRSV: So, after Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Sarai, Abram’s wife, took Hagar the Egyptian, her slave-girl, and gave her to her husband Abram as a wife.
text.Rotherham: So Sarai, Abram’s wife, took Hagar the Egyptian. her handmaid, at the end of ten years of Abram’s dwelling in the land of Canaan, and gave her to Abram her husband, to be to him as a wife.
text.KJV: And Sarai Abrams wife, tooke Hagar her maid, the Egyptian, after Abram had dwelt ten yeeres in the land of Canaan, and gaue her to her husband Abram, to be his wife.
text.NABRE: Thus, after Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, his wife Sarai took her maid, Hagar the Egyptian, and gave her to her husband Abram to be his wife.
text.DRB: she took Agar the Ægyptian her handmaid ten years after that they first dwelled in the land of Chanaan, and gave her unto her husband to wife.

text.NRSV: He went in to Hagar, and she conceived; and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked with contempt on her mistress.
text.Rotherham: And he went in unto Hagar and she conceived,—and when she saw that she had conceived, lightly esteemed, was her lady, in her eyes.
text.KJV: ¶ And he went in vnto Hagar, and she conceiued: And when shee saw that shee had conceiued, her mistresse was despised in her eyes.
text.NABRE: He had intercourse with her, and she became pregnant. As soon as Hagar knew she was pregnant, her mistress lost stature in her eyes.
text.DRB: Who did company with her, but she perceiving that she was with child, despised her mistress.

text.NRSV: Then Sarai said to Abram, “May the wrong done to me be on you! I gave my slave-girl to your embrace, and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked on me with contempt. May the Lord judge between you and me!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Sarai unto Abram: My wrong, is, on thee! I, gave my handmaid into thy bosom, and when she seeth that she hath conceived, then am I lightly esteemed in her eyes Yahweh judge betwixt me and her.
text.KJV: And Sarai said vnto Abram, My wrong be vpon thee: I haue giuen my maid into thy bosome, and when shee saw that she had conceiued, I was despised in her eyes: the LORD iudge betweene me and thee.
text.NABRE: So Sarai said to Abram: “This outrage against me is your fault. I myself gave my maid to your embrace; but ever since she knew she was pregnant, I have lost stature in her eyes. May the Lord decide between you and me!”
text.DRB: And Sarai said to Abram: Thou doest unjustly against me: I gave my handmaid into thy bosom, who perceiving herself to be with child, despiseth me. Our Lord judge between me and thee.

text.NRSV: But Abram said to Sarai, “Your slave-girl is in your power; do to her as you please.” Then Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she ran away from her.
text.Rotherham: And Abram said unto Sarai, Lo! thy handmaid, is in thy hand, do to her what is good in thine eyes. So Sarai humbled her, and she fled from her face.
text.KJV: But Abram said vnto Sarai, Behold, thy maid is in thy hand; doe to her as it pleaseth thee. And when Sarai dealt hardly with her, shee fled from her face.
text.NABRE: Abram told Sarai: “Your maid is in your power. Do to her what you regard as right.” Sarai then mistreated her so much that Hagar ran away from her.
text.DRB: To whom Abram making answer: Behold, saith he, thy handmaid is in thine own hand, use her as it pleaseth thee. When Sarai therefore did afflict her, she ran away.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord found her by a spring of water in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh found her by the fountain of water in the desert,—by the fountain in the way to Shur.
text.KJV: ¶ And the Angel of the LORD found her by a fountaine of water, in the wildernesse, by the fountaine, in the way to Shur:
text.NABRE: The Lord’s angel found her by a spring in the wilderness, the spring on the road to Shur,
text.DRB: And an Angel of our Lord having found her beside a fountain of water in the wilderness, which is in the way to Sur in the desert,

text.NRSV: And he said, “Hagar, slave-girl of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going?” She said, “I am running away from my mistress Sarai.”
text.Rotherham: So he said Hagar! handmaid of Sarai! Whence hast thou come, and whither wouldst thou go? And she said, From the face of Sarai, my lady, am, I, fleeing.
text.KJV: And he said, Hagar Sarais maid, whence camest thou? and whither wilt thou goe? And she said, I flee from the face of my mistresse Sarai.
text.NABRE: and he asked, “Hagar, maid of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going?” She answered, “I am running away from my mistress, Sarai.”
text.DRB: he said to her: Agar, the handmaid of Sarai, whence comest thou? And whither goest thou? Who answered: From the face of Sarai my mistress do I fly.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord said to her, “Return to your mistress, and submit to her.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said to her, Return unto thy lady,—and humble thyself under her hands.
text.KJV: And the Angel of the LORD said vnto her, Returne to thy mistresse, and submit thy selfe vnder her hands.
text.NABRE: But the Lord’s angel told her: “Go back to your mistress and submit to her authority.
text.DRB: And the Angel of our Lord said to her: Return to thy mistress, and humble thyself under her hand.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord also said to her, “I will so greatly multiply your offspring that they cannot be counted for multitude.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said to her, I will, greatly multiply, thy seed—so that it shall not be numbered for multitude.
text.KJV: And the Angel of the LORD said vnto her, I will multiply thy seede exceedingly, that it shall not be numbred for multitude.
text.NABRE: I will make your descendants so numerous,” added the Lord’s angel, “that they will be too many to count.”
text.DRB: And again: Multiplying, saith he, will I multiply thy seed, and it shall not be numbered for the multitude thereof.

text.NRSV: And the angel of the Lord said to her, “Now you have conceived and shall bear a son; you shall call him Ishmael, for the Lord has given heed to your affliction.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said to her, Behold thee! with child, and about bearing a son—and thou shalt call his name Ishmael [="God hearkeneth"] for Yahweh hath hearkened unto thy humiliation.
text.KJV: And the Angel of the LORD said vnto her, Behold, thou art with child, and shalt beare a sonne, and shalt call his name Ishmael; because the LORD hath heard thy affliction.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord’s angel said to her: “You are now pregnant and shall bear a son; you shall name him Ishmael, For the Lord has heeded your affliction.
text.DRB: And again after that: Behold, saith he, thou art with child, and thou shalt bring forth a son; and thou shalt call his name Ismael, because the Lord hath heard thine affliction.

text.NRSV: He shall be a wild ass of a man, with his hand against everyone, and everyone’s hand against him; and he shall live at odds with all his kin.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, will be a wild ass of a man, his hand, against every one, and every one’s hand against him—yet, in presence of all his brethren, shall he have his habitation.
text.KJV: And he will be a wilde man; his hand will be against euery man, and euery mans hand against him: & he shal dwell in the presence of all his brethren.
text.NABRE: He shall be a wild ass of a man, his hand against everyone, and everyone’s hand against him; Alongside all his kindred shall he encamp.”
text.DRB: He shall be a wild man: his hand shall be against all men, and all men's hands against him; and over against all his brethren shall he pitch his tents.

text.NRSV: So she named the Lord who spoke to her, “You are El-roi”; for she said, “Have I really seen God and remained alive after seeing him?”
text.Rotherham: And she called the name of Yahweh, who had spoken unto her, Thou GOD of vision! For she said. Do I even here, retain my vision after a vision?
text.KJV: And shee called the name of the LORD that spake vnto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Haue I also here looked after him that seeth me?
text.NABRE: To the Lord who spoke to her she gave a name, saying, “You are God who sees me”; she meant, “Have I really seen God and remained alive after he saw me?”
text.DRB: And she called the name of our Lord that spake unto her: Thou the God which hast seen me. For she said: verily here have I seen the back parts of him that hast seen me.

text.NRSV: Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; it lies between Kadesh and Bered.
text.Rotherham: On this account, is the well called, the well of the life of vision, lo! it is between Kadesh and Bered.
text.KJV: Wherefore the well was called, Beer-lahai-roi: Behold, It is betweene Cadesh and Bered.
text.NABRE: That is why the well is called Beer-lahai-roi. It is between Kadesh and Bered.
text.DRB: Therefore she called that well, the well of him that liveth and seeth me. The same is between Cadesse and Barad.

text.NRSV: Hagar bore Abram a son; and Abram named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.
text.Rotherham: So Hagar bare to Abram a son,—and Abram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bare, Ishmael.
text.KJV: ¶ And Hagar bare Abram a sonne: and Abram called his sonnes name, which Hagar bare, Ishmael.
text.NABRE: Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram named the son whom Hagar bore him Ishmael.
text.DRB: And Agar brought forth a son to Abram: Who called his name Ismael.

text.NRSV: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore him Ishmael.
text.Rotherham: Now, Abram, was eighty—six years old,—when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.
text.KJV: And Abram was fourescore and sixe yeeres old, when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.
text.NABRE: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore him Ishmael.
text.DRB: Eighty and six years old was Abram when Agar brought him forth Ismael.

text.NRSV: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the Lord appeared to Abram, and said to him, “I am God Almighty; walk before me, and be blameless.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, when Abram was ninety and nine years old, Yahweh appeared unto Abram, and said unto him, I, am GOD Almighty,—Walk, thou before me and become thou blameless:
text.KJV: And when Abram was ninetie yeres old and nine, the LORD appeared to Abram, and said vnto him, I am the almightie God, walke before me, and be thou perfect.
text.NABRE: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the Lord appeared to Abram and said: I am God the Almighty. Walk in my presence and be blameless.
text.DRB: And after that he began to be ninety and nine years old, our Lord appeared unto him, and said unto him: I am the God almighty: walk before me, and be perfect.

text.NRSV: And I will make my covenant between me and you, and will make you exceedingly numerous.”
text.Rotherham: That I may set my covenant betwixt me and thee, And may multiply thee, exceedingly.
text.KJV: And I wil make my couenant betweene me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.
text.NABRE: Between you and me I will establish my covenant, and I will multiply you exceedingly.
text.DRB: And I will make my covenant between me and thee: and I will multiply thee exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Then Abram fell on his face; and God said to him,
text.Rotherham: And Abram fell on his face,—and God spake with him, saying:
text.KJV: And Abram fell on his face, and God talked with him, saying,
text.NABRE: Abram fell face down and God said to him:
text.DRB: Abram fell flat on his face.

text.NRSV: “As for me, this is my covenant with you: You shall be the ancestor of a multitude of nations.
text.Rotherham: As for me, lo! my covenant is with thee,—So shalt thou become—father of a multitude of nations;
text.KJV: As for me, behold, my couenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.
text.NABRE: For my part, here is my covenant with you: you are to become the father of a multitude of nations.
text.DRB: And God said to him: I am, and my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many Nations.

text.NRSV: No longer shall your name be Abram, but your name shall be Abraham; for I have made you the ancestor of a multitude of nations.
text.Rotherham: And thy name shall no more be called Abram,—but thy name shall become Abraham, for father of a multitude of nations, have I appointed thee;
text.KJV: Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall bee Abraham: for a father of many nations haue I made thee.
text.NABRE: No longer will you be called Abram; your name will be Abraham, for I am making you the father of a multitude of nations.
text.DRB: Neither shall thy name be called any more Abram; but thou shalt be called Abraham: because a father of many Nations I have made thee.

text.NRSV: I will make you exceedingly fruitful; and I will make nations of you, and kings shall come from you.
text.Rotherham: And I will make thee fruitful, exceedingly, and grant thee to be nations,—Yea kings, out of thee, shall come forth;
text.KJV: And I will make thee exceeding fruitfull, and I will make nations of thee, and Kings shall come out of thee.
text.NABRE: I will make you exceedingly fertile; I will make nations of you; kings will stem from you.
text.DRB: And I will make thee increase exceedingly, and I will make thee into Nations, and kings shall come forth of thee.

text.NRSV: I will establish my covenant between me and you, and your offspring after you throughout their generations, for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your offspring after you.
text.Rotherham: And I will confirm my covenant betwixt me and thee and thy seed after thee to their generations for an age-abiding covenant,—to become to thee a God, and to thy seed after thee;
text.KJV: And I will establish my couenant betweene me and thee, and thy seede after thee, in their generations for an euerlasting couenant, to bee a God vnto thee, and to thy seed after thee.
text.NABRE: I will maintain my covenant between me and you and your descendants after you throughout the ages as an everlasting covenant, to be your God and the God of your descendants after you.
text.DRB: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee, and between thy seed after thee in their generations by a perpetual covenant: to be thy God, and thy seeds after thee.

text.NRSV: And I will give to you, and to your offspring after you, the land where you are now an alien, all the land of Canaan, for a perpetual holding; and I will be their God.”
text.Rotherham: And I will give to thee and to thy seed after thee the land of thy sojournings—all the land of Canaan, for an age-abiding possession—And I will be to them a God.
text.KJV: And I will giue vnto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger, all the land of Canaan, for an euerlasting possession, and I will be their God.
text.NABRE: I will give to you and to your descendants after you the land in which you are now residing as aliens, the whole land of Canaan, as a permanent possession; and I will be their God.
text.DRB: And I will give to thee, and to thy seed the land of thy peregrination, all the land of Chanaan for a perpetual possession, and I will be their God.

text.NRSV: God said to Abraham, “As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Abraham, But, as for thee, my covenant, must thou keep, thou and thy seed after thee, to their generations:—
text.KJV: ¶ And God said vnto Abraham, Thou shalt keepe my couenant therefore, thou, and thy seede after thee, in their generations.
text.NABRE: God said to Abraham: For your part, you and your descendants after you must keep my covenant throughout the ages.
text.DRB: Again God said to Abraham: And thou therefore shalt keep my covenant, and thy seed after thee in their generations.

text.NRSV: This is my covenant, which you shall keep, between me and you and your offspring after you: Every male among you shall be circumcised.
text.Rotherham: This, is my covenant which ye shall keep, betwixt me and you, and thy seed after thee,—To circumcise to you every male:
text.KJV: This is my couenant, which yee shall keepe betweene me and you, and thy seed after thee: euery man child among you shall be circumcised.
text.NABRE: This is the covenant between me and you and your descendants after you that you must keep: every male among you shall be circumcised.
text.DRB: This is my covenant which you shall observe between me and you, and thy seed after thee: All the male kind of you shall be circumcised:

text.NRSV: You shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskin,—So shall it become a sign of a covenant, betwixt me and you.
text.KJV: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskinne; and it shal be a token of the couenant betwixt me and you.
text.NABRE: Circumcise the flesh of your foreskin. That will be the sign of the covenant between me and you.
text.DRB: and you shall circumcise the flesh of your prepuce, that it may be for a sign of the covenant between me and you.

text.NRSV: Throughout your generations every male among you shall be circumcised when he is eight days old, including the slave born in your house and the one bought with your money from any foreigner who is not of your offspring.
text.Rotherham: And he that is eight days old, shall be circumcised to you every male to your generations,—he that is born of the house, and he that is bought with silver of any son of a stranger, who is, not of thy seed,
text.KJV: And he that is eight dayes olde, shalbe circumcised among you, euery man child in your generations, he that is borne in the house, or bought with money of any stranger, which is not of thy seed.
text.NABRE: Throughout the ages, every male among you, when he is eight days old, shall be circumcised, including houseborn slaves and those acquired with money from any foreigner who is not of your descendants.
text.DRB: An infant of eight days shall be circumcised among you, all male-kind in your generations: as well the homebred shall be circumcised, as the bought servant of whosoever he is, not of your stock:

text.NRSV: Both the slave born in your house and the one bought with your money must be circumcised. So shall my covenant be in your flesh an everlasting covenant.
text.Rotherham: He must surely be circumcised, born of thy house or bought with thy silver,—So shall my covenant be in your flesh for an age-abiding covenant.
text.KJV: He that is borne in thy house, and he that is bought with thy money, must needs be circumcised: and my couenant shall be in your flesh, for an euerlasting couenant.
text.NABRE: Yes, both the houseborn slaves and those acquired with money must be circumcised. Thus my covenant will be in your flesh as an everlasting covenant.
text.DRB: and my covenant shall be in your flesh for a perpetual covenant.

text.NRSV: Any uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.”
text.Rotherham: But, as for the uncircumcised male who shall not be circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin,—that person shall be cut off from among his people,—my covenant, hath he made void.
text.KJV: And the vncircumcised man-child, whose flesh of his foreskinne is not circumcised, that soule shall be cut off from his people: hee hath broken my couenant.
text.NABRE: If a male is uncircumcised, that is, if the flesh of his foreskin has not been cut away, such a one will be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.
text.DRB: The male, whose flesh of his prepuce shall not be circumcised, that soul shall be destroyed out of his people: because he hath broken my covenant.

text.NRSV: God said to Abraham, “As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai,—but, Sarah, is her name;
text.KJV: ¶ And God said vnto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be.
text.NABRE: God further said to Abraham: As for Sarai your wife, do not call her Sarai; her name will be Sarah.
text.DRB: God said also to Abraham: Sarai thy wife thou shalt not call Sarai, but Sara.

text.NRSV: I will bless her, and moreover I will give you a son by her. I will bless her, and she shall give rise to nations; kings of peoples shall come from her.”
text.Rotherham: and I will bless her, yea moreover will give—from her—to thee, a son,—And I will bless her, and she shall become nations. Kings of peoples, from her, shall arise.
text.KJV: And I will blesse her, and giue thee a sonne also of her: yea I wil blesse her, and she shalbe a mother of nations; Kings of people shall be of her.
text.NABRE: I will bless her, and I will give you a son by her. Her also will I bless; she will give rise to nations, and rulers of peoples will issue from her.
text.DRB: And I will bless her, and of her I will give thee a son, whom I will bless, and he shall be into Nations, and kings of peoples shall spring of him.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said to himself, “Can a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Can Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham fell on his face and laughed,—and said in his heart To one a hundred years old, shall a child be born? And shall even Sarah, who is ninety years old, give birth?
text.KJV: Then Abraham fell vpon his face, and laughed, and said in his heart, Shall a child be borne vnto him that is an hundred yeeres old? and shal Sarah that is ninetie yeeres old, beare?
text.NABRE: Abraham fell face down and laughed as he said to himself, “Can a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Can Sarah give birth at ninety?”
text.DRB: Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, saying in his heart: Shall trowest thou to him that is an hundred year old a son be born? And Sara that is ninety years old shall she bear?

text.NRSV: And Abraham said to God, “O that Ishmael might live in your sight!”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said unto God,—Oh that, Ishmael, might live before thee!
text.KJV: And Abraham said vnto God, O that Ishmael might liue before thee.
text.NABRE: So Abraham said to God, “If only Ishmael could live in your favor!”
text.DRB: And he said to God: I would that Ismael may live before thee.

text.NRSV: God said, “No, but your wife Sarah shall bear you a son, and you shall name him Isaac. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his offspring after him.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Truly, Sarah thy wife, is about to bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name, Isaac,—and I will establish my covenant with him as an age-abiding covenant, to his seed after him.
text.KJV: And God said, Sarah thy wife shall beare thee a sonne in deede, and thou shalt call his name Isaac: and I will establish my couenant with him, for an euerlasting couenant, and with his seed after him.
text.NABRE: God replied: Even so, your wife Sarah is to bear you a son, and you shall call him Isaac. It is with him that I will maintain my covenant as an everlasting covenant and with his descendants after him.
text.DRB: And God said to Abraham: Sara thy wife shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name Isaac, and I will establish my covenant to him for a perpetual covenant, and to his seed after him.

text.NRSV: As for Ishmael, I have heard you; I will bless him and make him fruitful and exceedingly numerous; he shall be the father of twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation.
text.Rotherham: Yet as for Ishmael, I have heard thee; lo! I have blessed him and will make him fruitful, and multiply him, exceedingly,—twelve princes, shall he beget, and I will grant him to be a great nation;
text.KJV: And as for Ishmael, I haue heard thee: behold, I haue blessed him, and will make him fruitfull, and will multiplie him exceedingly: Twelue princes shall he beget, and I will make him a great nation.
text.NABRE: Now as for Ishmael, I will heed you: I hereby bless him. I will make him fertile and will multiply him exceedingly. He will become the father of twelve chieftains, and I will make of him a great nation.
text.DRB: Concerning Ismael also I have heard thee. Behold, I will bless him, and increase and multiply him exceedingly: twelve Dukes shall he beget, and I will make him into a great Nation.

text.NRSV: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this season next year.”
text.Rotherham: But my covenant, will I establish with Isaac,—whom Sarah shall bear, to thee, by this set time, in the next year.
text.KJV: But my couenant wil I establish with Isaac, which Sarah shall beare vnto thee, at this set time, in the next yeere.
text.NABRE: But my covenant I will maintain with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you by this time next year.
text.DRB: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sara shall bring forth to thee at this time another year.

text.NRSV: And when he had finished talking with him, God went up from Abraham.
text.Rotherham: And he left off speaking with him,—and God went up from Abraham.
text.KJV: And he left off talking with him, and God went vp from Abraham.
text.NABRE: When he had finished speaking with Abraham, God departed from him.
text.DRB: And when he had left off speaking with him, God ascended from Abraham.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham took his son Ishmael and all the slaves born in his house or bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham’s house, and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very day, as God had said to him.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham took Ishmael his son and all born of his house and all bought with his silver—every male among the men of the house of Abraham,—and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin on this selfsame day, according to that which God had spoken with him.
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham tooke Ishmael his sonne, and all that were borne in his house, and all that were bought with his money, euery male, among the men of Abrahams house, and circumcised the flesh of their foreskinne, in the selfesame day, as God had said vnto him.
text.NABRE: Then Abraham took his son Ishmael and all his slaves, whether born in his house or acquired with his money—every male among the members of Abraham’s household—and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins on that same day, as God had told him to do.
text.DRB: And Abraham took Ismael his son, and all the homebred of his house: and all whom he had bought, all the males of all the men of his house: and he circumcised the flesh of their prepuce forthwith the very same day, as God had commanded him.

text.NRSV: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
text.Rotherham: Now Abraham, was ninety-nine years old,—when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
text.KJV: And Abraham was ninety yeeres old and nine, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskinne.
text.NABRE: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when the flesh of his foreskin was circumcised,
text.DRB: Abraham was ninety and nine years old, when he circumcised the flesh of his prepuce.

text.NRSV: And his son Ishmael was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
text.Rotherham: And Ishmael, his son, was thirteen years old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
text.KJV: And Ishmael his sonne was thirteene yeeres old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskinne.
text.NABRE: and his son Ishmael was thirteen years old when the flesh of his foreskin was circumcised.
text.DRB: And Ismael his son was full thirteen years old at the time of his circumcision.

text.NRSV: That very day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised;
text.Rotherham: On this selfsame day, was Abraham circumcised,—and Ishmael his son;
text.KJV: In the selfe same day was Abraham circumcised, and Ishmael his sonne.
text.NABRE: Thus, on that same day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised;
text.DRB: The self-same day was Abraham circumcised and Ismael his son.

text.NRSV: and all the men of his house, slaves born in the house and those bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.
text.Rotherham: and, all the men of his house, born of his house, and bought with silver from the son of a stranger, were circumcised with him.
text.KJV: And all the men of his house, borne in the house, and bought with money of the stranger, were circumcised with him.
text.NABRE: and all the males of his household, including the slaves born in his house or acquired with his money from foreigners, were circumcised with him.
text.DRB: And all the men of his house, as well the homebred as the bought servants and strangers were circumcised together.

text.NRSV: The Lord appeared to Abraham by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the entrance of his tent in the heat of the day.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appeared unto him among the oaks of Mamre,—as, he, was sitting at the opening of the tent in the heat of the day.
text.KJV: And the LORD appeared vnto him, in the plaines of Mamre: and he sate in the tent doore, in the heat of the day.
text.NABRE: The Lord appeared to Abraham by the oak of Mamre, as he sat in the entrance of his tent, while the day was growing hot.
text.DRB: And God appeared to him in the vale of Mambre as he sat in the door of his tent, in the very heat of the day.

text.NRSV: He looked up and saw three men standing near him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent entrance to meet them, and bowed down to the ground.
text.Rotherham: And he lifted up his eyes and looked, and lo! three men, standing over against him,—so he looked and ran to meet them from the opening of the tent, and bowed himself to the earth;
text.KJV: And he lift vp his eyes and looked, and loe, three men stood by him: and when he saw them, hee ranne to meete them from the tent doore, and bowed himselfe toward the ground,
text.NABRE: Looking up, he saw three men standing near him. When he saw them, he ran from the entrance of the tent to greet them; and bowing to the ground,
text.DRB: And when he had lifted up his eyes, there appeared to him three men standing near unto him: whom after he had seen, he ran to meet them from the door of his tent, and adored to the ground.

text.NRSV: He said, “My lord, if I find favor with you, do not pass by your servant.
text.Rotherham: and said,—O My Lord! if, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, do not I pray thee pass on from thy servant.
text.KJV: And said, My Lord, If now I haue found fauour in thy sight, passe not away, I pray thee, frō thy seruant:
text.NABRE: he said: “Sir, if it please you, do not go on past your servant.
text.DRB: And He said Lord, if I have found grace in thy sight, go not past thy servant:

text.NRSV: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.
text.Rotherham: Let there be fetched, I pray thee a little water, and bathe ye your feet,—and rest yourselves under the tree.
text.KJV: Let a little water, I pray you, be fetched, and wash your feete, and rest your selues vnder the tree:
text.NABRE: Let some water be brought, that you may bathe your feet, and then rest under the tree.
text.DRB: but I will fetch a little water, and wash ye your feet, and rest ye under the tree.

text.NRSV: Let me bring a little bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on—since you have come to your servant.” So they said, “Do as you have said.”
text.Rotherham: And let me fetch a morsel of bread, and stay ye your heart, Afterwards, ye may pass on, For on this account, have ye passed by, over against your servant. And they said, Thus, shalt thou do as thou hast spoken.
text.KJV: And I will fetch a morsell of bread; and comfort ye your hearts, after that you shall passe on: for therefore are you come to your seruant. And they said; So doe, as thou hast said.
text.NABRE: Now that you have come to your servant, let me bring you a little food, that you may refresh yourselves; and afterward you may go on your way.” “Very well,” they replied, “do as you have said.”
text.DRB: And I will set a morsel of bread, and strengthen your heart, afterward you shall pass: for therefore are you come aside to your servant. Who said: Do as thou hast spoken.

text.NRSV: And Abraham hastened into the tent to Sarah, and said, “Make ready quickly three measures of choice flour, knead it, and make cakes.”
text.Rotherham: So Abraham hastened towards the tent unto Sarah,—and said, Hasten thou three measures of fine meal, knead it, and make hearth-cakes.
text.KJV: And Abraham hastened into the tent, vnto Sarah, & said; Make ready quickly three measures of fine meale, knead it, and make cakes vpon the hearth.
text.NABRE: Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah and said, “Quick, three measures of bran flour! Knead it and make bread.”
text.DRB: Abraham made haste into the tent to Sara, and said to her: Make haste, temper together three measures of flour, and make hearth cakes.

text.NRSV: Abraham ran to the herd, and took a calf, tender and good, and gave it to the servant, who hastened to prepare it.
text.Rotherham: And unto the herd, ran Abraham,—and took a calf tender and good and gave unto the young man, and he hastened to make it ready.
text.KJV: And Abraham ranne vnto the heard, and fetcht a calfe, tender and good, and gaue it vnto a yong man: and he hasted to dresse it.
text.NABRE: He ran to the herd, picked out a tender, choice calf, and gave it to a servant, who quickly prepared it.
text.DRB: But himself ran to the herd, and took from thence a calf very tender and very good, and gave it to a young man: who made haste and boiled it.

text.NRSV: Then he took curds and milk and the calf that he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree while they ate.
text.Rotherham: And he took butter and milk and the calf that he had made ready, and set before them,—while, he, was standing near them under the tree, they did eat.
text.KJV: And he tooke butter, and milke, and the calfe which he had dressed, and set it before them; and he stood by them vnder the tree: and they did eate.
text.NABRE: Then he got some curds and milk, as well as the calf that had been prepared, and set these before them, waiting on them under the tree while they ate.
text.DRB: He took also butter and milk, and the calf which he had boiled, and set before them: but himself did stand beside them under the tree.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Where is your wife Sarah?” And he said, “There, in the tent.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto him, As to Sarah thy wife… And he said Lo! [she is] in the tent.
text.KJV: ¶ And they said vnto him, Where is Sarah thy wife? And he said, Behold, in the tent.
text.NABRE: “Where is your wife Sarah?” they asked him. “There in the tent,” he replied.
text.DRB: And when they had eaten, they said to him: Where is Sara thy wife? He answered: Lo she is in the tent.

text.NRSV: Then one said, “I will surely return to you in due season, and your wife Sarah shall have a son.” And Sarah was listening at the tent entrance behind him.
text.Rotherham: And he said, I will, surely return, unto thee at the quickening season,—and lo! a son for Sarah thy wife. Now Sarah, was hearkening at the opening of the tent, it, being behind him.
text.KJV: And he said, I will certainly returne vnto thee according to the time of life; and loe, Sarah thy wife shall haue a sonne. And Sarah heard it in the tent doore, which was behind him.
text.NABRE: One of them said, “I will return to you about this time next year, and Sarah will then have a son.” Sarah was listening at the entrance of the tent, just behind him.
text.DRB: To whom he said: Returning I will come to thee at this time, life accompanying, and Sara thy wife shall have a son . Which when Sara heard, she laughed behind the door of the tent.

text.NRSV: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in age; it had ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.
text.Rotherham: But Abraham and Sarah, were old, far gone in days,—it had ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.
text.KJV: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, and well stricken in age: and it ceased to be with Sarah after the maner of women.
text.NABRE: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in years, and Sarah had stopped having her menstrual periods.
text.DRB: And they were both aged, and far entered in years, and it ceased to be with Sara after the manner of women.

text.NRSV: So Sarah laughed to herself, saying, “After I have grown old, and my husband is old, shall I have pleasure?”
text.Rotherham: So then Sarah laughed within herself, saying: After I am past age, hath there come to me pleasure, my lord, also being old?
text.KJV: Therefore Sarah laughed within her selfe, saying, After I am waxed old, shall I haue pleasure, my lord being old also?
text.NABRE: So Sarah laughed to herself and said, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, am I still to have sexual pleasure?”
text.DRB: Who laughed secretly saying: After I am waxen old, and my Lord is an old one, shall I give myself to pleasure?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh, and say, ‘Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Abraham,—Wherefore now did Sarah laugh saying. Can it really and truly be that I should bear, seeing that I have become old?
text.KJV: And the LORD said vnto Abraham, Wherefore did Sarah laugh, saying; Shall I of a surety beare a childe, which am old?
text.NABRE: But the Lord said to Abraham: “Why did Sarah laugh and say, ‘Will I really bear a child, old as I am?’
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Abraham: Why did Sara laugh, saying: Shall I an old woman bear a child indeed?

text.NRSV: Is anything too wonderful for the Lord? At the set time I will return to you, in due season, and Sarah shall have a son.”
text.Rotherham: Is anything, too wonderful for Yahweh? At the appointed time, I will return unto thee, at the quickening season and Sarah, shall have a son.
text.KJV: Is any thing too hard for the LORD? At the time appointed will I returne vnto thee, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall haue a sonne.
text.NABRE: Is anything too marvelous for the Lord to do? At the appointed time, about this time next year, I will return to you, and Sarah will have a son.”
text.DRB: Is there any thing hard to God? According to appointment I will return to thee this very self-same time, life accompanying, & Sara shall have a son.

text.NRSV: But Sarah denied, saying, “I did not laugh”; for she was afraid. He said, “Oh yes, you did laugh.”
text.Rotherham: And Sarah denied, saying, I laughed not, For she was afraid. And he said—Nay! but thou didst laugh!
text.KJV: Then Sarah denied, saying, I laughed not: for she was afraid. And he said, Nay, but thou diddest laugh.
text.NABRE: Sarah lied, saying, “I did not laugh,” because she was afraid. But he said, “Yes, you did.”
text.DRB: Sara denied, saying: I laughed not; being much afraid. But our Lord: It is not so, saith he, but thou didst laugh.

text.NRSV: Then the men set out from there, and they looked toward Sodom; and Abraham went with them to set them on their way.
text.Rotherham: And the men rose up from thence, and looked out over the face of Sodom,—Abraham also going with them to set them on their way.
text.KJV: ¶ And the men rose vp from thence, and looked toward Sodome: and Abraham went with them, to bring them on the way.
text.NABRE: With Abraham walking with them to see them on their way, the men set out from there and looked down toward Sodom.
text.DRB: When the men therefore were risen up from thence, they turned their eyes against Sodome: and Abraham did go with them, bringing them on the way.

text.NRSV: The Lord said, “Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, had said,—Am I going to hide from Abraham what I do,
text.KJV: And the LORD said, Shall I hide from Abraham that thing which I doe;
text.NABRE: The Lord considered: Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Can I conceal from Abraham the things which I will do,

text.NRSV: seeing that Abraham shall become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?
text.Rotherham: when, Abraham, shall surely become, a great and mighty nation,—and all the nations of the earth, shall be blessed in him?
text.KJV: Seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?
text.NABRE: now that he is to become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth are to find blessing in him?
text.DRB: whereas he shall be into a Nation great, and very strong, and in him are TO BE Blessed All The Nations of The Earth?

text.NRSV: No, for I have chosen him, that he may charge his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing righteousness and justice; so that the Lord may bring about for Abraham what he has promised him.”
text.Rotherham: For I have become his intimate friend, To the end that he may command his sons and his house after him, so shall they keep the way of Yahweh, by doing righteousness and justice. To the end that Yahweh may bring in for Abraham, what he hath spoken concerning him.
text.KJV: For I know him, that hee will command his children, and his household after him, and they shall keepe the way of the LORD, to doe iustice and iudgement, that the LORD may bring vpon Abraham, that which hee hath spoken of him.
text.NABRE: Indeed, I have singled him out that he may direct his children and his household in the future to keep the way of the Lord by doing what is right and just, so that the Lord may put into effect for Abraham the promises he made about him.
text.DRB: For I know that he will command his children, and his house after him that they keep the way of the Lord, & do judgement and justice: that for Abraham's sake the Lord may bring to effect all the things that he hath spoken unto him. Therefore said our Lord:

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “How great is the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah and how very grave their sin!
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh said, The outcry of Sodom and Gomorrah, because it hath become great,—And their sin, because it hath become exceeding grievous,
text.KJV: And the LORD said, Because the cry of Sodome and Gomorrah is great, and because their sinne is very grieuous:
text.NABRE: So the Lord said: The outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is so great, and their sin so grave,
text.DRB: The cry of Sodome and Gomorre is multiplied, and their sin is aggravated exceedingly.

text.NRSV: I must go down and see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me; and if not, I will know.”
text.Rotherham: Let me go down, pray, and let me behold, whether according to their outcry which hath come in unto me, they have done altogether, And if not. I must know!
text.KJV: I will goe downe now, and see whether they haue done altogether according to the cry of it, which is come vnto me: and if not, I will know.
text.NABRE: that I must go down to see whether or not their actions are as bad as the cry against them that comes to me. I mean to find out.
text.DRB: I will descend, and see whether they have in act accomplished the cry that is come to me: or whether it be not so, that I may know.

text.NRSV: So the men turned from there, and went toward Sodom, while Abraham remained standing before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the men turned from thence, and went their way, towards Sodom,—but, Yahweh was yet standing before Abraham.
text.KJV: And the men turned their faces from thence, and went toward Sodome: but Abraham stood yet before the LORD.
text.NABRE: As the men turned and walked on toward Sodom, Abraham remained standing before the Lord.
text.DRB: And they turned themselves from thence, and went their way to Sodome: but Abraham as yet stood before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham came near and said, “Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?
text.Rotherham: So then Abraham drew near and said, Wilt thou really sweep away, the righteous with the lawless?
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham drew neere, and said, Wilt thou also destroy the righteous with the wicked?
text.NABRE: Then Abraham drew near and said: “Will you really sweep away the righteous with the wicked?
text.DRB: And approaching he said: What! Wilt thou destroy the just with the wicked?

text.NRSV: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city; will you then sweep away the place and not forgive it for the fifty righteous who are in it?
text.Rotherham: Peradventure there are fifty righteous, in the midst of the city, Wilt thou really sweep away, and not spare the place, for the sake of the fifty righteous which are therein?
text.KJV: Peraduenture there be fifty righteous within the citie; wilt thou also destroy, and not spare the place for the fiftie righteous, that are therein?
text.NABRE: Suppose there were fifty righteous people in the city; would you really sweep away and not spare the place for the sake of the fifty righteous people within it?
text.DRB: If there shall be fifty just persons in the city, shall they perish withal? And wilt thou spare that place for fifty just, if they be therein?

text.NRSV: Far be it from you to do such a thing, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous fare as the wicked! Far be that from you! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?”
text.Rotherham: Far be it from thee! to do after this manner. to put to death the righteous with the lawless! Then should righteous and lawless be alike, Far be it from thee! Shall, the Judge of all the earth not do justice?
text.KJV: That be farre from thee, to do after this maner, to slay the righteous with the wicked, and that the righteous should be as the wicked, that be farre from thee: Shall not the Iudge of all the earth doe right?
text.NABRE: Far be it from you to do such a thing, to kill the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous and the wicked are treated alike! Far be it from you! Should not the judge of all the world do what is just?”
text.DRB: Be it far from thee, that thou do this thing, and that thou kill the just with the wicked, and that the just be in like case as the wicked, this is not beseeming thee, which judgest all the earth: no, thou wilt not do this judgement.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “If I find at Sodom fifty righteous in the city, I will forgive the whole place for their sake.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, If I find in Sodom fifty righteous in the midst of the city, then will I spare all the place for their sake.
text.KJV: And the LORD said, If I find in Sodom fiftie righteous, within the citie, then I will spare all the place for their sakes.
text.NABRE: The Lord replied: If I find fifty righteous people in the city of Sodom, I will spare the whole place for their sake.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: If I shall find in Sodome fifty just persons within the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.

text.NRSV: Abraham answered, “Let me take it upon myself to speak to the Lord, I who am but dust and ashes.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham responded and said,—Behold I pray thee I have ventured to speak unto My Lord, though I am dust and ashes:
text.KJV: And Abraham answered, and said, Behold now, I haue taken vpon me to speake vnto the LORD, which am but dust and ashes.
text.NABRE: Abraham spoke up again: “See how I am presuming to speak to my Lord, though I am only dust and ashes!
text.DRB: And Abraham answered, and said: Because I have once begun, I will speak to my Lord, whereas I am dust and ashes.

text.NRSV: Suppose five of the fifty righteous are lacking? Will you destroy the whole city for lack of five?” And he said, “I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.”
text.Rotherham: Peradventure there may lack, of the fifty righteous, five, Wilt thou destroy for five all the city? And he said, I will not destroy, if I find there forty and five.
text.KJV: Peraduenture there shall lacke fiue of the fiftie righteous: wilt thou destroy all the citie for lacke of fiue? And he said, If I find there fourtie and fiue, I will not destroy it.
text.NABRE: What if there are five less than fifty righteous people? Will you destroy the whole city because of those five?” I will not destroy it, he answered, if I find forty-five there.
text.DRB: What if there shall be five less than fifty just persons? Wilt thou for forty five destroy the whole city? And he said: I will not destroy it, if I shall find five and forty.

text.NRSV: Again he spoke to him, “Suppose forty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of forty I will not do it.”
text.Rotherham: And he added, yet once more, to speak unto him, and said, Peradventure there may be found there—forty. And he said—I will not do it, for the sake of the forty.
text.KJV: And hee spake vnto him yet againe, and said, Peraduenture there shall be fourtie found there: and he said, I will not doe it for fourties sake.
text.NABRE: But Abraham persisted, saying, “What if only forty are found there?” He replied: I will refrain from doing it for the sake of the forty.
text.DRB: And again he said unto him: But if forty shall be found there, what wilt thou do? He said: I will not strike it for forty's sake.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Oh do not let the Lord be angry if I speak. Suppose thirty are found there.” He answered, “I will not do it, if I find thirty there.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Let it not, I pray thee, be vexing to My Lord, but let me speak, Peradventure there may be found there—thirty And he said, I will not to it, if I find there—thirty.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speake: Peraduenture there shall thirtie bee found there. And he said, I will not doe it, if I find thirtie there.
text.NABRE: Then he said, “Do not let my Lord be angry if I go on. What if only thirty are found there?” He replied: I will refrain from doing it if I can find thirty there.
text.DRB: Lord, saith he, be not angry I beseech thee, if I speak: What if thirty shall be found there? He answered: I will not do it, if I shall find thirty there.

text.NRSV: He said, “Let me take it upon myself to speak to the Lord. Suppose twenty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of twenty I will not destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Behold, I pray thee, I have ventured to speak unto My Lord, Peradventure there may be found there—twenty. And he said I will not destroy it, for the sake of the twenty.
text.KJV: And he said, Behold now, I haue taken vpon mee to speake vnto the Lord: Peraduenture there shall bee twenty found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for twenties sake.
text.NABRE: Abraham went on, “Since I have thus presumed to speak to my Lord, what if there are no more than twenty?” I will not destroy it, he answered, for the sake of the twenty.
text.DRB: Because, saith he, I have once begun, I will speak to my Lord: What if twenty shall be found there? He said: I will not destroy it for twenty's sake.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Oh do not let the Lord be angry if I speak just once more. Suppose ten are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of ten I will not destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Let it not, I pray thee, be vexing to My Lord, but let me speak only this once, Peradventure there may be found there—ten. And he said, I will not destroy [it], for the sake of the ten!
text.KJV: And hee saide, Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speake yet but this once: Peraduenture ten shall be found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for tennes sake.
text.NABRE: But he persisted: “Please, do not let my Lord be angry if I speak up this last time. What if ten are found there?” For the sake of the ten, he replied, I will not destroy it.
text.DRB: I beseech thee, saith he, be not angry Lord, if I speak yet once more: What if ten shall be found there? And he said: I will not destroy it for ten's sake.

text.NRSV: And the Lord went his way, when he had finished speaking to Abraham; and Abraham returned to his place.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh went his way, when he had made an end. of speaking unto Abraham,—Abraham, also returned to his place.
text.KJV: And the LORD went his way, assoone as hee had left communing with Abraham: and Abraham returned vnto his place.
text.NABRE: The Lord departed as soon as he had finished speaking with Abraham, and Abraham returned home.
text.DRB: And our Lord departed after that he ceased to speak unto Abraham: and Abraham returned into his place.

text.NRSV: The two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gateway of Sodom. When Lot saw them, he rose to meet them, and bowed down with his face to the ground.
text.Rotherham: So the two messengers went in towards Sodom at eventide, Lot, being seated in the gate of Sodom,—so Lot beheld and rose up to meet them, and bowed himself with his face to the earth.
text.KJV: And there came two Angels to Sodome at euen, and Lot sate in the gate of Sodome: and Lot seeing them, rose vp to meet them, and he bowed himselfe with his face toward the ground.
text.NABRE: The two angels reached Sodom in the evening, as Lot was sitting at the gate of Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up to greet them; and bowing down with his face to the ground,
text.DRB: And the two Angels came to Sodome at even, and Lot sitting in the gates of the city. Who when he had seen them, rose up & went to meet them: and adored prostrate unto the ground,

text.NRSV: He said, “Please, my lords, turn aside to your servant’s house and spend the night, and wash your feet; then you can rise early and go on your way.” They said, “No; we will spend the night in the square.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Behold, I pray you my lords turn aside, I pray you, into the house of your servant,—and tarry the night and bathe your feet, so shall ye rise early, and go on your way. And they said, Nay, but, in the broad way, will we tarry the night.
text.KJV: And he said, Beholde now my Lords, turne in, I pray you, into your seruants house, and tarie all night, and wash your feete, and ye shall rise vp early and goe on your wayes. And they said, Nay: but we wil abide in the street all night.
text.NABRE: he said, “Please, my lords, come aside into your servant’s house for the night, and bathe your feet; you can get up early to continue your journey.” But they replied, “No, we will pass the night in the town square.”
text.DRB: and said: I beseech you, my Lords, turn into the house of your servant, and lodge there: Wash your feet, and in the morning you shall go forth on your way. Who said: No, but we will abide in the street.

text.NRSV: But he urged them strongly; so they turned aside to him and entered his house; and he made them a feast, and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.
text.Rotherham: But he became exceeding urgent with them, so they turned aside unto him and entered into his house,—and he made for them a banquet, unleavened cakes, also did he bake, and they did eat.
text.KJV: And he pressed vpon them greatly, and they turned in vnto him, and entred into his house: and he made them a feast, and did bake vnleauened bread, and they did eate.
text.NABRE: He urged them so strongly, however, that they turned aside to his place and entered his house. He prepared a banquet for them, baking unleavened bread, and they dined.
text.DRB: He compelled them earnestly to turn in unto him: and when they were entered into his house, he made them a banquet, and baked unleavened bread, and they did eat.

text.NRSV: But before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, all the people to the last man, surrounded the house;
text.Rotherham: Ere yet they lay down, the men of the city—the men of Sodom, had come round against the house, from youth even unto age,—all the people from every quarter,
text.KJV: ¶ But before they lay downe, the men of the citie, euen the men of Sodom, compassed the house round, both old and yong, all the people from euery quarter.
text.NABRE: Before they went to bed, the townsmen of Sodom, both young and old—all the people to the last man—surrounded the house.
text.DRB: And before they went to bed, the men of the city beset the house from young to old, all the people together.

text.NRSV: and they called to Lot, “Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, so that we may know them.”
text.Rotherham: And they called unto Lot and said to him, Where are the men who have come in unto thee tonight? Bring them forth unto us, and let us know them!
text.KJV: And they called vnto Lot, and said vnto him, Where are the men which came in to thee this night? bring them out vnto vs, that we may know them.
text.NABRE: They called to Lot and said to him, “Where are the men who came to your house tonight? Bring them out to us that we may have sexual relations with them.”
text.DRB: And they called Lot, and said to him: Where are the men that came in to thee at night? Bring them forth hither that we may know them.

text.NRSV: Lot went out of the door to the men, shut the door after him,
text.Rotherham: And Lot went forth unto them to the entrance; but the door, closed he behind him.
text.KJV: And Lot went out at the doore vnto them, & shut the doore after him,
text.NABRE: Lot went out to meet them at the entrance. When he had shut the door behind him,
text.DRB: Lot going forth to them, and shutting the door after him, said:

text.NRSV: and said, “I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly.
text.Rotherham: And he said,—Do not I pray you my brethren act wickedly.
text.KJV: And said, I pray you, brethren, doe not so wickedly.
text.NABRE: he said, “I beg you, my brothers, do not do this wicked thing!
text.DRB: Do not so, I beseech you, my brethren, do not commit this evil.

text.NRSV: Look, I have two daughters who have not known a man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please; only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof.”
text.Rotherham: Behold, I pray you, I, have two daughters who have not known man, I must needs now bring, them, forth unto you, and do ye to them, as may be good in your eyes,—Only to these men, ye may do nothing; for on this account, have they come under the shade of my roof.
text.KJV: Behold now, I haue two daughters, which haue not knowen man; let mee, I pray you, bring them out vnto you, and doe ye to them, as is good in your eyes: onely vnto these men do nothing: for therefore came they vnder the shadow of my roofe.
text.NABRE: I have two daughters who have never had sexual relations with men. Let me bring them out to you, and you may do to them as you please. But do not do anything to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof.”
text.DRB: I have two daughters, which as yet have not known man: I will bring them forth to you, and abuse you them as it shall please you, so that you do no evil to these men, because they are entered under the shadow of my roof.

text.NRSV: But they replied, “Stand back!” And they said, “This fellow came here as an alien, and he would play the judge! Now we will deal worse with you than with them.” Then they pressed hard against the man Lot, and came near the door to break it down.
text.Rotherham: And they said, Stand back, And they said, This one, by himself, hath come in to sojourn and must always be acting the judge, Now, will we do more wickedly to thee, than to them. So they pressed sore upon the man—upon Lot—exceedingly, and drew near to break open the door.
text.KJV: And they said, Stand backe. And they said againe, This one fellow came in to soiourne, and he will needs bee a Iudge: Now wil we deale worse with thee, then with them. And they pressed sore vpon the man, euen Lot, and came neere to breake the doore.
text.NABRE: They replied, “Stand back! This man,” they said, “came here as a resident alien, and now he dares to give orders! We will treat you worse than them!” With that, they pressed hard against Lot, moving in closer to break down the door.
text.DRB: But they said: Get thee back thither. And again: Thou camest in, said they, as a stranger, what to be a Judge? Thyself therefore we will afflict more than these. And they did violence to Lot exceedingly: and it was even now at the point that they would break the doors.

text.NRSV: But the men inside reached out their hands and brought Lot into the house with them, and shut the door.
text.Rotherham: And the men thrust forth their hand, and brought Lot in unto them into the house—and, the door, they closed:
text.KJV: But the men put forth their hand, and pulled Lot into the house to them, and shut to the doore.
text.NABRE: But his guests put out their hands, pulled Lot inside with them, and closed the door;
text.DRB: And behold the men put forth their hand, and drew in Lot unto them, and shut the door:

text.NRSV: And they struck with blindness the men who were at the door of the house, both small and great, so that they were unable to find the door.
text.Rotherham: the men also who were at the entrance of the house, smote they with blindness, from small even unto great, so that they wearied themselves to find the entrance.
text.KJV: And they smote the men that were at the doore of the house, with blindnes, both small and great: so that they wearied themselues to finde the doore.
text.NABRE: they struck the men at the entrance of the house, small and great, with such a blinding light that they were utterly unable to find the doorway.
text.DRB: and them that were without they struck with blindness from the least to the greatest, so that they could not find the door.

text.NRSV: Then the men said to Lot, “Have you anyone else here? Sons-in-law, sons, daughters, or anyone you have in the city—bring them out of the place.
text.Rotherham: And the men said unto Lot, Whom besides hast thou here? Son-in-law and thy sons and thy daughters, and all that thou hast in the city, bring thou forth out of the place;
text.KJV: ¶ And the men said vnto Lot, Hast thou here any besides? sonne in law, and thy sonnes, and thy daughters, and whatsoeuer thou hast in the citie, bring them out of this place.
text.NABRE: Then the guests said to Lot: “Who else belongs to you here? Sons-in-law, your sons, your daughters, all who belong to you in the city—take them away from this place!
text.DRB: And they said to Lot: Hast thou here any of thine? Son in law, or sons, or daughters, all that are thine, bring them out of this city:

text.NRSV: For we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before the Lord, and the Lord has sent us to destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: For we are about to destroy this place,—for, great, is the outcry of them unto the face of Yahweh, so that Yahweh hath sent us to destroy it.
text.KJV: For we will destroy this place, because the crie of them is waxen great before the face of the LORD: and the LORD hath sent vs to destroy it.
text.NABRE: We are about to destroy this place, for the outcry reaching the Lord against those here is so great that the Lord has sent us to destroy it.”
text.DRB: for we will destroy this place, for that their cry is waxen loud before our Lord, who hath sent us to destroy them.

text.NRSV: So Lot went out and said to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters, “Up, get out of this place; for the Lord is about to destroy the city.” But he seemed to his sons-in-law to be jesting.
text.Rotherham: And Lot went forth and spake unto his sons-in-law who were about to take his two daughters and said Arise come forth out of this place, for Yahweh is about to destroy the city. And it came to pass, that he was as one that laughed, in the eyes of his sons-in-law.
text.KJV: And Lot went out, and spake vnto his sonnes in law, which married his daughters, and said, Up, get yee out of this place: for the LORD wil destroy this citie: but hee seemed as one that mocked, vnto his sonnes in law.
text.NABRE: So Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law, who had contracted marriage with his daughters. “Come on, leave this place,” he told them; “the Lord is about to destroy the city.” But his sons-in-law thought he was joking.
text.DRB: Therefore Lot went forth, and spake to his sons in law that were to take his daughters, and said: Arise, get you forth out of this place, because our Lord will destroy this city. And he seemed unto them to speak as it were in jest.

text.NRSV: When morning dawned, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Get up, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or else you will be consumed in the punishment of the city.”
text.Rotherham: But as soon as, the dawn had sprung up, the messengers hastened Lot saying,—Arise! take thy wife and thy two daughters that are at hand, lest thou be swept away in the punishment of the city.
text.KJV: ¶ And when the morning arose, then the Angels hastened Lot, saying, Arise, take thy wife, & thy two daughters, which are here, lest thou be consumed in the iniquitie of the citie.
text.NABRE: As dawn was breaking, the angels urged Lot on, saying, “Come on! Take your wife with you and your two daughters who are here, or you will be swept away in the punishment of the city.”
text.DRB: And when it was morning, the Angels urged him, saying: Arise, take thy wife, and the two daughters which thou hast: lest thou also perish withal in the wickedness of the city.

text.NRSV: But he lingered; so the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the Lord being merciful to him, and they brought him out and left him outside the city.
text.Rotherham: And he lingered—so the men laid hold of his hand and of the hand of his wife and of the hand of his two daughters, in the tenderness of Yahweh towards him,—and they brought him out and led him forth outside the city.
text.KJV: And while he lingred, the men laid hold vpon his hand, and vpon the hand of his wife, and vpon the hand of his two daughters, the LORD being mercifull vnto him: and they brought him forth, and set him without the citie.
text.NABRE: When he hesitated, the men, because of the Lord’s compassion for him, seized his hand and the hands of his wife and his two daughters and led them to safety outside the city.
text.DRB: He lingering, they took his hand, and the hand of his wife, and of his two daughters, because our Lord spared him.

text.NRSV: When they had brought them outside, they said, “Flee for your life; do not look back or stop anywhere in the Plain; flee to the hills, or else you will be consumed.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when they had brought them forth outside, that they said, Escape for thy life, Look not behind thee, neither stand still in all the vale,—To the mountain, escape thou lest thou be swept away.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, when they had brought them forth abroad, that he said, Escape for thy life, looke not behind thee, neither stay thou in all the plaine: escape to the mountaine, lest thou bee consumed.
text.NABRE: As soon as they had brought them outside, they said: “Flee for your life! Do not look back or stop anywhere on the Plain. Flee to the hills at once, or you will be swept away.”
text.DRB: And they led him forth, and set him without the city; and there they spake to him, saying: Save thy life; look not back, neither stay thou in all the country about: but save thyself in the mountain, lest thou also perish withal.

text.NRSV: And Lot said to them, “Oh, no, my lords;
text.Rotherham: And Lot said unto them,—No, I pray thee, My Lord!
text.KJV: And Lot said vnto them, Oh not so, my Lord.
text.NABRE: “Oh, no, my lords!” Lot replied to them.
text.DRB: And Lot said to them: I beseech thee my Lord,

text.NRSV: your servant has found favor with you, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life; but I cannot flee to the hills, for fear the disaster will overtake me and I die.
text.Rotherham: Behold, I pray thee thy servant hath found favour in thine eyes, so that thou hast magnified thy lovingkindness which thou hast performed with me in keeping alive my soul.—But, I, cannot escape to the mountain, lest calamity overtake me so shall I die.
text.KJV: Beholde now, thy seruant hath found grace in thy sight, and thou hast magnified thy mercy, which thou hast shewed vnto me, in sauing my life, and I cannot escape to the mountaine, lest some euill take me, and I die.
text.NABRE: “You have already shown favor to your servant, doing me the great kindness of saving my life. But I cannot flee to the hills, or the disaster will overtake and kill me.
text.DRB: because thy servant hath found grace before thee, and thou hast magnified thy mercy, which thou hast wrought with me, in that thou wouldest save my life, and safe I cannot be in the mountain, lest perhaps the evil catch me, and I die.

text.NRSV: Look, that city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one. Let me escape there—is it not a little one?—and my life will be saved!”
text.Rotherham: Behold, I pray thee, this city, is near for fleeing thither And, it, is a little one, let me I pray thee, escape thither—is it not a, little, one? That my soul may live!
text.KJV: Behold now, this citie is neere to flee vnto, and it is a litle one: Oh let me escape thither, (is it not a litle one?) and my soule shall liue.
text.NABRE: Look, this town ahead is near enough to escape to. It is only a small place. Let me flee there—is it not a small place?—to save my life.”
text.DRB: There is this city here-by at hand, whereunto I may fly, a little one, and I shall be safe in it: is it not a little one, and my life shall be saved?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Very well, I grant you this favor too, and will not overthrow the city of which you have spoken.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, Behold! I have lifted up thy countenance, even as to this thing,—so that I will not overthrow the city, of which thou hast spoken.
