text.KJV: And he said vnto him, See, I haue accepted thee concerning this thing, that I will not ouerthrow this citie, for the which thou hast spoken.
text.NABRE: “Well, then,” he replied, “I grant you this favor too. I will not overthrow the town you have mentioned.
text.DRB: And he said to him: Behold also in this point I have heard thy prayers, not to overthrow the city for which thou hast spoken.

text.NRSV: Hurry, escape there, for I can do nothing until you arrive there.” Therefore the city was called Zoar.
text.Rotherham: Haste thee escape thither, for I cannot do anything, until thou have come in thither. For this cause: was the name of the city called Zoar.
text.KJV: Haste thee, escape thither: for I cannot doe any thing till thou bee come thither: therefore the name of the citie was called Zoar.
text.NABRE: Hurry, escape there! I cannot do anything until you arrive there.” That is why the town is called Zoar.
text.DRB: Make haste, and be saved there: because I cannot do any thing till thou enter in thither. Therefore the name of that city was called Segor.

text.NRSV: The sun had risen on the earth when Lot came to Zoar.
text.Rotherham: When, the sun, had come forth on the earth, Lot had entered into Zoar.
text.KJV: ¶ The sunne was risen vpon the earth, when Lot entred into Zoar.
text.NABRE: The sun had risen over the earth when Lot arrived in Zoar,
text.DRB: The sun was risen upon the earth, and Lot entered into Segor.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord rained on Sodom and Gomorrah sulfur and fire from the Lord out of heaven;
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, rained, upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah, brimstone and fire,—from Yahweh, out of the heavens:
text.KJV: Then the LORD rained vpon Sodome & vpon Gomorrah, brimstone and fire, from the LORD out of heauen.
text.NABRE: and the Lord rained down sulfur upon Sodom and Gomorrah, fire from the Lord out of heaven.
text.DRB: Therefore our Lord rained upon Sodome and Gomorre brimstone & fire from our Lord out of heaven:

text.NRSV: and he overthrew those cities, and all the Plain, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.
text.Rotherham: so he overthrew these cities, and all the circuit,—and all the inhabitants of the cities, and the produce of the ground.
text.KJV: And he ouerthrew those cities, and all the plaine, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and that which grew vpon the ground.
text.NABRE: He overthrew those cities and the whole Plain, together with the inhabitants of the cities and the produce of the soil.
text.DRB: and he subverted these cities, and all the country about, all the inhabitants of the cities, and all things that spring of the earth.

text.NRSV: But Lot’s wife, behind him, looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
text.Rotherham: But his wife looked from behind him,—and became—a pillar of salt.
text.KJV: ¶ But his wife looked backe from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.
text.NABRE: But Lot’s wife looked back, and she was turned into a pillar of salt.
text.DRB: And his wife looking behind her, was turned into a statua of salt.

text.NRSV: Abraham went early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And Abraham gat up early in the morning, unto the place where he had stood before Yahweh;
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham gate vp earely in the morning, to the place, where hee stood before the LORD.
text.NABRE: The next morning Abraham hurried to the place where he had stood before the Lord.
text.DRB: And Abraham getting up early in the morning, there where before he had stood with our Lord,

text.NRSV: and he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and toward all the land of the Plain and saw the smoke of the land going up like the smoke of a furnace.
text.Rotherham: and he looked out over the face of Sodom and Gomorrah, and over all the face of the land of the circuit, and beheld and lo! the smoke of the land went up, like the smoke of a furnace.
text.KJV: And he looked toward Sodome and Gomorrah, & toward all the land of the plaine, and beheld, and loe, the smoke of the countrey went vp as the smoke of a furnace.
text.NABRE: As he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and the whole region of the Plain, he saw smoke over the land rising like the smoke from a kiln.
text.DRB: beheld Sodome and Gomorre, and the whole land of that country: and he saw the cinders rise up from the earth as it were the smoke of a furnace.

text.NRSV: So it was that, when God destroyed the cities of the Plain, God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities in which Lot had settled.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when God destroyed the cities of the circuit, that God remembered Abraham, and sent forth Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities wherein Lot had dwelt.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, when God destroyed the cities of the plaine, that God remembred Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the ouerthrow, when he ouerthrew the cities, in the which Lot dwelt.
text.NABRE: When God destroyed the cities of the Plain, he remembered Abraham and sent Lot away from the upheaval that occurred when God overthrew the cities where Lot had been living. Moabites and Ammonites.
text.DRB: For when God subverted the cities of that country, he remembering Abraham, delivered Lot out of the subversion of the cities wherein he had dwelt.

text.NRSV: Now Lot went up out of Zoar and settled in the hills with his two daughters, for he was afraid to stay in Zoar; so he lived in a cave with his two daughters.
text.Rotherham: And Lot went up out of Zoar, and dwelt in the mountain and his two daughters with him. for he feared to dwell in Zoar, so he dwelt in a cave, he, and his two daughters.
text.KJV: ¶ And Lot went vp out of Zoar, and dwelt in the mountaine, and his two daughters with him: for hee feared to dwell in Zoar, and he dwelt in a caue, he and his two daughters.
text.NABRE: Since Lot was afraid to stay in Zoar, he and his two daughters went up from Zoar and settled in the hill country, where he lived with his two daughters in a cave.
text.DRB: And Lot ascended out of Segor, and abode in the mountain, his two daughters also with him (for he was afraid to abide in Segor) and he abode in a cave himself, and his two daughters with him.

text.NRSV: And the firstborn said to the younger, “Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us after the manner of all the world.
text.Rotherham: And the firstborn said unto the younger. Our father is old,—and, a man, there is not in the earth to come in unto us, after the way of all the earth.
text.KJV: And the first borne saide vnto the yonger, Our father is old, and there is not a man in the earth, to come in vnto vs, after the maner of all the earth.
text.NABRE: The firstborn said to the younger: “Our father is getting old, and there is not a man in the land to have intercourse with us as is the custom everywhere.
text.DRB: And the elder said to the younger: Our father is old, and there is no man left on the earth, that may company with us after the manner of the whole earth.

text.NRSV: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, so that we may preserve offspring through our father.”
text.Rotherham: Come on! let us cause our father to drink wine and let us lie with him, that we may keep alive from our father, a seed.
text.KJV: Come, let vs make our father drinke wine, and we will lye with him, that we may preserue seed of our father.
text.NABRE: Come, let us ply our father with wine and then lie with him, that we may ensure posterity by our father.”
text.DRB: Come, let us make him drunk with wine, and let us lie with him, that we may preserve seed of our father.

text.NRSV: So they made their father drink wine that night; and the firstborn went in, and lay with her father; he did not know when she lay down or when she rose.
text.Rotherham: So they caused their father to drink wine that night,—and the firstborn went in. and lay with her father, but he noticed not her lying down nor her rising up.
text.KJV: And they made their father drinke wine that night, & the first borne went in, and lay with her father: and he perceiued not, when shee lay downe, nor when she arose.
text.NABRE: So that night they plied their father with wine, and the firstborn went in and lay with her father; but he was not aware of her lying down or getting up.
text.DRB: They therefore made their father to drink wine that night: and the elder went in, and lay with her father: but he perceived not, neither when his daughter lay down, nor when she rose up.

text.NRSV: On the next day, the firstborn said to the younger, “Look, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine tonight also; then you go in and lie with him, so that we may preserve offspring through our father.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the firstborn said unto the younger, Lo! I lay, last night with my father, let us cause him to drink wine this night also, and go thou in—lie with him, that we may keep alive from our father, a seed.
text.KJV: And it came to passe on the morrow, that the first borne said vnto the yonger, Behold, I lay yesternight with my father: let vs make him drinke wine this night also, and goe thou in, and lye with him, that we may preserue seed of our father.
text.NABRE: The next day the firstborn said to the younger: “Last night I lay with my father. Let us ply him with wine again tonight, and then you go in and lie with him, that we may ensure posterity by our father.”
text.DRB: The next day also the elder said to the younger: Behold I lay yesternight with my father, let us make him drink wine also this night, and thou shalt lie with him, that we may save seed of our father.

text.NRSV: So they made their father drink wine that night also; and the younger rose, and lay with him; and he did not know when she lay down or when she rose.
text.Rotherham: So they caused their father, on that night also to drink wine, and the younger arose, and lay with him, but he noticed not her lying down, nor her rising up.
text.KJV: And they made their father drinke wine that night also, and the yonger arose, and lay with him: and he perceiued not, when she lay downe, nor when she arose.
text.NABRE: So that night, too, they plied their father with wine, and then the younger one went in and lay with him; but he was not aware of her lying down or getting up.
text.DRB: They made their father drink wine that night also, and the younger daughter went in, and lay with him: and neither then truly did he perceive when she lay down, or when she rose up.

text.NRSV: Thus both the daughters of Lot became pregnant by their father.
text.Rotherham: Thus did, the two daughters of Lot conceive from their father.
text.KJV: Thus were both the daughters of Lot with childe by their father.
text.NABRE: Thus the two daughters of Lot became pregnant by their father.
text.DRB: The two daughters therefore of Lot were with child by their father.

text.NRSV: The firstborn bore a son, and named him Moab; he is the ancestor of the Moabites to this day.
text.Rotherham: And the firstborn bare a son, and called his name Moab,—the same, is the father of Moab, unto this day.
text.KJV: And the first borne bare a sonne, and called his name Moab: the same is the father of the Moabites vnto this day.
text.NABRE: The firstborn gave birth to a son whom she named Moab, saying, “From my father.” He is the ancestor of the Moabites of today.
text.DRB: And the elder bare a son and she called his name Moab: he is the father of the Moabites even to this present day.

text.NRSV: The younger also bore a son and named him Ben-ammi; he is the ancestor of the Ammonites to this day.
text.Rotherham: And the younger she also bare a son, and called his name Ben-ammi,—the same, is the father of the sons of Ammon—unto this day.
text.KJV: And the yonger, she also bare a sonne, and called his name, Ben-ammi: the same is the father of the children of Ammon, vnto this day.
text.NABRE: The younger one, too, gave birth to a son, and she named him Ammon, saying, “The son of my kin.” He is the ancestor of the Ammonites of today.
text.DRB: The younger also bare a son, and she called his name Ammon, that is the son of my people: he is the father of the Ammonites even to this day.

text.NRSV: From there Abraham journeyed toward the region of the Negeb, and settled between Kadesh and Shur. While residing in Gerar as an alien,
text.Rotherham: And Abraham brake up from thence, towards the land of the South, and fixed his dwelling between Kadesh and Shur, so he sojourned in Gerar.
text.KJV: And Abraham iourneyed from thence, toward the South-Countrey, and dwelled betweene Cadesh and Shur, and soiourned in Gerar.
text.NABRE: From there Abraham journeyed on to the region of the Negeb, where he settled between Kadesh and Shur. While he resided in Gerar as an alien,
text.DRB: Abraham removed from thence into the south country and dwelt between Cades, and Sur: and he lived as a pilgrim in Gerara.

text.NRSV: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, “She is my sister.” And King Abimelech of Gerar sent and took Sarah.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife: My, sister, is she,—So Abimelech king of Gerar sent, and took Sarah.
text.KJV: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, She is my sister: And Abimelech King of Gerar sent, and tooke Sarah.
text.NABRE: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, “She is my sister.” So Abimelech, king of Gerar, sent and took Sarah.
text.DRB: And he said of Sara his wife: She is my sister. Abimelech therefore, the king of Gerara, sent and took her.

text.NRSV: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, “You are about to die because of the woman whom you have taken; for she is a married woman.”
text.Rotherham: Then God went in unto Abimelech, in a dream of the night,—and said to him, Behold thee dead! because of the woman whom thou hast taken, seeing that, she, is a married woman.
text.KJV: But God came to Abimelech in a dreame by night, and said to him, Behold, thou art but a dead man, for the woman which thou hast taken: for shee is a mans wife.
text.NABRE: But God came to Abimelech in a dream one night and said to him: You are about to die because of the woman you have taken, for she has a husband.
text.DRB: And God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him: Lo thou shalt die for the woman that thou hast taken: for she hath a husband.

text.NRSV: Now Abimelech had not approached her; so he said, “Lord, will you destroy an innocent people?
text.Rotherham: Now, Abimelech, had not come near unto her,—so he said, O My Lord! a nation even a righteous one, wilt thou slay?
text.KJV: But Abimelech had not come neere her: and he said, LORD, wilt thou slay also a righteous nation?
text.NABRE: Abimelech, who had not approached her, said: “O Lord, would you kill an innocent man?
text.DRB: But Abimelech had not touched her, and he said: Lord wilt thou kill a Nation that is ignorant & just?

text.NRSV: Did he not himself say to me, ‘She is my sister’? And she herself said, ‘He is my brother.’ I did this in the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands.”
text.Rotherham: Had not, he himself, said to me, My sister, is she? and even she herself, said, My brother, is he? In the integrity of my heart and in the pureness of my hand, have I done this!
text.KJV: Said he not vnto me, She is my sister? and she, euen she herselfe said, Hee is my brother: in the integritie of my heart, and innocencie of my hands haue I done this.
text.NABRE: Was he not the one who told me, ‘She is my sister’? She herself also stated, ‘He is my brother.’ I acted with pure heart and with clean hands.”
text.DRB: Did not he say to me: She is my sister? And she say, He is my brother? In simplicity of my heart and cleanness of my hands have I done this.

text.NRSV: Then God said to him in the dream, “Yes, I know that you did this in the integrity of your heart; furthermore it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto him in a dream, I, also, knew, that in the integrity of thy heart, thou didst this, so then, even I myself, withheld thee from sinning against me, for this reason, have I not suffered thee to touch her.
text.KJV: And God saide vnto him in a dreame, Yea, I know that thou didst this in the integritie of thy heart: for I also withheld thee from sinning against mee, therefore suffered I thee not to touch her.
text.NABRE: God answered him in the dream: Yes, I know you did it with a pure heart. In fact, it was I who kept you from sinning against me; that is why I did not let you touch her.
text.DRB: And God said to him: And I do know that thou didst it with a sincere heart: and therefore I kept thee that thou shouldest not sin against me, and I permitted not that thou shouldest touch her.

text.NRSV: Now then, return the man’s wife; for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you and you shall live. But if you do not restore her, know that you shall surely die, you and all that are yours.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore restore the man’s wife, for a prophet, is he, that he may pray for thee and live thou,—But if thou art not going to restore her, know, that thou shalt die, thou—and all that are thine.
text.KJV: Now therefore restore the man his wife: for he is a Prophet, and he shal pray for thee, and thou shalt liue: and if thou restore her not, know thou that thou shalt surely die, thou, and all that are thine.
text.NABRE: So now, return the man’s wife so that he may intercede for you, since he is a prophet, that you may live. If you do not return her, you can be sure that you and all who are yours will die.
text.DRB: Now therefore restore the wife to her husband, because he is a Prophet: and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live. But if thou wilt not restore her, know thou that dying thou shalt die, thou and all things that are thine.

text.NRSV: So Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants and told them all these things; and the men were very much afraid.
text.Rotherham: So Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and spake all these words in their ears, and the men feared greatly.
text.KJV: Therefore Abimelech rose earely in the morning, and called all his seruants, and told all these things in their eares: and the men were sore afraid.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning Abimelech called all his servants and informed them of everything that had happened, and the men were filled with fear.
text.DRB: And Abimelech forthwith rising up in the night, called all his servants: and he spake all these words to their ears, and all the men were sore afraid.

text.NRSV: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said to him, “What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought such great guilt on me and my kingdom? You have done things to me that ought not to be done.”
text.Rotherham: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said to him, What hast thou done to us? and wherein had I sinned against thee, that thou shouldst have brought in over me and over my kingdom, a sin so great? Deeds, which should not be done, hast thou done with me.
text.KJV: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said vnto him, What hast thou done vnto vs? and what haue I offended thee, that thou hast brought on me, and on my kingdome a great sinne? thou hast done deeds vnto mee that ought not to be done.
text.NABRE: Then Abimelech summoned Abraham and said to him: “What have you done to us! What wrong did I do to you that you would have brought such great guilt on me and my kingdom? You have treated me in an intolerable way.
text.DRB: And Abimelech called also for Abraham, & said to him: What hast thou done to us? What have we offended against thee, that thou hast brought upon me and upon my kingdom a great sin? That which thou oughtest not to do, thou hast done to us.

text.NRSV: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What were you thinking of, that you did this thing?”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said unto Abraham,—What hadst thou seen, that thou shouldst have done this thing?
text.KJV: And Abimelech said vnto Abraham, What sawest thou, that thou hast done this thing?
text.NABRE: What did you have in mind,” Abimelech asked him, “that you would do such a thing?”
text.DRB: And again expostulating, he said: What sawest thou, that thou didest this?

text.NRSV: Abraham said, “I did it because I thought, There is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said, Because I thought, Surely there is no fear of God, in this place,—therefore will they slay me for the sake of my wife.
text.KJV: And Abraham said, Because I thought, Surely the feare of God is not in this place: and they will slay mee for my wiues sake.
text.NABRE: Abraham answered, “I thought there would be no fear of God in this place, and so they would kill me on account of my wife.
text.DRB: Abraham answered: I thought with myself, saying: Perhaps there is not the fear of God in this place, & they will kill me for my wife:

text.NRSV: Besides, she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, in truth, my sister daughter of my father, she is, only not daughter of my mother,—so she became my wife.
text.KJV: And yet indeed shee is my sister: she is the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother; and shee became my wife.
text.NABRE: Besides, she really is my sister, but only my father’s daughter, not my mother’s; and so she became my wife.
text.DRB: howbeit otherwise also in very deed she is my sister, the daughter of my father, & not the daughter of my mother, & I took her to wife.

text.NRSV: And when God caused me to wander from my father’s house, I said to her, ‘This is the kindness you must do me: at every place to which we come, say of me, He is my brother.’ ”
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass when the Gods caused me to wander from my father’s house, that I said to her, This, is thy lovingkindness, wherewith thou shalt deal with me,—Into whatsoever place we enter, say of me My brother, is he.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when God caused me to wander from my fathers house, that I said vnto her, This is thy kindnesse which thou shalt shew vnto me; at euery place whither wee shall come, say of me, He is my brother.
text.NABRE: When God sent me wandering from my father’s house, I asked her: ‘Would you do me this favor? In whatever place we come to, say: He is my brother.’”
text.DRB: And after that God brought me out of my father's house, I said to her: This mercy thou shalt do with me: In every place which we shall come unto, thou shalt say that I am thy brother.

text.NRSV: Then Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and male and female slaves, and gave them to Abraham, and restored his wife Sarah to him.
text.Rotherham: Then took Abimelech sheep and oxen and men—servants and maid-servants, and gave to Abraham,—and restored to him Sarah his wife.
text.KJV: And Abimelech tooke sheepe and oxen, and men-seruants, and women seruants, and gaue them vnto Abraham, and restored him Sarah his wife.
text.NABRE: Then Abimelech took flocks and herds and male and female slaves and gave them to Abraham; and he restored his wife Sarah to him.
text.DRB: Therefore Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and servants, and handmaids, and gave to Abraham: and restored to him Sara his wife,

text.NRSV: Abimelech said, “My land is before you; settle where it pleases you.”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech, said, Lo! my land is before thee,—wherever it may seem good in thine eyes, dwell.
text.KJV: And Abimelech said, Behold, my land is before thee; dwel where it pleaseth thee.
text.NABRE: Then Abimelech said, “Here, my land is at your disposal; settle wherever you please.”
text.DRB: and said: The land is before you, dwell wheresoever it shalt please thee.

text.NRSV: To Sarah he said, “Look, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; it is your exoneration before all who are with you; you are completely vindicated.”
text.Rotherham: And to Sarah, he said, Lo! I have given a thousand of silver unto thy brother: Lo! that is for thee as a covering of eyes, to all who are with thee,—And so in every way, hath right been done.
text.KJV: And vnto Sarah hee said, Behold, I haue giuen thy brother a thousand pieces of siluer: behold, he is to thee a couering of the eyes, vnto all that are with thee, and with all other: thus shee was reproued.
text.NABRE: To Sarah he said: “I hereby give your brother a thousand shekels of silver. This will preserve your honor before all who are with you and will exonerate you before everyone.”
text.DRB: And to Sara he said: Behold I have given thy brother a thousand pieces of silver, this shall serve thee for a veil of thine eyes to all that are with thee, and whithersoever thou shalt go, remember also thou wast taken.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham prayed to God; and God healed Abimelech, and also healed his wife and female slaves so that they bore children.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham prayed unto God,—and God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maid-servants, so that they bare children,
text.KJV: ¶ So Abraham prayed vnto God: and God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maid-seruants, and they bare children.
text.NABRE: Abraham then interceded with God, and God restored health to Abimelech, to his wife, and his maidservants, so that they bore children;
text.DRB: And Abraham praying, God healed Abimelech and his wife, and his handmaids, and they bare children:

text.NRSV: For the Lord had closed fast all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham’s wife.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh, had restrained from bearing, every female of the house of Abimelech,—because of Sarah, wife of Abraham.
text.KJV: For the LORD had fast closed vp all the wombes of the house of Abimelech, because of Sarah Abrahams wife.
text.NABRE: for the Lord had closed every womb in Abimelech’s household on account of Abraham’s wife Sarah.
text.DRB: for our Lord had closed up every matrice of the house of Abimelech for Sara Abraham's wife.

text.NRSV: The Lord dealt with Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did for Sarah as he had promised.
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, visited Sarah, as he had said,—And Yahweh did for Sarah as he had spoken.
text.KJV: And the LORD visited Sarah as he had said, and the LORD did vnto Sarah as he had spoken.
text.NABRE: The Lord took note of Sarah as he had said he would; the Lord did for her as he had promised.
text.DRB: And our Lord visited Sara, as he had promised: and fulfilled the things which he spake.

text.NRSV: Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the time of which God had spoken to him.
text.Rotherham: So, Sarah, conceived and bare, to Abraham, a son, for his old age, at the set time, of which God had spoken with him.
text.KJV: For Sarah conceiued, and bare Abraham a sonne in his old age, at the set time, of which God had spoken to him.
text.NABRE: Sarah became pregnant and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the set time that God had stated.
text.DRB: And she conceived & bare a son in her old age, at the time that God had foretold her.

text.NRSV: Abraham gave the name Isaac to his son whom Sarah bore him.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham called the name of his son that was born to him, whom, Sarah, bare to him, Isaac.
text.KJV: And Abraham called the name of his sonne, that was borne vnto him, whom Sarah bare to him, Isaac.
text.NABRE: Abraham gave the name Isaac to this son of his whom Sarah bore him.
text.DRB: And Abraham called the name of his son, which Sara bare him, Isaac:

text.NRSV: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham circumcised Isaac his son, when eight days old,—as God commanded him.
text.KJV: And Abraham circumcised his sonne Isaac, being eight dayes old, as God had commanded him.
text.NABRE: When his son Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, as God had commanded.
text.DRB: and he circumcised him the eighth day, as God had commanded him,

text.NRSV: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
text.Rotherham: Now Abraham was a hundred years old,—when Isaac his son was born to him.
text.KJV: And Abraham was an hundred yeeres old, when his sonne Isaac was borne vnto him.
text.NABRE: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
text.DRB: when he was an hundred years old: for at this age of his father, was Isaac born.

text.NRSV: Now Sarah said, “God has brought laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh with me.”
text.Rotherham: And Sarah said, A laughing, hath God made for me,—Everyone that heareth will laugh with me.
text.KJV: ¶ And Sarah said, God hath made me to laugh, so that all that heare, will laugh with me.
text.NABRE: Sarah then said, “God has given me cause to laugh, and all who hear of it will laugh with me.
text.DRB: And Sara said: Laughter God hath made to me: whosoever shall hear of it, will laugh with me.

text.NRSV: And she said, “Who would ever have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—Who would have announced to Abraham, Sarah hath suckled children? yet have I borne a son, for his old age!
text.KJV: And she said, Who would haue said vnto Abraham, that Sarah should haue giuen children sucke? for I haue borne him a sonne in his old age.
text.NABRE: Who would ever have told Abraham,” she added, “that Sarah would nurse children! Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
text.DRB: And again she said: Who that shall hear of it would believe Abraham, that Sara gave suck to a son, which she bare him now being an old man?

text.NRSV: The child grew, and was weaned; and Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
text.Rotherham: And the child grew, and was weaned,—and Abraham made a great banquet, on the day of the weaning of Isaac.
text.KJV: And the child grew, and was weaned: and Abraham made a great feast, the same day that Isaac was weaned.
text.NABRE: The child grew and was weaned, and Abraham held a great banquet on the day of the child’s weaning.
text.DRB: The child therefore grew and was weaned: and Abraham made a great feast in the day of his weaning.

text.NRSV: But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, playing with her son Isaac.
text.Rotherham: And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian woman whom she had borne to Abraham laughing!
text.KJV: ¶ And Sarah saw the sonne of Hagar the Egyptian, which shee had borne vnto Abraham, mocking.
text.NABRE: Sarah noticed the son whom Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham playing with her son Isaac;
text.DRB: And when Sara had seen the son of Agar the Ægyptian playing with Isaac her son, she said to Abraham:

text.NRSV: So she said to Abraham, “Cast out this slave woman with her son; for the son of this slave woman shall not inherit along with my son Isaac.”
text.Rotherham: So she said to Abraham, Cast out this bondwoman and her son,—for the son of this bondwoman must not inherit with my son with Isaac.
text.KJV: Wherfore she said vnto Abraham, Cast out this bond woman, and her sonne: for the sonne of this bond woman shall not be heire with my sonne, euen with Isaac.
text.NABRE: so she demanded of Abraham: “Drive out that slave and her son! No son of that slave is going to share the inheritance with my son Isaac!”
text.DRB: Cast out this handmaid, & her son: for the son of the handmaid shall not be heir with my son Isaac.

text.NRSV: The matter was very distressing to Abraham on account of his son.
text.Rotherham: And the word was very grievous in the eyes of Abraham,—on account of his son.
text.KJV: And the thing was very grieuous in Abrahams sight, because of his sonne.
text.NABRE: Abraham was greatly distressed because it concerned a son of his.
text.DRB: Abraham took this grievously for his son.

text.NRSV: But God said to Abraham, “Do not be distressed because of the boy and because of your slave woman; whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for it is through Isaac that offspring shall be named for you.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Abraham Let it not be grievous in thine eyes concerning the boy and concerning thy bondwoman, In all that Sarah may say unto thee, hearken to her voice,—For in Isaac, shall there be called to thee—a seed.
text.KJV: ¶ And God said vnto Abraham, Let it not be grieuous in thy sight, because of the lad, and because of thy bond woman. In all that Sarah hath said vnto thee, hearken vnto her voice: for in Isaac shall thy seed be called.
text.NABRE: But God said to Abraham: Do not be distressed about the boy or about your slave woman. Obey Sarah, no matter what she asks of you; for it is through Isaac that descendants will bear your name.
text.DRB: To whom God said: Let it not seem grievous to thee for the boy, and for thy handmaid: all things that Sara shall say to thee, hear her voice: because in Isaac shall seed be called to thee.

text.NRSV: As for the son of the slave woman, I will make a nation of him also, because he is your offspring.”
text.Rotherham: Yet even the son of the bondwoman, will I appoint to become, a nation, because thy seed, he is.
text.KJV: And also, of the sonne of the bond woman will I make a nation, because he is thy seed.
text.NABRE: As for the son of the slave woman, I will make a nation of him also, since he too is your offspring.
text.DRB: But the son also of the handmaid I will make into a great nation, because he is thy seed.

text.NRSV: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and took bread and a skin of water, and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed, and wandered about in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham rose up early in the morning—and took bread and a skin of water and gave unto hagar,—putting them on her shoulder and the child, and sent her forth,—so she went her way and wandered, in the desert of Beer-sheba.
text.KJV: And Abraham rose vp earely in the morning, and tooke bread, and a bottle of water, and gaue it vnto Hagar, (putting it on her shoulder,) and the child, and sent her away: and shee departed, and wandered in the wildernesse of Beer-sheba.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning Abraham got some bread and a skin of water and gave them to Hagar. Then, placing the child on her back, he sent her away. As she roamed aimlessly in the wilderness of Beer-sheba,
text.DRB: Abraham therefore rose up in the morning, and taking bread and a bottle of water, put it upon her shoulder, and delivered the boy, and dismissed her. Who went away, and wandered in the wilderness of Bersabee.

text.NRSV: When the water in the skin was gone, she cast the child under one of the bushes.
text.Rotherham: And the water out of the skin was spent,—so she cast the child under one of the shrubs;
text.KJV: And the water was spent in the bottle, and shee cast the child vnder one of the shrubs.
text.NABRE: the water in the skin was used up. So she put the child down under one of the bushes,
text.DRB: And when the water in the bottle was spent, she cast the boy under one of the trees that were there.

text.NRSV: Then she went and sat down opposite him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot; for she said, “Do not let me look on the death of the child.” And as she sat opposite him, she lifted up her voice and wept.
text.Rotherham: and went and sat her down over against him at a distance like as of such as draw the bow, for she said Let me not look upon the death of the child,—So she sat down over against him, and the boy lifted up his voice, and wept
text.KJV: And she went, and sate her downe ouer against him, a good way off, as it were a bow shoot: for she said, Let me not see the death of the child. And shee sate ouer against him, and lift vp her voice, and wept.
text.NABRE: and then went and sat down opposite him, about a bowshot away; for she said to herself, “I cannot watch the child die.” As she sat opposite him, she wept aloud.
text.DRB: And she went her way, and sat over against a great way off as far as a bow can cast, for she said: I will no see the child dying. And sitting over against, she lifted up her voice and wept.

text.NRSV: And God heard the voice of the boy; and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven, and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Do not be afraid; for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.
text.Rotherham: And God heard the voice of the boy, and a messenger of God called unto Hagar out of the heavens; and said to her, What aileth thee, Hagar? Do not fear, for God hath hearkened unto the voice of the boy where he is.
text.KJV: And God heard the voice of the lad, and the Angel of God called to Hagar out of heauen, and said vnto her, What aileth thee, Hagar? feare not: for God hath heard the voice of the ladde, where he is.
text.NABRE: God heard the boy’s voice, and God’s angel called to Hagar from heaven: “What is the matter, Hagar? Do not fear; God has heard the boy’s voice in this plight of his.
text.DRB: And God heard the voice of the boy: and an Angel of God called Agar from heaven, saying: What doest thou Agar? Fear not; for God hath heard the voice of the boy, from the place wherein he is.

text.NRSV: Come, lift up the boy and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make a great nation of him.”
text.Rotherham: Arise lift up the boy, and hold him up with thy hand,—for, a great nation, will I make him.
text.KJV: Arise, lift vp the lad, and hold him in thine hand: for I will make him a great nation.
text.NABRE: Get up, lift up the boy and hold him by the hand; for I will make of him a great nation.”
text.DRB: Arise, take up the boy, & hold his hand; for into a great nation will I make him.

text.NRSV: Then God opened her eyes and she saw a well of water. She went, and filled the skin with water, and gave the boy a drink.
text.Rotherham: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water,—and went and filled the skin with water, and gave drink unto the boy
text.KJV: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water, and shee went, and filled the bottle with water, and gaue the lad drinke.
text.NABRE: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. She went and filled the skin with water, and then let the boy drink.
text.DRB: And God opened her eyes: who seeing a well of water, went, and filled the bottle, and gave the boy to drink.

text.NRSV: God was with the boy, and he grew up; he lived in the wilderness, and became an expert with the bow.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that God was with the boy, and he grew,—and dwelt in the desert, and he became as he grew up, an archer,
text.KJV: And God was with the lad, and he grew, and dwelt in the wildernesse, and became an archer.
text.NABRE: God was with the boy as he grew up. He lived in the wilderness and became an expert bowman.
text.DRB: And God was with him: who grew, and abode in the wilderness, and became a young man archer.

text.NRSV: He lived in the wilderness of Paran; and his mother got a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And he dwelt in the desert of Paran,—and his mother took him a wife out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And hee dwelt in the wildernesse of Paran: and his mother tooke him a wife out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: He lived in the wilderness of Paran. His mother got a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Pharan, and his mother took a wife for him out of the land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: At that time Abimelech, with Phicol the commander of his army, said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at that time, that Abimelech, with Phicol chieftain of his host, spake unto Abraham, saying,—God, is with thee in all that, thou art doing.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe at that time, that Abimelech and Phichol the chiefe captaine of his hoste spake vnto Abraham, saying, God is with thee in all that thou doest.
text.NABRE: The Covenant at Beer-sheba. At that time Abimelech, accompanied by Phicol, the commander of his army, said to Abraham: “God is with you in everything you do.
text.DRB: The same time said Abimelech, and Phicol the General of his army, to Abraham: God is with thee in all things which thou doest.

text.NRSV: now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my offspring or with my posterity, but as I have dealt loyally with you, you will deal with me and with the land where you have resided as an alien.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, swear to me by God, here, that thou wilt not deal falsely by me, nor by mine offspring, nor by my descendants,—according to the lovingkindness wherewith I have dealt with thee, shalt thou deal with me, and with the land wherein thou hast sojourned.
text.KJV: Now therefore sweare vnto mee here by God, that thou wilt not deale falsly with me, nor with my sonne, nor with my sonnes sonne: but according to the kindnesse that I haue done vnto thee, thou shalt doe vnto me, and to the land wherein thou hast soiourned.
text.NABRE: So now, swear to me by God at this place that you will not deal falsely with me or with my progeny and posterity, but will act as loyally toward me and the land in which you reside as I have acted toward you.”
text.DRB: Swear therefore by God, not to hurt me, and my posterity, and my stock: but according to the mercy, that I have done thee, thou shalt do to me, and to the land wherein thou hast lived a stranger.

text.NRSV: And Abraham said, “I swear it.”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said, I, will swear.
text.KJV: And Abraham saide, I will sweare.
text.NABRE: Abraham replied, “I so swear.”
text.DRB: And Abraham said: I will swear.

text.NRSV: When Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well of water that Abimelech’s servants had seized,
text.Rotherham: But Abraham reproved Abimelech,—on account of the well of water, which the servants of Abimelech had seized.
text.KJV: And Abraham reproued Abimelech, because of a well of water, which Abimelechs seruants had violently taken away.
text.NABRE: Abraham, however, reproached Abimelech about a well that Abimelech’s servants had seized by force.
text.DRB: And he rebuked Abimelech for the well of water, which his servants had taken away by force.

text.NRSV: Abimelech said, “I do not know who has done this; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said, I know not, who hath done this thing,—nor hast even thou, ever told me, nor have even I, ever heard, save to-day.
text.KJV: And Abimelech saide, I wote not who hath done this thing: neither didst thou tell me, neither yet heard I of it, but to day.
text.NABRE: “I have no idea who did that,” Abimelech replied. “In fact, you never told me about it, nor did I ever hear of it until now.”
text.DRB: And Abimelech answered: I knew not who did this thing: yea and thyself didst not tell me, and I heard not of it but today.

text.NRSV: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave to Abimelech,—and they two solemnised a covenant
text.KJV: And Abraham tooke sheepe and oxen, and gaue them vnto Abimelech: and both of them made a couenant.
text.NABRE: Then Abraham took sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelech and the two made a covenant.
text.DRB: Abraham therefore took sheep and oxen, and gave to Abimelech: and both of them made a league.

text.NRSV: Abraham set apart seven ewe lambs of the flock.
text.Rotherham: So then Abraham set seven young sheep of the flock by them-selves,
text.KJV: And Abraham set seuen ewe lambes of the flocke by themselues.
text.NABRE: Abraham also set apart seven ewe lambs of the flock,
text.DRB: And Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart.

text.NRSV: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What, then mean these seven young sheep here, which thou hast set by themselves?
text.KJV: And Abimelech said vnto Abraham, What meane these seuen ewe lambes, which thou hast set by themselues?
text.NABRE: and Abimelech asked him, “What is the purpose of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
text.DRB: To whom Abimelech said: What mean these seven ewe lambs, which thou hast made to stand apart?

text.NRSV: He said, “These seven ewe lambs you shall accept from my hand, in order that you may be a witness for me that I dug this well.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, The seven young sheep, shalt thou take at my hand,—that they may serve as my witness, that I digged this well.
text.KJV: And he said, For these seuen ewe lambs shalt thou take of my hand, that they may be a witnesse vnto me, that I haue digged this well.
text.NABRE: Abraham answered, “The seven ewe lambs you shall accept from me that you may be my witness that I dug this well.”
text.DRB: But he said: Seven ewe lambs shalt thou take at my hand: that they may be a testimony for me, that I digged this well.

text.NRSV: Therefore that place was called Beer-sheba; because there both of them swore an oath.
text.Rotherham: On this account, was the name of that place called Beer-sheba [="oath well"],—because there, had they two sworn.
text.KJV: Wherefore he called that place, Beer-sheba: because there they sware both of them.
text.NABRE: This is why the place is called Beer-sheba; the two of them took an oath there.
text.DRB: Therefore was that place called Bersabee: because there both did swear.

text.NRSV: When they had made a covenant at Beer-sheba, Abimelech, with Phicol the commander of his army, left and returned to the land of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And when they had solemnised a covenant in Beer-sheba,—then arose Abimelech, with Phicol chief of his host, and returned unto the land of the Philistines.
text.KJV: Thus they made a couenant at Beeer-sheba: then Abimelech rose vp, and Phichol the chiefe captaine of his hoste, and they returned into the land of the Philistines.
text.NABRE: When they had thus made the covenant in Beer-sheba, Abimelech, along with Phicol, the commander of his army, left to return to the land of the Philistines.
text.DRB: And they made a league for the well of oath.

text.NRSV: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the Lord, the Everlasting God.
text.Rotherham: Then planted he a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba,—and called there on the name of Yahweh the age-abiding GOD.
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham planted a groue in Beer-sheba, and called there on the Name of the LORD, the euerlasting God.
text.NABRE: Abraham planted a tamarisk at Beer-sheba, and there he invoked by name the Lord, God the Eternal.
text.DRB: And Abimelech arose, and Phicol the General of his army, and they returned to the land of the Palestines. But Abraham planted a grove in Bersabee, and called there upon the name of our Lord God eternal.

text.NRSV: And Abraham resided as an alien many days in the land of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.
text.KJV: And Abraham soiourned in the Philistines land, many dayes.
text.NABRE: Abraham resided in the land of the Philistines for a long time.
text.DRB: And he was a sojourner in the land of the Palestines many days.

text.NRSV: After these things God tested Abraham. He said to him, “Abraham!” And he said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that God, did prove Abraham,—and he said unto him, Abraham! And he said Behold me!
text.KJV: And it came to passe after these things, that God did tempt Abraham, and said vnto him, Abraham. And hee said, Beholde, heere I am.
text.NABRE: Some time afterward, God put Abraham to the test and said to him: Abraham! “Here I am!” he replied.
text.DRB: Which things being done, God tempted Abraham, and said to him: Abraham, Abraham. But he answered: Here I am.

text.NRSV: He said, “Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains that I shall show you.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Take, I pray thee, thy son, thine only one, whom thou lovest, even, Isaac, and get thee into the land of Moriah and cause him to ascend there as an ascending-sacrifice, on one of the mountains which I shall name unto thee.
text.KJV: And he said, Take now thy sonne, thine onely sonne Isaac, whom thou louest, and get thee into the land of Moriah: and offer him there for a burnt offering vpon one of the Mountaines which I will tell thee of.
text.NABRE: Then God said: Take your son Isaac, your only one, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah. There offer him up as a burnt offering on one of the heights that I will point out to you.
text.DRB: He said to him: Take thy only begotten son, whom thou lovest, Isaac, and go into the Land of vision: and there thou shalt offer him for an holocaust upon one of the mountains which I will shew thee.

text.NRSV: So Abraham rose early in the morning, saddled his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, and his son Isaac; he cut the wood for the burnt offering, and set out and went to the place in the distance that God had shown him.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and saddled his ass, and took his two young men with him, and Isaac his son, and clave the pieces of wood for an ascending-sacrifice, and mounted and went his way unto the place which God had named to him.
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham rose vp earely in the morning, and sadled his asse, and tooke two of his yong men with him, and Isaac his sonne, and claue the wood for the burnt offering, and rose vp, and went vnto the place of which God had told him.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning Abraham saddled his donkey, took with him two of his servants and his son Isaac, and after cutting the wood for the burnt offering, set out for the place of which God had told him.
text.DRB: Therefore Abraham rising up in the night, saddled his ass: taking with him two young men, and Isaac his son: and when he had cut wood for the holocaust, he went his way to the place which God had commanded him.

text.NRSV: On the third day Abraham looked up and saw the place far away.
text.Rotherham: It was, on the third day, that Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw the place afar off.
text.KJV: Then on the third day Abraham lift vp his eyes, and saw the place afarre off.
text.NABRE: On the third day Abraham caught sight of the place from a distance.
text.DRB: And the third day, lifting up his eyes, he saw the place a far off:

text.NRSV: Then Abraham said to his young men, “Stay here with the donkey; the boy and I will go over there; we will worship, and then we will come back to you.”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said unto his young men—Tarry by yourselves here with the ass, but I and the young man must go yonder,—that we may bow ourselves down and return unto you.
text.KJV: And Abraham said vnto his yong men, Abide you here with the asse, and I and the lad will goe yonder and worship, and come againe to you.
text.NABRE: Abraham said to his servants: “Stay here with the donkey, while the boy and I go on over there. We will worship and then come back to you.”
text.DRB: and he said to his young men: Tarry you here with the ass: I and the boy going with speed as far as yonder, after we have adored will return to you.

text.NRSV: Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering and laid it on his son Isaac, and he himself carried the fire and the knife. So the two of them walked on together.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham took the pieces of wood for the ascending-sacrifice, and laid them on Isaac his son, and took in his own hand the fire and the knife, and they went on their way, both of them together.
text.KJV: And Abraham tooke the wood of the burnt offering, and layd it vpon Isaac his sonne: and he tooke the fire in his hand, and a knife: and they went both of them together.
text.NABRE: So Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and laid it on his son Isaac, while he himself carried the fire and the knife. As the two walked on together,
text.DRB: He took also the wood of the holocaust, and laid it upon Isaac his son: and himself carried in his hand fire and a sword. And as they went on together,

text.NRSV: Isaac said to his father Abraham, “Father!” And he said, “Here I am, my son.” He said, “The fire and the wood are here, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaac unto Abraham his father, then said he; My father! And he said, Behold me, my son, And he said, Behold the fire, and the pieces of wood,—but where is the lamb, for an ascending-sacrifice?
text.KJV: And Isaac spake vnto Abraham his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I, my sonne. And hee said, Behold the fire and wood: but where is the lambe for a burnt offring?
text.NABRE: Isaac spoke to his father Abraham. “Father!” he said. “Here I am,” he replied. Isaac continued, “Here are the fire and the wood, but where is the sheep for the burnt offering?”
text.DRB: Isaac said to his father: My father. And he answered: What wilt thou son? Behold, saith he, fire & wood; where is the victim of the holocaust?

text.NRSV: Abraham said, “God himself will provide the lamb for a burnt offering, my son.” So the two of them walked on together.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said, God, will provide for himself the lamb for an ascending-sacrifice, my son! So they went on their way, both of them, together.
text.KJV: And Abraham said, My sonne, God will prouide himselfe a lambe for a burnt offering: so they went both of them together.
text.NABRE: “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the sheep for the burnt offering.” Then the two walked on together.
text.DRB: And Abraham said: God will provide unto himself the victim of the holocaust, my son. They went on therefore together:

text.NRSV: When they came to the place that God had shown him, Abraham built an altar there and laid the wood in order. He bound his son Isaac, and laid him on the altar, on top of the wood.
text.Rotherham: Then came they into the place which God had named to him, and Abraham built there the altar, and laid in order the pieces of wood, and bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar, above the pieces of wood.
text.KJV: And they came to the place which God had tolde him of, and Abraham built an Altar there, and layd the wood in order, and bound Isaac his sonne, and layde him on the Altar vpon the wood.
text.NABRE: When they came to the place of which God had told him, Abraham built an altar there and arranged the wood on it. Next he bound his son Isaac, and put him on top of the wood on the altar.
text.DRB: and they came to the place which God had shewed him, wherein he builded an altar, and laid the wood in order upon it: and when he had bound Isaac his son, he laid him on the altar upon the pile of wood.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to kill his son.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham put forth his hand, and took the knife,—to slay his son.
text.KJV: And Abraham stretched foorth his hand, and tooke the knife to slay his sonne.
text.NABRE: Then Abraham reached out and took the knife to slaughter his son.
text.DRB: And he stretched forth his hand, and caught the sword, for to sacrifice his son.

text.NRSV: But the angel of the Lord called to him from heaven, and said, “Abraham, Abraham!” And he said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: Then called out unto him the messenger of Yahweh out of the heavens, and said. Abraham, Abraham! And he said Behold me!
text.KJV: And the Angel of the LORD called vnto him out of heauen, and said, Abraham, Abraham. And he said, Here am I.
text.NABRE: But the angel of the Lord called to him from heaven, “Abraham, Abraham!” “Here I am,” he answered.
text.DRB: And behold an Angel of our Lord from heaven cried, saying: Abraham, Abraham. Who answered: Here I am.

text.NRSV: He said, “Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him; for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Do not put forth thy hand unto the young man, neither do to him—anything at all,—for, now, know I that one who reverest God, thou art, when thou hast not withheld thy son, thine only one, from me.
text.KJV: And he said, Lay not thine hand vpon the lad, neither do thou any thing vnto him: for now I know that thou fearest God, seeing thou hast not withhelde thy sonne, thine onely sonne from mee.
text.NABRE: “Do not lay your hand on the boy,” said the angel. “Do not do the least thing to him. For now I know that you fear God, since you did not withhold from me your son, your only one.”
text.DRB: And he said to him: Stretch not forth thy hand upon the boy, neither do thou any thing to him: now have I known that thou fearest God, and hast not spared thine only begotten son for my sake.

text.NRSV: And Abraham looked up and saw a ram, caught in a thicket by its horns. Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham lifted up his eyes and looked, and lo! a ram, behind, caught in a thicket by his horns, and Abraham went, and took the ram, and caused him to ascend as an ascending-sacrifice instead of his son.
text.KJV: And Abraham lifted vp his eyes, and looked, and beholde, behinde him a Ramme caught in a thicket by his hornes: And Abraham went and tooke the Ramme, and offered him vp for a burnt offering, in the stead of his sonne.
text.NABRE: Abraham looked up and saw a single ram caught by its horns in the thicket. So Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering in place of his son.
text.DRB: Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw behind his back a ram amongst the briers sticking fast by the horns, which he took & offered an holocaust instead of his son.

text.NRSV: So Abraham called that place “The Lord will provide”; as it is said to this day, “On the mount of the Lord it shall be provided.”
text.Rotherham: So Abraham called the name of that place Yahweh-yireh,—as to which it is still said to-day, In the mountain of Yahweh, will provision be made.
text.KJV: And Abraham called the name of that place Iehouah-ijreh, as it is said to this day, In the Mount of the LORD it shalbe seene.
text.NABRE: Abraham named that place Yahweh-yireh; hence people today say, “On the mountain the Lord will provide.”
text.DRB: And he called the name of that place, Our Lord seeth. Whereupon even to this day it is said, In the mountain our Lord will see.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord called to Abraham a second time from heaven,
text.Rotherham: Then called out the messenger of Yahweh unto Abraham,—a second time out of the heavens;
text.KJV: ¶ And the Angel of the LORD called vnto Abraham out of heauen the second time,
text.NABRE: A second time the angel of the Lord called to Abraham from heaven
text.DRB: And the Angel of our Lord called Abraham the second time from heaven, saying:

text.NRSV: and said, “By myself I have sworn, says the Lord: Because you have done this, and have not withheld your son, your only son,
text.Rotherham: and said, By myself, have I sworn is the oracle of Yahweh,—Because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only one,
text.KJV: And said, By my selfe haue I sworne, saith the LORD, for because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy sonne, thine onely sonne,
text.NABRE: and said: “I swear by my very self—oracle of the Lord—that because you acted as you did in not withholding from me your son, your only one,
text.DRB: By my own self have I sworn, saith the Lord: because thou hast done this thing, and hast not spared thine only begotten son for my sake;

text.NRSV: I will indeed bless you, and I will make your offspring as numerous as the stars of heaven and as the sand that is on the seashore. And your offspring shall possess the gate of their enemies,
text.Rotherham: That I will, richly bless, thee, and, abundantly multiply, thy seed, as the stars of the heavens, and as the sand which is on the lip of the sea,—that thy seed may take possession of the gate of his foes:
text.KJV: That in blessing I will blesse thee, and in multiplying, I will multiply thy seed as the starres of the heauen, and as the sand which is vpon the sea shore, and thy seed shall possesse the gate of his enemies.
text.NABRE: I will bless you and make your descendants as countless as the stars of the sky and the sands of the seashore; your descendants will take possession of the gates of their enemies,
text.DRB: I will bless thee, and I will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that is by the sea shore: thy seed shall possess the gates of his enemies,

text.NRSV: and by your offspring shall all the nations of the earth gain blessing for themselves, because you have obeyed my voice.”
text.Rotherham: So shall all the nations of the earth bless themselves in thy seed,—Because thou didst hearken unto my voice.
text.KJV: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because thou hast obeyed my voice.
text.NABRE: and in your descendants all the nations of the earth will find blessing, because you obeyed my command.”
text.DRB: and IN Thy Seed Shall be Blessed All The Nations of The Earth, because thou hast obeyed my voice.

text.NRSV: So Abraham returned to his young men, and they arose and went together to Beer-sheba; and Abraham lived at Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham returned unto his young men, and they mounted, and went on their way together unto Beer-sheba,—and Abraham dwelt in Beer-sheba.
text.KJV: So Abraham returned vnto his yong men, and they rose vp, and went together to Beer-sheba, and Abraham dwelt at Beer-sheba.
text.NABRE: Abraham then returned to his servants, and they set out together for Beer-sheba, where Abraham lived. Nahor’s Descendants.
text.DRB: Abraham returned to his young men, and they went to Bersabee together, and he dwelt there.

text.NRSV: Now after these things it was told Abraham, “Milcah also has borne children, to your brother Nahor:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying,—Behold! Milcah—she also, hath borne sons, to Nahor thy brother:
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying, Behold Milcah, shee hath also borne children vnto thy brother Nahor,
text.NABRE: Some time afterward, the news came to Abraham: “Milcah too has borne sons to your brother Nahor:
text.DRB: These things so being done, it was told Abraham that Melcha also had born children to Nachor his brother,

text.NRSV: Uz the firstborn, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram,
text.Rotherham: Uz his firstborn and Buz his brother,—and Kemuel, father of Aram—
text.KJV: Huz his first borne, and Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram,
text.NABRE: Uz, his firstborn, his brother Buz, Kemuel the father of Aram,
text.DRB: Hus the first-begotten, and Buz his brother, and Camuel the father of the Sirians,

text.NRSV: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”
text.Rotherham: and Chesed and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph,—and Bethuel;
text.KJV: And Chesed, and Hazo, and Pildash, and Iidlaph, and Bethuel.
text.NABRE: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”
text.DRB: and Cased, and Azau, Pheldas also and Iedlaph,

text.NRSV: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.
text.Rotherham: And, Bethuel, hath begotten Rebekah,—These eight, hath Milcah borne to Nahor brother of Abraham.
text.KJV: And Bethuel begate Rebekah: these eight Milcah did beare to Nahor, Abrahams brother.
text.NABRE: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.
text.DRB: and Bathuel, of whom was born Rebecca: these eight did Melcha bear to Nachor Abraham's brother.

text.NRSV: Moreover, his concubine, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And his concubine, whose name was Reumah, she also, hath borne Tebah and Gaham, and Tahash, and Maacah.
text.KJV: And his concubine whose name was Reumah, she bare also Tebah, and Gaham, and Thahash, and Maachah.
text.NABRE: His concubine, whose name was Reumah, also bore children: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text.DRB: And his concubine, named Roma, bare Tabee, and Gaham, and Tahas, and Maacha.

text.NRSV: Sarah lived one hundred twenty-seven years; this was the length of Sarah’s life.
text.Rotherham: And the life of Sarah came to be, a hundred and twenty-seven years,—the years of the life of Sarah.
text.KJV: And Sarah was an hundred and seuen and twenty yeeres olde: these were the yeeres of the life of Sarah.
text.NABRE: The span of Sarah’s life was one hundred and twenty-seven years.
text.DRB: And Sara lived an hundred twenty seven years.

text.NRSV: And Sarah died at Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan; and Abraham went in to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
text.Rotherham: And Sarah died in Kiriath-arba, the same, is Hebron in the land of Canaan,—and Abraham went in to wail for Sarah and to weep for her.
text.KJV: And Sarah died in Kiriath arba, the same is Hebron in the land of Canaan: And Abraham came to mourne for Sarah, and to weepe for her.
text.NABRE: She died in Kiriath-arba—now Hebron—in the land of Canaan, and Abraham proceeded to mourn and weep for her.
text.DRB: And she died in the city of Arbee which is Hebron, in the land of Chanaan: And Abraham came to mourn and weep for her.

text.NRSV: Abraham rose up from beside his dead, and said to the Hittites,
text.Rotherham: And Abraham rose up from over the face of his dead,—and spake unto the sons of Heth, saying;
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham stood vp from before his dead, & spake vnto the sonnes of Heth, saying,
text.NABRE: Then he left the side of his deceased wife and addressed the Hittites:
text.DRB: And after that he was risen up from the funeral obsequies, he spake to the children of Heth, saying:

text.NRSV: “I am a stranger and an alien residing among you; give me property among you for a burying place, so that I may bury my dead out of my sight.”
text.Rotherham: A sojourner and settler, am I with you,—Give me a possession of a buryingplace with you, That I may bury my dead, from before me.
text.KJV: I am a stranger and a soiourner with you: giue me a possession of a burying place with you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.
text.NABRE: “Although I am a resident alien among you, sell me from your holdings a burial place, that I may bury my deceased wife.”
text.DRB: I am a stranger and pilgrim among you: give me the right of a sepulchre with you, that I may bury my dead.

text.NRSV: The Hittites answered Abraham,
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him:
text.KJV: And the children of Heth answered Abraham, saying vnto him,
text.NABRE: The Hittites answered Abraham: “Please,
text.DRB: The children of Heth answered, saying:

text.NRSV: “Hear us, my lord; you are a mighty prince among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our burial places; none of us will withhold from you any burial ground for burying your dead.”
text.Rotherham: Hear us, my lord! A prince of God, art thou in our midst, In the choice of our buryingplaces, bury thy dead,—Not, a man from among us his, burying-place, will withhold from thee, from burying thy dead.
text.KJV: Heare vs, my Lord, thou art a mightie Prince amongst vs: in the choise of our sepulchres bury thy dead: none of vs shall withhold from thee his sepulchre, but that thou mayest bury thy dead.
text.NABRE: sir, listen to us! You are a mighty leader among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our burial sites. None of us would deny you his burial ground for the burial of your dead.”
text.DRB: My lord hear us, thou art a prince of God among us: in our principal sepulchres bury thou thy dead: and no man can let thee but that in his own monument thou mayest bury thy dead.

text.NRSV: Abraham rose and bowed to the Hittites, the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham rose up, and bowed himself down to the people of the land, to the sons of Heth;
text.KJV: And Abraham stood vp and bowed himselfe to the people of the land, euen to the children of Heth.
text.NABRE: Abraham, however, proceeded to bow low before the people of the land, the Hittites,
text.DRB: Abraham rose up and adored the people of the land, to wit the children of Heth;

text.NRSV: He said to them, “If you are willing that I should bury my dead out of my sight, hear me, and entreat for me Ephron son of Zohar,
text.Rotherham: and spake with them, saying,—If it is with the consent of your minds that I should bury my dead from before me, hear me, and intercede for me, with Ephron son of Zohar:
text.KJV: And hee communed with them, saying, if it be your mind that I should bury my dead out of my sight, heare me, and entreat for me to Ephron the sonne of Zohar:
text.NABRE: and said to them: “If you will allow me room for burial of my dead, listen to me! Intercede for me with Ephron, son of Zohar,
text.DRB: and he said to them: If it please your soul that I bury my dead, hear me, and be intercessors for me to Ephron the son of Seor:

text.NRSV: so that he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which he owns; it is at the end of his field. For the full price let him give it to me in your presence as a possession for a burying place.”
text.Rotherham: That he would give me, the cave of Machpelah, which pertaineth unto him, which is within the bounds of his field,—For full silver, let him give it me in your midst, For a possession of a buryingplace.
text.KJV: That he may giue me the caue of Machpelah, which he hath, which is in the end of his field: for as much money as it is worth he shall giue it mee, for a possession of a burying place amongst you.
text.NABRE: so that he will sell me the cave of Machpelah that he owns; it is at the edge of his field. Let him sell it to me in your presence at its full price for a burial place.”
text.DRB: that he give me the double cave, which he hath in the uttermost part of his field: for money to the worth therefore let him deliver it to me before you for possession of a sepulchre.

text.NRSV: Now Ephron was sitting among the Hittites; and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the hearing of the Hittites, of all who went in at the gate of his city,
text.Rotherham: Now, Ephron, was sitting in the midst of the sons of Heth,—so Ephron the Hittite responded to Abraham, in the ears of the sons of Heth, even all that were entering the gate of his city, saying:
text.KJV: And Ephron dwelt amongst the children of Heth. And Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the audience of the children of Heth, euen of all that went in at the gates of his citie, saying,
text.NABRE: Now Ephron was sitting with the Hittites. So Ephron the Hittite replied to Abraham in the hearing of the Hittites, all who entered the gate of his city:
text.DRB: And Ephron dwelt in the midst of the children of Heth. And Ephron made answer to Abraham in the hearing of all that went in at the gate of the city, saying:

text.NRSV: “No, my lord, hear me; I give you the field, and I give you the cave that is in it; in the presence of my people I give it to you; bury your dead.”
text.Rotherham: Nay, my lord, hear me, The field, have I given to thee And, the cave that is therein, to thee, have I given it,—In the eyes of the sons of my people, have I given it thee Bury thy dead.
text.KJV: Nay, my lord, heare mee: the field giue I thee, and the caue that is therein, I giue it thee, in the presence of the sonnes of my people giue I it thee: bury thy dead.
text.NABRE: “Please, sir, listen to me! I give you both the field and the cave in it; in the presence of my people I give it to you. Bury your dead!”
text.DRB: No, it shall not be so, my lord, but thou rather hearken to that which I do say: The field I deliver to thee, and the cave that is therein, in the presence of the children of my people, bury thy dead.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham bowed down before the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham boweth himself down, before the people of the land:
text.KJV: And Abraham bowed downe himselfe before the people of the land.
text.NABRE: But Abraham, after bowing low before the people of the land,
text.DRB: Abraham adored before the people of the land.

text.NRSV: He said to Ephron in the hearing of the people of the land, “If you only will listen to me! I will give the price of the field; accept it from me, so that I may bury my dead there.”
text.Rotherham: and spake unto Ephron in the ears of the people of the land saying, Only if, thou, wouldst, hear me,—I have given the silver of the field Take it of me, That I may bury my dead here.
text.KJV: And he spake vnto Ephron in the audience of the people of the land, saying, But if thou wilt giue it, I pray thee, heare mee: I will giue thee money for the field: take it of me, and I will bury my dead there.
text.NABRE: addressed Ephron in the hearing of these men: “If only you would please listen to me! I will pay you the price of the field. Accept it from me, that I may bury my dead there.”
text.DRB: And he spoke to Ephron, his people standing round about: I beseech thee to hear me: I will give money for the field: take it, and so I will bury my dead in it.

text.NRSV: Ephron answered Abraham,
text.Rotherham: And Ephron responded to Abraham, saying to him,
text.KJV: And Ephron answered Abraham, saying vnto him,
text.NABRE: Ephron replied to Abraham, “Please,
text.DRB: And Ephron answered:

text.NRSV: “My lord, listen to me; a piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver—what is that between you and me? Bury your dead.”
text.Rotherham: My lord, hear me,—Land worth four hundred shekels of silver—betwixt me and thee, what is that? And thy dead., bury thou.
text.KJV: My lord, hearken vnto mee: the land is worth foure hundred shekels of siluer: what is that betwixt mee and thee? bury therefore thy dead.
text.NABRE: sir, listen to me! A piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver—what is that between you and me? Bury your dead!”
text.DRB: My lord hear me. The ground which thou desirest is worth four hundred sicles of silver: this is the price between me and thee: but how much is this? Bury thy dead.

text.NRSV: Abraham agreed with Ephron; and Abraham weighed out for Ephron the silver that he had named in the hearing of the Hittites, four hundred shekels of silver, according to the weights current among the merchants.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham hearkened unto Ephron, and Abraham weighed out to Ephron the silver of which he had spoken in the ears of the sons of Heth,—four hundred shekels of silver, current with the merchant,
text.KJV: And Abraham hearkened vnto Ephron, and Abraham weighed to Ephron the siluer, which he had named, in the audience of the sonnes of Heth, foure hundred shekels of siluer, currant money with the merchant.
text.NABRE: Abraham accepted Ephron’s terms; he weighed out to him the silver that Ephron had stipulated in the hearing of the Hittites, four hundred shekels of silver at the current market value.
text.DRB: Which when Abraham had heard, he weighed the money that Ephron had asked, in the hearing of the children of Heth, four hundred sicles of silver of common current money.

text.NRSV: So the field of Ephron in Machpelah, which was to the east of Mamre, the field with the cave that was in it and all the trees that were in the field, throughout its whole area, passed
text.Rotherham: So was confirmed the field of Ephron, which is in Machpelah, which is before Mamre,—the field and the cave which is therein, and all the timber which was in the field, which was in all the boundary thereof round about,
text.KJV: ¶ And the field of Ephron which was in Machpelah, which was before Mamre, the fielde and the caue which was therein, and all the trees that were in the field, that were in all the borders round about, were made sure
text.NABRE: Thus Ephron’s field in Machpelah, facing Mamre, together with its cave and all the trees anywhere within its limits, was conveyed
text.DRB: And the field that before time was Ephron's, wherein was the double cave, looking towards Mambre, as well it, as the cave, and all the trees thereof in all the limits thereof round about,

text.NRSV: to Abraham as a possession in the presence of the Hittites, in the presence of all who went in at the gate of his city.
text.Rotherham: to Abraham as a purchase in the eyes of the sons of Heth,—with all who were entering the gate of his city.
text.KJV: Unto Abraham for a possession in the presence of the children of Heth, before all that went in at the gates of his Citie.
text.NABRE: to Abraham by purchase in the presence of the Hittites, all who entered the gate of Ephron’s city.
text.DRB: was made sure to Abraham for a possession, in the sight of the children of Heth, and of all that went in at the gate of his city.

text.NRSV: After this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah facing Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And after this, did Abraham bury Sarah his wife, within the cave of the field of Machpelah, over against Mamre, the same, is Hebron,—in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: And after this Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the caue of the field of Machpelah, before Mamre: the same is Hebron in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave of the field of Machpelah, facing Mamre—now Hebron—in the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: And so Abraham buried Sara his wife in the double cave of the field, that looked towards Mambre, this is Hebron in the land of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: The field and the cave that is in it passed from the Hittites into Abraham’s possession as a burying place.
text.Rotherham: So was confirmed the field with the cave which was therein to Abraham, for a possession of a buryingplace,—from the sons of Heth.
text.KJV: And the field, and the caue that is therein, were made sure vnto Abraham, for a possession of a burying place, by the sonnes of Heth.
text.NABRE: Thus the field with its cave was transferred from the Hittites to Abraham as a burial place.
text.DRB: And the field was made sure to Abraham, and the cave that was in it, for a possession to bury in, of the Children of Heth.

text.NRSV: Now Abraham was old, well advanced in years; and the Lord had blessed Abraham in all things.
text.Rotherham: Now, Abraham, was old, far gone in days,—and, Yahweh, had blessed Abraham, in all things.
text.KJV: And Abraham was olde and well stricken in age: And the LORD had blessed Abraham in all things.
text.NABRE: Abraham was old, having seen many days, and the Lord had blessed him in every way.
text.DRB: And Abraham was old, and of many days, and our Lord had blessed him in all things.

text.NRSV: Abraham said to his servant, the oldest of his house, who had charge of all that he had, “Put your hand under my thigh
text.Rotherham: So Abraham said unto his servant, elder of his house, ruler of all that he had,—Place, I pray thee thy hand under my thigh;
text.KJV: And Abraham said vnto his eldest seruant of his house, that ruled ouer all that he had, Put, I pray thee, thy hand vnder my thigh:
text.NABRE: Abraham said to the senior servant of his household, who had charge of all his possessions: “Put your hand under my thigh,
