text.DRB: And he said to the elder servant of his house, which was ruler over all that he had: Put thy hand under my thigh,

text.NRSV: and I will make you swear by the Lord, the God of heaven and earth, that you will not get a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I live,
text.Rotherham: that I may put thee on oath, by Yahweh God of the heavens and God of the earth—That thou wilt not take a wife for my son, from among the daughters of the Canaanites in the midst of whom, I, am dwelling:
text.KJV: And I will make thee sweare by the LORD the God of heauen, and the God of the earth, that thou shalt not take a wife vnto my sonne of the daughters of the Canaanites amongst whom I dwell.
text.NABRE: and I will make you swear by the Lord, the God of heaven and the God of earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites among whom I live,
text.DRB: that I may adjure thee by our Lord God of heaven and earth, that thou take not a wife for my son of the daughters of the Chananites, among whom I dwell:

text.NRSV: but will go to my country and to my kindred and get a wife for my son Isaac.”
text.Rotherham: but unto my own land and unto my own kindred, wilt go,—So shalt thou take a wife for my son—for Isaac.
text.KJV: But thou shalt go vnto my countrey, and to my kinred, and take a wife vnto my sonne Isaac.
text.NABRE: but that you will go to my own land and to my relatives to get a wife for my son Isaac.”
text.DRB: but that thou go unto mine own country and kindred, and thence take a wife for my son Isaac.

text.NRSV: The servant said to him, “Perhaps the woman may not be willing to follow me to this land; must I then take your son back to the land from which you came?”
text.Rotherham: And the servant said unto him, Peradventure the woman may not be willing to follow me into this land,—May I take back thy son, into the land from whence thou camest?
text.KJV: And the seruant said vnto him, Peraduenture the woman will not bee willing to follow mee vnto this land: must I needes bring thy sonne againe, vnto the land from whence thou camest?
text.NABRE: The servant asked him: “What if the woman is unwilling to follow me to this land? Should I then take your son back to the land from which you came?”
text.DRB: The servant answered: If the woman will not come with me into this land, whether must I bring thy son back again to the place, from whence thou didst come forth?

text.NRSV: Abraham said to him, “See to it that you do not take my son back there.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said unto him,—Beware that thou do not take back my son thither!
text.KJV: And Abraham said vnto him, Beware thou, that thou bring not my sonne thither againe.
text.NABRE: Abraham told him, “Never take my son back there for any reason!
text.DRB: And Abraham said: Beware thou never bring my son back again thither.

text.NRSV: The Lord, the God of heaven, who took me from my father’s house and from the land of my birth, and who spoke to me and swore to me, ‘To your offspring I will give this land,’ he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, God of the heavens, and God of the earth, who took me out of the house of my father, and out of the land of my kindred, and, who spake to me, and who sware to me, saying, To thy seed, will I give this land, he, will send his messenger before thee, so shalt thou take a wife for my son from thence.
text.KJV: ¶ The LORD God of heauen which tooke mee from my fathers house, and from the land of my kindred, and which spake vnto mee, and that sware vnto me, saying, Unto thy seed will I giue this land, he shall send his Angel before thee, and thou shalt take a wife vnto my sonne from thence.
text.NABRE: The Lord, the God of heaven, who took me from my father’s house and the land of my relatives, and who confirmed by oath the promise he made to me, ‘I will give this land to your descendants’—he will send his angel before you, and you will get a wife for my son there.
text.DRB: Our Lord God of heaven, which took me out of my father's house, and out of my native country, which spake to me, and sware to me, saying: To thy seed will I give this land; he shall send his Angel before thee, and thou shall take from thence a wife for my son:

text.NRSV: But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you will be free from this oath of mine; only you must not take my son back there.”
text.Rotherham: But if unwilling be the woman to follow thee, then shalt thou be clear from this mine oath,—Only my son, shalt thou not take back thither
text.KJV: And if the woman wil not be willing to follow thee, then thou shalt bee cleare from this my othe: onely bring not my sonne thither againe.
text.NABRE: If the woman is unwilling to follow you, you will be released from this oath to me. But never take my son back there!”
text.DRB: but if the woman will not follow thee, thou shalt not be bound by the oath: only bring not my son thither again.

text.NRSV: So the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master and swore to him concerning this matter.
text.Rotherham: So the servant placed his hand, under the thigh of Abraham, his lord,—and sware to him, over this thing.
text.KJV: And the seruant put his hand vnder the thigh of Abraham his master, and sware to him concerning that matter.
text.NABRE: So the servant put his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham and swore to him concerning this matter.
text.DRB: The servant therefore put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his lord, and sware to him upon this word.

text.NRSV: Then the servant took ten of his master’s camels and departed, taking all kinds of choice gifts from his master; and he set out and went to Aram-naharaim, to the city of Nahor.
text.Rotherham: Then took the servant ten camels, from among the camels of his lord, and went his way,—all the goods of his master being in his hand,—so be mounted, and went his way unto Mesopotamia, unto the city of Nahor.
text.KJV: ¶ And the seruant tooke ten camels, of the camels of his master, and departed, ( for all the goods of his master were in his hand) and he arose, and went to Mesopotamia, vnto the citie of Nahor.
text.NABRE: The servant then took ten of his master’s camels, and bearing all kinds of gifts from his master, he made his way to the city of Nahor in Aram Naharaim.
text.DRB: And he took ten camels of his lord's herd, and departed, of all his goods carrying something with him, and setting forward went on into Mesopotamia to the city of Nachor.

text.NRSV: He made the camels kneel down outside the city by the well of water; it was toward evening, the time when women go out to draw water.
text.Rotherham: And he made the camels kneel down outside the city, against the well of water,—at the time of evening, at the time of the coming forth of the women that drew water,
text.KJV: And he made his camels to kneele downe without the citie, by a well of water, at the time of the euening, euen the time that women goe out to draw water.
text.NABRE: Near evening, at the time when women go out to draw water, he made the camels kneel by the well outside the city.
text.DRB: And when he had made the camels lie down without the town beside a well of water at even, at the time when women are wont to come forth to draw water, he said:

text.NRSV: And he said, “O Lord, God of my master Abraham, please grant me success today and show steadfast love to my master Abraham.
text.Rotherham: Then he said, O Yahweh, God of my lord Abraham! I pray thee cause it to fall out before me today, that thou deal in lovingkindness with my lord Abraham.
text.KJV: And he said, O LORD, God of my master Abraham, I pray thee send me good speed this day, and shew kindnesse vnto my master Abraham.
text.NABRE: Then he said: “Lord, God of my master Abraham, let it turn out favorably for me today and thus deal graciously with my master Abraham.
text.DRB: O Lord God of my lord Abraham, meet me today, I beseech thee, and do mercifully with my master Abraham.

text.NRSV: I am standing here by the spring of water, and the daughters of the townspeople are coming out to draw water.
text.Rotherham: Here am, I, stationed by the fountain of water,—and, the daughters of the men of the city, are coming forth to draw water:
text.KJV: Behold, I stand here by the well of water; and the daughters of the men of the Citie come out to draw water:
text.NABRE: While I stand here at the spring and the daughters of the townspeople are coming out to draw water,
text.DRB: Behold I stand nigh to the fountain of water and the daughters of the inhabiters of this city will come forth to draw water.

text.NRSV: Let the girl to whom I shall say, ‘Please offer your jar that I may drink,’ and who shall say, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels’—let her be the one whom you have appointed for your servant Isaac. By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master.”
text.Rotherham: so it shall come to pass that the young woman unto whom I shall say. Let down pray. thy pitcher that I may drink, and she shall say, Drink, and to thy camels also, will I give to drink, that, her! hast thou appointed for thy servant for Isaac, And, hereby, shall I get to know, that thou hast dealt in lovingkindness with my lord!
text.KJV: And let it come to passe, that the damsell to whom I shall say, Let downe thy pitcher, I pray thee, that I may drinke, and she shall say, Drinke, and I will giue thy camels drinke also; let the same be shee that thou hast appointed for thy seruant Isaac: and thereby shall I know that thou hast shewed kindnesse vnto my master.
text.NABRE: if I say to a young woman, ‘Please lower your jug, that I may drink,’ and she answers, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels, too,’ then she is the one whom you have decided upon for your servant Isaac. In this way I will know that you have dealt graciously with my master.”
text.DRB: Therefore the maid, to whom I shall say: Bow down thy tankard that I may drink; and she shall answer: Drink, yea to thy camels also will I give drink; she it is, whom thou hast provided for thy servant Isaac: and by this I shall understand, that thou hast dealt mercifully with my master.

text.NRSV: Before he had finished speaking, there was Rebekah, who was born to Bethuel son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham’s brother, coming out with her water jar on her shoulder.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, ere yet, he, had done speaking, that lo! Rebekah, was coming forth who had been born to Bethuel son of Milcah, wife of Nahor, brother of Abraham,—with her pitcher upon her shoulder,
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe before hee had done speaking, that behold, Rebekah came out, who was borne to Bethuel, sonne of Milcah, the wife of Nahor Abrahams brother, with her pitcher vpon her shoulder.
text.NABRE: He had scarcely finished speaking when Rebekah—who was born to Bethuel, son of Milcah, the wife of Abraham’s brother Nahor—came out with a jug on her shoulder.
text.DRB: Neither had he yet ended these words within himself, & behold Rebecca came forth, the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Melcha, wife to Nachor the brother of Abraham, having a tankard on her shoulder;

text.NRSV: The girl was very fair to look upon, a virgin, whom no man had known. She went down to the spring, filled her jar, and came up.
text.Rotherham: Now the young woman! was of very pleasing appearance, a virgin whom, no man, had known,—and she went down unto the fountain, and filled her pitcher and came up.
text.KJV: And the damsell was very faire to looke vpon, a virgine, neither had any man knowen her; and shee went downe to the wel, and filled her pitcher, and came vp.
text.NABRE: The young woman was very beautiful, a virgin, untouched by man. She went down to the spring and filled her jug. As she came up,
text.DRB: a passing comely maid, & most beautiful virgin, & not known to man: and she was gone down to the fountain, and had filled her tankard, and came back.

text.NRSV: Then the servant ran to meet her and said, “Please let me sip a little water from your jar.”
text.Rotherham: And the servant ran to meet her,—and said, I pray thee let me drink a little water out of thy pitcher.
text.KJV: And the seruant ranne to meete her, and said, Let mee (I pray thee) drinke a little water of thy pitcher.
text.NABRE: the servant ran toward her and said, “Please give me a sip of water from your jug.”
text.DRB: And the servant ran to meet her, and said: Give me a little water to drink of thy tankard.

text.NRSV: “Drink, my lord,” she said, and quickly lowered her jar upon her hand and gave him a drink.
text.Rotherham: And she said, Drink my lord,—And she hastened and lowered her pitcher on her hand and let him drink.
text.KJV: And she said, Drinke, my lord: and she hasted, and let downe her pitcher vpon her hand, and gaue him drinke.
text.NABRE: “Drink, sir,” she replied, and quickly lowering the jug into her hand, she gave him a drink.
text.DRB: Who answered: Drink my lord. And quickly she let down the tankard upon her arm, and gave him drink.

text.NRSV: When she had finished giving him a drink, she said, “I will draw for your camels also, until they have finished drinking.”
text.Rotherham: And when she had done giving him drink,—she said For thy camels also, will I draw, until they have done drinking.
text.KJV: And when shee had done giuing him drinke, she said, I will draw water for thy camels also, vntill they haue done drinking.
text.NABRE: When she had finished giving him a drink, she said, “I will draw water for your camels, too, until they have finished drinking.”
text.DRB: And when he had drunk, she added: but for thy camels also I will draw water, till all do drink.

text.NRSV: So she quickly emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to the well to draw, and she drew for all his camels.
text.Rotherham: So she hastened and emptied her pitcher into the drinking-trough, and ran again unto the well, to draw,—and she drew for all his camels.
text.KJV: And she hasted and emptied her pitcher into the trough, and ranne againe vnto the well to draw water, and drew for all his camels.
text.NABRE: With that, she quickly emptied her jug into the drinking trough and ran back to the well to draw more water, until she had drawn enough for all the camels.
text.DRB: And pouring out the tankard into the troughs, she ran back to the well to draw water: and being drawn gave it to all the camels.

text.NRSV: The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether or not the Lord had made his journey successful.
text.Rotherham: Now the man, was eagerly watching her,—keeping silence to see—whether Yahweh had prospered his way., or not.
text.KJV: And the man wondering at her, helde his peace, to wit, whether the LORD had made his iourney prosperous, or not.
text.NABRE: The man watched her the whole time, silently waiting to learn whether or not the Lord had made his journey successful.
text.DRB: But he musing beheld her with silence, desirous to know whether our Lord had made his journey prosperous or not.

text.NRSV: When the camels had finished drinking, the man took a gold nose-ring weighing a half shekel, and two bracelets for her arms weighing ten gold shekels,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when the camels had done drinking, that the man took a ring of gold, half a shekel its weight,—and two bracelets for her hands, ten of gold their weight;
text.KJV: And it came to passe as the camels had done drinking, that the man tooke a golden eare-ring, of halfe a shekel weight, & two bracelets for her handes, of ten shekels weight of gold,
text.NABRE: When the camels had finished drinking, the man took out a gold nose-ring weighing half a shekel, and two gold bracelets weighing ten shekels for her wrists.
text.DRB: And after that the camels had drunk, the man plucked forth golden earlets, weighing two sicles, and as many bracelets of ten sicles weight.

text.NRSV: and said, “Tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your father’s house for us to spend the night?”
text.Rotherham: and said Whose daughter art, thou? I pray thee, tell me. Is there in the house of thy father a place for us to tarry the night?
text.KJV: And said, whose daughter art thou? tell mee, I pray thee: is there roome in thy fathers house for vs to lodge in?
text.NABRE: Then he asked her: “Whose daughter are you? Tell me, please. And is there a place in your father’s house for us to spend the night?”
text.DRB: And he said to her: Whose daughter art thou? Shew me; is there any place in thy father's house to lodge?

text.NRSV: She said to him, “I am the daughter of Bethuel son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.”
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him, Daughter of Bethuel, am, I,—[Bethuel] son of Milcah whom she bare to Nahor.
text.KJV: And she said vnto him, I am the daughter of Bethuel the sonne of Milcah, which she bare vnto Nahor:
text.NABRE: She answered: “I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.
text.DRB: Who answered: I am the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Melcha, whom she bare to Nachor.

text.NRSV: She added, “We have plenty of straw and fodder and a place to spend the night.”
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him, Both straw and fodder in plenty, have we,—also a place to tarry the night.
text.KJV: She said moreouer vnto him, We haue both straw & prouender ynough, and roome to lodge in.
text.NABRE: We have plenty of straw and fodder,” she added, “and also a place to spend the night.”
text.DRB: And she added, saying: Of straw also and hay we have good store, and a large place to lodge in.

text.NRSV: The man bowed his head and worshiped the Lord
text.Rotherham: Then the man bowed his head, and bent himself down unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the man bowed downe his head, and worshipped the LORD.
text.NABRE: The man then knelt and bowed down to the Lord,
text.DRB: The man bowed himself, and adored our Lord,

text.NRSV: and said, “Blessed be the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken his steadfast love and his faithfulness toward my master. As for me, the Lord has led me on the way to the house of my master’s kin.”
text.Rotherham: and said, Blessed be Yahweh. God of my lord Abraham, who hath not withdrawn his lovingkindness and his faithfulness, from my lord,—I—being in the way, Yahweh led me unto the house of the brethren of my lord!
text.KJV: And hee saide, Blessed bee the LORD God of my master Abraham, who hath not left destitute my master of his mercy, and his trueth: I being in the way, the LORD led me to the house of my masters brethren.
text.NABRE: saying: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who has not let his kindness and fidelity toward my master fail. As for me, the Lord has led me straight to the house of my master’s brother.”
text.DRB: saying: blessed be the Lord God of my lord Abraham, that hath not taken away his mercy & truth from my lord, and hath brought me the straight way into the house of my lord's brother.

text.NRSV: Then the girl ran and told her mother’s household about these things.
text.Rotherham: And the young woman ran, and told the household of her mother,—according to these words.
text.KJV: And the damsell ranne, and told them of her mothers house, these things.
text.NABRE: Then the young woman ran off and told her mother’s household what had happened.
text.DRB: The maid therefore ran, and reported into her mother's house all things that she had heard.

text.NRSV: Rebekah had a brother whose name was Laban; and Laban ran out to the man, to the spring.
text.Rotherham: Now, Rebekah, had a brother, whose name, was Laban,—so Laban ran unto the man outside, unto the fountain.
text.KJV: ¶ And Rebekah had a brother, and his name was Laban: and Laban ranne out vnto the man, vnto the well.
text.NABRE: Now Rebekah had a brother named Laban. Laban rushed outside to the man at the spring.
text.DRB: And Rebecca had a brother named Laban, who in all haste went forth to the man, where the fountain was.

text.NRSV: As soon as he had seen the nose-ring, and the bracelets on his sister’s arms, and when he heard the words of his sister Rebekah, “Thus the man spoke to me,” he went to the man; and there he was, standing by the camels at the spring.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he saw the ring, and the bracelets on the hands of his sister, and when he heard the words of Rebekah his sister saying, Thus, did the man speak unto me, that he came unto the man, and lo! he was standing by the camels near the fountain;
text.KJV: And it came to passe when he saw the eare-ring, and bracelets vpon his sisters hands, and when hee heard the wordes of Rebekah his sister, saying, Thus spake the man vnto me, that he came vnto the man; and behold, hee stood by the camels, at the well.
text.NABRE: When he saw the nose-ring and the bracelets on his sister’s arms and when he heard Rebekah repeating what the man had said to her, he went to him while he was standing by the camels at the spring.
text.DRB: And when he had seen the earlets and bracelets in his sister's hands, and hand heard all her words reporting: These words spake the man unto me; he came to the man which stood beside the camels, and nigh to the fountain of water:

text.NRSV: He said, “Come in, O blessed of the Lord. Why do you stand outside when I have prepared the house and a place for the camels?”
text.Rotherham: and he said, Come in. thou blessed of Yahweh,—Wherefore shouldst thou stand outside, when I have made ready the house and a place for the camels?
text.KJV: And he said, Come in, thou blessed of the LORD, wherefore standest thou without? for I haue prepared the house, and roome for the camels.
text.NABRE: He said: “Come, blessed of the Lord! Why are you standing outside when I have made the house ready, as well as a place for the camels?”
text.DRB: and said to him: Come in, thou blessed of our Lord: Why standest thou without? I have prepared the house, and a place for the camels.

text.NRSV: So the man came into the house; and Laban unloaded the camels, and gave him straw and fodder for the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.
text.Rotherham: So the man came in towards the house, and ungirded the camels,—and there was given him straw and fodder for the camels, and water for bathing his own feet and the feet of the men who were with him;
text.KJV: ¶ And the man came into the house: and he vngirded his camels, and gaue straw and prouender for the camels, and water to wash his feet, and the mens feet that were with him.
text.NABRE: The man then went inside; and while the camels were being unloaded and provided with straw and fodder, water was brought to bathe his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.
text.DRB: And he brought him into his lodging: and he unharnessed the camels, and gave straw and hay, and water to wash his feet, and of the men that were come with him.

text.NRSV: Then food was set before him to eat; but he said, “I will not eat until I have told my errand.” He said, “Speak on.”
text.Rotherham: and [food] was set before him to eat, But he said, I cannot eat, until I have spoken my words. And he said—Speak!
text.KJV: And there was set meat before him to eate: but he said, I will not eate, vntill I haue tolde mine errand. And hee said, Speake on.
text.NABRE: But when food was set before him, he said, “I will not eat until I have told my story.” “Go ahead,” they replied.
text.DRB: And bread was set before him. Who said: I will not eat till I speak my message. He answered him: Speak.

text.NRSV: So he said, “I am Abraham’s servant.
text.Rotherham: Then said he,—Servant of Abraham, am I.
text.KJV: And he said, I am Abrahams seruant.
text.NABRE: “I am Abraham’s servant,” he began.
text.DRB: And he said: I am the servant of Abraham:

text.NRSV: The Lord has greatly blessed my master, and he has become wealthy; he has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male and female slaves, camels and donkeys.
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, having blessed my lord exceedingly, and made him great,—and given him flocks and herds and silver and gold, and men-servants and maid-servants, and camels, and asses,
text.KJV: And the LORD hath blessed my master greatly, and hee is become great: and hee hath giuen him flocks, and heards, and siluer, and gold, and men seruants, and mayd seruants, and camels, and asses.
text.NABRE: “The Lord has blessed my master so abundantly that he has become wealthy; he has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male and female slaves, and camels and donkeys.
text.DRB: And our Lord hath blessed my lord wonderfully, and he is magnified: and he hath given him sheep, & oxen, silver and gold, men servants & women servants, camels, and asses.

text.NRSV: And Sarah my master’s wife bore a son to my master when she was old; and he has given him all that he has.
text.Rotherham: then did Sarah wife of my lord, bear a son to my lord, after she had become old,—so he gave to him all that he had;
text.KJV: And Sarah my masters wife bare a sonne to my master when shee was old: and vnto him hath hee giuen all that he hath.
text.NABRE: My master’s wife Sarah bore a son to my master in her old age, and he has given him everything he owns.
text.DRB: And Sara my lord's wife hath born my lord a son in her old age, and he hath given him all things that he had.

text.NRSV: My master made me swear, saying, ‘You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I live;
text.Rotherham: And my lord put me on oath saying,—Thou shalt not take a wife for my son, of the daughters of the Canaanites in whose land, I, am dwelling;
text.KJV: And my master made me sweare, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife to my sonne, of the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I dwell:
text.NABRE: My master put me under oath, saying: ‘You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites in whose land I live;
text.DRB: And my lord adjured me saying: Thou shalt not take a wife for my son of the Chananites, in whose land I dwell:

text.NRSV: but you shall go to my father’s house, to my kindred, and get a wife for my son.’
text.Rotherham: but, unto the house of my father, shalt thou go, and unto my family,—and take a wife for my son.
text.KJV: But thou shalt goe vnto my fathers house, and to my kinred, and take a wife vnto my sonne.
text.NABRE: instead, you must go to my father’s house, to my own family, to get a wife for my son.’
text.DRB: but thou shalt go to my father's house, and of mine own kindred shalt thou take a wife for my son:

text.NRSV: I said to my master, ‘Perhaps the woman will not follow me.’
text.Rotherham: So I said unto my lord,—Peradventure the woman will not follow me.
text.KJV: And I said vnto my master, Peraduenture the woman will not followe me.
text.NABRE: When I asked my master, ‘What if the woman will not follow me?’
text.DRB: but I answered my lord: What if the woman will not come with me?

text.NRSV: But he said to me, ‘The Lord, before whom I walk, will send his angel with you and make your way successful. You shall get a wife for my son from my kindred, from my father’s house.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me,—Yahweh, before whom I have walked to and fro, will send his messenger with thee, so shall he prosper thy way, and thou shalt take a wife for my son out of my kindred and out of the house of my father.
text.KJV: And hee saide vnto me, The LORD, before whom I walke, will send his Angel with thee, and prosper thy way: and thou shalt take a wife for my sonne, of my kinred, and of my fathers house.
text.NABRE: he replied: ‘The Lord, in whose presence I have always walked, will send his angel with you and make your journey successful, and so you will get a wife for my son from my own family and my father’s house.
text.DRB: Our Lord, saith he, in whose sight I walk, will send his Angel with thee, & will direct thy way: and thou shalt take a wife for my son of mine own kindred, and of my father's house.

text.NRSV: Then you will be free from my oath, when you come to my kindred; even if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath.’
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou clear thyself from mine oath, for thou shalt go in unto my kindred,—and, if they will not grant her unto thee, then shalt thou be clear from mine oath.
text.KJV: Then shalt thou bee cleare from this my oath, when thou commest to my kinred, and if they giue not thee one, thou shalt be cleare from my oath.
text.NABRE: Then you will be freed from my curse. If you go to my family and they refuse you, then, too, you will be free from my curse.’
text.DRB: Thou shalt be innocent from my curse, when thou shalt come to my kin, and they will not give her thee.

text.NRSV: “I came today to the spring, and said, ‘O Lord, the God of my master Abraham, if now you will only make successful the way I am going!
text.Rotherham: So I came in to-day, unto the fountain,—and I said O Yahweh God of my lord Abraham! if, I pray thee thou art prospering my way whereon I am going,
text.KJV: And I came this day vnto the well, and said, O LORD God of my master Abraham, if now thou doe prosper my way, which I goe:
text.NABRE: “When I came to the spring today, I said: ‘Lord, God of my master Abraham, please make successful the journey I am on.
text.DRB: I came therefore today to the well of water, and said: O Lord God of my lord Abraham, if thou hast directed my way, wherein I now walk,

text.NRSV: I am standing here by the spring of water; let the young woman who comes out to draw, to whom I shall say, “Please give me a little water from your jar to drink,”
text.Rotherham: here, am I stationed by the fountain of water,—so then it shall come to pass that, the maiden that is coming forth to draw, and I shall say unto her Let me drink. I pray thee a little water out of thy pitcher;
text.KJV: Behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to passe, that when the virgine commeth foorth to draw water, and I say to her, Giue me, I pray thee, a litle water of thy pitcher to drinke;
text.NABRE: While I stand here at the spring, if I say to a young woman who comes out to draw water, ‘Please give me a little water from your jug,’
text.DRB: behold I stand besides the well of water, and the virgin, that shall come forth to draw water, when she shall hear me say: Give me a little water to drink of thy tankard;

text.NRSV: and who will say to me, “Drink, and I will draw for your camels also”—let her be the woman whom the Lord has appointed for my master’s son.’
text.Rotherham: and she shall say unto me Both, thou, drink, and for thy camels also, will I draw, the same, shall be the woman whom Yahweh hath appointed for the son of my lord.
text.KJV: And she say to me, Both drinke thou, and I will also draw for thy camels: let the same be the woman, whō the LORD hath appointed out for my masters sonne.
text.NABRE: and she answers, ‘Drink, and I will draw water for your camels, too—then she is the woman whom the Lord has decided upon for my master’s son.’
text.DRB: and she shall say to me: Drink both thou and for thy camels I will draw also; that is the woman, which our Lord hath prepared for my master's son.

text.NRSV: “Before I had finished speaking in my heart, there was Rebekah coming out with her water jar on her shoulder; and she went down to the spring, and drew. I said to her, ‘Please let me drink.’
text.Rotherham: Ere yet, I, could make an end of speaking unto mine own heart, lo! then Rebekah, coming forth, with her pitcher on her shoulder, and she went down to the fountain, and drew,—and I said unto her Let me drink I pray thee!
text.KJV: And before I had done speaking in mine heart, behold, Rebekah came forth, with her pitcher on her shoulder; and she went downe vnto the well, and drew water: and I said vnto her, Let me drinke, I pray thee.
text.NABRE: “I had scarcely finished saying this to myself when Rebekah came out with a jug on her shoulder. After she went down to the spring and drew water, I said to her, ‘Please let me have a drink.’
text.DRB: And whilst I pondered these things secretly with myself, Rebecca appeared coming with a tankard, which she carried upon her shoulder: & she went down to the fountain, & drew water.

text.NRSV: She quickly let down her jar from her shoulder, and said, ‘Drink, and I will also water your camels.’ So I drank, and she also watered the camels.
text.Rotherham: So she hastened and lowered her pitcher from off her, and said. Drink! and to thy camels also, will I give to drink,—So I drank and the camels also, she let drink.
text.KJV: And she made haste, & let downe her pitcher from her shoulder, and saide, Drinke, and I will giue thy camels drinke also: so I dranke, and she made the camels drinke also.
text.NABRE: She quickly lowered the jug she was carrying and said, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels, too.’ So I drank, and she watered the camels also.
text.DRB: And I said to her: Give me a little to drink. Who speedily let down the tankard from her shoulder, and said to me: Drink Both thou, and to thy camels I will give drink. I drank, and she watered the camels.

text.NRSV: Then I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ She said, ‘The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor’s son, whom Milcah bore to him.’ So I put the ring on her nose, and the bracelets on her arms.
text.Rotherham: Then I asked her, and said, Whose daughter art thou? And she said Daughter of Bethuel son of Nahor, whom, Milcah, bare to him,—Then put I the ring upon her nose, and the bracelets upon her hands;
text.KJV: And I asked her, and said, whose daughter art thou? and she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahors sonne, whom Milcah bare vnto him: and I put the earering vpon her face, and the bracelets vpon her hands.
text.NABRE: When I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ she answered, ‘The daughter of Bethuel, son of Nahor, borne to Nahor by Milcah.’ So I put the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her wrists.
text.DRB: And I asked her, and said: Whose daughter art thou? Who answered: I am the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Nachor, whom Melcha bare him. I hung therefore earlets to adorn her face, and I put bracelets upon her hands.

text.NRSV: Then I bowed my head and worshiped the Lord, and blessed the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me by the right way to obtain the daughter of my master’s kinsman for his son.
text.Rotherham: and bowed my head and bent myself down unto Yahweh,—and I blessed Yahweh God of my lord Abraham, who had led me in a way of faithfulness, to take the daughter of the brother of my lord for his son.
text.KJV: And I bowed downe my head, and worshipped the LORD, and blessed the LORD God of my master Abraham, which had led mee in the right way to take my masters brothers daughter vnto his sonne.
text.NABRE: Then I knelt and bowed down to the Lord, blessing the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me on the right road to obtain the daughter of my master’s kinsman for his son.
text.DRB: And prostrate I adored our Lord, blessing the Lord God of my lord Abraham, who hath brought me the straight way to take the daughter of my lord's brother for his son.

text.NRSV: Now then, if you will deal loyally and truly with my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, so that I may turn either to the right hand or to the left.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, if ye are dealing in lovingkindness and faithfulness with my lord, tell me,—and, if not, tell me, that I may turn unto the right hand or unto the left.
text.KJV: And now if you wil deale kindly and truely with my master, tell me: and if not, tell me, that I may turne to the right hand, or to the left.
text.NABRE: Now, if you will act with kindness and fidelity toward my master, let me know; but if not, let me know that too. I can then proceed accordingly.”
text.DRB: Wherefore if you do according to mercy and truth with my lord, shew me: but if it please you otherwise, that also tell me, that I may go to the right hand or to the left.

text.NRSV: Then Laban and Bethuel answered, “The thing comes from the Lord; we cannot speak to you anything bad or good.
text.Rotherham: Then answered Laban and Bethuel and said, from Yahweh, hath come forth the word,—we cannot speak unto thee ill or well.
text.KJV: Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said, The thing proceedeth from the LORD: we cannot speake vnto thee bad or good.
text.NABRE: Laban and Bethuel said in reply: “This thing comes from the Lord; we can say nothing to you either for or against it.
text.DRB: And Laban and Bathuel answered: From our Lord the word hath proceeded: we cannot speak any other thing with thee besides his pleasure.

text.NRSV: Look, Rebekah is before you, take her and go, and let her be the wife of your master’s son, as the Lord has spoken.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! Rebekah, is before thee, Take her and go thy way,—And let her become wife unto the son of thy lord, as Yahweh hath spoken.
text.KJV: Behold, Rebekah is before thee, take her, and goe, and let her be thy masters sonnes wife, as the LORD hath spoken.
text.NABRE: Here is Rebekah, right in front of you; take her and go, that she may become the wife of your master’s son, as the Lord has said.”
text.DRB: Behold Rebecca is before thee, take her and go thy ways, & let her be the wife of thy lord's son, as our Lord hath spoken.

text.NRSV: When Abraham’s servant heard their words, he bowed himself to the ground before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Abraham’s servant heard their words, that he bowed himself down towards the earth unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that when Abrahams seruant heard their words, he worshipped the LORD, bowing himselfe to the earth.
text.NABRE: When Abraham’s servant heard their answer, he bowed to the ground before the Lord.
text.DRB: Which when Abraham's servant heard, falling down he adored our Lord to the ground.

text.NRSV: And the servant brought out jewelry of silver and of gold, and garments, and gave them to Rebekah; he also gave to her brother and to her mother costly ornaments.
text.Rotherham: Then the servant brought forth jewels of silver and jewels of gold and raiment, and gave unto Rebekah,—and precious things, gave he to her brother and to her mother.
text.KJV: And the seruant brought foorth iewels of siluer, and iewels of gold, and raiment, and gaue them to Rebekah: He gaue also to her brother, and to her mother precious things.
text.NABRE: Then he brought out objects of silver and gold and clothing and presented them to Rebekah; he also gave costly presents to her brother and mother.
text.DRB: And taking forth vessels of silver, and gold, and garments, gave them to Rebecca for a present. To her brothers also, and to her mother he offered gifts.

text.NRSV: Then he and the men who were with him ate and drank, and they spent the night there. When they rose in the morning, he said, “Send me back to my master.”
text.Rotherham: And they did eat and drink he, and the men who were with him, and they tarried the night,—and when they arose in the morning, he said Let me go unto my lord!
text.KJV: And they did eate and drinke, he and the men that were with him, and taried all night, and they rose vp in the morning, and he said, Send me away vnto my master.
text.NABRE: After he and the men with him had eaten and drunk, they spent the night there. When they got up the next morning, he said, “Allow me to return to my master.”
text.DRB: Abanquet was made, and eating and drinking together they lodged there. And in the morning the servant arose, and said: Dismiss me, that I may go to my lord.

text.NRSV: Her brother and her mother said, “Let the girl remain with us a while, at least ten days; after that she may go.”
text.Rotherham: Then said her brother and her mother, Let the young woman remain with us some days or rather ten,—After that, she shall go.
text.KJV: And her brother and her mother said, Let the damsell abide with vs a few dayes, at the least ten; after that, she shall goe.
text.NABRE: Her brother and mother replied, “Let the young woman stay with us a short while, say ten days; after that she may go.”
text.DRB: And her brother and mother answered: Let the maid tarry at the least ten days with us, & after she shall depart.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “Do not delay me, since the Lord has made my journey successful; let me go that I may go to my master.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them, Do not hinder me, when, Yahweh, hath prospered my journey,—Let me go, that I may take my journey unto my lord!
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Hinder me not, seeing the LORD hath prospered my way: send me away, that I may goe to my master.
text.NABRE: But he said to them, “Do not detain me, now that the Lord has made my journey successful; let me go back to my master.”
text.DRB: Stay me not, said he, because our Lord hath directed my way: dismiss me that I may go on to my lord.

text.NRSV: They said, “We will call the girl, and ask her.”
text.Rotherham: Then said they, We must call the young woman and ask at her mouth.
text.KJV: And they said, wee will call the Damsell, and enquire at her mouth.
text.NABRE: They answered, “Let us call the young woman and see what she herself has to say about it.”
text.DRB: And they said: Let us call the maid, and ask her will.

text.NRSV: And they called Rebekah, and said to her, “Will you go with this man?” She said, “I will.”
text.Rotherham: So they called Rebekah and said unto her, Wilt thou go with this man? And she said, I will go.
text.KJV: And they called Rebekah, and said vnto her, Wilt thou go with this man? and she said, I will goe.
text.NABRE: So they called Rebekah and asked her, “Will you go with this man?” She answered, “I will.”
text.DRB: And being called, when she was come, they asked: Wilt thou go with this man? Who said: I will go.

text.NRSV: So they sent away their sister Rebekah and her nurse along with Abraham’s servant and his men.
text.Rotherham: So they let go Rebekah their sister, and her nurse, and Abraham’s servant and his men.
text.KJV: And they sent away Rebekah their sister, and her nurse, and Abrahams seruant, and his men.
text.NABRE: At this they sent off their sister Rebekah and her nurse with Abraham’s servant and his men.
text.DRB: They dismissed her therefore, and her nurse, and Abraham's servant, and his company,

text.NRSV: And they blessed Rebekah and said to her, “May you, our sister, become thousands of myriads; may your offspring gain possession of the gates of their foes.”
text.Rotherham: And they blessed Rebekah and said to her, Our sister! become thou, thousands of ten thousands,—And let thy seed take possession of the gate of them that hate them!
text.KJV: And they blessed Rebekah, and said vnto her, Thou art our sister, bee thou the mother of thousands of millions, and let thy seed possesse the gate of those which hate them.
text.NABRE: They blessed Rebekah and said: “Sister, may you grow into thousands of myriads; And may your descendants gain possession of the gates of their enemies!”
text.DRB: wishing prosperity to their sister, and saying: Thou art our sister, increase thou into thousand thousands, and thy seed possess the gates of their enemies.

text.NRSV: Then Rebekah and her maids rose up, mounted the camels, and followed the man; thus the servant took Rebekah, and went his way.
text.Rotherham: Then mounted Rebekah and her young woman and they rode upon the camels, and followed the man,—so the servant took Rebekah. and went his way.
text.KJV: ¶ And Rebekah arose, and her damsels, & they rode vpon the camels, and followed the man: and the seruant tooke Rebekah, and went his way.
text.NABRE: Then Rebekah and her attendants started out; they mounted the camels and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and went on his way.
text.DRB: Therefore Rebecca and her maids being set upon camels, followed the man: who with speed returned unto his lord; and

text.NRSV: Now Isaac had come from Beer-lahai-roi, and was settled in the Negeb.
text.Rotherham: Now, Isaac, had come in at the entrance, of Beer-lahai-roi,—for, he, was dwelling in the land of the South:
text.KJV: And Isaac came from the way of the well Lahai-roi, for he dwelt in the South countrey.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile Isaac had gone from Beer-lahai-roi and was living in the region of the Negeb.
text.DRB: the same time Isaac walked along the way, that leadeth to the well of the Living and the seeing, so called; for he dwelt in the south country:

text.NRSV: Isaac went out in the evening to walk in the field; and looking up, he saw camels coming.
text.Rotherham: and Isaac came forth to meditate in the field at the approach of evening,—so he lifted up his eyes and looked, and lo! camels, coming in.
text.KJV: And Isaac went out, to meditate in the field, at the euentide: and hee lift vp his eyes, and saw, and behold, the camels were comming.
text.NABRE: One day toward evening he went out to walk in the field, and caught sight of camels approaching.
text.DRB: and he was gone forth to meditate in the field, the day now being well spent: and when he head cast up his eyes, he saw camels coming a far off.

text.NRSV: And Rebekah looked up, and when she saw Isaac, she slipped quickly from the camel,
text.Rotherham: And Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and saw Isaac,—so she alighted with haste from off the camel;
text.KJV: And Rebekah lift vp her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she lighted off the camel.
text.NABRE: Rebekah, too, caught sight of Isaac, and got down from her camel.
text.DRB: Rebecca also, when she saw Isaac, lighted off the camel,

text.NRSV: and said to the servant, “Who is the man over there, walking in the field to meet us?” The servant said, “It is my master.” So she took her veil and covered herself.
text.Rotherham: and said unto the servant, Who is this man that is walking in the field to meet us? And the servant said, That, is my lord. So she took the veil and covered herself.
text.KJV: For she had said vnto the seruant, What man is this that walketh in the field to meet vs? and the seruant had said, It is my master: therefore shee tooke a vaile and couered her selfe.
text.NABRE: She asked the servant, “Who is the man over there, walking through the fields toward us?” “That is my master,” replied the servant. Then she took her veil and covered herself.
text.DRB: and said to the servant: Who is that man which cometh towards us along the field? And he said to her: The same is my lord. But she quickly taking her cloak, covered herself.

text.NRSV: And the servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.
text.Rotherham: And the servant recounted to Isaac,—all the things which he had done.
text.KJV: And the seruant tolde Isaac all things that he had done.
text.NABRE: The servant recounted to Isaac all the things he had done.
text.DRB: And the servant told Isaac all things that he had done.

text.NRSV: Then Isaac brought her into his mother Sarah’s tent. He took Rebekah, and she became his wife; and he loved her. So Isaac was comforted after his mother’s death.
text.Rotherham: And Isaac brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother; thus he took Rebekah and she became his wife and he loved her,—and Isaac consoled himself, for the loss of his mother.
text.KJV: And Isaac brought her into his mother Sarahs tent, and tooke Rebekah, and she became his wife, and he loued her: and Isaac was comforted after his mothers death.
text.NABRE: Then Isaac brought Rebekah into the tent of his mother Sarah. He took Rebekah as his wife. Isaac loved her and found solace after the death of his mother.
text.DRB: Who brought her into the tent of Sara his mother, & took her to wife: and he loved her so much, that it did moderate the sorrow which was chanced of his mother's death.

text.NRSV: Abraham took another wife, whose name was Keturah.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham took another wife and her name, was Keturah;
text.KJV: Then againe Abraham tooke a wife, & her name was Keturah.
text.NABRE: Abraham took another wife, whose name was Keturah.
text.DRB: And Abraham married another wife named Cetura:

text.NRSV: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text.Rotherham: and she bare to him Zimran, and Yokshan, and Medan, and Midian,—and Ishbak, and Shuah.
text.KJV: And shee bare him Zimran, and Iokshan, and Medan, and Midian, and Ishbak, and Shuah.
text.NABRE: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text.DRB: Which bare him Zamran, and Jecsan, and Madan, and Madian, and Jesboc, and Sue.

text.NRSV: Jokshan was the father of Sheba and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text.Rotherham: And, Yokshan, begat Sheba and Dedan,—and, the sons of Dedan, were Asshurim and Letushim, and Leummim.
text.KJV: And Iokshan begat Sheba, and Dedan. And the sonnes of Dedan were Asshurim, and Letushim, and Leummim.
text.NABRE: Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan. The descendants of Dedan were the Asshurim, the Letushim, and the Leummim.
text.DRB: Jecsan also begat Saba & Dadan. The Children of Dadan were Assurim, and Latusim, and Loomim.

text.NRSV: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Midian, Ephah and Epher and Hanoch, and Abida. and Eldaah,—all these, were the sons of Keturah.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Midian, Ephah, and Epher, and Hanoch, and Abida, and Eldaah: all these were the children of Keturah.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All of these were descendants of Keturah.
text.DRB: But also of Madian was born Epha, and Opher, and Henoch, and Abida, and Eldaa: all these were the children of Cetura.

text.NRSV: Abraham gave all he had to Isaac.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham gave all that he had to Isaac;
text.KJV: ¶ And Abraham gaue all that he had, vnto Isaac.
text.NABRE: Abraham gave everything that he owned to his son Isaac.
text.DRB: And Abraham gave all his possessions to Isaac:

text.NRSV: But to the sons of his concubines Abraham gave gifts, while he was still living, and he sent them away from his son Isaac, eastward to the east country.
text.Rotherham: but, to the sons of the concubines whom Abraham had, Abraham gave gifts,—and then sent them away from Isaac his son while he himself yet lived, eastward unto the land of the east.
text.KJV: But vnto the sonnes of the concubines which Abraham had, Abraham gaue gifts, and sent them away from Isaac his sonne (while he yet liued) Eastward, vnto the East country.
text.NABRE: To the sons of his concubines, however, he gave gifts while he was still living, as he sent them away eastward, to the land of Kedem, away from his son Isaac.
text.DRB: and to the children of his concubines he gave gifts, and separated them from Isaac his son, whilst himself yet lived, to the east country.

text.NRSV: This is the length of Abraham’s life, one hundred seventy-five years.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the days of the years of the life of Abraham which he lived—a hundred and seventy-five years.
text.KJV: And these are the dayes of the yeres of Abrahams life which he liued; an hundred, threescore & fifteene yeeres.
text.NABRE: The whole span of Abraham’s life was one hundred and seventy-five years.
text.DRB: And the days of Abraham's life were an hundred seventy and five years.

text.NRSV: Abraham breathed his last and died in a good old age, an old man and full of years, and was gathered to his people.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham breathed his last, and died in a fine old age, old and satisfied,—and was gathered unto his people,
text.KJV: Then Abraham gaue vp the ghost, and died in a good old age, an old man, and full of yeeres, and was gathered to his people.
text.NABRE: Then he breathed his last, dying at a ripe old age, grown old after a full life; and he was gathered to his people.
text.DRB: And decaying died in a good old age, and having lived a great time, and being full of days and was gathered to his people.

text.NRSV: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite, east of Mamre,
text.Rotherham: And Isaac and Ishmael his sons buried him, in the cave of Machpelah,—in the field of Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite, which is over against Mamre;
text.KJV: And his sonnes Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the caue of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the sonne of Zohar the Hittite, which is before Mamre;
text.NABRE: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron, son of Zohar the Hittite, which faces Mamre,
text.DRB: And there buried him Isaac and Ismael his sons in the double cave, which was situated in the field of Ephron the son of Seor the Hethite, over-against Mambre,

text.NRSV: the field that Abraham purchased from the Hittites. There Abraham was buried, with his wife Sarah.
text.Rotherham: the field which Abraham purchased of the sons of Heth,—there, was buried Abraham, with Sarah his wife.
text.KJV: The field which Abraham purchased of the sonnes of Heth: there was Abraham buried, and Sarah his wife.
text.NABRE: the field that Abraham had bought from the Hittites; there he was buried next to his wife Sarah.
text.DRB: which he had bought of the children of Heth: there was he buried, and Sara his wife.

text.NRSV: After the death of Abraham God blessed his son Isaac. And Isaac settled at Beer-lahai-roi.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after the death of Abraham, that God blessed Isaac his son,—and Isaac dwelt by Beer-lahai-roi.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe after the death of Abraham, that God blessed his sonne Isaac, and Isaac dwelt by the well Lahai-roi.
text.NABRE: After the death of Abraham, God blessed his son Isaac, who lived near Beer-lahai-roi.
text.DRB: And after his death God blessed Isaac his son, who dwelled beside the well of the Living & Seeing, so named.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Ishmael, Abraham’s son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s slave-girl, bore to Abraham.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the generations of Ishmael son of Abraham,—whom hagar the Egyptian woman the handmaid of Sarah bare to Abraham;
text.KJV: ¶ Now these are the generations of Ishmael Abrahams sonne, whom Hagar the Egyptian Sarahs handmayd, bare vnto Abraham:
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Abraham’s son Ishmael, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s slave, bore to Abraham.
text.DRB: These are the generations of Ismael the son of Abraham, whom Agar the Ægyptian bare him, Sara's servant: &

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, named in the order of their birth: Nebaioth, the firstborn of Ishmael; and Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text.Rotherham: and these, are the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names after their generations,—the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth, and Kedar and Adbeel and Mibsam;
text.KJV: And these are the names of the sonnes of Ishmael, by their names, according to their generations; The first borne of Ishmael, Nebaioth, and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam,
text.NABRE: These are the names of Ishmael’s sons, listed in the order of their birth: Ishmael’s firstborn Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text.DRB: These are the names of his children according to their calling and generations. The first begotten of Ishmael Nabajoth, then Cedar, and Adbeel, and Mabsam,

text.NRSV: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text.Rotherham: and Mishma and Dumah, and Massa;
text.KJV: And Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,
text.NABRE: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text.DRB: Masma also, and Duma, and Massa,

text.NRSV: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text.Rotherham: Hadad, and Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah:
text.KJV: Hadar, and Tema, Ietur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text.NABRE: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text.DRB: Hadar, and Thema, and Iethur, and Naphis, and Cedma.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Ishmael and these are their names, by their villages and by their encampments, twelve princes according to their tribes.
text.Rotherham: these, same, are the sons of Ishmael and these, their names, by their villages and by their encampments,—twelve princes, after their tribes.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Ishmael, and these are their names, by their townes and by their castels; twelue princes according to their nations.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Ishmael, their names by their villages and encampments; twelve chieftains of as many tribal groups.
text.DRB: These are the sons of Ismael: and these are their names by their castles and towns, twelve Princes of their tribes.

text.NRSV: (This is the length of the life of Ishmael, one hundred thirty-seven years; he breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people.)
text.Rotherham: And these, are the years of the life of Ishmael, a hundred and thirty-seven years,—and he breathed his last and died, and was gathered unto his people.
text.KJV: And these are the yeeres of the life of Ishmael; an hundred and thirty and seuen yeeres: and he gaue vp the ghost and died, and was gathered vnto his people.
text.NABRE: The span of Ishmael’s life was one hundred and thirty-seven years. After he had breathed his last and died, he was gathered to his people.
text.DRB: And the years of Ismael's life came to an hundred thirty seven, and decaying died, and was put unto his people.

text.NRSV: They settled from Havilah to Shur, which is opposite Egypt in the direction of Assyria; he settled down alongside of all his people.
text.Rotherham: And they stretched their habitations from Havilah as far as to Shur, which is over against Egypt, as thou goest in towards Assyria,—over against all his brethren, he settled down.
text.KJV: And they dwelt from Hauilah vnto Shur, that is before Egypt, as thou goest towards Assyria: and hee died in the presence of all his brethren.
text.NABRE: The Ishmaelites ranged from Havilah, by Shur, which is on the border of Egypt, all the way to Asshur; and they pitched camp alongside their various kindred.
text.DRB: And he dwelt from Hevila even to Sur, which looketh towards Ægypt, as they enter to the Assyrians, before the face of all his brethren died he.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Isaac, Abraham’s son: Abraham was the father of Isaac,
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the generations of Isaac, son of Abraham,—Abraham, begat Isaac;
text.KJV: ¶ And these are the generations of Isaac, Abrahams sonne: Abraham begate Isaac.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Isaac, son of Abraham; Abraham begot Isaac.
text.DRB: These also are the generations of Isaac the son of Abraham: Abraham begat Isaac:

text.NRSV: and Isaac was forty years old when he married Rebekah, daughter of Bethuel the Aramean of Paddan-aram, sister of Laban the Aramean.
text.Rotherham: and it came to pass that, Isaac, was forty years old, when he took Rebekah daughter of Bethuel the Syrian, of the Plain of Syria,—sister of Laban the Syrian. to himself to wife.
text.KJV: And Isaac was fortie yeeres old when hee tooke Rebekah to wife, the daughter of Bethuel the Syrian of Padan Aram, the sister to Laban the Syrian.
text.NABRE: Isaac was forty years old when he married Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean of Paddan-aram and the sister of Laban the Aramean.
text.DRB: Who when he was forty years old, took to wife Rebecca the daughter of Bathuel the Syrian of Mesopotamia, sister to Laban.

text.NRSV: Isaac prayed to the Lord for his wife, because she was barren; and the Lord granted his prayer, and his wife Rebekah conceived.
text.Rotherham: So then Isaac made entreaty unto Yahweh in behalf of his wife, for she was, barren,—and Yahweh suffered himself to be entreated by him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
text.KJV: And Isaac intreated the LORD for his wife, because she was barren: and the LORD was intreated of him, and Rebekah his wife conceiued.
text.NABRE: Isaac entreated the Lord on behalf of his wife, since she was sterile. The Lord heard his entreaty, and his wife Rebekah became pregnant.
text.DRB: And Isaac besought our Lord for his wife, because she was barren: who heard him, and made Rebecca to conceive.

text.NRSV: The children struggled together within her; and she said, “If it is to be this way, why do I live?” So she went to inquire of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the sons within her struggled together, so she said—If so, wherefore now am, I, [thus] ? And she went to seek Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the children struggled together within her; and she said, If it be so, why am I thus? and shee went to enquire of the LORD.
text.NABRE: But the children jostled each other in the womb so much that she exclaimed, “If it is like this, why go on living!” She went to consult the Lord,
text.DRB: But the little ones struggled in her womb; who said: If it should be so with me, what need was there to conceive? And she went to consult our Lord.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to her, “Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples born of you shall be divided; the one shall be stronger than the other, the elder shall serve the younger.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said to her. Two nations, are in thy womb, And two races, from thy body, shall be parted,—And, one race, shall be stronger, than the other race, And the elder, shall serve the younger.
text.KJV: And the LORD said vnto her, Two nations are in thy wombe, and two maner of people shall be separated from thy bowels: and the one people shalbe stronger then the other people: and the elder shall serue the yonger.
text.NABRE: and the Lord answered her: Two nations are in your womb, two peoples are separating while still within you; But one will be stronger than the other, and the older will serve the younger.
text.DRB: Who answering said: Two nations are in thy womb, and two peoples shall be divided out of thy womb, and one people shall overcome the other, and the elder shall serve the younger.

text.NRSV: When her time to give birth was at hand, there were twins in her womb.
text.Rotherham: Then were fulfilled her days to bring forth,—and lo! twins in her womb.
text.KJV: ¶ And when her dayes to be deliuered were fulfilled, behold, there were twinnes in her wombe.
text.NABRE: When the time of her delivery came, there were twins in her womb.
text.DRB: Now her time was come to be delivered, and behold twins were found in her womb.

text.NRSV: The first came out red, all his body like a hairy mantle; so they named him Esau.
text.Rotherham: And the first came forth red, all over, as a mantle of hair,—so they called his name, Esau,
text.KJV: And the first came out red, all ouer like an hairy garment: and they called his name, Esau.
text.NABRE: The first to emerge was reddish, and his whole body was like a hairy mantle; so they named him Esau.
text.DRB: He that came forth first, was red, and all hairy in manner of a skin: and his name was called Esau. Immediately the other coming forth, held his brother's plant in his hand: and therefore he called him Jacob.

text.NRSV: Afterward his brother came out, with his hand gripping Esau’s heel; so he was named Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.
text.Rotherham: And, after that, came forth his brother with his hand fast hold of the heel of Esau, so they called his name Jacob—Now, Isaac, was sixty years old when she bare them,
text.KJV: And after that came his brother out, and his hand tooke holde on Esaus heele; and his name was called Iacob: and Isaac was threescore yeres old, when shee bare them.
text.NABRE: Next his brother came out, gripping Esau’s heel; so he was named Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when they were born.
text.DRB: Threescore years old was Isaac, when the little ones were born unto him.

text.NRSV: When the boys grew up, Esau was a skillful hunter, a man of the field, while Jacob was a quiet man, living in tents.
text.Rotherham: And when the youths grew up, it came to pass that Esau was a man skilled in game a man of the field,—but, Jacob, was a ready man, dwelling in tents.
text.KJV: And the boyes grew; and Esau was a cunning hunter, a man of the fielde: and Iacob was a plaine man, dwelling in tents.
text.NABRE: When the boys grew up, Esau became a skillful hunter, a man of the open country; whereas Jacob was a simple man, who stayed among the tents.
text.DRB: Who being grown up, Esau became a man cunning in hunting, & a husband man: but Jacob a plain man dwelled in tents:

text.NRSV: Isaac loved Esau, because he was fond of game; but Rebekah loved Jacob.
text.Rotherham: And, Isaac, loved, Esau, because of the game he put in his mouth,—but, Rebekah, was a lover of Jacob!.
text.KJV: And Isaac loued Esau, because he did eate of his venison: but Rebekah loued Iacob.
text.NABRE: Isaac preferred Esau, because he was fond of game; but Rebekah preferred Jacob.
text.DRB: Isaac loved Esau, because he did eat of his hunting: and Rebecca loved Jacob.

text.NRSV: Once when Jacob was cooking a stew, Esau came in from the field, and he was famished.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob had boiled pottage,—when Esau came in from the field, he, being famished.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob sod pottage: and Esau came from the field, and hee was faint.
text.NABRE: Once, when Jacob was cooking a stew, Esau came in from the open country, famished.
text.DRB: & Jacob boiled broth: to whom Esau being come faint out of the field,

text.NRSV: Esau said to Jacob, “Let me eat some of that red stuff, for I am famished!” (Therefore he was called Edom.)
text.Rotherham: So Esau said unto Jacob. Do let me devour some of the red—this red, for, famished, I am. For this cause, was his name called Edom.
text.KJV: And Esau said to Iacob, Feed me, I pray thee, with that same red pottage: for I am faint; therefore was his name called Edom.
text.NABRE: He said to Jacob, “Let me gulp down some of that red stuff; I am famished.” That is why he was called Edom.
text.DRB: said: Give me of this red broth, because I am exceeding faint. For which cause his name was called Edom.

text.NRSV: Jacob said, “First sell me your birthright.”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said,—Come sell, just now, thy birthright unto me.
text.KJV: And Iacob said, Sell me this day thy birthright.
text.NABRE: But Jacob replied, “First sell me your right as firstborn.”
text.DRB: To whom Jacob said: sell me thy first-birth-right.

text.NRSV: Esau said, “I am about to die; of what use is a birthright to me?”
text.Rotherham: And Esau said, Here am I, on the point of dying,—wherefore, then, should I, have, a birthright?
text.KJV: And Esau said, Behold, I am at the point to die: and what profit shall this birthright doe to me?
text.NABRE: “Look,” said Esau, “I am on the point of dying. What good is the right as firstborn to me?”
text.DRB: He answered, Lo I die, what will the first-birth-right avail me?

text.NRSV: Jacob said, “Swear to me first.” So he swore to him, and sold his birthright to Jacob.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said, Come swear to me just now! And he sware to him,—and sold his birthright to Jacob.
text.KJV: And Iacob said, Sweare to mee this day: and he sware to him: and he sold his birthright vnto Iacob.
text.NABRE: But Jacob said, “Swear to me first!” So he sold Jacob his right as firstborn under oath.
text.DRB: Jacob said: Swear therefore to me. Esau sware to him, and sold his first-birth-right.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew, and he ate and drank, and rose and went his way. Thus Esau despised his birthright.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob, having given to Esau bread and a dish of lentils, he did eat and drink and rose up, and went his way,—thus Esau despised his birthright!
text.KJV: Then Iacob gaue Esau bread and pottage of lentiles; and he did eate and drinke, and rose up, and went his way: thus Esau despised his birthright.
text.NABRE: Jacob then gave him some bread and the lentil stew; and Esau ate, drank, got up, and went his way. So Esau treated his right as firstborn with disdain.
text.DRB: And so taking bread and the rice broth, did eat, and drink, and went his way; little esteeming that he had sold his first-birth-right.

text.NRSV: Now there was a famine in the land, besides the former famine that had occurred in the days of Abraham. And Isaac went to Gerar, to King Abimelech of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And there came to be a famine in the land, besides the first famine which happened in the days of Abraham,—so Isaac went his way unto Abimelech, king of the Philistines towards Gerar;
text.KJV: And there was a famine in the land, besides the first famine that was in the dayes of Abraham. And Isaac went vnto Abimelech King of the Philistims, vnto Gerar.
text.NABRE: There was a famine in the land, distinct from the earlier one that had occurred in the days of Abraham, and Isaac went down to Abimelech, king of the Philistines in Gerar.
text.DRB: And when a famine was risen in the land, after that sterility that had chanced in the days of Abraham, Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Palestines into Gerara.

text.NRSV: The Lord appeared to Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt; settle in the land that I shall show you.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh appeared unto him, and said, do not go down towards Egypt,—Make thy habitation in the land, which I may name unto thee:
text.KJV: And the LORD appeared vnto him and said, Goe not downe into Egypt; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of.
text.NABRE: The Lord appeared to him and said: Do not go down to Egypt, but camp in this land wherever I tell you.
text.DRB: And our Lord appeared to him, and said: Go not down into Ægypt, but rest in the land which I shall tell thee.

text.NRSV: Reside in this land as an alien, and I will be with you, and will bless you; for to you and to your descendants I will give all these lands, and I will fulfill the oath that I swore to your father Abraham.
text.Rotherham: Sojourn in this land, that I may be with thee and bless thee,—for, to thee, and to thy seed, will I give all these lands, So will I establish the oath which I sware to Abraham thy father;
text.KJV: Soiourne in this land, and I wil be with thee, and will blesse thee: for vnto thee, and vnto thy seed I will giue all these countreys, and I wil performe the othe, which I sware vnto Abraham thy father.
text.NABRE: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and bless you; for to you and your descendants I will give all these lands, in fulfillment of the oath that I swore to your father Abraham.
text.DRB: And sojourn in it, and I will be with thee, and will bless thee: for to thee and to thy seed I will give all these countries, accomplishing the oath which I sware to Abraham thy father.

text.NRSV: I will make your offspring as numerous as the stars of heaven, and will give to your offspring all these lands; and all the nations of the earth shall gain blessing for themselves through your offspring,
text.Rotherham: and will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heavens, and will give to thy seed, all these lands,—And all the nations of the earth shall bless themselves in thy seed:
text.KJV: And I wil make thy seed to multiply as the starres of heauen, and will giue vnto thy seed all these countreys: and in thy Seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed:
text.NABRE: I will make your descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky, and I will give them all these lands, and in your descendants all the nations of the earth will find blessing—
text.DRB: And I will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven: and I will give to thy posterity all these countries: and in thy seed Shall be Blessed All The Nations of The Earth,

text.NRSV: because Abraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.”
text.Rotherham: Because that Abraham hearkened unto my voice,—and so kept my charge, my commandments my statutes and my laws.
text.KJV: Because that Abraham obeyed my voyce, and kept my charge, my Commandements, my Statutes and my Lawes.
text.NABRE: this because Abraham obeyed me, keeping my mandate, my commandments, my ordinances, and my instructions.
text.DRB: for because Abraham obeyed my voice, & kept my precepts and commandments, and observed my ceremonies and laws.

text.NRSV: So Isaac settled in Gerar.
text.Rotherham: So Isaac dwelt in Gerar.
text.KJV: ¶ And Isaac dwelt in Gerar.
text.NABRE: So Isaac settled in Gerar.
text.DRB: Therefore Isaac abode in Gerara.

text.NRSV: When the men of the place asked him about his wife, he said, “She is my sister”; for he was afraid to say, “My wife,” thinking, “or else the men of the place might kill me for the sake of Rebekah, because she is attractive in appearance.”
text.Rotherham: Then asked the men of the place as to his wife, and he said, My sister, is she,—For he feared to say My wife, lest [said he] the men of the place should slay me on account of Rebekah, for fair to look on, she is.
text.KJV: And the men of the place asked him of his wife: and he said, She is my sister: for he feared to say, She is my wife; lest, said he, the men of the place should kill me for Rebekah, because shee was faire to looke vpon.
text.NABRE: When the men of the place asked questions about his wife, he answered, “She is my sister.” He was afraid that, if he called her his wife, the men of the place would kill him on account of Rebekah, since she was beautiful.
text.DRB: Who when he was asked by the men of that place concerning his wife, answered: She is my sister; for he was afraid to confess that she was married to him, thinking lest peradventure they would kill him because of her beauty.

text.NRSV: When Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech of the Philistines looked out of a window and saw him fondling his wife Rebekah.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when the days had lengthened out to him there, that Abimelech king of the Philistines, looked out through the lattice of a window,—and he beheld and lo! Isaac—laughing with Rebekah his wife.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when he had bene there a long time, that Abimelech king of the Philistims looked out at a window, and saw, and behold, Isaac was sporting with Rebekah his wife.
text.NABRE: But when they had been there for a long time, Abimelech, king of the Philistines, looked out of a window and saw Isaac fondling his wife Rebekah.
text.DRB: And when very many days were passed, & he abode there, Abimelech the king of the Palestines looking forth through a window saw him sporting with Rebecca his wife.

text.NRSV: So Abimelech called for Isaac, and said, “So she is your wife! Why then did you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac said to him, “Because I thought I might die because of her.”
text.Rotherham: So Abimelech called for Isaac, and said, But lo she is, thy wife! How then, saidst thou, She is, my sister? And Isaac said unto him, Because, I said, Lest I die on her account.
text.KJV: And Abimelech called Isaac and said, Behold, of a suretie she is thy wife: and how saidst thou, She is my sister? And Isaac said vnto him, Because I said, Lest I die for her.
text.NABRE: He called for Isaac and said: “She must certainly be your wife! How could you have said, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac replied, “I thought I might lose my life on her account.”
text.DRB: And calling for him, he said: it is evident that she is thy wife: why didst thou feign her to be thy sister? He answered: I feared lest I should die for her.

text.NRSV: Abimelech said, “What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us.”
text.Rotherham: Then, said, Abimelech, What is this thou hast done to us? A little more, and one of the people might have lien with thy wife, so shouldst thou have brought upon us, guilt.
text.KJV: And Abimelech said, What is this thou hast done vnto vs? one of the people might lightly haue lien with thy wife, and thou shouldest haue brought guiltinesse vpon vs.
text.NABRE: “How could you have done this to us!” exclaimed Abimelech. “It would have taken very little for one of the people to lie with your wife, and so you would have brought guilt upon us!”
text.DRB: And Abimelech said: Why hast thou deceived us? Some man of the people might have lien with thy wife, and thou hadst brought upon us a great sin. And he commanded all the people, saying:

text.NRSV: So Abimelech warned all the people, saying, “Whoever touches this man or his wife shall be put to death.”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech commanded all the people saying,—He that toucheth this man or his wife, shall surely be put to death
text.KJV: And Abimelech charged all his people, saying, Hee that toucheth this man or his wife, shall surely bee put to death.
text.NABRE: Abimelech then commanded all the people: “Anyone who maltreats this man or his wife shall be put to death.”
text.DRB: He that shall touch this man's wife, dying shall die.

text.NRSV: Isaac sowed seed in that land, and in the same year reaped a hundredfold. The Lord blessed him,
text.Rotherham: And Isaac sowed in that land, and found in the same year, a hundredfold,—seeing that Yahweh had blessed him.
text.KJV: Then Isaac sowed in that land, and receiued in the same yeere an hundred fold: & the LORD blessed him.
text.NABRE: Isaac sowed a crop in that region and reaped a hundredfold the same year. Since the Lord blessed him,
text.DRB: And Isaac sowed in that land, and he found that same year and hundred fold: and our Lord blessed him.

text.NRSV: and the man became rich; he prospered more and more until he became very wealthy.
text.Rotherham: So the man waxed great,—and went on and on waxing great until that he had waxed exceeding great.
text.KJV: And the man waxed great, and went forward, and grew vntill he became very great.
text.NABRE: he became richer and richer all the time, until he was very wealthy.
text.DRB: And the man was made rich, and he went prospering and increasing, till he was made exceeding great:

text.NRSV: He had possessions of flocks and herds, and a great household, so that the Philistines envied him.
text.Rotherham: So he came to have possessions of flocks and possessions of herds, and a large body of servants,—and the Philistines envied him.
text.KJV: For he had possession of flocks, and possession of heards, and great store of seruants, and the Philistims enuied him.
text.NABRE: He acquired flocks and herds, and a great work force, and so the Philistines became envious of him.
text.DRB: and he had also possessions of sheep and of herds, and a very great family. For this the Palestines envying him,

text.NRSV: (Now the Philistines had stopped up and filled with earth all the wells that his father’s servants had dug in the days of his father Abraham.)
text.Rotherham: And as for all the wells which the servants of his father digged, in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines stopped them up, and filled them with dust.
text.KJV: For all the wels which his fathers seruants had digged in the dayes of Abraham his father, the Philistims had stopped them, & filled them with earth.
text.NABRE: The Philistines had stopped up and filled with dirt all the wells that his father’s servants had dug back in the days of his father Abraham.
text.DRB: stopped at that time all the wells, that the servants of his father Abraham had digged, filling them up with earth:

text.NRSV: And Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us; you have become too powerful for us.”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said unto Isaac,—Get thee from us, for thou hast become far mightier than we.
text.KJV: And Abimelech said vnto Isaac, Goe from vs: for thou art much mightier then we.
text.NABRE: So Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us; you have become far too numerous for us.”
text.DRB: in so much that Abimelech himself said to Isaac: Depart from us, because thou art become mightier than we a great deal.
