text.NRSV: So Isaac departed from there and camped in the valley of Gerar and settled there.
text.Rotherham: So Isaac went from thence,—and encamped in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.
text.KJV: ¶ And Isaac departed thence, and pitched his tent in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.
text.NABRE: Isaac left there and camped in the Wadi Gerar where he stayed.
text.DRB: And departing, to come to the Torrent of Gerara, and to dwell there,

text.NRSV: Isaac dug again the wells of water that had been dug in the days of his father Abraham; for the Philistines had stopped them up after the death of Abraham; and he gave them the names that his father had given them.
text.Rotherham: And Isaac again digged the wells of water which they had digged in the days of Abraham his father, and which the Philistines stopped up after the death of Abraham,—and he called their names, after the names which his father called them.
text.KJV: And Isaac digged againe the wels of water, which they had digged in the dayes of Abraham his father: for the Philistims had stopped them after the death of Abraham, and he called their names after the names by which his father had called them.
text.NABRE: Isaac reopened the wells which his father’s servants had dug back in the days of his father Abraham and which the Philistines had stopped up after Abraham’s death; he gave them names like those that his father had given them.
text.DRB: again he digged other wells, which the servants of his father Abraham had digged, and which after his death the Philistines had stopped up of old: & he called them by the same names which his father before had called them.

text.NRSV: But when Isaac’s servants dug in the valley and found there a well of spring water,
text.Rotherham: And Isaac’s servants digged in the valley,—and found there a well of living water.
text.KJV: And Isaacs seruants digged in the valley, and found there a well of springing water.
text.NABRE: But when Isaac’s servants dug in the wadi and reached spring water in their well,
text.DRB: And they digged in the Torrent, and found living water:

text.NRSV: the herders of Gerar quarreled with Isaac’s herders, saying, “The water is ours.” So he called the well Esek, because they contended with him.
text.Rotherham: And the herdmen of Gerar disputed with the herdmen of Isaac saying, Ours, is the, water! So he called the name of the well Esek, because they had stirred up a quarrel with him.
text.KJV: And the heardmen of Gerar did striue with Isaacs heardmen, saying, The water is ours; and hee called the name of the well, Esek, because they stroue with him.
text.NABRE: the shepherds of Gerar argued with Isaac’s shepherds, saying, “The water belongs to us!” So he named the well Esek, because they had quarreled there.
text.DRB: but there also the pastors of Gerara made a brawl against the pastors of Isaac, saying: it is our water. For which cause he called the name of the well, by occasion of that which had happened, Calumny.

text.NRSV: Then they dug another well, and they quarreled over that one also; so he called it Sitnah.
text.Rotherham: And they digged another well, and they disputed over that also,—so he called the name thereof Sitnah.
text.KJV: And they digged another well, and stroue for that also: and hee called the name of it, Sitnah.
text.NABRE: Then they dug another well, and they argued over that one too; so he named it Sitnah.
text.DRB: And they digged also another; and for that they brawled likewise, and he called the name of it, Enmity.

text.NRSV: He moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it; so he called it Rehoboth, saying, “Now the Lord has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.”
text.Rotherham: And he moved on from thence and digged another well, and they disputed not for it,—so he called the name thereof. Rehoboth, and said, For, now, hath Yahweh made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.
text.KJV: And he remoued from thence, and digged another well, and for that they stroue not: and he called the name of it Rehoboth: and he said, For now the LORD hath made roome for vs, and we shall be fruitfull in the land.
text.NABRE: So he moved on from there and dug still another well, but over this one they did not argue. He named it Rehoboth, and said, “Because the Lord has now given us ample room, we shall flourish in the land.”
text.DRB: Going forward from thence he digged another well, for which they contended not: therefore he called the name thereof, Latitude, saying: Now hath our Lord dilated us, and made us to increase upon the earth.

text.NRSV: From there he went up to Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: And he went up from thence, to Beer-sheba.
text.KJV: And he went vp from thence to Beer-sheba.
text.NABRE: From there Isaac went up to Beer-sheba.
text.DRB: And he went up from that place unto Bersabee,

text.NRSV: And that very night the Lord appeared to him and said, “I am the God of your father Abraham; do not be afraid, for I am with you and will bless you and make your offspring numerous for my servant Abraham’s sake.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appeared unto him the same night, and said, I, am the God of Abraham thy father,—Do not fear for with thee, am, I, And I will bless thee and multiply thy seed, For the sake of Abraham my servant.
text.KJV: And the LORD appeared vnto him the same night, and saide, I am the God of Abraham thy father: feare not, for I am with thee, and will blesse thee, and multiply thy seede, for my seruant Abrahams sake.
text.NABRE: The same night the Lord appeared to him and said: I am the God of Abraham, your father. Do not fear, for I am with you. I will bless you and multiply your descendants for the sake of Abraham, my servant.
text.DRB: Where our Lord appeared to him that same night, saying: I am the God of Abraham thy father, do not fear, because I am with thee: I will bless thee, and multiply thy seed for my servant Abraham's sake.

text.NRSV: So he built an altar there, called on the name of the Lord, and pitched his tent there. And there Isaac’s servants dug a well.
text.Rotherham: So he builded there all altar and called on the name of Yahweh, and spread out there, his tent,—and the servants of Isaac cut out there a well.
text.KJV: And he builded an altar there, and called vpon the name of the LORD, and pitched his tent there: and there Isaacs seruants digged a well.
text.NABRE: So Isaac built an altar there and invoked the Lord by name. After he had pitched his tent there, Isaac’s servants began to dig a well nearby.
text.DRB: Therefore he builded there an altar: and having called upon the name of our Lord, he pitched his tent: & commanded his servants that they should dig a well.

text.NRSV: Then Abimelech went to him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his adviser and Phicol the commander of his army.
text.Rotherham: And, Abimelech, came unto him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his friend, and Phicol, commander of his host.
text.KJV: ¶ Then Abimelech went to him from Gerar, and Ahuzzath one of his friends, and Phichol the chiefe captaine of his armie.
text.NABRE: Then Abimelech came to him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath, his councilor, and Phicol, the general of his army.
text.DRB: To the which place when there were come from Gerara Abimelech, and Ocozath his friend, & Phicol chief Captain of his soldiers,

text.NRSV: Isaac said to them, “Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?”
text.Rotherham: And Isaac said unto them, Wherefore have ye come in mite me, seeing that, ye, hated me, and sent me away from you?
text.KJV: And Isaac saide vnto them, Wherefore come ye to me, seeing ye hate me, and haue sent me away from you?
text.NABRE: Isaac asked them, “Why have you come to me, since you hate me and have driven me away from you?”
text.DRB: Isaac spake to them: Why are ye come to me a man whom you hated, and have thrust out from you?

text.NRSV: They said, “We see plainly that the Lord has been with you; so we say, let there be an oath between you and us, and let us make a covenant with you
text.Rotherham: And they said: We, saw plainly, that Yahweh was with thee, so we said, Let there be we pray thee an oath betwixt us both, betwixt us and thee,—and let us solemnise a covenant with thee:—
text.KJV: And they said, we saw certainly that the LORD was with thee: and wee said, Let there be now an othe betwixt vs, euen betwixt vs and thee, and let vs make a couenant with thee,
text.NABRE: They answered: “We clearly see that the Lord has been with you, so we thought: let there be a sworn agreement between our two sides—between you and us. Let us make a covenant with you:
text.DRB: Who answered: We saw that the Lord is with thee, and therefore we said: Let there be an oath between us, and let us make a league,

text.NRSV: so that you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you and have done to you nothing but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: That, thou wilt not do wrong by us, according as we touched not thee, and according as we did by thee nothing but good, and then sent thee away in peace, thee who, now, art blessed of Yahweh!
text.KJV: That thou wilt doe vs no hurt, as we haue not touched thee, and as we haue done vnto thee nothing but good, and haue sent thee away in peace: thou art now the blessed of the LORD.
text.NABRE: you shall do no harm to us, just as we have not maltreated you, but have always acted kindly toward you and have let you depart in peace. So now, may you be blessed by the Lord!”
text.DRB: that thou do us no harm, as we also have touched nothing of thine, neither have we done that which might hurt thee: but with peace have we dismissed thee increased with the blessing of the Lord.

text.NRSV: So he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.
text.Rotherham: So he made for them a banquet, and they did eat and drink,—
text.KJV: And he made them a feast, and they did eate and drinke.
text.NABRE: Isaac then made a feast for them, and they ate and drank.
text.DRB: Therefore he made them a feast, and after they had eaten and drunken,

text.NRSV: In the morning they rose early and exchanged oaths; and Isaac set them on their way, and they departed from him in peace.
text.Rotherham: and rose up early in the morning, and took an oath each man to his brother, so Isaac let them go, and they departed from him in peace.
text.KJV: And they rose vp betimes in the morning, and sware one to another: and Isaac sent them away, and they departed from him in peace.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning they exchanged oaths. Then Isaac sent them on their way, and they departed from him in peace.
text.DRB: Arising in the morning, they sware one to another: and Isaac dismissed them peaceably into their place.

text.NRSV: That same day Isaac’s servants came and told him about the well that they had dug, and said to him, “We have found water!”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the same day, that the servants of Isaac came in, and told him concerning the well which they had digged, and said to him, We have found water!
text.KJV: And it came to passe the same day, that Isaacs seruants came, and tolde him concerning the well which they had digged, and said vnto him, we haue found water.
text.NABRE: That same day Isaac’s servants came and informed him about the well they had been digging; they told him, “We have reached water!”
text.DRB: And behold the same day came the servants of Isaac telling him of a well, which they had digged, and saying: We have found water.

text.NRSV: He called it Shibah; therefore the name of the city is Beer-sheba to this day.
text.Rotherham: So he called it, Shibah. For this cause, hath the name of the city, been Beer-sheba, unto this day.
text.KJV: And he called it Shebah: therefore the name of the citie is Beer-sheba vnto this day.
text.NABRE: He called it Shibah; hence the name of the city is Beer-sheba to this day.
text.DRB: Whereupon he called it Abundance: and the name of the city was given Bersabee, even unto this present day.

text.NRSV: When Esau was forty years old, he married Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite;
text.Rotherham: And when Esau was forty years old, he took to wife Judith, daughter of Beeri. the Hittite; and Basemath, daughter of Elon, the Hittite;
text.KJV: ¶ And Esau was forty yeeres old, when he tooke to wife Iudith, the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Bashemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite:
text.NABRE: When Esau was forty years old, he married Judith, daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath, daughter of Elon the Hivite.
text.DRB: But Esau being forty years old married wives, Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hethite, and Basemath the daughter of Elon of the same place:

text.NRSV: and they made life bitter for Isaac and Rebekah.
text.Rotherham: and they became a bitterness of spirit, to Isaac and to Rebekah.
text.KJV: Which were a griefe of minde vnto Isaac and to Rebekah.
text.NABRE: But they became a source of bitterness to Isaac and Rebekah.
text.DRB: Both which had offended the mind of Isaac and Rebecca.

text.NRSV: When Isaac was old and his eyes were dim so that he could not see, he called his elder son Esau and said to him, “My son”; and he answered, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that Isaac, was old, and his eyes became too dim to see, so he called Esau his elder son and said unto him My son! And he said unto him, Behold me!
text.KJV: And it came to passe that when Isaac was old, and his eyes were dimme, so that he could not see, hee called Esau his eldest son, and said vnto him, My sonne. And hee said vnto him, Behold, here am I.
text.NABRE: When Isaac was so old that his eyesight had failed him, he called his older son Esau and said to him, “My son!” “Here I am!” he replied.
text.DRB: And Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, and he could not see: and he called Esau his elder son, and said to him: My son? Who answered: Here I am.

text.NRSV: He said, “See, I am old; I do not know the day of my death.
text.Rotherham: And he said, Behold, I pray thee, I am old,—I know not the day of my death.
text.KJV: And he said, Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death.
text.NABRE: Isaac then said, “Now I have grown old. I do not know when I might die.
text.DRB: To whom his father: Thou seest, quoth he, that I am old, and know not the day of my death.

text.NRSV: Now then, take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field, and hunt game for me.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, take I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow, and go out to the field, and catch for me game;
text.KJV: Now therefore take, I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiuer, and thy bow, and goe out to the field, and take mee some venison.
text.NABRE: So now take your hunting gear—your quiver and bow—and go out into the open country to hunt some game for me.
text.DRB: Take thy instruments, thy quiver, and bow, and go abroad: and when thou hast taken any thing by hunting,

text.NRSV: Then prepare for me savory food, such as I like, and bring it to me to eat, so that I may bless you before I die.”
text.Rotherham: and make for me dainty meats, such as I love and bring in to me, that I may eat,—To the end my soul may bless thee, ere yet I die!
text.KJV: And make me sauoury meat, such as I loue, and bring it to mee, that I may eate, that my soule may blesse thee before I die.
text.NABRE: Then prepare for me a dish in the way I like, and bring it to me to eat, so that I may bless you before I die.”
text.DRB: make me broth thereof, as thou knowest I like, and bring that I may eat, and my soul may bless thee before I die.

text.NRSV: Now Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. So when Esau went to the field to hunt for game and bring it,
text.Rotherham: Now, Rebekah, was hearkening, when Isaac spake unto Esau his son, and Esau went his way to the field, to catch game, to bring in.
text.KJV: And Rebekah heard when Isaac spake to Esau his sonne: and Esau went to the fielde to hunt for venison, and to bring it.
text.NABRE: Rebekah had been listening while Isaac was speaking to his son Esau. So when Esau went out into the open country to hunt some game for his father,
text.DRB: Which when Rebecca had heard, and he was gone into the field to fulfill his father's commandment,

text.NRSV: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, “I heard your father say to your brother Esau,
text.Rotherham: Rebekah, therefore spake unto Jacob her son saying,—Lo! I heard thy father, speaking unto Esau thy brother, saying,
text.KJV: ¶ And Rebekah spake vnto Iacob her sonne, saying, Behold, I heard thy father speake vnto Esau thy brother, saying,
text.NABRE: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, “Listen! I heard your father tell your brother Esau,
text.DRB: she said to her son Jacob: I heard thy father talking with Esau thy brother, and saying to him:

text.NRSV: ‘Bring me game, and prepare for me savory food to eat, that I may bless you before the Lord before I die.’
text.Rotherham: Bring in for me game and make for me dainty meats, that I may eat; and let me bless thee in the presence of Yahweh before my death.
text.KJV: Bring me venison, and make mee sauoury meat, that I may eate, and blesse thee before the LORD, before my death.
text.NABRE: ‘Bring me some game and prepare a dish for me to eat, that I may bless you with the Lord’s approval before I die.’
text.DRB: Bring me of thy hunting, and make me meats that I may eat, and bless thee in the sight of our Lord before I die.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, my son, obey my word as I command you.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore my son, hearken unto my voice,—in that which I am commanding thee:
text.KJV: Now therefore, my sonne, obey my voyce, according to that which I command thee.
text.NABRE: Now, my son, obey me in what I am about to order you.
text.DRB: Now therefore, my son, assent to my counsel:

text.NRSV: Go to the flock, and get me two choice kids, so that I may prepare from them savory food for your father, such as he likes;
text.Rotherham: Go, I pray thee, unto the flock, and fetch me from thence two kids of the goats, fine ones,—that I may make of them dainty meats for thy father such as he loveth:
text.KJV: Goe now to the flocke, and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goates, and I will make them sauoury meat for thy father, such as he loueth.
text.NABRE: Go to the flock and get me two choice young goats so that with these I might prepare a dish for your father in the way he likes.
text.DRB: and go thy way to the flock, bring me two kids of the best, that I may make of them meat for thy father, such as he gladly eateth:

text.NRSV: and you shall take it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies.”
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou take them in unto thy father and he shall eat,—To the end he may bless thee before his death.
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring it to thy father, that he may eate, and that he may blesse thee, before his death.
text.NABRE: Then bring it to your father to eat, that he may bless you before he dies.”
text.DRB: Which when thou hast brought in, and he hath eaten, he may bless thee before he die.

text.NRSV: But Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, “Look, my brother Esau is a hairy man, and I am a man of smooth skin.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said unto Rebekah his mother, Lo! Esau my brother, is a hairy man, whereas, I, am a smooth man:
text.KJV: And Iacob said to Rebekah his mother, Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.
text.NABRE: But Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, “But my brother Esau is a hairy man and I am smooth-skinned!
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Thou knowest that Esau my brother is an hairy man, and I am smooth:

text.NRSV: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be mocking him, and bring a curse on myself and not a blessing.”
text.Rotherham: peradventure my father might feel me, then should I be in his eyes as one that mocketh,—and should bring upon myself a reproach, and not a blessing!
text.KJV: My father peraduenture will feele me, and I shall seeme to him as a deceiuer, and I shall bring a curse vpon me, and not a blessing.
text.NABRE: Suppose my father feels me? He will think I am making fun of him, and I will bring on myself a curse instead of a blessing.”
text.DRB: if my father shall feel me, and perceive it, I fear lest he will think I would have deluded him, and I shall bring upon me a curse for a blessing.

text.NRSV: His mother said to him, “Let your curse be on me, my son; only obey my word, and go, get them for me.”
text.Rotherham: And his mother said to him, Upon me, be thy reproach my son,—only hearken unto my voice and go fetch [them] for me.
text.KJV: And his mother said vnto him, Upon me be thy curse, my sonne: onely obey my voice, and go fetch me them.
text.NABRE: His mother, however, replied: “Let any curse against you, my son, fall on me! Just obey me. Go and get me the young goats.”
text.DRB: To whom his mother said: This curse, my son, light upon me, only hear thou my voice, and go, fetch me the things which I have said.

text.NRSV: So he went and got them and brought them to his mother; and his mother prepared savory food, such as his father loved.
text.Rotherham: So he went, and fetched [them], and brought them in to his mother, and his mother made dainty meats, such as his father loved.
text.KJV: And hee went, and fetched, and brought them to his mother, and his mother made sauoury meat, such as his father loued.
text.NABRE: So Jacob went and got them and brought them to his mother, and she prepared a dish in the way his father liked.
text.DRB: He went, and brought, and gave them to his mother. She dressed meats, even as she knew his father liked.

text.NRSV: Then Rebekah took the best garments of her elder son Esau, which were with her in the house, and put them on her younger son Jacob;
text.Rotherham: Then took Rebekah the garments of Esau her elder son, the costly ones, which were with her in the house,—and put them on Jacob her younger son:
text.KJV: And Rebekah tooke goodly raiment of her eldest sonne Esau, which were with her in the house, and put them vpon Iacob her yonger sonne:
text.NABRE: Rebekah then took the best clothes of her older son Esau that she had in the house, and gave them to her younger son Jacob to wear;
text.DRB: And she did on him the garments of Esau very good, which she had at home with her:

text.NRSV: and she put the skins of the kids on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.
text.Rotherham: and the, skins of the kids of the goats, put she upon his hands,—and on the smooth part of his neck;
text.KJV: And shee put the skinnes of the kids of the goats vpon his hands, and vpon the smooth of his necke.
text.NABRE: and with the goatskins she covered up his hands and the hairless part of his neck.
text.DRB: and the little skins of the kids she put about his hands, and covered the bare of his neck.

text.NRSV: Then she handed the savory food, and the bread that she had prepared, to her son Jacob.
text.Rotherham: then placed she the dainty meats and the bread, which she had made ready, in the hand of Jacob her son.
text.KJV: And she gaue the sauoury meate, and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her sonne Iacob.
text.NABRE: Then she gave her son Jacob the dish and the bread she had prepared.
text.DRB: And she gave him the broth, and delivered him bread that she had baked.

text.NRSV: So he went in to his father, and said, “My father”; and he said, “Here I am; who are you, my son?”
text.Rotherham: So he went in unto his father, and said My father! And he said Behold me! who art, thou, my son?
text.KJV: ¶ And he came vnto his father, and said, My father: And he said, Here am I: who art thou, my sonne?
text.NABRE: Going to his father, Jacob said, “Father!” “Yes?” replied Isaac. “Which of my sons are you?”
text.DRB: Which when he had carried in, he said: My father? But he answered: I hear. Who art thou my son?

text.NRSV: Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau your firstborn. I have done as you told me; now sit up and eat of my game, so that you may bless me.”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said unto his father I, am Esau thy firstborn, I have made ready, as thou didst bid me. Rise, I pray thee, sit up, and eat thou of my game, To the end thy soul may bless me.
text.KJV: And Iacob said vnto his father, I am Esau, thy first borne; I haue done according as thou badest mee: arise, I pray thee, sit, and eate of my venison, that thy soule may blesse me.
text.NABRE: Jacob answered his father: “I am Esau, your firstborn. I did as you told me. Please sit up and eat some of my game, so that you may bless me.”
text.DRB: And Jacob said: I am thy first begotten Esau: I have done as thou didst command me: arise, sit, and eat of my hunting, that thy soul may bless me.

text.NRSV: But Isaac said to his son, “How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?” He answered, “Because the Lord your God granted me success.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaac unto his son. How is it thou hast been so quick in finding, my son? And he said, Because Yahweh thy God caused it so to fall out before me.
text.KJV: And Isaac said vnto his sonne, How is it that thou hast found it so quickly, my sonne? And he said, Because the LORD thy God brought it to me.
text.NABRE: But Isaac said to his son, “How did you get it so quickly, my son?” He answered, “The Lord, your God, directed me.”
text.DRB: And again Isaac to his son: How couldest thou, said he, find it so quickly, my son? Who answered: It was the will of God that that which I would came quickly in my way.

text.NRSV: Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Come near, that I may feel you, my son, to know whether you are really my son Esau or not.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaac unto Jacob. Come near, I pray thee, that I may feel thee my son—whether, thou thyself, art my son Esau, or not.
text.KJV: And Isaac saide vnto Iacob, Come neere, I pray thee, that I may feele thee, my sonne, whether thou bee my very sonne Esau, or not.
text.NABRE: Isaac then said to Jacob, “Come closer, my son, that I may feel you, to learn whether you really are my son Esau or not.”
text.DRB: And Isaac said: Come hither, that I may feel thee my son, and may prove whether thou be my son Esau, or not.

text.NRSV: So Jacob went up to his father Isaac, who felt him and said, “The voice is Jacob’s voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.”
text.Rotherham: So Jacob came near unto Isaac his father, and he felt him; then said he the voice is the voice of Jacob; But, the hands, are the hands of Esau.
text.KJV: And Iacob went neere vnto Isaac his father: and hee felt him, and said, The voyce is Iacobs voyce, but the hands are the hands of Esau.
text.NABRE: So Jacob moved up closer to his father. When Isaac felt him, he said, “Although the voice is Jacob’s, the hands are Esau’s.”
text.DRB: He came near to his father, and when he had felt him, Isaac said: The voice verily, is the voice of Jacob: but the hands are the hands of Esau.

text.NRSV: He did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like his brother Esau’s hands; so he blessed him.
text.Rotherham: So he did not find him out, because, his hands were like the hands of Esau his brother hairy,—so he blessed him.
text.KJV: And he discerned him not, because his hands were hairie, as his brother Esaus hands: So he blessed him.
text.NABRE: (He failed to identify him because his hands were hairy, like those of his brother Esau; so he blessed him.)
text.DRB: And he knew him not, because his hairy hands had made him like unto the elder. Blessing him therefore,

text.NRSV: He said, “Are you really my son Esau?” He answered, “I am.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Thou thyself, art my son Esau? And he said, I am!
text.KJV: And he said, Art thou my very sonne Esau? and he said, I am.
text.NABRE: Again Isaac said, “Are you really my son Esau?” And Jacob said, “I am.”
text.DRB: he said: Art thou my son Esau? He answered: I am.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Bring it to me, that I may eat of my son’s game and bless you.” So he brought it to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.
text.Rotherham: So he said, Bring it near to me., that I may eat of the game of my son, To the end my soul may bless thee. And he brought it near to him, and he did eat, and he brought in to him wine, and he drank.
text.KJV: And he said, Bring it neere to me, and I will eate of my sonnes venison, that my soule may blesse thee: and hee brought it neere to him, and he did eate: and he brought him wine, & he dranke.
text.NABRE: Then Isaac said, “Serve me, my son, and let me eat of the game so that I may bless you.” Jacob served it to him, and Isaac ate; he brought him wine, and he drank.
text.DRB: But he said: Bring me the meats of thy hunting, my son, that my soul may bless thee. Which when they were brought and he had eaten, he offered him wine also, which after he had drunk,

text.NRSV: Then his father Isaac said to him, “Come near and kiss me, my son.”
text.Rotherham: Then Isaac his father said unto him,—Come thou near I pray thee and kiss me my son.
text.KJV: And his father Isaac saide vnto him, Come neere now, and kisse me, my sonne.
text.NABRE: Finally his father Isaac said to him, “Come closer, my son, and kiss me.”
text.DRB: he said to him: Come near me, and give me a kiss, my son.

text.NRSV: So he came near and kissed him; and he smelled the smell of his garments, and blessed him, and said, “Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field that the Lord has blessed.
text.Rotherham: So he came near, and kissed him, and he smelled the smell of his garments and blessed him,—and said. See! the smell of my son, As the smell of a field, which Yahweh hath blessed;
text.KJV: And hee came neere, and kissed him: and he smelled the smell of his raiment, and blessed him, and said, See, the smell of my sonne is as the smell of a field, which the LORD hath blessed.
text.NABRE: As Jacob went up to kiss him, Isaac smelled the fragrance of his clothes. With that, he blessed him, saying, “Ah, the fragrance of my son is like the fragrance of a field that the Lord has blessed!
text.DRB: He came near, and kissed him. And immediately as he felt the fragrant savour of his garments, blessing him, he said: Behold the savour of my son is as the savour of a plentiful field, which our Lord hath blessed.

text.NRSV: May God give you of the dew of heaven, and of the fatness of the earth, and plenty of grain and wine.
text.Rotherham: Then, may God give thee of the dew of the heavens, And of the fatness of the earth,—And abundance of corn and new wine:
text.KJV: Therefore God giue thee of the dew of heauen, and the fatnesse of the earth, and plenty of corne and wine.
text.NABRE: May God give to you of the dew of the heavens And of the fertility of the earth abundance of grain and wine.
text.DRB: God give thee of the dew of heaven, & of the fatness of the earth abundance of corn and wine.

text.NRSV: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and may your mother’s sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be everyone who blesses you!”
text.Rotherham: Let peoples serve thee And races bow down to thee, Become thou lord to thy brethren, And let the sons of thy mother bow down to thee,—He that curseth thee, be accursed! And he that blesseth thee, be blessed!
text.KJV: Let people serue thee, and nations bow downe to thee: bee lord ouer thy brethren, & let thy mothers sonnes bow downe to thee: Cursed bee euery one that curseth thee, and blessed be hee that blesseth thee.
text.NABRE: May peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you; Be master of your brothers, and may your mother’s sons bow down to you. Cursed be those who curse you, and blessed be those who bless you.”
text.DRB: And let peoples serve thee, and tribes adore thee: be thou lord of thy brethren, and thy mother's children bow they before thee. He that shall curse thee, be he cursed: and he that shall bless thee, be he replenished with blessings.

text.NRSV: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, when Jacob had scarcely gone out from the presence of his father Isaac, his brother Esau came in from his hunting.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as soon as Isaac had made an end of blessing Jacob,—yea it came to pass when Jacob, had only just gone forth, from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother, came in from his hunting.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, as soone as Isaac had made an ende of blessing Iacob, and Iacob was yet scarce gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
text.NABRE: Jacob had scarcely left his father after Isaac had finished blessing him, when his brother Esau came back from his hunt.
text.DRB: Isaac had scarce ended his words, and Jacob now gone forth abroad, but Esau came,

text.NRSV: He also prepared savory food, and brought it to his father. And he said to his father, “Let my father sit up and eat of his son’s game, so that you may bless me.”
text.Rotherham: Then, he too, made dainty meats, and brought in to his father,—and said to his father, Let my father rise that he may eat of the game of his son, To the end thy soul may bless me!
text.KJV: And hee also had made sauoury meate, and brought it vnto his father, and said vnto his father, Let my father arise, and eat of his sonnes venison, that thy soule may blesse me.
text.NABRE: Then he too prepared a dish, and bringing it to his father, he said, “Let my father sit up and eat some of his son’s game, that you may then give me your blessing.”
text.DRB: and brought in to his father meats made of his hunting, saying: Arise my father, and eat of thy son's hunting; that thy soul may bless me.

text.NRSV: His father Isaac said to him, “Who are you?” He answered, “I am your firstborn son, Esau.”
text.Rotherham: And Isaac his father said to him Who art, thou? And he said, I, am thy son thy firstborn, Esau.
text.KJV: And Isaac his father said vnto him, Who art thou? and he said, I am thy sonne, thy first borne Esau.
text.NABRE: His father Isaac asked him, “Who are you?” He said, “I am your son, your firstborn son, Esau.”
text.DRB: And Isaac said to him: Why! Who art thou? Who answered: I am thy first begotten son Esau.

text.NRSV: Then Isaac trembled violently, and said, “Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him?—yes, and blessed he shall be!”
text.Rotherham: Then did Isaac tremble with an exceeding great trembling, and said Who then was it that caught game and brought in to me and I did eat of all ere yet thou didst come in and I blessed him? Yea blessed, shall he remain!
text.KJV: And Isaac trembled very exceedingly, and said, Who? Where is he that hath taken venison, and brought it me, and I haue eaten of all before thou camest, and haue blessed him? yea and he shalbe blessed.
text.NABRE: Isaac trembled greatly. “Who was it, then,” he asked, “that hunted game and brought it to me? I ate it all just before you came, and I blessed him. Now he is blessed!”
text.DRB: Isaac was amazed and astonished exceedingly, and marveling more than a man can believe, said: Who is he then that even now brought me venison that he had taken, and I did eat of all things before thou camest, and I have blessed him, and he shall be blessed.

text.NRSV: When Esau heard his father’s words, he cried out with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father, “Bless me, me also, father!”
text.Rotherham: When Esau heard the words of his father, then cried he out with an outcry loud and bitter exceedingly,—and said to his father, Bless even me also, O my father!
text.KJV: And when Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with a great and exceeding bitter cry, and said vnto his father, Blesse mee, euen me also, O my father.
text.NABRE: As he heard his father’s words, Esau burst into loud, bitter sobbing and said, “Father, bless me too!”
text.DRB: Esau having heard his father's words, roared out with a great cry; and being dismayed, said: Bless me also, my father.

text.NRSV: But he said, “Your brother came deceitfully, and he has taken away your blessing.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Thy brother came in with deceit,—and took away thy blessing.
text.KJV: And hee said, Thy brother came with subtilty, and hath taken away thy blessing.
text.NABRE: When Isaac said, “Your brother came here by a ruse and carried off your blessing,”
text.DRB: Who said: Thy brother came deceitfully and took thy blessing.

text.NRSV: Esau said, “Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright; and look, now he has taken away my blessing.” Then he said, “Have you not reserved a blessing for me?”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Is it because his name, is called, Jacob, that he hath tricked me, now twice? My birthright, he took away, And lo! now, he hath taken away my blessing! And he said, Hast thou not reserved for me a blessing?
text.KJV: And he said, Is not he rightly naned Iacob? for he hath supplanted me these two times: hee tooke away my birthright, and behold, now he hath taken away my blessing: and hee said, Hast thou not reserued a blessing for mee?
text.NABRE: Esau exclaimed, “He is well named Jacob, is he not! He has supplanted me twice! First he took away my right as firstborn, and now he has taken away my blessing.” Then he said, “Have you not saved a blessing for me?”
text.DRB: But he said again: Rightly is his name called Jacob; for he hath supplanted me lo the second time: my first-birth-right he took before, and now the second time he hath stolen my blessing. And again to his father he said: Hast thou not reserved me also a blessing?

text.NRSV: Isaac answered Esau, “I have already made him your lord, and I have given him all his brothers as servants, and with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?”
text.Rotherham: Then answered Isaac and said to Esau,—Lo a lord, have I appointed him unto thee, And, all his brethren, have I given to him as servants, And with corn and new wine, have I sustained him,—And for thee—now, what can I do, my son?
text.KJV: And Isaac answered and saide vnto Esau, Behold, I haue made him thy lord, and all his brethren haue I giuen to him for seruants: and with corne and wine haue I susteined him: and what shall I doe now vnto thee, my sonne?
text.NABRE: Isaac replied to Esau: “I have already appointed him your master, and I have assigned to him all his kindred as his servants; besides, I have sustained him with grain and wine. What then can I do for you, my son?”
text.DRB: Isaac answered: I have appointed him thy lord, and all his brethren I have made subject to his service: with corn and wine I have established him, and for thee, my son, what shall I do more after this?

text.NRSV: Esau said to his father, “Have you only one blessing, father? Bless me, me also, father!” And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
text.Rotherham: And Esau said unto his father, But one blessing, hast thou, O my father? Bless, me also, O my father! And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
text.KJV: And Esau said vnto his father, Hast thou but one blessing, my father? blesse mee, euen mee also, O my father. And Esau lift vp his voyce, and wept.
text.NABRE: But Esau said to his father, “Have you only one blessing, father? Bless me too, father!” and Esau wept aloud.
text.DRB: To whom Esau said: Hast thou one only blessing, father? I beseech thee bless me also. And when he wept that he howled again,

text.NRSV: Then his father Isaac answered him: “See, away from the fatness of the earth shall your home be, and away from the dew of heaven on high.
text.Rotherham: Then answered Isaac his father and said unto him,—Lo! of the fat parts of the earth, shall be thy dwelling, And of the dew of the heavens, above;
text.KJV: And Isaac his father answered, and said vnto him, Behold, thy dwelling shall be the fatnesse of the earth, and of the dew of heauen from aboue.
text.NABRE: His father Isaac said in response: “See, far from the fertile earth will be your dwelling; far from the dew of the heavens above!
text.DRB: Isaac being moved, said to him: In the fat of the earth, and in the dew of heaven from above,

text.NRSV: By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; but when you break loose, you shall break his yoke from your neck.”
text.Rotherham: And on thy sword, shalt thou live, And thy brother, shalt thou serve: But it shall come to pass when thou shalt rove at large, Then shalt thou break his yoke from off thy neck.
text.KJV: And by thy sword shalt thou liue, and shalt serue thy brother: and it shall come to passe when thou shalt haue the dominion, that thou shalt breake his yoke from off thy necke.
text.NABRE: By your sword you will live, and your brother you will serve; But when you become restless, you will throw off his yoke from your neck.”
text.DRB: shall thy blessing be. Thou shalt live by the sword, and shalt serve thy brother: and the time shall come, when thou shalt shake off and loose his yoke from thy neck.

text.NRSV: Now Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, “The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob.”
text.Rotherham: And Esau lay in wait for Jacob, on account of the blessing wherewith his father had blessed him,—and Esau said in his heart, The days of mourning for my father draw near, when I can slay Jacob my brother.
text.KJV: ¶ And Esau hated Iacob, because of the blessing, wherewith his father blessed him: and Esau said in his heart, The dayes of mourning for my father are at hand; then will I slay my brother Iacob.
text.NABRE: Esau bore a grudge against Jacob because of the blessing his father had given him. Esau said to himself, “Let the time of mourning for my father come, so that I may kill my brother Jacob.”
text.DRB: Esau therefore always hated Jacob for the blessing wherewith his father had blessed him; and he said in his heart: The days will come of the mourning of my father, and I will kill Jacob my brother.

text.NRSV: But the words of her elder son Esau were told to Rebekah; so she sent and called her younger son Jacob and said to him, “Your brother Esau is consoling himself by planning to kill you.
text.Rotherham: Then were told to Rebekah, the words of Esau her elder son, so she sent and called for Jacob, her younger son, and said unto him—Lo! Esau, thy brother, is consoling himself as touching thee, to slay thee.
text.KJV: And these words of Esau her elder sonne were told to Rebekah: And shee sent and called Iacob her yonger sonne, and said vnto him, Behold, thy brother Esau, as touching thee, doeth comfort himselfe, purposing to kill thee.
text.NABRE: When Rebekah got news of what her older son Esau had in mind, she summoned her younger son Jacob and said to him: “Listen! Your brother Esau intends to get his revenge by killing you.
text.DRB: These things were told to Rebecca: who sending and calling Jacob her son, said to him: Behold Esau thy brother threateneth to kill thee.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice; flee at once to my brother Laban in Haran,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, my son, hearken to my voice,—and rise flee thou to Laban my brother to Haran;
text.KJV: Now therefore my sonne, obey my voice: and arise, flee thou to Laban my brother, to Haran.
text.NABRE: So now, my son, obey me: flee at once to my brother Laban in Haran,
text.DRB: Now therefore, my son, hear my voice, and get thee up and fly to Laban my brother into Haran:

text.NRSV: and stay with him a while, until your brother’s fury turns away—
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt dwell with him a few days,—until that the wrath of thy brother turn away:
text.KJV: And tary with him a few dayes, vntill thy brothers furie turne away;
text.NABRE: and stay with him a while until your brother’s fury subsides—
text.DRB: and thou shalt dwell with him a few days, till the fury of thy brother be assuaged,

text.NRSV: until your brother’s anger against you turns away, and he forgets what you have done to him; then I will send, and bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in one day?”
text.Rotherham: until the turning away of the anger of thy brother from thee, and he forget what thou hast done to him, and I send and fetch thee from thence. Wherefore should I lose, you both, in one day?
text.KJV: Untill thy brothers anger turne away from thee, and hee forget that, which thou hast done to him: then I will send, and fetch thee from thence: why should I be depriued also of you both in one day?
text.NABRE: until your brother’s anger against you subsides and he forgets what you did to him. Then I will send for you and bring you back. Why should I lose both of you in a single day?”
text.DRB: and his indignation cease, and he forget those things which thou hast done to him: afterward I will send, and bring thee from thence hither. Why shall I be deprived of both sons in one day?

text.NRSV: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, “I am weary of my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women such as these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?”
text.Rotherham: So then Rebekah said unto Isaac, I am disgusted with my life because of the daughters of Heth,—Should Jacob be taking a wife of the daughters of Heth, like these, of the daughters of the land, wherefore could I wish for life?
text.KJV: And Rebekah said to Isaac, I am weary of my life, because of the daughters of Heth: If Iacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these which are of the daughters of the land, what good shall my life doe me?
text.NABRE: Rebekah said to Isaac: “I am disgusted with life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob also should marry a Hittite woman, a native of the land, like these women, why should I live?”
text.DRB: And Rebecca said to Isaac: I am weary of my life for the daughters of Heth: if Jacob take a wife of the stock of this land, I list not live.

text.NRSV: Then Isaac called Jacob and blessed him, and charged him, “You shall not marry one of the Canaanite women.
text.Rotherham: Then Isaac called Jacob, and blessed him,—and commanded him and said to him, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan.
text.KJV: And Isaac called Iacob, and blessed him, and charged him, and saide vnto him, Thou shalt not take a wife, of the daughters of Canaan.
text.NABRE: Isaac therefore summoned Jacob and blessed him, charging him: “You shall not marry a Canaanite woman!
text.DRB: Isaac therefore called Jacob, and blessed him, and commanded him saying: Take not a wife of the stock of Chanaan;

text.NRSV: Go at once to Paddan-aram to the house of Bethuel, your mother’s father; and take as wife from there one of the daughters of Laban, your mother’s brother.
text.Rotherham: Rise, go thy way to Padan-aram to the house of Bethuel, thy mother’s father,—and take thee from thence a wife, of the daughters of Laban, thy mother’s brother.
text.KJV: Arise, goe to Padan Aram, to the house of Bethuel thy mothers father, and take thee a wife from thence, of the daughters of Laban thy mothers brother.
text.NABRE: Go now to Paddan-aram, to the home of your mother’s father Bethuel, and there choose a wife for yourself from among the daughters of Laban, your mother’s brother.
text.DRB: but go and make a journey into Mesopotamia of Syria; to the house of Bathuel thy mother's father, and take thee a wife thence of the daughters of Laban thine uncle.

text.NRSV: May God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and numerous, that you may become a company of peoples.
text.Rotherham: And, GOD Almighty, bless thee, and make thee fruitful, and multiply thee, so shalt thou become a multitude of peoples.
text.KJV: And God Almighty blesse thee, and make thee fruitfull, and multiply thee, that thou mayest be a multitude of people:
text.NABRE: May God Almighty bless you and make you fertile, multiply you that you may become an assembly of peoples.
text.DRB: And God Almighty bless thee, and make thee increase, and multiply thee: that thou mayest be into multitudes of peoples.

text.NRSV: May he give to you the blessing of Abraham, to you and to your offspring with you, so that you may take possession of the land where you now live as an alien—land that God gave to Abraham.”
text.Rotherham: And may he give thee the blessing of Abraham, to thee, and to thy seed with thee,—that thou mayest possess the land of thy sojournings, which God gave to Abraham.
text.KJV: And giue thee the blessing of Abraham, to thee and to thy seede with thee, that thou mayest inherit the lande wherein thou art a stranger, which God gaue vnto Abraham.
text.NABRE: May God extend to you and your descendants the blessing of Abraham, so that you may gain possession of the land where you are residing, which he assigned to Abraham.”
text.DRB: And give he thee the blessings of Abraham, and to thy seed after thee: that thou mayest possess the land of thy peregrination, which he promised to thy grandfather.

text.NRSV: Thus Isaac sent Jacob away; and he went to Paddan-aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, Jacob’s and Esau’s mother.
text.Rotherham: So Isaac sent away Jacob, and he took his journey to Padan-aram,—unto Laban son of Bethuel, the Syrian, brother of Rebekah, mother of Jacob and Esau.
text.KJV: And Isaac sent away Iacob, and hee went to Padan-Aram vnto Laban, sonne of Bethuel the Syrian, the brother of Rebekah, Iacobs and Esaus mother.
text.NABRE: Then Isaac sent Jacob on his way; he went to Paddan-aram, to Laban, son of Bethuel the Aramean, and brother of Rebekah, the mother of Jacob and Esau.
text.DRB: And when Isaac had dismissed him, taking his journey he came to Mesopotamia of Syria to Laban, the son of Bathuel the Syrian, brother to Rebecca his mother.

text.NRSV: Now Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him away to Paddan-aram to take a wife from there, and that as he blessed him he charged him, “You shall not marry one of the Canaanite women,”
text.Rotherham: And Esau beheld that Isaac, when he blessed Jacob, and sent him to Padan-aram, to take to himself from thence a wife, that in blessing him, he laid command upon him, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan;
text.KJV: ¶ When Esau sawe that Isaac had blessed Iacob, and sent him away to Padan-Aram, to take him a wife from thence; and that as he blessed him, he gaue him a charge, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan;
text.NABRE: Esau noted that Isaac had blessed Jacob when he sent him to Paddan-aram to get himself a wife there, and that, as he gave him his blessing, he charged him, “You shall not marry a Canaanite woman,”
text.DRB: And Esau seeing that his father had blessed Jacob, and had sent him into Mesopotamia of Syria, to marry a wife thence; and that after the blessing he had commanded him, saying: Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Chanaan;

text.NRSV: and that Jacob had obeyed his father and his mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
text.Rotherham: And that Jacob hearkened unto his father and unto his mother,—and took his journey to Padan-aram.
text.KJV: And that Iacob obeyed his father, and his mother, and was gone to Padan-Aram;
text.NABRE: and that Jacob had obeyed his father and mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
text.DRB: and that Jacob obeying his parents was gone into Syria:

text.NRSV: So when Esau saw that the Canaanite women did not please his father Isaac,
text.Rotherham: So then Esau beheld that displeasing, were the daughters of Caanan,—in the eyes of Isaac his father:
text.KJV: And Esau seeing that the daughters of Canaan pleased not Isaac his father.
text.NABRE: Esau realized how displeasing the Canaanite women were to his father Isaac,
text.DRB: having trial also that his father did not willingly see the daughters of Chanaan,

text.NRSV: Esau went to Ishmael and took Mahalath daughter of Abraham’s son Ishmael, and sister of Nebaioth, to be his wife in addition to the wives he had.
text.Rotherham: Esau therefore went unto Ishmael,—and took Mahalath daughter of Ishmael son of Abraham sister of Nebaioth, to himself to wife, in addition to the wives that he had.
text.KJV: Then went Esau vnto Ishmael, and tooke vnto the wiues which hee had, Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael Abrahams sonne, the sister of Nebaioth, to be his wife.
text.NABRE: so Esau went to Ishmael, and in addition to the wives he had, married Mahalath, the daughter of Abraham’s son Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth. Jacob’s Dream at Bethel.
text.DRB: he went to Ismael, and took to wife besides them which he had before, Maheleth the daughter of Ismael Abraham's son, sister to Nabajoth.

text.NRSV: Jacob left Beer-sheba and went toward Haran.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob went forth from Beer-sheba, and journeyed towards Haran.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob went out from Beer-sheba, and went toward Haran.
text.NABRE: Jacob departed from Beer-sheba and proceeded toward Haran.
text.DRB: Therefore Jacob being departed from Bersabee, went on to Haran.

text.NRSV: He came to a certain place and stayed there for the night, because the sun had set. Taking one of the stones of the place, he put it under his head and lay down in that place.
text.Rotherham: And he lighted on a place, and tarried the night there, because the sun had gone in,—and he took of the stones of the place, and put for his pillow, and lay down in that place,
text.KJV: And hee lighted vpon a certaine place, and taried there all night, because the sunne was set: and hee tooke of the stones of that place, and put them for his pillowes, and lay downe in that place to sleepe.
text.NABRE: When he came upon a certain place, he stopped there for the night, since the sun had already set. Taking one of the stones at the place, he put it under his head and lay down in that place.
text.DRB: And when he was come to a certain place, and would rest in it after sun set, he took one of the stones that lay there, and putting it under his head, slept in the same place.

text.NRSV: And he dreamed that there was a ladder set up on the earth, the top of it reaching to heaven; and the angels of God were ascending and descending on it.
text.Rotherham: And he dreamed, and lo! a stairway, planted earthwards, with, its top, reaching towards the heavens,—and lo! messengers of God, ascending and descending thereon;
text.KJV: And he dreamed, and beholde, a ladder set vp on the earth, and the top of it reached to heauen: and beholde the Angels of God ascending and descending on it.
text.NABRE: Then he had a dream: a stairway rested on the ground, with its top reaching to the heavens; and God’s angels were going up and down on it.
text.DRB: And he saw in his sleep a ladder standing upon the earth, & the top thereof touching heaven: the Angels also of God ascending & descending by it,

text.NRSV: And the Lord stood beside him and said, “I am the Lord, the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac; the land on which you lie I will give to you and to your offspring;
text.Rotherham: and lo! Yahweh, standing by him,—and he said, I, am Yahweh, God of Abraham thy father and God of Isaac,—The land whereon, thou, art lying, to thee, will I give it and to thy seed;
text.KJV: And behold, the LORD stood aboue it, and said, I am the LORD God of Abraham thy father, and the God of Isaac: the land whereon thou liest, to thee will I giue it, and to thy seede.
text.NABRE: And there was the Lord standing beside him and saying: I am the Lord, the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac; the land on which you are lying I will give to you and your descendants.
text.DRB: and our Lord leaning upon the ladder saying to him: I am the Lord God of Abraham thy father, & the God of Isaac: the Land wherein thou sleepest, I will give to thee and to thy seed.

text.NRSV: and your offspring shall be like the dust of the earth, and you shall spread abroad to the west and to the east and to the north and to the south; and all the families of the earth shall be blessed in you and in your offspring.
text.Rotherham: And thy seed shall become as the dust of the earth, and thou shalt break forth westward and eastward, and northward and southward,—And all the families of the ground shall be blessed in thee and in thy seed.
text.KJV: And thy seed shall be as the dust of the earth, and thou shalt spread abroad to the West, and to the East, and to the North, and to the South: and in thee, and in thy seed, shall all the families of the earth be blessed.
text.NABRE: Your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and through them you will spread to the west and the east, to the north and the south. In you and your descendants all the families of the earth will find blessing.
text.DRB: And thy seed shall be as the dust of the earth: thou shalt be dilated to the West, and to the East, and to the North, and to the South: and IN Thee And Thy Seed All The Tribes of The Earth Shall be Blessed.

text.NRSV: Know that I am with you and will keep you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land; for I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! then, I, am with thee, so will I keep thee in every place whithersoever thou mayest go, and will bring thee back unto this soil,—For I will not forsake thee until I have done that of which I have spoken to thee.
text.KJV: And behold, I am with thee, and will keepe thee in all places whither thou goest, and will bring thee againe into this land: for I will not leaue thee, vntill I haue done that which I haue spoken to thee of.
text.NABRE: I am with you and will protect you wherever you go, and bring you back to this land. I will never leave you until I have done what I promised you.
text.DRB: And Iwill be thy keeper whither soever thou goest, and will bring thee back into this land: neither will I leave thee, till I shall have accomplished all things which I have said.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob woke from his sleep and said, “Surely the Lord is in this place—and I did not know it!”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob awoke from his sleep, and said. Surely, Yahweh was in this place, And I, knew it not.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob awaked out of his sleepe, and he said, Surely the LORD is in this place, and I knew it not.
text.NABRE: When Jacob awoke from his sleep, he said, “Truly, the Lord is in this place and I did not know it!”
text.DRB: And when Jacob was awaked out of sleep, he said: Indeed our Lord is in this place, and I wist not.

text.NRSV: And he was afraid, and said, “How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven.”
text.Rotherham: And he feared and said, How fearful is this place! None other this, than the house of God! Nor this than the gate of the heavens
text.KJV: And he was afraid, and said, How dreadful is this place? this is none other, but the house of God, and this is the gate of heauen.
text.NABRE: He was afraid and said: “How awesome this place is! This is nothing else but the house of God, the gateway to heaven!”
text.DRB: And trembling he said: How terrible is this place! This is none other but the house of God, and the gate of heaven.

text.NRSV: So Jacob rose early in the morning, and he took the stone that he had put under his head and set it up for a pillar and poured oil on the top of it.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob rose early in the morning, and took the stone which he had put for his pillow, and put it for a pillar,—and poured out oil upon the top thereof;
text.KJV: And Iacob rose vp earely in the morning, and tooke the stone that hee had put for his pillowes, and set it vp for a pillar, and powred oile vpon the top of it.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning Jacob took the stone that he had put under his head, set it up as a sacred pillar, and poured oil on top of it.
text.DRB: And Jacob arising in the morning took the stone, which he had laid under his head, and erected it for a title, pouring oil upon the top.

text.NRSV: He called that place Bethel; but the name of the city was Luz at the first.
text.Rotherham: and called the name of that place—Beth-el,—nevertheless, Luz, was the name of the city, aforetime.
text.KJV: And hee called the name of that place Beth-el: but the name of that citie was called Luz, at the first.
text.NABRE: He named that place Bethel, whereas the former name of the town had been Luz.
text.DRB: And he called the name of the city Bethel, which before was called Luza.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, “If God will be with me, and will keep me in this way that I go, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,
text.Rotherham: And Jacob vowed a vow, saying,—If God will be with me, and will keep me in this way whereon, I, am going, and give me bread to eat and raiment to put on;
text.KJV: And Iacob vowed a vow, saying, If God will be with me, and will keepe me in this way that I goe, and will giue me bread to eate, and raiment to put on,
text.NABRE: Jacob then made this vow: “If God will be with me and protect me on this journey I am making and give me food to eat and clothes to wear,
text.DRB: And he vowed a vow, saying: If God shall be with me, and shall keep me in the way, by the which I walk, and shall give me bread to eat, and raiment to put on,

text.NRSV: so that I come again to my father’s house in peace, then the Lord shall be my God,
text.Rotherham: and I come back in prosperity unto the house of my father, Then will Yahweh prove to be my God,—
text.KJV: So that I come againe to my fathers house in peace: then shall the LORD be my God.
text.NABRE: and I come back safely to my father’s house, the Lord will be my God.
text.DRB: and I shall be returned prosperously to my father's house, the Lord shall be my God,

text.NRSV: and this stone, which I have set up for a pillar, shall be God’s house; and of all that you give me I will surely give one tenth to you.”
text.Rotherham: And, this stone which I have put for a pillar, shall be the house of God,—And, of all which thou shalt give me, a tenth, will I tithe unto thee.
text.KJV: And this stone which I haue set for a pillar, shall be Gods house: and of all that thou shalt giue me, I will surely giue the tenth vnto thee.
text.NABRE: This stone that I have set up as a sacred pillar will be the house of God. Of everything you give me, I will return a tenth part to you without fail.”
text.DRB: and this stone, which I have erected for a title, shall be called the House of God: and of all things that thou shalt give to me, I will offer tithes to thee.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob went on his journey, and came to the land of the people of the east.
text.Rotherham: Then Jacob lifted up his feet,—and went his way towards the land of the sons of the East.
text.KJV: Then Iacob went on his iourney, and came into the land of the people of the East.
text.NABRE: After Jacob resumed his journey, he came to the land of the Kedemites.
text.DRB: Jacob therefore going on his journey, came into the East country.

text.NRSV: As he looked, he saw a well in the field and three flocks of sheep lying there beside it; for out of that well the flocks were watered. The stone on the well’s mouth was large,
text.Rotherham: And he looked, and lo! a well, in the field and lo! there, three flocks of sheep, lying down near it, for out of that well, do they water the flocks, but, the stone, is great, on the mouth of the well:
text.KJV: And he looked, and behold, a well in the field, and loe, there were three flocks of sheepe lying by it: for out of that wel they watered the flocks: and a great stone was vpon the welles mouth.
text.NABRE: Looking about, he saw a well in the open country, with three flocks of sheep huddled near it, for flocks were watered from that well. A large stone covered the mouth of the well.
text.DRB: And he saw a well in the field, and three flocks of sheep lying beside it: for of it the beasts were watered, and the mouth thereof was closed with a great stone.

text.NRSV: and when all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the mouth of the well, and water the sheep, and put the stone back in its place on the mouth of the well.
text.Rotherham: so they gather together thither all the flocks, and roll away the stone from off the mouth of the well, and water the sheep,—and put back the stone upon the mouth of the well, to its place.
text.KJV: And thither were all the flockes gathered, and they rolled the stone from the wels mouth, & watered the sheepe, and put the stone againe vpon the wels mouth in his place.
text.NABRE: When all the shepherds were assembled there they would roll the stone away from the mouth of the well and water the sheep. Then they would put the stone back again in its place over the mouth of the well.
text.DRB: And the manner was when all the sheep were gathered together they did roll off the stone, and after the sheep were refreshed they put it on the mouth of the well again.

text.NRSV: Jacob said to them, “My brothers, where do you come from?” They said, “We are from Haran.”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said to them, My brethren, whence are ye? And they said: From haran, are we.
text.KJV: And Iacob said vnto them, My brethren, whence be ye? and they saide, Of Haran are we.
text.NABRE: Jacob said to them, “My brothers, where are you from?” “We are from Haran,” they replied.
text.DRB: And he said to the shepherds: Brethren, whence are you? Who answered: Of Haran.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Do you know Laban son of Nahor?” They said, “We do.”
text.Rotherham: And he said to them, Know ye Laban, son of Nahor? And they said, We know him.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Know ye Laban the sonne of Nahor? And they sayde, We knowe him.
text.NABRE: Then he asked them, “Do you know Laban, son of Nahor?” “We do,” they answered.
text.DRB: And he asked them, saying: Know you Laban the son of Nachor? They said: We do know him.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Is it well with him?” “Yes,” they replied, “and here is his daughter Rachel, coming with the sheep.”
text.Rotherham: And he said to them, Is it well with him? And they said, Well, but lo! Rachel his daughter, coming in with the sheep.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Is hee well? and they said, He is well: and behold, Rachel his daughter commeth with the sheepe.
text.NABRE: He inquired further, “Is he well?” “He is,” they answered; “and here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text.DRB: Is he in health? Quoth he: He is in health, say they: And behold Rachel his daughter cometh with his flock.

text.NRSV: He said, “Look, it is still broad daylight; it is not time for the animals to be gathered together. Water the sheep, and go, pasture them.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Lo! the day is yet high, it is not time for gathering together the cattle,—water the sheep, and go feed.
text.KJV: And hee said, Loe, it is yet high day, neither is it time that the cattell should be gathered together: water yee the sheepe, and goe and feed them.
text.NABRE: Then he said: “There is still much daylight left; it is hardly the time to bring the animals home. Water the sheep, and then continue pasturing them.”
text.DRB: And Jacob said: There is yet much day remaining, neither is it time to bring the flocks into the folds again: first give the sheep drink, and so bring them back to feed.

text.NRSV: But they said, “We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together, and the stone is rolled from the mouth of the well; then we water the sheep.”
text.Rotherham: And they said, We cannot, until that all the flocks are gathered together, and they roll away the stone, from off the mouth of the well,—and then can we water the sheep.
text.KJV: And they said, We cannot, vntill all the flockes bee gathered together, and till they rolle the stone from the welles mouth: then wee water the sheepe.
text.NABRE: They replied, “We cannot until all the shepherds are here to roll the stone away from the mouth of the well; then can we water the flocks.”
text.DRB: Who answered: we cannot, till all the cattle be gathered together: and we remove the stone from the well's mouth, that we may water the flocks.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with her father’s sheep; for she kept them.
text.Rotherham: While yet he was speaking with them, Rachel, had come in with the sheep which belonged to her father, for a shepherdess, was she.
text.KJV: ¶ And while hee yet spake with them, Rachel came with her fathers sheepe: for she kept them.
text.NABRE: While he was still talking with them, Rachel arrived with her father’s sheep, for she was the one who tended them.
text.DRB: They were yet speaking, and behold Rachel came with her father's sheep: for she fed the flock.

text.NRSV: Now when Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of his mother’s brother Laban, and the sheep of his mother’s brother Laban, Jacob went up and rolled the stone from the well’s mouth, and watered the flock of his mother’s brother Laban.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Jacob saw Rachel daughter of Laban, his mother’s brother, and the sheep of Laban, his mother’s brother, that Jacob went near, and rolled away the stone from off the mouth of the well, and watered the sheep of Laban his mother’s brother,
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when Iacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mothers brother, and the sheepe of Laban his mothers brother; that Iacob went neere, and rolled the stone from the wels mouth, and watered the flocke of Laban his mothers brother.
text.NABRE: As soon as Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of his mother’s brother Laban, and the sheep of Laban, he went up, rolled the stone away from the mouth of the well, and watered Laban’s sheep.
text.DRB: Whom when Jacob had seen, & knew her to be his cousin-german, & that they were the sheep of Laban his uncle, he removed the stone, wherewith the well was closed.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob kissed Rachel, and wept aloud.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob kissed Rachel,—and lifted up his voice, and wept.
text.KJV: And Iacob kissed Rachel, and lifted vp his voyce, and wept.
text.NABRE: Then Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.
text.DRB: And having watered the flock, he kissed her: and lifting up his voice wept,

text.NRSV: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father’s kinsman, and that he was Rebekah’s son; and she ran and told her father.
text.Rotherham: And when Jacob told Rachel that he was her father’s brother, and that he was Rebekah’s son, then ran she, and told her father.
text.KJV: And Iacob told Rachel, that hee was her fathers brother, and that hee was Rebekahs sonne: and she ranne, and told her father.
text.NABRE: Jacob told Rachel that he was her father’s relative, Rebekah’s son. So she ran to tell her father.
text.DRB: and he told her that he was her father's brother, and the son of Rebecca: but she in haste went & told her father.

text.NRSV: When Laban heard the news about his sister’s son Jacob, he ran to meet him; he embraced him and kissed him, and brought him to his house. Jacob told Laban all these things,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when Laban heard the tidings of Jacob his sister’s son, that he ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fondly kissed him, and brought him into his house,—when he recounted to Laban all these things.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when Laban heard the tidings of Iacob his sisters sonne, that he ranne to meete him, and imbraced him, and kissed him, & brought him to his house: and hee tolde Laban all these things.
text.NABRE: When Laban heard the news about Jacob, his sister’s son, he ran to meet him. After embracing and kissing him, he brought him to his house. Jacob then repeated to Laban all these things,
text.DRB: Who when he heard that Jacob his sister's son was come, he ran forth to meet him, & embracing him & heartily kissing him, brought him into his house. And when he had heard the causes of his journey,

text.NRSV: and Laban said to him, “Surely you are my bone and my flesh!” And he stayed with him a month.
text.Rotherham: And Laban said to him, Surely, my bone and my flesh, art thou. So he abode with him a month of days.
text.KJV: And Laban said to him, Surely thou art my bone and my flesh: and he abode with him the space of a moneth.
text.NABRE: and Laban said to him, “You are indeed my bone and my flesh.” Marriage to Leah and Rachel. After Jacob had stayed with him a full month,
text.DRB: he answered: Thou art my bone and my flesh. And after the days of one month were expired,

text.NRSV: Then Laban said to Jacob, “Because you are my kinsman, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Laban to Jacob, Is it because my brother, thou art, that thou shouldst serve me for naught? Come tell me! What shall be thy wages?
text.KJV: ¶ And Laban said vnto Iacob, Because thou art my brother, shouldest thou therefore serue me for nought? tell me, what shall thy wages be?
text.NABRE: Laban said to him: “Should you serve me for nothing just because you are a relative of mine? Tell me what your wages should be.”
text.DRB: he said to him: because thou art my brother, shalt thou serve me gratis? Tell me, what wages wilt thou take?

text.NRSV: Now Laban had two daughters; the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
text.Rotherham: Now, Laban, had two daughters,—the name of the elder, Leah, and the name of the younger, Rachel,
text.KJV: And Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the yonger was Rachel.
text.NABRE: Now Laban had two daughters; the older was called Leah, the younger Rachel.
text.DRB: He had indeed two daughters, the name of the elder was Lia; & the younger was called Rachel.

text.NRSV: Leah’s eyes were lovely, and Rachel was graceful and beautiful.
text.Rotherham: But, the eyes of Leah were weak,—whereas, Rachel, was comely in form and comely in countenance.
text.KJV: Leah was tender eyed: but Rachel was beautiful and well fauoured.
text.NABRE: Leah had dull eyes, but Rachel was shapely and beautiful.
text.DRB: But Lia was blear-eyed, Rachel well favoured, & of a beautiful countenance.

text.NRSV: Jacob loved Rachel; so he said, “I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.”
text.Rotherham: So Jacob loved Rachel,—and he said, I will serve thee seven years, for Rachel thy younger daughter.
text.KJV: And Iacob loued Rachel, and said, I will serue thee seuen yeeres for Rachel thy yonger daughter.
text.NABRE: Because Jacob loved Rachel, he answered, “I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.”
text.DRB: Whom Jacob loving, said: I will serve thee for Rachel thy younger daughter seven years.

text.NRSV: Laban said, “It is better that I give her to you than that I should give her to any other man; stay with me.”
text.Rotherham: And Laban said, Better that I give her to thee, than that I should give her to another man,—Abide with me!
text.KJV: And Laban said, It is better that I giue her to thee, then that I should giue her to another man: abide with mee.
text.NABRE: Laban replied, “It is better to give her to you than to another man. Stay with me.”
text.DRB: Laban answered: It is better that I give her to thee than to another man, tarry with me.

text.NRSV: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob served for Rachel—seven years,—and they became, in his eyes, as single days, for his love to her.
text.KJV: And Iacob serued seuen yeeres for Rachel: and they seemed vnto him but a few dayes, for the loue hee had to her.
text.NABRE: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, yet they seemed to him like a few days because of his love for her.
text.DRB: Jacob therefore served for Rachel seven years: and they seemed a few days because of the greatness of his love.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife that I may go in to her, for my time is completed.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jacob unto Laban: Come give me my wife, for fulfilled are my days,—that I may go in unto her.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob said vnto Laban, Giue me my wife (for my dayes are fulfilled) that I may goe in vnto her.
text.NABRE: Then Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife, that I may consummate my marriage with her, for my term is now completed.”
text.DRB: And he said to Laban: give me my wife: because now the time is complete, that I may company with her.

text.NRSV: So Laban gathered together all the people of the place, and made a feast.
text.Rotherham: So Laban gathered together all the men of the place and made a banquet,
text.KJV: And Laban gathered together all the men of the place, and made a feast.
text.NABRE: So Laban invited all the local inhabitants and gave a banquet.
text.DRB: Who having bid a great number of his friends to the feast, made the marriage.

text.NRSV: But in the evening he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob; and he went in to her.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter and brought her in unto him,—and he went in unto her.
text.KJV: And it came to passe in the euening, that he tooke Leah his daughter, and brought her to him, and he went in vnto her.
text.NABRE: At nightfall he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob, and he consummated the marriage with her.
text.DRB: And at night he brought in Lia his daughter to him,

text.NRSV: (Laban gave his maid Zilpah to his daughter Leah to be her maid.)
text.Rotherham: And Laban gave her Zilpah, his handmaid, unto Leah his daughter. as handmaid.
text.KJV: And Laban gaue vnto his daughter Leah, Zilpah his mayde, for a handmayd.
text.NABRE: Laban assigned his maidservant Zilpah to his daughter Leah as her maidservant.
text.DRB: giving his daughter a handmaid, named Zelpha. With whom when Jacob had companied after the manner, when morning was come he saw Lia:

text.NRSV: When morning came, it was Leah! And Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the morning, that lo! it was Leah,—and he said unto Laban, What is this thou hast done to me? Was it not, for Rachel, I served with thee? Wherefore then hast thou deceived me?
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that in the morning, behold it was Leah: and he said to Laban, What is this thou hast done vnto mee? did not I serue with thee for Rachel? wherefore then hast thou beguiled me?
text.NABRE: In the morning, there was Leah! So Jacob said to Laban: “How could you do this to me! Was it not for Rachel that I served you? Why did you deceive me?”
text.DRB: and he said to his father in law: What is it that thou didst mean to do? Did not I serve thee for Rachel? Why hast thou deceived me?

text.NRSV: Laban said, “This is not done in our country—giving the younger before the firstborn.
text.Rotherham: And Laban said, It must not be done so in our place,—to give the later-born before the firstborn.
text.KJV: And Laban said, It must not be so done in our countrey, to giue the yonger, before the first borne.
text.NABRE: Laban replied, “It is not the custom in our country to give the younger daughter before the firstborn.
text.DRB: Laban answered: It is not the custom in this place, that we bestow the younger in marriage first.

text.NRSV: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years.”
text.Rotherham: Fulfil the week of this one,—then must we give thee, the other one also, for the service wherewith thou shalt serve with me, yet seven years more.
