text.NABRE: Jacob was very much frightened. In his anxiety, he divided the people who were with him, as well as his flocks, herds and camels, into two camps.
text.DRB: saying: If Esau come to one troop, and strike it, the other troop that remaineth shall be saved.

text.NRSV: And Jacob said, “O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, O Lord who said to me, ‘Return to your country and to your kindred, and I will do you good,’
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said, God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac,—Yahweh, who wast saying unto me,—Return to thy land and to thy kindred that I may deal well with thee:
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob said, O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, the LORD which saidst vnto me, Returne vnto thy countrey, and to thy kinred, and I will deale well with thee:
text.NABRE: “If Esau should come and attack one camp,” he reasoned, “the remaining camp may still escape.”
text.DRB: And Jacob said: O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac; O Lord that didst say to me: Return into thy land, and into the place of thy nativity, and I will do thee good:

text.NRSV: I am not worthy of the least of all the steadfast love and all the faithfulness that you have shown to your servant, for with only my staff I crossed this Jordan; and now I have become two companies.
text.Rotherham: I am too small for all the lovingkindnesses and for all the faithfulness, which thou hast done unto thy servant,—For with my staff, passed I over this Jordan, But, now, have I become two camps.
text.KJV: I am not worthy of the least of all the mercies, and of all the trueth, which thou hast shewed vnto thy seruant: for with my staffe I passed ouer this Iordan, and now I am become two bands.
text.NABRE: Then Jacob prayed: “God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac! You, Lord, who said to me, ‘Go back to your land and your relatives, and I will be good to you.’
text.DRB: I am inferior to all thy mercies, and thy truth that thou hast fulfilled to thy servant. With my staff I passed over this Jordan: and now with two troops I do return.

text.NRSV: Deliver me, please, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I am afraid of him; he may come and kill us all, the mothers with the children.
text.Rotherham: Deliver me I pray thee out of the hand of my brother out of the hand of Esau, for I, am afraid of him, lest he come in and smite mother as well as sons
text.KJV: Deliuer me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I feare him, lest he will come, and smite me, and the mother with the children.
text.NABRE: I am unworthy of all the acts of kindness and faithfulness that you have performed for your servant: although I crossed the Jordan here with nothing but my staff, I have now grown into two camps.
text.DRB: Deliver me from the hand of my brother Esau, because I am sore afraid of him: lest perhaps he come, and strike the mother with the children.

text.NRSV: Yet you have said, ‘I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which cannot be counted because of their number.’ ”
text.Rotherham: But, thou thyself, saidst,—I will deal well with thee. So will I make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which is not to be counted, for multitude.
text.KJV: And thou saidst, I will surely doe thee good, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbred for multitude.
text.NABRE: Save me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau! Otherwise I fear that he will come and strike me down and the mothers with the children.
text.DRB: Thou didst say that thou wouldst do good to me, and dilate my seed as the sand of the sea, which for multitude cannot be numbered.

text.NRSV: So he spent that night there, and from what he had with him he took a present for his brother Esau,
text.Rotherham: And he tarried there that night, and took of that which came to his hand a present for Esau his brother:
text.KJV: ¶ And he lodged there that same night, and tooke of that which came to his hand, a present for Esau his brother:
text.NABRE: You yourself said, ‘I will be very good to you, and I will make your descendants like the sands of the sea, which are too numerous to count.’”
text.DRB: And when he had slept there that night, he separated of those things which he had, gifts to his brother Esau,

text.NRSV: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
text.Rotherham: She-goats, two hundred; And he-goats, twenty; Ewes, two hundred; And rams, twenty;
text.KJV: Two hundred shee goats, and twentie hee goats, two hundred ewes, and twentie rammes,
text.NABRE: After passing the night there, Jacob selected from what he had with him a present for his brother Esau:
text.DRB: she goats two hundred, he goats twenty, ewes two hundred, and rams twenty,

text.NRSV: thirty milch camels and their colts, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.
text.Rotherham: Milch camels, with their colts, thirty; Cows, forty; And bulls, ten; She-asses, twenty; And young he asses ten.
text.KJV: Thirtie milch camels with their colts, fortie kine, and ten bulles, twenty shee ashes, and ten foales.
text.NABRE: two hundred she-goats and twenty he-goats; two hundred ewes and twenty rams;
text.DRB: thirty milch camels with their colts, forty kine, and twenty bulls, twenty she asses, and their foals ten.

text.NRSV: These he delivered into the hand of his servants, every drove by itself, and said to his servants, “Pass on ahead of me, and put a space between drove and drove.”
text.Rotherham: And he gave them into the hand of his Servants, each drove by itself, and said unto his servants—Pass over before me, and, a breathing-space, shall ye put betwixt drove and drove.
text.KJV: And hee deliuered them into the hand of his seruants, euery droue by themselues, and said vnto his seruants, Passe ouer before me, and put a space betwixt droue and droue.
text.NABRE: thirty female camels and their young; forty cows and ten bulls; twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.
text.DRB: And he sent by the hands of his servants every flock by it-self, and he said to his servants: Go before me, and let there be a space between flock and flock.

text.NRSV: He instructed the foremost, “When Esau my brother meets you, and asks you, ‘To whom do you belong? Where are you going? And whose are these ahead of you?’
text.Rotherham: And he commanded the first one saying: When Esau my brother shall fall in with thee, and shall ask thee, saying Whose art thou? and whither wouldst thou go? and to whom belong these before thee?
text.KJV: And he commanded the formost, saying, When Esau my brother meeteth thee, and asketh thee, saying, whose art thou? and whither goest thou? and whose are these before thee?
text.NABRE: He put these animals in the care of his servants, in separate herds, and he told the servants, “Go on ahead of me, but keep some space between the herds.”
text.DRB: And he commanded the former, saying: If thou meet my brother Esau, and he ask thee, whose art thou? Or whither goest thou? Or whose are these that thou doest follow?

text.NRSV: then you shall say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob; they are a present sent to my lord Esau; and moreover he is behind us.’ ”
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou say, To thy servant Jacob: It is, a present, sent to my lord, to Esau; And behold he himself also is behind us,
text.KJV: Then thou shalt say, They be thy seruant Iacobs: it is a present sent vnto my lord Esau: and behold also, he is behind vs.
text.NABRE: He ordered the servant in the lead, “When my brother Esau meets you and asks, ‘To whom do you belong? Where are you going? To whom do these animals ahead of you belong?’
text.DRB: Thou shalt answer: Jacob's thy servant, he hath sent them for gifts to my lord Esau: himself also cometh after us.

text.NRSV: He likewise instructed the second and the third and all who followed the droves, “You shall say the same thing to Esau when you meet him,
text.Rotherham: And he commanded the second also, and the third yea all that were going on behind the droves, saying,—According to this word, shall ye speak unto Esau, when ye find him.
text.KJV: And so commanded he the second, and the third, and all that followed the droues, saying, On this maner shal you speake vnto Esau, when you find him.
text.NABRE: tell him, ‘To your servant Jacob, but they have been sent as a gift to my lord Esau. Jacob himself is right behind us.’”
text.DRB: In like manner he gave commandments to the second, and the third, and to all that followed the flocks, saying: With the self-same words speak ye to Esau, when you shall find him.

text.NRSV: and you shall say, ‘Moreover your servant Jacob is behind us.’ ” For he thought, “I may appease him with the present that goes ahead of me, and afterwards I shall see his face; perhaps he will accept me.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye say, Also, lo! thy servant Jacob is behind us. For he said: I must pacify him with the present that goeth on before me. And, after that, will I see his face: Peradventure he will accept me.
text.KJV: And say ye moreouer, Beholde, thy seruant Iacob is behind vs: for he said, I will appease him with the present that goeth before me, and afterward I will see his face; peraduenture he will accept of me.
text.NABRE: He also ordered the second servant and the third and all the others who followed behind the herds: “Thus and so you shall say to Esau, when you reach him;
text.DRB: And ye shall add: Jacob also thy servant himself followeth on after us; for he said: I will pacify him with the gifts that go before, and afterward I will see him, perhaps he will be gracious unto me.

text.NRSV: So the present passed on ahead of him; and he himself spent that night in the camp.
text.Rotherham: So the present passed over before him,—whereas he himself tarried that night in the camp.
text.KJV: So went the present ouer before him: and himselfe lodged that night in the company.
text.NABRE: and also tell him, ‘Your servant Jacob is right behind us.’” For Jacob reasoned, “If I first appease him with a gift that precedes me, then later, when I face him, perhaps he will forgive me.”
text.DRB: The gifts therefore went before him, but himself lodged that night in the camp.

text.NRSV: The same night he got up and took his two wives, his two maids, and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
text.Rotherham: And he arose in that night and took his two wives, and his two handmaids, and his eleven sons,—and passed over the ford of Jabbok.
text.KJV: And hee rose vp that night, and tooke his two wiues, and his two women seruants, and his eleuen sonnes, and passed ouer the foord Iabbok.
text.NABRE: So the gifts went on ahead of him, while he stayed that night in the camp. Jacob’s New Name.
text.DRB: And when he was risen early, he took his two wives, and his handmaids as many, with his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Iaboc.

text.NRSV: He took them and sent them across the stream, and likewise everything that he had.
text.Rotherham: So he took them, and sent them over the brook,—and sent over that which he had.
text.KJV: And he tooke them, and sent them ouer the brooke, and sent ouer that hee had.
text.NABRE: That night, however, Jacob arose, took his two wives, with the two maidservants and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
text.DRB: And when he had set over all things that appertained to him,

text.NRSV: Jacob was left alone; and a man wrestled with him until daybreak.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob was left alone,—and there wrestled a man with him, until the uprisings of the dawn.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob was left alone: and there wrestled a man with him, vntill the breaking of the day.
text.NABRE: After he got them and brought them across the wadi and brought over what belonged to him,
text.DRB: he tarried alone: and behold a man wrestled with him till morning.

text.NRSV: When the man saw that he did not prevail against Jacob, he struck him on the hip socket; and Jacob’s hip was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.
text.Rotherham: And when he saw, that he prevailed not against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh,—and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was put out of joint, as he wrestled with him.
text.KJV: And when he saw, that he preuailed not against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh: and the hollow of Iacobs thigh was out of ioynt, as hee wrestled with him.
text.NABRE: Jacob was left there alone. Then a man wrestled with him until the break of dawn.
text.DRB: Who when he saw that he could not overcome him, he touched the sinew of his thigh, and forthwith it shrank.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Let me go, for the day is breaking.” But Jacob said, “I will not let you go, unless you bless me.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he—Let me go, for uprisen hath the dawn. And he said: I will not let thee go, except thou have blessed me
text.KJV: And he said, Let me goe, for the day breaketh: and he said, I will not let thee goe, except thou blesse me.
text.NABRE: When the man saw that he could not prevail over him, he struck Jacob’s hip at its socket, so that Jacob’s socket was dislocated as he wrestled with him.
text.DRB: And he said to him: Let me go for it is break of day. He answered: I will not let thee go, unless thou bless me.

text.NRSV: So he said to him, “What is your name?” And he said, “Jacob.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said unto him—What is thy name? And he said—Jacob.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, what is thy name? and he said, Iacob.
text.NABRE: The man then said, “Let me go, for it is daybreak.” But Jacob said, “I will not let you go until you bless me.”
text.DRB: He therefore said: What is thy name? He answered: Jacob.

text.NRSV: Then the man said, “You shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with humans, and have prevailed.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said—Not Jacob, shall thy name be called any more, but Israel,—For thou hast contended with God and with men and hast prevailed.
text.KJV: And he said, Thy name shall be called no more Iacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God, and with men, and hast preuailed.
text.NABRE: “What is your name?” the man asked. He answered, “Jacob.”
text.DRB: But he, no, thy name, quoth he, shall not be called Jacob. But Israel: for if thou hast been strong against God, how much more shalt thou prevail against men?

text.NRSV: Then Jacob asked him, “Please tell me your name.” But he said, “Why is it that you ask my name?” And there he blessed him.
text.Rotherham: Then asked Jacob and said, Do, I pray thee, tell me thy name! And he said—Wherefore now, shouldest thou ask for my name? And he blessed him there,
text.KJV: And Iacob asked him, and saide, Tell me, I pray thee, thy name: and he said, wherefore is it, that thou doest aske after my name? and he blessed him there.
text.NABRE: Then the man said, “You shall no longer be named Jacob, but Israel, because you have contended with divine and human beings and have prevailed.”
text.DRB: Jacob asked him: Tell me by what name art thou called? He answered: Why dost thou ask my name? and blessed him in the same place.

text.NRSV: So Jacob called the place Peniel, saying, “For I have seen God face to face, and yet my life is preserved.”
text.Rotherham: So Jacob called the name of the place Peniel; For I saw God face to face; and my soul was delivered,
text.KJV: And Iacob called the name of the place Peniel: for I haue seene God face to face, and my life is preserued.
text.NABRE: Jacob then asked him, “Please tell me your name.” He answered, “Why do you ask for my name?” With that, he blessed him.
text.DRB: And Jacob called the name of the place Phanuel, saying: I have seen God face to face, and my soul was made safe.

text.NRSV: The sun rose upon him as he passed Penuel, limping because of his hip.
text.Rotherham: And the sun rose on him as soon as he had passed over Penuel,—he, moreover was halting upon his thigh,
text.KJV: And as he passed ouer Penuel, the sunne rose vpon him, and he halted vpon his thigh.
text.NABRE: Jacob named the place Peniel, “because I have seen God face to face,” he said, “yet my life has been spared.”
text.DRB: And immediately the sun rose to him, after that he was past Phanuel; but he halted on his foot.

text.NRSV: Therefore to this day the Israelites do not eat the thigh muscle that is on the hip socket, because he struck Jacob on the hip socket at the thigh muscle.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, the sons of Israel eat not of the nerve of the large hip-sinew, which is by the hollow of the thigh, until this day,—because he touched the hollow of the thigh of Jacob, in the nerve of the hip-sinew.
text.KJV: Therefore the children of Israel eate not of the sinewe which shranke, which is vpon the hollow of the thigh, vnto this day: because hee touched the hollow of Iacobs thigh, in the sinewe that shranke.
text.NABRE: At sunrise, as he left Penuel, Jacob limped along because of his hip.
text.DRB: For which cause the children of Israel eat not the sinew, that shrunk in Jacob's thigh, unto this present day: because he touched the sinew of his thigh, and it shrunk.

text.NRSV: Now Jacob looked up and saw Esau coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two maids.
text.Rotherham: Then Jacob lifted up his eyes and looked, and lo! Esau, coming in,—and with him, four hundred men. So he divided the children—unto Leah and unto Rachel, and unto the two handmaids;
text.KJV: And Iacob lifted vp his eyes, and looked, and behold, Esau came, and with him foure hundreth men: and hee diuided the children vnto Leah, and vnto Rachel, and vnto the two handmaids.
text.NABRE: Jacob looked up and saw Esau coming, and with him four hundred men. So he divided his children among Leah, Rachel, and the two maidservants,
text.DRB: And Jacob lifting up his eyes, saw Esau coming, and with him four hundred men: and he divided the children of Lia, and of Rachel, and of the two handmaids:

text.NRSV: He put the maids with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
text.Rotherham: and put the handmaids and their children first,—and Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph next;
text.KJV: And he put the handmaides, and their chidren foremost, and Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Ioseph hindermost.
text.NABRE: putting the maidservants and their children first, Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph last.
text.DRB: and he put both the handmaids and their children foremost: and Lia and her children in the second place: and Rachel and Joseph last.

text.NRSV: He himself went on ahead of them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near his brother.
text.Rotherham: but he himself, passed over before them, and bowed himself to the earth seven times, until he had come near unto his brother.
text.KJV: And hee passed ouer before them, and bowed himselfe to the ground seuen times, vntill hee came neere to his brother.
text.NABRE: He himself went on ahead of them, bowing to the ground seven times, until he reached his brother.
text.DRB: And himself going forward adored prostrate to the ground seven times, until his brother came near.

text.NRSV: But Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
text.Rotherham: Then ran Esau to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck,—and they wept.
text.KJV: And Esau ran to meete him, and imbraced him, and fell on his necke, and kissed him, and they wept.
text.NABRE: Esau ran to meet him, embraced him, and flinging himself on his neck, kissed him as he wept.
text.DRB: Esau therefore running to meet his brother, embraced him: and clasping him fast about the neck, and kissing him wept.

text.NRSV: When Esau looked up and saw the women and children, he said, “Who are these with you?” Jacob said, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”
text.Rotherham: Then lifted he up his eyes, and beheld the women and the children, and said What are these to thee? And he said—The children wherewith God hath favoured thy servant,
text.KJV: And he lift vp his eyes, and sawe the women, and the children, and said, who are those with thee? And he said, The children which God hath graciously giuen thy seruant.
text.NABRE: Then Esau looked up and saw the women and children and asked, “Who are these with you?” Jacob answered, “They are the children with whom God has graciously favored your servant.”
text.DRB: And casting up his eyes he saw the women and their little ones, and said: What mean these? And do they pertain to thee? He answered: They are the little ones which God hath given to me thy servant.

text.NRSV: Then the maids drew near, they and their children, and bowed down;
text.Rotherham: Then came near the handmaids, they—and their children,—and bowed themselves.
text.KJV: Then the handmaidens came neere; they and their children, and they bowed themselues.
text.NABRE: Then the maidservants and their children came forward and bowed low;
text.DRB: And the handmaids and their children coming near bowed themselves.

text.NRSV: Leah likewise and her children drew near and bowed down; and finally Joseph and Rachel drew near, and they bowed down.
text.Rotherham: Then came near Leah also with her children,—and they bowed themselves. And afterwards, came near Joseph with Rachel, and they bowed themselves,
text.KJV: And Leah also with her children came neere, and bowed themselues: and after came Ioseph neere and Rachel, and they bowed themselues.
text.NABRE: next, Leah and her children came forward and bowed low; lastly, Joseph and Rachel came forward and bowed low.
text.DRB: Lia also with her children came near: and when they had adored in like manner, last Joseph and Rachel adored.

text.NRSV: Esau said, “What do you mean by all this company that I met?” Jacob answered, “To find favor with my lord.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he, What to thee is all this camp, which I have fallen in with? And he said,—To find favour in the eyes of my lord.
text.KJV: And he said, What meanest thou by all this droue, which I met? And he said, These are to find grace in the sight of my lord.
text.NABRE: Then Esau asked, “What did you intend with all those herds that I encountered?” Jacob answered, “It was to gain my lord’s favor.”
text.DRB: And Esau said: What are the troops that I did meet? He answered: That I might find grace before my lord.

text.NRSV: But Esau said, “I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Esau, I have an abundance,—my brother, be thine what thou hast!
text.KJV: And Esau said, I haue enough: my brother, keepe that thou hast vnto thy selfe.
text.NABRE: Esau replied, “I have plenty; my brother, you should keep what is yours.”
text.DRB: But he said: I have plenty, my brother, be thy things to thyself,

text.NRSV: Jacob said, “No, please; if I find favor with you, then accept my present from my hand; for truly to see your face is like seeing the face of God—since you have received me with such favor.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jacob, Nay, I pray thee, if, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, then thou wilt take my present at my hand,—For on this account, hath my seeing thy face been like seeing the face of God in that thou wast well-pleased with me.
text.KJV: And Iacob saide, Nay, I pray thee: if now I haue found grace in thy sight, then receiue my present at my hand: for therefore I haue seene thy face, as though I had seene the face of God; and thou wast pleased with me.
text.NABRE: “No, I beg you!” said Jacob. “If you will do me the favor, accept this gift from me, since to see your face is for me like seeing the face of God—and you have received me so kindly.
text.DRB: And Jacob said: Do not so, I beseech thee, but if I have found grace in thine eyes, take a little present at my hands: for so have I seen thy face as if I should have seen the countenance of God: be gracious to me,

text.NRSV: Please accept my gift that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have everything I want.” So he urged him, and he took it.
text.Rotherham: Take, I pray thee my blessing which hath been brought in to thee, because God hath shewed me favour and because I have everything. So he was urgent with him and he took it.
text.KJV: Take, I pray thee, my blessing that is brought to thee; because God hath dealt graciously with mee, and because I haue enough: and hee vrged him, and he tooke it.
text.NABRE: Accept the gift I have brought you. For God has been generous toward me, and I have an abundance.” Since he urged him strongly, Esau accepted.
text.DRB: and take the blessing which I have brought thee, and which God hath given me, who giveth all things. Scarce at his brother's great instance taking it,

text.NRSV: Then Esau said, “Let us journey on our way, and I will go alongside you.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Let us break up and go, and let me go on before thee.
text.KJV: And he said, Let vs take our iourney, and let vs goe, and I will goe before thee.
text.NABRE: Then Esau said, “Let us break camp and be on our way; I will travel in front of you.”
text.DRB: he said: let us march on together, and I will accompany thee in thy journey.

text.NRSV: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail and that the flocks and herds, which are nursing, are a care to me; and if they are overdriven for one day, all the flocks will die.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, My lord, is taking note, that, the children are tender,—and the flocks and the herds are giving suck with me,—and, should I overdrive them a single day, then would all the flocks die.
text.KJV: And hee said vnto him, My lord knoweth, that the children are tender, and the flockes and heards with yong are with mee: and if men should ouer-driue them one day, all the flocke will die.
text.NABRE: But Jacob replied: “As my lord knows, the children are too young. And the flocks and herds that are nursing are a concern to me; if overdriven for even a single day, the whole flock will die.
text.DRB: And Jacob said: My lord thou knowest that I have with me little ones, and sheep, and kine with young: which if I cause to overlabour themselves in going, in one day all the flocks will die.

text.NRSV: Let my lord pass on ahead of his servant, and I will lead on slowly, according to the pace of the cattle that are before me and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.”
text.Rotherham: Let my lord, I pray thee pass over before his servant, and let, me, lead on in my own easy way, at the pace of the cattle that are before me, and at the pace of the children, until that I come in unto my lord, towards Seir.
text.KJV: Let my lord, I pray thee, passe ouer before his seruant, and I will leade on softly, according as the cattell that goeth before me, and the children be able to endure, vntill I come vnto my lord vnto Seir.
text.NABRE: Let my lord, then, go before his servant, while I proceed more slowly at the pace of the livestock before me and at the pace of my children, until I join my lord in Seir.”
text.DRB: It may please my lord to go before his servant: and I will follow softly after him, as I shall see my little ones to be able, until I come to my lord in Seir.

text.NRSV: So Esau said, “Let me leave with you some of the people who are with me.” But he said, “Why should my lord be so kind to me?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Esau: Let me leave, I pray thee, along with thee, some of the people who are with me! And he said—Why so? let me find favour in the eyes of my lord!
text.KJV: And Esau said, Let me now leaue with thee some of the folke that are with me: And hee said, What needeth it? let me finde grace in the sight of my lord.
text.NABRE: Esau replied, “Let me at least put at your disposal some of the people who are with me.” But Jacob said, “Why is this that I am treated so kindly, my lord?”
text.DRB: Esau answered: I beseech thee, that of my people at the leastwise, which is with me, there may remain some to accompany thee in the way. It is not needful, said he, this only I have need of, that I may find grace (my lord) in thy sight.

text.NRSV: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
text.Rotherham: So Esau returned, that day, to his journey towards Mount Seir.
text.KJV: ¶ So Esau returned that day, on his way vnto Seir.
text.NABRE: So on that day Esau went on his way back to Seir,
text.DRB: Esau therefore returned that day the same way that he came, into Seir.

text.NRSV: But Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house, and made booths for his cattle; therefore the place is called Succoth.
text.Rotherham: But, Jacob, brake up towards Succoth, and built for himself a house, for his cattle, made he places of shelter, for which cause, the name of the place was called, Succoth.
text.KJV: And Iacob iourneyed to Succoth, and built him an house, and made boothes for his cattell: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
text.NABRE: and Jacob broke camp for Succoth. There Jacob built a home for himself and made booths for his livestock. That is why the place was named Succoth.
text.DRB: And Jacob cometh into Socoth: where having built a house, and pitched his tents, he called the name of that place Socoth, that is, Tabernacles.

text.NRSV: Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, on his way from Paddan-aram; and he camped before the city.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob came in peace to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan,—when he came in from Padan-aram,—and he encamped before the city.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob came to Shalem, a citie of Shechem, which is the land of Canaan, when he came from Padan Aram, and pitched his tent before the Citie.
text.NABRE: Jacob arrived safely at the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Paddan-aram. He encamped in sight of the city.
text.DRB: and he passed into Salem a city of the Sichimites, which is in the land of Chanaan, after he returned from Mesopotamia of Syria: and he dwelt beside the town.

text.NRSV: And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem’s father, he bought for one hundred pieces of money the plot of land on which he had pitched his tent.
text.Rotherham: And he bought the portion of the field where he had spread out his tent, at the hand of the sons of Hamor, father of Shechem,—for a hundred kesitahs.
text.KJV: And he bought a parcell of a field where hee had spread his tent, at the hand of the children of Hamor Shechems father, for an hundred pieces of money.
text.NABRE: The plot of ground on which he had pitched his tent he bought for a hundred pieces of money from the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem.
text.DRB: And he bought that part of the field, wherein he had pitched his tents, of the children of Hemor, the father of Sichem, for an hundred lambs.

text.NRSV: There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
text.Rotherham: And he set up there an altar,—and called it, El-elohe-Israel.
text.KJV: And hee erected there an Altar, and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
text.NABRE: He set up an altar there and invoked “El, the God of Israel.”
text.DRB: And erecting an altar there, on it he called upon the most Mighty God of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now Dinah the daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to visit the women of the region.
text.Rotherham: Then went forth Dinah, the daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Jacob,—to see the daughters of the land.
text.KJV: And Dinah the daughter of Leah, which shee bare vnto Iacob, went out to see the daughters of the land.
text.NABRE: Dinah, the daughter whom Leah had borne to Jacob, went out to visit some of the women of the land.
text.DRB: And Dina the daughter of Lia went forth to see the women of that country.

text.NRSV: When Shechem son of Hamor the Hivite, prince of the region, saw her, he seized her and lay with her by force.
text.Rotherham: And Shechem, son of Hamor the Hivite, prince of the land, saw her,—so he took her, and lay with her, and humbled her.
text.KJV: And when Shechem the sonne of Hamor the Hiuite, prince of the countrey saw her, he tooke her, and lay with her, and defiled her.
text.NABRE: When Shechem, son of Hamor the Hivite, the leader of the region, saw her, he seized her and lay with her by force.
text.DRB: Whom when Sichem had seen, the son of Hemor the Hevite, the Prince of that land, he was in love with her: and he took her away, and lay with her, by force ravishing the virgin.

text.NRSV: And his soul was drawn to Dinah daughter of Jacob; he loved the girl, and spoke tenderly to her.
text.Rotherham: And his soul clave unto Dinah, daughter of Jacob,—and he loved the young woman, and spake to the heart of the young woman.
text.KJV: And his soule claue vnto Dinah the daughter of Iacob, and hee loued the damsell, and spake kindly vnto the damsell.
text.NABRE: He was strongly attracted to Dinah, daughter of Jacob, and was in love with the young woman. So he spoke affectionately to her.
text.DRB: And his soul was fast knit unto her, and whereas she was sad, he comforted her with sweet words.

text.NRSV: So Shechem spoke to his father Hamor, saying, “Get me this girl to be my wife.”
text.Rotherham: Then spake Shechem unto Hamor his father, saying,—Take me this girl to wife!
text.KJV: And Shechem spake vnto his father Hamor, saying, Get mee this damsell to wife.
text.NABRE: Shechem said to his father Hamor, “Get me this young woman for a wife.”
text.DRB: And going to Hemor his father, he said: Take me this wench to be my wife.

text.NRSV: Now Jacob heard that Shechem had defiled his daughter Dinah; but his sons were with his cattle in the field, so Jacob held his peace until they came.
text.Rotherham: Now, Jacob, had heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter, but, his sons, happened to be with his cattle in the field,—so Jacob kept quiet until they came in.
text.KJV: And Iacob heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter (now his sonnes were with his cattel in the field) and Iacob helde his peace vntill they were come.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, Jacob heard that Shechem had defiled his daughter Dinah; but since his sons were out in the field with his livestock, Jacob kept quiet until they came home.
text.DRB: Which when Jacob had heard, his sons being absent and occupied in feeding of the cattle, he held his peace till they returned.

text.NRSV: And Hamor the father of Shechem went out to Jacob to speak with him,
text.Rotherham: Then came forth Hamor, father of Shechem, unto Jacob,—to speak with him.
text.KJV: ¶ And Hamor the father of Shechem went out vnto Iacob to commune with him.
text.NABRE: Now Hamor, the father of Shechem, went out to discuss the matter with Jacob,
text.DRB: And when Hemor, Sichem's father was come forth to speak unto Jacob,

text.NRSV: just as the sons of Jacob came in from the field. When they heard of it, the men were indignant and very angry, because he had committed an outrage in Israel by lying with Jacob’s daughter, for such a thing ought not to be done.
text.Rotherham: Now the sons of Jacob came in from the field as soon as they heard, and the men were grieved, and it was vexing to them exceedingly,—for, a disgraceful deed, had he done with Israel in lying with Jacob’s daughter, seeing that so, it should not be done.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Iacob came out of the field when they heard it, and the men were grieued: and they were very wroth, because hee had wrought folly in Israel, in lying with Iacobs daughter; which thing ought not to be done.
text.NABRE: just as Jacob’s sons were coming in from the field. When they heard the news, the men were indignant and extremely angry. Shechem had committed an outrage in Israel by lying with Jacob’s daughter; such a thing is not done.
text.DRB: behold his sons came out of the field; and hearing what had passed, they were passing wrath, because he had done a foul thing in Israel, and committed an unlawful fact in ravishing Jacob's daughter.

text.NRSV: But Hamor spoke with them, saying, “The heart of my son Shechem longs for your daughter; please give her to him in marriage.
text.Rotherham: So then Hamor spake with them saying—As for Shechem my son, his soul hath become attached to your daughter,—I pray you give her to him, to wife.
text.KJV: And Hamor communed with them, saying, The soule of my sonne Shechem longeth for your daughter: I pray you giue her him to wife.
text.NABRE: Hamor appealed to them, saying: “My son Shechem has his heart set on your daughter. Please give her to him as a wife.
text.DRB: Hemor therefore spake to them: The soul of my son Sichem is fastened to your daughter: Give her unto him to wife:

text.NRSV: Make marriages with us; give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.
text.Rotherham: And exchange ye daughters with us in marriage,—your daughters, shall ye give to us, and our daughters, shall ye take to you.
text.KJV: And make ye mariages with vs, and giue your daughters vnto vs, and take our daughters vnto you.
text.NABRE: Intermarry with us; give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.
text.DRB: and let us contract marriages one with another: give us your daughters, and take you our daughters.

text.NRSV: You shall live with us; and the land shall be open to you; live and trade in it, and get property in it.”
text.Rotherham: And with us, shall ye dwell,—and, the land, shall be before you, tarry, and go through it, and get possessions therein.
text.KJV: And ye shall dwell with vs, and the land shall be before you: dwell and trade you therein, and get you possessions therein.
text.NABRE: Thus you can live among us. The land is open before you. Settle and move about freely in it and acquire holdings here.”
text.DRB: And dwell with us: the land is at your commandment, till, occupy, and possess it.

text.NRSV: Shechem also said to her father and to her brothers, “Let me find favor with you, and whatever you say to me I will give.
text.Rotherham: Then said Shechem unto her father and unto her brethren, Let me find favour in your eyes,—and, whatsoever ye may say unto me, will I give:
text.KJV: And Shechem said vnto her father, and vnto her brethren, Let mee finde grace in your eyes, and what yee shall say vnto me, I will giue.
text.NABRE: Then Shechem appealed to Dinah’s father and brothers: “Do me this favor, and whatever you ask from me, I will give.
text.DRB: Yea and Sichem also said to her father and to her brethren: Let me find grace in your sight: and what soever you shall appoint I will give:

text.NRSV: Put the marriage present and gift as high as you like, and I will give whatever you ask me; only give me the girl to be my wife.”
text.Rotherham: Heap on me, exceedingly, dowry and gift, and I will give, just as ye may say unto me,—but give me the young woman, to wife.
text.KJV: Aske mee neuer so much dowrie and gift, and I will giue according as yee shall say vnto mee: but giue me the damsell to wife.
text.NABRE: No matter how high you set the bridal price and gift, I will give you whatever you ask from me; only give me the young woman as a wife.”
text.DRB: raise the dowry, and require gifts, and I shall gladly give what you shall demand: only give me this wench to wife.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jacob answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father deceitfully, and spake (because he had defiled Dinah their sister)—
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Iacob answered Shechem, and Hamor his father deceitfully, and said, because he had defiled Dinah their sister.
text.NABRE: Jacob’s sons replied to Shechem and his father Hamor with guile, speaking as they did because he had defiled their sister Dinah.
text.DRB: Jacob's sons answered Sichem & his father in guile, being wrath for the deflowering of their sister:

text.NRSV: They said to them, “We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us.
text.Rotherham: and said unto them—We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to a man that is uncircumcised,—for a reproach, it would be to us.
text.KJV: And they saide vnto them, wee cannot doe this thing, to giue our sister to one that is vncircumcised: for that were a reproch vnto vs.
text.NABRE: They said to them, “We are not able to do this thing: to give our sister to an uncircumcised man. For that would be a disgrace for us.
text.DRB: We cannot do that which you demand, nor give our sister to an uncircumcised person; which with us is an unlawful & abominable thing.

text.NRSV: Only on this condition will we consent to you: that you will become as we are and every male among you be circumcised.
text.Rotherham: Only herein, can we consent to you,—If ye will become like us, by circumcising to you every male,
text.KJV: But in this will we consent vnto you: If ye will be as we be, that euery male of you be circumcised:
text.NABRE: Only on this condition will we agree to that: that you become like us by having every male among you circumcised.
text.DRB: But in this order we may be considerate, if you will be like to us, and all the man sex among you be circumcised:

text.NRSV: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters for ourselves, and we will live among you and become one people.
text.Rotherham: then will we give our daughters to you, and your daughters, will we take to us,—and we will dwell with you, and become one people.
text.KJV: Then wil we giue our daughters vnto you, and we wil take your daughters to vs, and we will dwell with you, and we will become one people.
text.NABRE: Then we will give you our daughters and take your daughters in marriage; we will settle among you and become one people.
text.DRB: then will we give and take mutually your daughters, and ours: and we will dwell with you, and will be one people:

text.NRSV: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our daughter and be gone.”
text.Rotherham: But if ye will not hearken unto us, to be circumcised, then will we take our daughter and be gone.
text.KJV: But if ye will not hearken vnto vs, to be circumcised, then will we take our daughter, and we will be gone.
text.NABRE: But if you do not listen to us and be circumcised, we will take our daughter and go.”
text.DRB: but if you will not be circumcised, we will take our daughter, and depart.

text.NRSV: Their words pleased Hamor and Hamor’s son Shechem.
text.Rotherham: And their words looked fair in the eyes of Hamor,—and in the eyes of Shechem, Hamor’s son;
text.KJV: And their words pleased Hamor, and Shechem Hamors sonne.
text.NABRE: Their proposal pleased Hamor and his son Shechem.
text.DRB: The offer pleased Hemor and Sichem his son:

text.NRSV: And the young man did not delay to do the thing, because he was delighted with Jacob’s daughter. Now he was the most honored of all his family.
text.Rotherham: and the young man deferred not to do the thing, because he had delight in Jacob’s daughter,—he, moreover being more honourable than all the house of his father.
text.KJV: And the yong man deferred not to doe the thing, because he had delight in Iacobs daughter: and he was more honourable then all the house of his father.
text.NABRE: The young man lost no time in acting on the proposal, since he wanted Jacob’s daughter. Now he was more highly regarded than anyone else in his father’s house.
text.DRB: neither did the young man make delay, but forthwith fulfilled that which was demanded: for he loved the wench exceedingly, and he was the greatest man in all his father's house.

text.NRSV: So Hamor and his son Shechem came to the gate of their city and spoke to the men of their city, saying,
text.Rotherham: So Hamor entered with Shechem his son into the gate of their city,—and they spake unto the men of their city, saying:
text.KJV: ¶ And Hamor and Shechem his sonne came vnto the gate of their citie, and communed with the men of their citie, saying:
text.NABRE: So Hamor and his son Shechem went to the gate of their city and said to the men of their city:
text.DRB: And going into the gate of the city, they spake to the people:

text.NRSV: “These people are friendly with us; let them live in the land and trade in it, for the land is large enough for them; let us take their daughters in marriage, and let us give them our daughters.
text.Rotherham: As for these men, in peace, they are with us—let them therefore dwell in the land and go through it, for, the land, lo! it is roomy on both hands before them,—their daughters, let us take to us for wives, and our daughters, let us give to them.
text.KJV: These men are peaceable with vs, therefore let them dwel in the land, and trade therein: for the land, behold, it is large enough for them: let vs take their daughters to vs for wiues, and let vs giue them our daughters.
text.NABRE: “These men are friendly toward us. Let them settle in the land and move about in it freely; there is ample room in the land for them. We can take their daughters in marriage and give our daughters to them.
text.DRB: These men are men of peace, and are willing to dwell with us: let them occupy in the land, and till it, which being large and wide doth lack men to till it: their daughters we shall take to wife, and ours we will give to them.

text.NRSV: Only on this condition will they agree to live among us, to become one people: that every male among us be circumcised as they are circumcised.
text.Rotherham: Only herein, will the men consent to us, to dwell with us, to become one people,—If we circumcise to us every male, even as they are circumcised.
text.KJV: Onely herein will the men consent vnto vs, for to dwell with vs to be one people, if euery male among vs bee circumcised, as they are circumcised.
text.NABRE: But only on this condition will the men agree to live with us and form one people with us: that every male among us be circumcised as they themselves are.
text.DRB: One thing there is for the which so great a good is deferred: If we circumcise our men sex, following the rite of the nation.

text.NRSV: Will not their livestock, their property, and all their animals be ours? Only let us agree with them, and they will live among us.”
text.Rotherham: Their herds and their substance, and all their beasts, shall they, not be, ours? Only, we must consent to them, that they may dwell with us.
text.KJV: Shall not their cattell, and their substance, and euery beast of theirs bee ours? onely let vs consent vnto them, and they will dwell with vs.
text.NABRE: Would not their livestock, their property, and all their animals then be ours? Let us just agree with them, so that they will settle among us.”
text.DRB: And their substance, and cattle, and all things that they possess, shall be ours: only in this let us condescend, and dwelling together we shall make one people.

text.NRSV: And all who went out of the city gate heeded Hamor and his son Shechem; and every male was circumcised, all who went out of the gate of his city.
text.Rotherham: And they hearkened unto Hamor, and unto Shechem his son, even all that were going forth out of the gate of his city,—and every male was circumcised, all that were going forth out of the gate of his city.
text.KJV: And vnto Hamor and vnto Shechem his sonne, hearkened all that went out of the gate of his citie; and euery male was circumcised, all that went out of the gate of his citie.
text.NABRE: All who went out of the gate of the city listened to Hamor and his son Shechem, and all the males, all those who went out of the gate of the city, were circumcised.
text.DRB: And they all assented, and circumcised all the man sex.

text.NRSV: On the third day, when they were still in pain, two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, took their swords and came against the city unawares, and killed all the males.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the third day, when they were in pain, that two of the sons of Jacob Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, took each man his sword, and came in upon the city, boldly,—and slew every, male;
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe on the thirde day when they were sore, that two of the sonnes of Iacob, Simeon and Leui, Dinahs brethren, tooke each man his sword and came vpon the citie boldly, and slew all the males.
text.NABRE: On the third day, while they were still in pain, two of Jacob’s sons, Simeon and Levi, brothers of Dinah, each took his sword, advanced against the unsuspecting city and massacred all the males.
text.DRB: And behold the third day, when the grief of the wounds is most painful: Jacob's two sons Simeon and Levi, the brothers of Dina, taking their swords, entered into the city boldly: and killing all the man sex,

text.NRSV: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword, and took Dinah out of Shechem’s house, and went away.
text.Rotherham: Hamor also, and Shechem his son, slew they with the edge of the sword,—and took Dinah out of the house of Shechem, and came forth,
text.KJV: And they slew Hamor and Shechem his sonne, with the edge of the sword, and tooke Dinah out of Shechems house, and went out.
text.NABRE: After they had killed Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword, they took Dinah from Shechem’s house and left.
text.DRB: murdered withal Hemor and Sichem, taking away Dina their sister out of Sichem's house.

text.NRSV: And the other sons of Jacob came upon the slain, and plundered the city, because their sister had been defiled.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Jacob, came in upon the slain and plundered the city,—because they had defiled their sister:
text.KJV: The sonnes of Iacob came vpon the slaine, and spoiled the citie, because they had defiled their sister.
text.NABRE: Then the other sons of Jacob followed up the slaughter and sacked the city because their sister had been defiled.
text.DRB: When they were gone forth, the other sons of Jacob ran in upon them that were slain, and spoiled the city in revenge of the rape.

text.NRSV: They took their flocks and their herds, their donkeys, and whatever was in the city and in the field.
text.Rotherham: their flocks, and their herds and their asses,—and that which was in the city, and that which was in the field, they took;
text.KJV: They tooke their sheepe, and their oxen, and their asses, and that which was in the citie, and that which was in the field.
text.NABRE: They took their sheep, cattle and donkeys, whatever was in the city and in the surrounding country.
text.DRB: And wasting all things that were in their houses and fields, their sheep and herds, and asses,

text.NRSV: All their wealth, all their little ones and their wives, all that was in the houses, they captured and made their prey.
text.Rotherham: and all their wealth and all their little ones, and their women, took they captive, and seized as plunder,—even all that was in their houses.
text.KJV: And all their wealth, and all their little ones, and their wiues tooke they captiue, and spoiled euen all that was in the house.
text.NABRE: They carried off all their wealth, their children, and their women, and looted whatever was in the houses.
text.DRB: their little ones also, and their wives they led away captive.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “You have brought trouble on me by making me odious to the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites; my numbers are few, and if they gather themselves against me and attack me, I shall be destroyed, both I and my household.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jacob unto Simeon and unto Levi—Ye have troubled me by making me odious among them that dwell in the land, among the Canaanites, and among the Perizzites,—I, having only men that may be counted, they will gather themselves, together against me and smite me,—and I shall be destroyed both I and my house.
text.KJV: And Iacob said to Simeon and Leui, Ye haue troubled me to make me to stinke among the inhabitants of the land, amongst the Canaanites, and the Perizzites: and I being few in number, they shall gather themselues together against me, and slay me, and I shal be destroyed, I and my house.
text.NABRE: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi: “You have brought trouble upon me by making me repugnant to the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites. I have so few men that, if these people unite against me and attack me, I and my household will be wiped out.”
text.DRB: Which things when they had boldly achieved, Jacob said to Simeon and Levi: You have troubled me, and made me odious to the Chananites, and Pherezites, the inhabiters of this land. We are few: they being gathered together will strike me; and I and my house shall be destroyed.

text.NRSV: But they said, “Should our sister be treated like a whore?”
text.Rotherham: And they said,—As with a harlot, was he to deal with our sister.
text.KJV: And they said, Should hee deale with our sister, as with an harlot?
text.NABRE: But they retorted, “Should our sister be treated like a prostitute?”
text.DRB: They answered: What? Should they abuse our sister as a strumpet?

text.NRSV: God said to Jacob, “Arise, go up to Bethel, and settle there. Make an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau.”
text.Rotherham: Then said God unto Jacob, Arise, go up to Bethel, and dwell there,—and make there an altar, to the GOD who appeared unto thee, when thou fleddest from the face of Esau thy brother.
text.KJV: And God said vnto Iacob, Arise, goe vp to Bethel, and dwel there: and make there an Altar vnto God, that appeared vnto thee, when thou fleddest from the face of Esau thy brother.
text.NABRE: God said to Jacob: Go up now to Bethel. Settle there and build an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you were fleeing from your brother Esau.
text.DRB: In the mean time God spake to Jacob: Arise, and go up to Bethel, and dwell there, and make an altar to God that appeared to thee when thou didst fly from Esau thy brother.

text.NRSV: So Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, “Put away the foreign gods that are among you, and purify yourselves, and change your clothes;
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said unto his house, and unto all who were with him,—Put away the gods of the alien which are in your midst, and purify yourselves, and change your garments;
text.KJV: Then Iacob said vnto his household, and to all that were with him, Put away the strange gods that are among you, and bee cleane, and change your garments,
text.NABRE: So Jacob told his household and all who were with him: “Get rid of the foreign gods among you; then purify yourselves and change your clothes.
text.DRB: And Jacob having called together all his house, said: Cast away the strange gods that are among you, and be cleansed and change your garments.

text.NRSV: then come, let us go up to Bethel, that I may make an altar there to the God who answered me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone.”
text.Rotherham: And let us arise, and go up to Beth-el,—that I may make there an altar to the GOD who answered me in the day of my distress, and was with me in the way that I went.
text.KJV: And let vs arise, and goe vp to Bethel, and I will make there an Altar vnto God, who answered me in the day of my distresse, and was with me in the way which I went.
text.NABRE: Let us now go up to Bethel so that I might build an altar there to the God who answered me in the day of my distress and who has been with me wherever I have gone.”
text.DRB: Arise, and let us go up into Bethel, that we may make there an altar unto God: who herd me in the day of my tribulation, and accompanied me in my journey.

text.NRSV: So they gave to Jacob all the foreign gods that they had, and the rings that were in their ears; and Jacob hid them under the oak that was near Shechem.
text.Rotherham: So they gave unto Jacob all the gods of the alien which were in their hand, and the earrings which were in their ears,—and Jacob hid them under the oak, which was by Shechem.
text.KJV: And they gaue vnto Iacob all the strange gods which were in their hand, and all their eare-rings which were in their eares, and Iacob hid them vnder the oke which was by Shechem.
text.NABRE: They gave Jacob all the foreign gods in their possession and also the rings they had in their ears and Jacob buried them under the oak that is near Shechem.
text.DRB: They gave to him therefore all the strange gods that they had, and the earlets which were in their ears: but he buried them under the terebinth that is behind the city of Sichem.

text.NRSV: As they journeyed, a terror from God fell upon the cities all around them, so that no one pursued them.
text.Rotherham: And they brake up,—and it came to pass that the terror of God, was on the cities which were round about them, so that they pursued not after the sons of Jacob.
text.KJV: And they iourneyed: and the terrour of God was vpon the cities that were round about them, and they did not pursue after the sonnes of Iacob.
text.NABRE: Then, as they set out, a great terror fell upon the surrounding towns, so that no one pursued the sons of Jacob.
text.DRB: And when they were departed, the terror of God invaded all the cities round about, and they durst not pursue them going away.

text.NRSV: Jacob came to Luz (that is, Bethel), which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him,
text.Rotherham: And Jacob came in towards Luz, which was in the land of Canaan, the same, is Beth-el,—he and all the people who were with him.
text.KJV: ¶ So Iacob came to Luz, which is in the land of Canaan (that is Bethel) hee and all the people that were with him.
text.NABRE: Thus Jacob and all the people who were with him arrived in Luz (now Bethel) in the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: And Jacob came to Luza, which is in the land of Chanaan, surnamed Bethel: he and all the people that was with him.

text.NRSV: and there he built an altar and called the place El-bethel, because it was there that God had revealed himself to him when he fled from his brother.
text.Rotherham: And he built there an altar, and called the place El-Beth-el,—because, there, God revealed himself unto him, when he fled from the face of his brother,
text.KJV: And hee built there an Altar, and called the place El-Bethel, because there God appeared vnto him, when he fled from the face of his brother.
text.NABRE: There he built an altar and called the place El-Bethel, for it was there that God had revealed himself to him when he was fleeing from his brother.
text.DRB: And he builded there an altar, and called the name of that place, The house of God: for there God appeared to him when he fled from his brother.

text.NRSV: And Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died, and she was buried under an oak below Bethel. So it was called Allon-bacuth.
text.Rotherham: And Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse died, and was buried below Beth-el, under the oak,—so the name thereof was called, Allon-bacuth.
text.KJV: But Deborah Rebekahs nurse died, and she was buried beneath Bethel vnder an oke: and the name of it was called Allon Bachuth.
text.NABRE: Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died. She was buried under the oak below Bethel, and so it was named Allon-bacuth.
text.DRB: The same time died Debora the nurse of Rebecca, and was buried at the foot of Bethel under an oak: and the name of that place was called The oak of weeping.

text.NRSV: God appeared to Jacob again when he came from Paddan-aram, and he blessed him.
text.Rotherham: And God appeared unto Jacob again, when he came in from Padan-aram,—and blessed him.
text.KJV: ¶ And God appeared vnto Iacob againe, when he came out of Padan Aram, and blessed him.
text.NABRE: On Jacob’s arrival from Paddan-aram, God appeared to him again and blessed him.
text.DRB: And God appeared again to Jacob after he returned from Mesopotamia of Syria, and he blessed him,

text.NRSV: God said to him, “Your name is Jacob; no longer shall you be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name.” So he was called Israel.
text.Rotherham: And God said to him, Thy name, is Jacob,—Thy name shall no longer be called Jacob But, Israel, shall be thy name. So he called his name, Israel.
text.KJV: And God said vnto him, Thy name is Iacob: thy name shall not bee called any more Iacob, but Israel shall bee thy name; and hee called his name Israel.
text.NABRE: God said to him: Your name is Jacob. You will no longer be named Jacob, but Israel will be your name. So he was named Israel.
text.DRB: saying: Thou shalt not be called any more Jacob, but Israel shall be thy name. And he called him Israel,

text.NRSV: God said to him, “I am God Almighty: be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall spring from you.
text.Rotherham: And God said to him—I, am GOD Almighty! Be fruitful and multiply, A nation, and a multitude of nations shall spring from thee,—And, kings, from thy loins, shall come forth;
text.KJV: And God saide vnto him, I am God Almightie: be fruitfull and multiply: a nation and a company of nations shall be of thee, and Kings shall come out of thy loynes.
text.NABRE: Then God said to him: I am God Almighty; be fruitful and multiply. A nation, indeed an assembly of nations, will stem from you, and kings will issue from your loins.
text.DRB: and said to him: I am God Almighty, increase thou and multiply: Of thee shall be nations and peoples of nations, kings shall come forth of thy loins.

text.NRSV: The land that I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you, and I will give the land to your offspring after you.”
text.Rotherham: And, the land which I gave to Abraham and to Isaac, To thee, will I give it,—And to thy seed after thee, will I give the land.
text.KJV: And the land which I gaue Abraham, and Isaac, to thee I will giue it, and to thy seed after thee will I giue the land.
text.NABRE: The land I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you; and to your descendants after you I will give the land.
text.DRB: And the land which I gave to Abraham and Isaac, I will give to thee, and to thy seed after thee.

text.NRSV: Then God went up from him at the place where he had spoken with him.
text.Rotherham: And God went up from him at the place where he had spoken with him.
text.KJV: And God went vp from him, in the place where he talked with him.
text.NABRE: Then God departed from him.
text.DRB: And he departed from him.

text.NRSV: Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a pillar of stone; and he poured out a drink offering on it, and poured oil on it.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a pillar of stone,—and he poured out thereon a drink-offering, and poured thereon oil.
text.KJV: And Iacob set vp a pillar in the place where he talked with him, euen a pillar of stone: and hee powred a drinke offering thereon, and he powred oile thereon.
text.NABRE: In the place where God had spoken with him, Jacob set up a sacred pillar, a stone pillar, and upon it he made a libation and poured out oil.
text.DRB: But he erected a title of stone in the place where God had spoken unto him: offering upon it liquid offerings, and pouring oil on it,

text.NRSV: So Jacob called the place where God had spoken with him Bethel.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob called the name of the place where God had spoken with him, Beth-el.
text.KJV: And Iacob called the name of the place where God spake with him, Bethel.
text.NABRE: Jacob named the place where God spoke to him Bethel.
text.DRB: and calling the name of that place, Bethel.

text.NRSV: Then they journeyed from Bethel; and when they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel was in childbirth, and she had hard labor.
text.Rotherham: Then brake they up from Beth-el, and it came to pass when there was yet a stretch of country, to enter into Ephrath, that Rachel was in childbirth, and had hard-labour in her child-birth.
text.KJV: ¶ And they iourneyed from Bethel: and there was but a litle way to come to Ephrath; and Rachel traueiled, and she had hard labour.
text.NABRE: Then they departed from Bethel; but while they still had some distance to go to Ephrath, Rachel went into labor and suffered great distress.
text.DRB: And being gone forth from thence, he came in the spring time to the land which leadeth to Ephrata: wherein when Rachel was in travail,

text.NRSV: When she was in her hard labor, the midwife said to her, “Do not be afraid; for now you will have another son.”
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when she was in hard-labour in her child-birth, that the midwife said to her—Do not fear, for this also of thine, is, a son.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when shee was in hard labour, that the midwife said vnto her, Feare not: thou shalt haue this sonne also.
text.NABRE: When her labor was most intense, the midwife said to her, “Do not fear, for now you have another son.”
text.DRB: because of difficulty in her travail, she began to be in danger, and the midwife said unto her: Fear not, for thou shalt have also this a son.

text.NRSV: As her soul was departing (for she died), she named him Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when her soul was going forth—for she died, that she called his name Ben-oni,—but, his father, called him, Ben-jamin.
text.KJV: And it came to passe as her soule was in departing, (for she died) that she called his name Ben-oni: but his father called him Beniamin.
text.NABRE: With her last breath—for she was at the point of death—she named him Ben-oni; but his father named him Benjamin.
text.DRB: And her soul departing for pain, and death now at hand, she called the name of her son Benoni, that is the son of my pain: but his father called him Benjamin, that is the son of the right hand.

text.NRSV: So Rachel died, and she was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem),
text.Rotherham: So Rachel died,—and was buried on the way to Ephrath, the same, is Bethlehem.
text.KJV: And Rachel died, and was buried in the way to Ephrath, which is Bethlehem.
text.NABRE: Thus Rachel died; and she was buried on the road to Ephrath (now Bethlehem).
text.DRB: Rachel therefore died, and was buried in the high way that leadeth to Ephrata, this same is Bethleem.

text.NRSV: and Jacob set up a pillar at her grave; it is the pillar of Rachel’s tomb, which is there to this day.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob set up a pillar, over her grave,—the same, is—The Pillar of the Grave of Rachel until this day.
text.KJV: And Iacob set a pillar vpon her graue: that is the pillar of Rachels graue vnto this day.
text.NABRE: Jacob set up a sacred pillar on her grave, and the same pillar marks Rachel’s grave to this day.
text.DRB: And Jacob erected a title over her sepulchre: This is the title of Rachel's monument, until this present day.

text.NRSV: Israel journeyed on, and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.
text.Rotherham: Then Israel brake up,—and spread his tent at a distance from Migdal Eder.
text.KJV: ¶ And Israel iourneyed and spread his tent beyond the towre of Edar.
text.NABRE: Israel moved on and pitched his tent beyond Migdal-eder.
text.DRB: Departing thence, he pitched his tent beyond the Flock tower.

text.NRSV: While Israel lived in that land, Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father’s concubine; and Israel heard of it. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass while Israel inhabited that land, that Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father’s concubine; and Israel heard of it. And so the Sons of Israel came to be Twelve.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when Israel dwelt in that land, that Reuben went & lay with Bilhah his fathers concubine: and Israel heard it. Now the sonnes of Iacob were twelue.
text.NABRE: While Israel was encamped in that region, Reuben went and lay with Bilhah, his father’s concubine. When Israel heard of it, he was greatly offended. The sons of Jacob were now twelve.
text.DRB: And when he dwelt in that country, Ruben went, and slept with Bala his father's concubine: which thing he was not ignorant of. And the sons of Jacob were twelve.

text.NRSV: The sons of Leah: Reuben (Jacob’s firstborn), Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Leah, the firstborn of Jacob, Reuben,—And Simeon and Levi and Judah, and Issachar and Zebulon:
text.KJV: The sonnes of Leah: Reuben Iacobs first borne, and Simeon, and Leui, and Iudah, and Issachar, and Zebulun.
text.NABRE: The sons of Leah: Reuben, Jacob’s firstborn, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun;
text.DRB: The sons of Lia: Ruben the first begotten, and Simeon, and Levi, and Judas, and Issachar, and Zabulon.

text.NRSV: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Rachel, Joseph and Benjamin:
text.KJV: The sonnes of Rachel: Ioseph, and Beniamin.
text.NABRE: the sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin;
text.DRB: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

text.NRSV: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel’s maid: Dan and Naphtali.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Bilhah, handmaid of Rachel, Dan, and Naphtali:
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Bilhah, Rachels handmaid: Dan and Naphtali.
text.NABRE: the sons of Rachel’s maidservant Bilhah: Dan and Naphtali;
text.DRB: The sons of Bala Rachel's handmaid: Dan and Nephthali.

text.NRSV: The sons of Zilpah, Leah’s maid: Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan-aram.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Zilpah, handmaid of Leah, Gad and Asher: These, are the sons of Jacob, who were born to him, in Padan-aram.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Zilpah, Leahs handmaid: Gad, and Asher. These are the sonnes of Iacob, which were borne to him in Padan Aram.
text.NABRE: the sons of Leah’s maidservant Zilpah: Gad and Asher. These are the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan-aram.
text.DRB: The sons of Zelpha Lia's handmaid: Gad and Aser: these are the sons of Jacob, that were born to him in Mesopotamia of Syria.

text.NRSV: Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, or Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had resided as aliens.
text.Rotherham: Then came Jacob unto Isaac his father, to Mamre, the city of Arba,—the same, is Hebron, where had sojourned Abraham and Isaac.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iacob came vnto Isaac his father vnto Mamre, vnto the citie of Arbah (which is Hebron) where Abraham and Isaac soiourned.
text.NABRE: Jacob went home to his father Isaac at Mamre, in Kiriath-arba (now Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had resided.
text.DRB: He came also to Isaac his father in Mambre, the city of Arbee, this is Hebron: wherein Abraham and Isaac sojourned.

text.NRSV: Now the days of Isaac were one hundred eighty years.
text.Rotherham: And the days of Isaac came to be—a hundred and eighty years.
text.KJV: And the dayes of Isaac were an hundred and fourescore yeeres.
text.NABRE: The length of Isaac’s life was one hundred and eighty years;
text.DRB: And the days of Isaac were complete an hundred eighty years.

text.NRSV: And Isaac breathed his last; he died and was gathered to his people, old and full of days; and his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
text.Rotherham: And Isaac breathed his last, and died and was added unto his people, old, and satisfied with days,—and Esau and Jacob his sons buried him.
text.KJV: And Isaac gaue vp the ghost and died, and was gathered vnto his people, being old and full of dayes: and his sonnes Esau and Iacob buried him.
text.NABRE: then he breathed his last. He died as an old man and was gathered to his people. After a full life, his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
text.DRB: And spent with age he died, and was put to his people, being old and full of days, and Esau and Jacob his sons buried him.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Esau (that is, Edom).
text.Rotherham: Now these, are the generations of Esau the same, is Edom.
text.KJV: Now these are the generations of Esau, who is Edom.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Esau (that is, Edom).
text.DRB: And these are the generations of Esau, the same is Edom.

text.NRSV: Esau took his wives from the Canaanites: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, Oholibamah daughter of Anah son of Zibeon the Hivite,
text.Rotherham: Esau, took his wives of the daughters of Canaan,—Even Adah, daughter of Elon, the Hittite, And Oholibamah daughter of Anah, son of Zibeon, the Hivite;
text.KJV: Esau tooke his wiues of the daughters of Canaan: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Aholibamah the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon the Hiuite:
text.NABRE: Esau took his wives from among the Canaanite women: Adah, daughter of Elon the Hittite; Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah the son of Zibeon the Hivite;
text.DRB: Esau took wives of the daughters of Chanaan: Ada the daughter of Elon the Hetheite, and Oolibama the daughter of Ana daughter of Sebeon the Heveite:

text.NRSV: and Basemath, Ishmael’s daughter, sister of Nebaioth.
text.Rotherham: And Basemath daughter of Ishmael sister of Nebaioth.
text.KJV: And Bashemath Ishmaels daughter, sister of Nebaioth.
text.NABRE: and Basemath, daughter of Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth.
text.DRB: Basemath also the daughter of Ismael sister of Nabajoth.

text.NRSV: Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau; Basemath bore Reuel;
text.Rotherham: So Adah bare to Esau, Eliphaz, and, Basemath, bare Reuel;
text.KJV: And Adah bare to Esau, Eliphaz: and Bashemath bare Reuel.
text.NABRE: Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau; Basemath bore Reuel;
text.DRB: And Ada bare Eliphaz: Basemath bare Rahuel:

text.NRSV: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: and Oholibamah, bare Jeush and Jalam and Korah. These, are the sons of Esau, who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: And Aholibamah bare Ieush, and Iaalam, and Korah: these are the sonnes of Esau, which were borne vnto him in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam and Korah. These are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: Oolibama bare Jehus and Ihelon and Core, these are the sons of Esau, that were born to him in the land of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: Then Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, his cattle, all his livestock, and all the property he had acquired in the land of Canaan; and he moved to a land some distance from his brother Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Then took Esau his wives, and his sons, and his daughters and all the souls of his house, and his herds and all his beasts, and all his possessions whatsoever he had gathered in the land of Canaan,—and went into a land, away from the face of Jacob his brother,
text.KJV: And Esau tooke his wiues, and his sonnes, and his daughters, and all the persons of his house, and his cattell, and all his beasts, and all his substance, which he had got in the lande of Canaan: and went into the countrey from the face of his brother Iacob.
text.NABRE: Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, as well as his livestock, all his cattle, and all the property he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and went to the land of Seir, away from his brother Jacob.
text.DRB: And Esau took his wives and sons and daughters, and every soul of his house, and his substance, and cattle, and all that he could have in the land of Chanaan: and he went into another country, and departed from his brother Jacob.

text.NRSV: For their possessions were too great for them to live together; the land where they were staying could not support them because of their livestock.
text.Rotherham: For their substance had become too great for them to dwell together,—neither could the land of their sojournings sustain them, because of their herds,
text.KJV: For their riches were more then that they might dwell together: and the land wherein they were strangers, could not beare them, because of their cattell.
text.NABRE: Their possessions had become too great for them to dwell together, and the land in which they were residing could not support them because of their livestock.
text.DRB: For they were exceeding rich, and could not dwell together: neither was the land of their peregrination able to bear them, for the multitude of flocks.

text.NRSV: So Esau settled in the hill country of Seir; Esau is Edom.
text.Rotherham: So Esau dwelt in Mount Seir, Esau, is Edom.
text.KJV: Thus dwelt Esau in mount Seir: Esau is Edom.
text.NABRE: So Esau settled in the highlands of Seir. (Esau is Edom.)
text.DRB: And Esau dwelt in Mount Seir, he is Edom.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Esau, ancestor of the Edomites, in the hill country of Seir.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the generations of Esau father of Edom,—in Mount Seir.
text.KJV: ¶ And these are the generations of Esau, the father of the Edomites in mount Seir.
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Esau, ancestor of the Edomites, in the highlands of Seir.
text.DRB: And these are the generations of Esau the father of Edom in mount Seir,

text.NRSV: These are the names of Esau’s sons: Eliphaz son of Adah the wife of Esau; Reuel, the son of Esau’s wife Basemath.
text.Rotherham: These, are the name of the sons of Esau,—Eliphaz son of Adah wife of Esau, Reuel son of Basemath wife of Esau.
text.KJV: These are the names of Esaus sonnes: Eliphaz the sonne of Adah the wife of Esau, Reuel the sonne of Bashemath, the wife of Esau.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the sons of Esau: Eliphaz, son of Adah, wife of Esau, and Reuel, son of Basemath, wife of Esau.
text.DRB: and these are the names of his sons: Eliphaz the son of Ada the wife of Esau: Rahuel also the son of Basemath his wife.

text.NRSV: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
text.Rotherham: Then came the sons of Eliphaz to be,—Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Eliphaz were, Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.
text.NABRE: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
text.DRB: And Eliphas had sons: Theman, Omar, Sepho, and Gathan, and Cenes.

text.NRSV: (Timna was a concubine of Eliphaz, Esau’s son; she bore Amalek to Eliphaz.) These were the sons of Adah, Esau’s wife.
text.Rotherham: Now, Timna, became the concubine of Eliphaz son of Esau, and she bare to Eliphaz Amalek. These, are the sons of Adah, wife of Esau.
text.KJV: And Timna was concubine to Eliphaz Esaus sonne, and shee bare to Eliphaz Amalek: these were the sonnes of Adah Esaus wife.
text.NABRE: Timna was a concubine of Eliphaz, the son of Esau, and she bore Amalek to Eliphaz. Those were the sons of Adah, the wife of Esau.
text.DRB: And Thamna was the concubine of Eliphas the son of Esau: which bare to him Amalech. These are the sons of Ada the wife of Esau.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These were the sons of Esau’s wife, Basemath.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Reuel, Nahath and Zerah Shammah and Mizzah,—These were the sons of Basemath wife of Esau.
text.KJV: And these are the sonnes of Reuel: Nahath and Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah: these were the sonnes of Bashemath, Esaus wife.
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. Those were the sons of Basemath, the wife of Esau.
text.DRB: And the sons of Rahuel were Nahath and Zara, Samma and Meza. These were the sons of Basemath the wife of Esau.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah, daughter of Anah son of Zibeon: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
