text.Rotherham: And, these, were the sons of Oholibamah daughter of Anah, son of Zibeon, wife of—Esau,—she bare to Esau Jeush, and Jalam, and Korah.
text.KJV: ¶ And these were the sonnes of Aholibamah, the daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon Esaus wife: and she bare to Esau, Ieush and Iaalam, and Korah.
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah—the daughter of Anah, son of Zibeon—whom she bore to Esau: Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text.DRB: These also were the sons of Oolibama, the daughter of Ana, the daughter of Sebeon, the wife of Esau, which she bare to him, Jehus, and Ihelon, and Core.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau: the clans Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text.Rotherham: These, are the chiefs of the sons of Esau:—The sons of Eliphaz, firstborn of Esau,—Chief Teman, chief Omar, chief Zepho, chief Kenaz,
text.KJV: ¶ These were dukes of the sonnes of Esau: the sonnes of Eliphaz the first borne sonne of Esau, duke Teman, duke Omar, duke Zepho, duke Kenaz,
text.NABRE: These are the clans of the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz, Esau’s firstborn: the clans of Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text.DRB: These were Dukes of the sons of Esau: the sons of Eliphaz the first-begotten of Esau: Duke Theman, Duke Omar, Duke Sepho, Duke Cenes,

text.NRSV: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek; these are the clans of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Adah.
text.Rotherham: chief Korah, chief Gatam, chief Amalek. These are the chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom, these, the sons of Adah.
text.KJV: Duke Korah, duke Gatam, and duke Amalek: These are the dukes that came of Eliphaz, in the land of Edom: These were the sonnes of Adah.
text.NABRE: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These are the clans of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Adah.
text.DRB: Duke Core, Duke Gatham, Duke Amalech, these are the sons of Eliphaz in the land of Edom, & these are the sons of Ada.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Esau’s son Reuel: the clans Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah; these are the clans of Reuel in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Esau’s wife Basemath.
text.Rotherham: And these, are the sons of Reuel, son of Esau—Chief Nahath, chief Zerah, chief Shammah, chief Mizzah,—These are the chiefs of Reuel, in the land of Edom, these, the sons of Basemath wife of Esau.
text.KJV: ¶ And these are the sonnes of Reuel Esaus sonne: duke Nahath, duke Zerah, duke Shammah, duke Mizzah. These are the dukes that came of Reuel, in the land of Edom: these are the sonnes of Bashemath, Esaus wife.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Reuel, son of Esau: the clans of Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These are the clans of Reuel in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Basemath, wife of Esau.
text.DRB: These also were the sons of Rahuel, the son of Esau: Duke Nahath, Duke Zara, Duke Samma, Duke Meza. And these be the Dukes of Rahuel, in the Land of Edom: these be the sons of Basemath the wife of Esau.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah: the clans Jeush, Jalam, and Korah; these are the clans born of Esau’s wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.
text.Rotherham: And these, are the sons of Oholibamah wife of Esau, Chief Jeush chief Jalam, chief Korah,—These are the chiefs of Oholibamah daughter of Anah wife of Esau.
text.KJV: ¶ And these are the sonnes of Aholibamah Esaus wife: duke Ieush, duke Iaalam, duke Korah: these were the dukes that came of Aholibamah the daughter of Anah Esaus wife.
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Oholibamah, wife of Esau: the clans of Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are the clans of Esau’s wife Oholibamah, daughter of Anah.
text.DRB: And these were the sons of Oolibama the wife of Esau: Duke Jehus, Duke Ihelon, Duke Core. These be the Dukes of Oolibama, the daughter of Ana, and wife of Esau.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these are their clans.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Esau, and, these, their chiefs—the same, is Edom.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Esau, (who is Edom) and these are their dukes.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Esau—that is, Edom—according to their clans.
text.DRB: These are the sons of Esau, and these are the Dukes of them: the same is Edom.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Seir, the Horite, who were dwelling in the land,—Lotan and Shobal and Zibeon and Anah;
text.KJV: ¶ These are the sonnes of Seir the Horite, who inhabited the land, Lotan, and Shobal, and Zibeon, and Anah.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text.DRB: These are the sons of Seir the Horreite, the inhabiters of the land: Lotan, and Sobal, and Sebeon, and Ana,

text.NRSV: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; these are the clans of the Horites, the sons of Seir in the land of Edom.
text.Rotherham: and Dishon and Ezer and Dishan,—These, are the chiefs of the Horites the sons of Seir, in the land of Edom.
text.KJV: And Dishon, and Ezer, and Dishan: these are the dukes of the Horites the children of Seir in the lande of Edom.
text.NABRE: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; those are the clans of the Horites, sons of Seir in the land of Edom.
text.DRB: and Dison, and Eser, and Disan. These are Dukes of the Horreite, the sons of Seir in the Land of Edom.

text.NRSV: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Heman; and Lotan’s sister was Timna.
text.Rotherham: Then came the sons of Lotan to be, Hori and Hemam,—And, the sister of Lotan was Timna.
text.KJV: And the children of Lotan, were Hori, and Hemam: and Lotans sister was Timna.
text.NABRE: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam, and Lotan’s sister was Timna.
text.DRB: And Lotan had sons: Hori and Heman. And the sister of Lotan was Thamna.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Shobal, Alvan and Manahath, and Ebal,—Shepho, and Onam.
text.KJV: And the children of Shobal were these: Aluan, and Manahath, and Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Mahanath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text.DRB: And these were the sons of Sobal: Alvan and Manahat, and Ebal, and Sepho, and Onam.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah; he is the Anah who found the springs in the wilderness, as he pastured the donkeys of his father Zibeon.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Zibeon: Both Aiah and Anah,—The same, is the Anah that found the warm springs in the desert, as he fed the asses of Zibeon his father.
text.KJV: And these are the children of Zibeon, both Aiah, and Anah: this was that Anah that found the mules in the wildernesse, as he fed the asses of Zibeon his father.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah. He is the Anah who found water in the desert while he was pasturing the donkeys of his father Zibeon.
text.DRB: And these were the sons of Sebeon: Aia and Ana. This is Ana that found the hot waters in the wilderness, when he fed the asses of Sebeon his father:

text.NRSV: These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah daughter of Anah.
text.Rotherham: And these are the sons of Anah Dishon,—and Oholibamah, is daughter of Anah.
text.KJV: And the children of Anah were these: Dishon, and Aholibamah the daughter of Anah.
text.NABRE: These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah, daughter of Anah.
text.DRB: and he had a son Dison, and a daughter Oolibama.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Dishon,—Hemdan and Eshban, and Ithran and Cheran.
text.KJV: And these are the children of Dishon: Hemdan and Eshban, & Ithran, and Cheran.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text.DRB: And these were the sons of Dison: Hamdan, and Eseban, and Iethram, and Charan.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Ezer,—Bilhan and Zaavan, and Akan.
text.KJV: The children of Ezer are these: Bilhan and Zaauan, and Akan.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text.DRB: These also were the sons of Eser: Balaan, and Zavan, and Acan.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Dishan,—Uz and Aran.
text.KJV: The children of Dishan are these: Uz, and Aran.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
text.DRB: And Disan had sons: Hus, and Aram.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Horites: the clans Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text.Rotherham: These, are the chiefs of the Horites,—Chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah;
text.KJV: These are the dukes that came of the Horites: duke Lotan, duke Shobal, duke Zibeon, duke Anah,
text.NABRE: These are the clans of the Horites: the clans of Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text.DRB: These were Dukes of the Horreites: Duke Lotan, Duke Sobal, Duke Sebeon, Duke Ana,

text.NRSV: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; these are the clans of the Horites, clan by clan in the land of Seir.
text.Rotherham: chief Dishon, chief Ezer, chief Dishan. These, are the chiefs of the Horites, after their chiefs in the land of Seir.
text.KJV: Duke Dishon, duke Ezer, duke Dishan: these are the dukes that came of Hori, among their dukes in the land of Seir.
text.NABRE: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; those are the clans of the Horites, clan by clan, in the land of Seir.
text.DRB: Duke Dison, Duke Eser, Duke Disan: these were Dukes of the Horreites that ruled in the Land Seir.

text.NRSV: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom, before any king reigned over the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the kings, who reigned in the land of Edom,—before there reigned a king of the sons of Israel:
text.KJV: ¶ And these are the kings that reigned in the land of Edom, before there reigned any king ouer the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the Israelites.
text.DRB: And the kings that ruled in the land of Edom, before that the children of Israel had a king, were these:

text.NRSV: Bela son of Beor reigned in Edom, the name of his city being Dinhabah.
text.Rotherham: There reigned in Edom, Bela, son of Beor,—and the name of his city, was Dinhabah.
text.KJV: And Bela the sonne of Beor reigned in Edom: and the name of his citie was Dinhabah.
text.NABRE: Bela, son of Beor, became king in Edom; the name of his city was Dinhabah.
text.DRB: Bela the son of Beor, and the name of his city Denaba.

text.NRSV: Bela died, and Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah succeeded him as king.
text.Rotherham: Then died Bela,—and there reigned in his stead, Jobab son of Zerah, from Bozrah.
text.KJV: And Bela died, and Iobab the sonne of Zerah of Bozra reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When Bela died, Jobab, son of Zerah, from Bozrah, succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Bela died, and Jobab the son of Zara of Bosra reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites succeeded him as king.
text.Rotherham: Then died Jobab,—and there reigned in his stead, Husham, from the land of the Temanites.
text.KJV: And Iobab died, and Husham of the land of Temani reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When Jobab died, Husham, from the land of the Temanites, succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And when Jobab was dead, Husam of the land of the Themans reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: Husham died, and Hadad son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, succeeded him as king, the name of his city being Avith.
text.Rotherham: Then died Husham,—and there reigned in his stead, Hadad, son of Bedad, who smote Midian in the field of Moab, and, the name of his city, was Avith.
text.KJV: And Husham died, and Hadad the sonne of Bedad, (who smote Midian in the field of Moab,) reigned in his stead: & the name of his citie was Auith.
text.NABRE: When Husham died, Hadad, son of Bedad, succeeded him as king. He is the one who defeated Midian in the country of Moab; the name of his city was Avith.
text.DRB: He also being dead, there reigned in his stead Adad the son of Badad, that struck Madian in the country of Moab: and the name of his city was Avith.

text.NRSV: Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah succeeded him as king.
text.Rotherham: Then died Hadad,—and there reigned in his stead, Samlah from Masrekah.
text.KJV: And Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah, reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When Hadad died, Samlah, from Masrekah, succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And when Adad was dead, there reigned for him Semla of Masreca.

text.NRSV: Samlah died, and Shaul of Rehoboth on the Euphrates succeeded him as king.
text.Rotherham: Then died Samlah,—and there reigned in his stead, Shaul, from Rohoboth of the River.
text.KJV: And Samlah died, and Saul of Rehoboth, by the riuer, reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When Samlah died, Shaul, from Rehoboth-on-the-River, succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: He also being dead, Saul of the river Rohoboth, reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: Shaul died, and Baal-hanan son of Achbor succeeded him as king.
text.Rotherham: Then died Shaul,—and there reigned in his stead, Baal-hanan, son of Achbor.
text.KJV: And Saul died, and Baal-hanan the sonne of Achbor reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When Shaul died, Baal-hanan, son of Achbor, succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And when he also was dead, Balanan the son of Achobor succeeded into the Kingdom.

text.NRSV: Baal-hanan son of Achbor died, and Hadar succeeded him as king, the name of his city being Pau; his wife’s name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, daughter of Me-zahab.
text.Rotherham: Then died Baal-hanan son of Achbor, and there reigned in his stead Hadar, and the name of his city, was Pau,—and, the name of his wife, Mehetabel, daughter of Matred, daughter of Me-zahab.
text.KJV: And Baal-hanan the sonne of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his stead: and the name of his citie was Pau, and his wiues name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.
text.NABRE: When Baal-hanan, son of Achbor, died, Hadad succeeded him as king; the name of his city was Pau. His wife’s name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, son of Mezahab.
text.DRB: This men also being dead Adar reigned in his place, and the name of his city was Phau: and his wife was called Meetabel, the daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezaab.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the clans of Esau, according to their families and their localities by their names: the clans Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text.Rotherham: These then are the names of the chiefs of Esau after their families, after their places by their names,—Chief Timnah, chief Alvah chief Jetheth;
text.KJV: And these are the names of the dukes that came of Esau, according to their families, after their places, by their names: duke Timnah, duke Aluah, duke Ietheth,
text.NABRE: These are the names of the clans of Esau identified according to their families and localities: the clans of Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text.DRB: These therefore be the names of the Dukes of Esau in their kindreds, and places, and callings: Duke Thamna, Duke Alva, Duke Jetheth,

text.NRSV: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text.Rotherham: chief Oholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon;
text.KJV: Duke Aholibamah, duke Elah, duke Pinon,
text.NABRE: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text.DRB: Duke Oolibama, Duke Ela, Duke Phinon,

text.NRSV: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text.Rotherham: chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar;
text.KJV: Duke Kenaz, duke Teman, duke Mibzar,
text.NABRE: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text.DRB: Duke Cenez, Duke Theman, Duke Mabsar,

text.NRSV: Magdiel, and Iram; these are the clans of Edom (that is, Esau, the father of Edom), according to their settlements in the land that they held.
text.Rotherham: chief Magdiel, chief Iram. These, are the chiefs of Edom—after their dwellings in the land of their possession, the same, is Esau, father of Edom.
text.KJV: Duke Magdiel, duke Iram. These be the dukes of Edom, according to their habitations, in the land of their possession: he is Esau the father of the Edomites.
text.NABRE: Magdiel, and Iram. Those are the clans of the Edomites, according to their settlements in their territorial holdings—that is, of Esau, the ancestor of the Edomites.
text.DRB: Duke Magdiel, Duke Hiram: these are the Dukes of Edom dwelling in the land of their Empire, the same is Esau the father of the Idumeans.

text.NRSV: Jacob settled in the land where his father had lived as an alien, the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob dwelt in the land of the sojournings of his father,—in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: And Iacob dwelt in the land wherein his father was a stranger, in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: Jacob settled in the land where his father had sojourned, the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: And Jacob dwelt in the land of Chanaan, wherein his father sojourned.

text.NRSV: This is the story of the family of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was shepherding the flock with his brothers; he was a helper to the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father’s wives; and Joseph brought a bad report of them to their father.
text.Rotherham: These, are the generations of Jacob—Joseph, when seventeen years old, was shepherding with his brethren among the flocks, and, he, being a youth, was with the sons of Bilhah and with the sons of Zilpah wives of his father,—so then Joseph brought in the talk about them—something bad, unto their father.
text.KJV: These are the generations of Iacob: Ioseph being seuenteene yeeres old, was feeding the flocke with his brethren, and the lad was with the sonnes of Bilhah, and with the sonnes of Zilpah, his fathers wiues: and Ioseph brought vnto his father their euill report.
text.NABRE: This is the story of the family of Jacob. When Joseph was seventeen years old, he was tending the flocks with his brothers; he was an assistant to the sons of his father’s wives Bilhah and Zilpah, and Joseph brought their father bad reports about them.
text.DRB: And these are his generations: Joseph when he was sixteen years old, fed the flock with his brethren being yet a boy: and he was with the sons of Bala and Zelpha his father's wives: and he accused his brethren to his father of a most wicked crime.

text.NRSV: Now Israel loved Joseph more than any other of his children, because he was the son of his old age; and he had made him a long robe with sleeves.
text.Rotherham: Now, Israel, loved Joseph more than any of his sons, because he was to him, the son of his old age, and he had made him, a long tunic.
text.KJV: Now Israel loued Ioseph more then all his children, because he was the sonne of his old age: and he made him a coat of many colours.
text.NABRE: Israel loved Joseph best of all his sons, for he was the child of his old age; and he had made him a long ornamented tunic.
text.DRB: And Israel loved Joseph above all his sons, because he had begotten him in his old age and he made him a coat of diverse colours.

text.NRSV: But when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him, and could not speak peaceably to him.
text.Rotherham: So his brethren saw that their father loved him, more than any of his brethren, and they hated him, and could not bid him prosper,
text.KJV: And when his brethren saw that their father loued him more then all his brethren, they hated him, and could not speake peaceably vnto him.
text.NABRE: When his brothers saw that their father loved him best of all his brothers, they hated him so much that they could not say a kind word to him.
text.DRB: And his brethren seeing that he was loved of his father more than all his sons, they hated him, neither could they speak any thing to him peaceably.

text.NRSV: Once Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers, they hated him even more.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph dreamed a dream, and told it to his brethren, and they went on yet more to hate him.
text.KJV: ¶ And Ioseph dreamed a dreame, and he told it his brethren, and they hated him yet the more.
text.NABRE: Once Joseph had a dream, and when he told his brothers, they hated him even more.
text.DRB: It chanced also that he reported to his brethren a dream that he had seen, which occasion was the seed of great hatred.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Listen to this dream that I dreamed.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them, Hear ye I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Heare, I pray you, this dreame which I haue dreamed.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Listen to this dream I had.
text.DRB: And he said to them: hear my dream which I have seen:

text.NRSV: There we were, binding sheaves in the field. Suddenly my sheaf rose and stood upright; then your sheaves gathered around it, and bowed down to my sheaf.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! then, we, were binding sheaves in the midst of the field, when lo my sheaf rose up, yea and took its stand,—and lo! round about came your sheaves, and bowed themselves down to my sheaf.
text.KJV: For beholde, wee were binding sheaues in the field, and loe, my sheafe arose, and also stood vpright; and behold, your sheaues stood round about, and made obeisance to my sheafe.
text.NABRE: There we were, binding sheaves in the field, when suddenly my sheaf rose to an upright position, and your sheaves formed a ring around my sheaf and bowed down to it.”
text.DRB: I thought we bound sheaves in the field: and my sheaf arose as it were, and stood, and your sheaves standing about did adore my sheaf.

text.NRSV: His brothers said to him, “Are you indeed to reign over us? Are you indeed to have dominion over us?” So they hated him even more because of his dreams and his words.
text.Rotherham: And his brethren said to him, Shalt thou, reign, over us, shalt thou, have dominion over us? So they went on yet more to hate him, because of his dreams and because of his words.
text.KJV: And his brethren saide to him, Shalt thou indeed reigne ouer vs? or shalt thou indeed haue dominion ouer vs? and they hated him yet the more, for his dreames, and for his words.
text.NABRE: His brothers said to him, “Are you really going to make yourself king over us? Will you rule over us?” So they hated him all the more because of his dreams and his reports.
text.DRB: His brethren answered: What? Shalt thou be our king? Or shall we be subject to thy dominion? This occasion of his dreams and words ministered nourishment to the envy and hatred.

text.NRSV: He had another dream, and told it to his brothers, saying, “Look, I have had another dream: the sun, the moon, and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”
text.Rotherham: Then dreamed he, yet another, dream, and related it to his brethren, and said:—Lo! I have dreamed a dream, yet again, Lo! then, the sun and the moon, and eleven stars, were bowing themselves down to me.
text.KJV: ¶ And hee dreamed yet another dreame, and told it his brethren, and said, Behold, I haue dreamed a dreame more: and behold, the sunne and the moone, and the eleuen starres made obeisance to me.
text.NABRE: Then he had another dream, and told it to his brothers. “Look, I had another dream,” he said; “this time, the sun and the moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”
text.DRB: He saw also another dream, which telling his brethren, he said: I saw in a dream, as it were the sun, and the moon, and eleven stars adore me.

text.NRSV: But when he told it to his father and to his brothers, his father rebuked him, and said to him, “What kind of dream is this that you have had? Shall we indeed come, I and your mother and your brothers, and bow to the ground before you?”
text.Rotherham: So he related it unto his father, and unto his brethren,—and his father rebuked him, and said unto him, What is this dream which thou hast dreamed? Shall we, indeed come in, I, and thy mother and thy brethren, to bow ourselves down to thee to the earth?
text.KJV: And he told it to his father, and to his brethren: and his father rebuked him, and said vnto him, What is this dreame that thou hast dreamed? shal I, and thy mother, and thy brethren indeed come to bow downe our selues to thee, to the earth?
text.NABRE: When he told it to his father and his brothers, his father reproved him and asked, “What is the meaning of this dream of yours? Can it be that I and your mother and your brothers are to come and bow to the ground before you?”
text.DRB: Which when he had reported to his father and brethren, his father rebuked him, and said: What meaneth this dream that thou hast seen? Why, shall I and thy mother, and thy brethren adore thee upon the earth?

text.NRSV: So his brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept the matter in mind.
text.Rotherham: Then were his brethren jealous of him, but his father, marked the word.
text.KJV: And his brethren enuied him: but his father obserued the saying.
text.NABRE: So his brothers were furious at him but his father kept the matter in mind.
text.DRB: His brethren therefore envied him: but his father considered the thing with himself.

text.NRSV: Now his brothers went to pasture their father’s flock near Shechem.
text.Rotherham: Now his brethren went their way,—to feed their father’s flock in Shechem.
text.KJV: ¶ And his brethren went to feed their fathers flocke in Shechem.
text.NABRE: One day, when his brothers had gone to pasture their father’s flocks at Shechem,
text.DRB: And when his brethren abode in Sichem feeding their father's flocks,

text.NRSV: And Israel said to Joseph, “Are not your brothers pasturing the flock at Shechem? Come, I will send you to them.” He answered, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: So Israel said unto Joseph Are not, thy brethren, feeding the flock in Shechem? Come on! and let me send thee unto them. And he said to him Behold me!
text.KJV: And Israel saide vnto Ioseph, Doe not thy brethren feed the flocke in Shechem? Come, and I will send thee vnto them: & he said to him, Here am I.
text.NABRE: Israel said to Joseph, “Are your brothers not tending our flocks at Shechem? Come and I will send you to them.” “I am ready,” Joseph answered.
text.DRB: Israel said to him: Thy brethren feed sheep in Sichem: come, I will send thee to them. Who answering,

text.NRSV: So he said to him, “Go now, see if it is well with your brothers and with the flock; and bring word back to me.” So he sent him from the valley of Hebron. He came to Shechem,
text.Rotherham: So he said to him—Go, I pray thee, look after the welfare of thy brethren, and the welfare of the flock, and bring me back word. And he sent him from the vale of Hebron, and he came in towards Shechem.
text.KJV: And he said to him, Goe, I pray thee, see whether it bee well with thy brethren, and well with the flockes, and bring me word againe: so hee sent him out of the vale of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
text.NABRE: “Go then,” he replied; “see if all is well with your brothers and the flocks, and bring back word.” So he sent him off from the valley of Hebron. When Joseph reached Shechem,
text.DRB: I am ready; he said to him: Go, and see if all things be well with thy brethren and the sheep, and bring me word again what they do. Being sent therefore from the Vale of Hebron, he came to Sichem:

text.NRSV: and a man found him wandering in the fields; the man asked him, “What are you seeking?”
text.Rotherham: And a man found him, and lo! he was wandering about in the field, so the man asked him saying—What seekest thou?
text.KJV: ¶ And a certaine man found him, and behold, hee was wandring in the field, and the man asked him, saying, What seekest thou?
text.NABRE: a man came upon him as he was wandering about in the fields. “What are you looking for?” the man asked him.
text.DRB: and a man found him there wandering in the field, and asked what he sought.

text.NRSV: “I am seeking my brothers,” he said; “tell me, please, where they are pasturing the flock.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, My brethren, am, I, seeking,—do tell me, I pray thee, where they are feeding their flock.
text.KJV: And he said, I seeke my brethren: tell me, I pray thee, where they feede their flockes.
text.NABRE: “I am looking for my brothers,” he answered. “Please tell me where they are tending the flocks.”
text.DRB: But he answered: I seek my brethren, shew me where they feed the flocks.

text.NRSV: The man said, “They have gone away, for I heard them say, ‘Let us go to Dothan.’ ” So Joseph went after his brothers, and found them at Dothan.
text.Rotherham: And the man said, They have broken up from hence, for I heard them saying Let us go our way towards Dothan. So Joseph went after his brethren, and found them, in Dothan.
text.KJV: And the man said, They are departed hence: for I heard them say, Let vs goe to Dothan. And Ioseph went after his brethren, and found them in Dothan.
text.NABRE: The man told him, “They have moved on from here; in fact, I heard them say, ‘Let us go on to Dothan.’” So Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.
text.DRB: And the man said to him: They are departed from this place: for I heard them say: Let us go into Dothain. Joseph therefore went forward after his brethren, and found them in Dothain.

text.NRSV: They saw him from a distance, and before he came near to them, they conspired to kill him.
text.Rotherham: And they saw him afar off,—and, ere yet he drew near unto them, they conspired against him, to put him to death.
text.KJV: And when they saw him a farre off, euen before he came neere vnto them, they conspired against him, to slay him.
text.NABRE: They saw him from a distance, and before he reached them, they plotted to kill him.
text.DRB: Who when they had seen him a far off, before he came nigh them, they devised to kill him:

text.NRSV: They said to one another, “Here comes this dreamer.
text.Rotherham: And they said each man unto his brother, Lo! that master of dreams yonder, coming in!
text.KJV: And they said one to another, Behold, this dreamer commeth.
text.NABRE: They said to one another: “Here comes that dreamer!
text.DRB: and spake among themselves: Behold the dreamer cometh,

text.NRSV: Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits; then we shall say that a wild animal has devoured him, and we shall see what will become of his dreams.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, come! let us slay him and cast him into one of the pits, and we will say A cruel beast, hath devoured him,—And let us see what will become of his dreams.
text.KJV: Come now therefore, and let vs slay him, and cast him into some pit, and we will say, Some euill beast hath deuoured him: and we shall see what will become of his dreames.
text.NABRE: Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the cisterns here; we could say that a wild beast devoured him. We will see then what comes of his dreams.”
text.DRB: come, let us kill him, and cast him into an old cistern: and we will say a naughty wild beast hath devoured him: and then it shall appear what his dreams do profit him.

text.NRSV: But when Reuben heard it, he delivered him out of their hands, saying, “Let us not take his life.”
text.Rotherham: And Reuben heard it, and rescued him out of their hand, and said Let us not smite him, so as to take his life!
text.KJV: And Reuben heard it, and he deliuered him out of their hands, and said; Let vs not kill him.
text.NABRE: But when Reuben heard this, he tried to save him from their hands, saying: “We must not take his life.”
text.DRB: And Ruben hearing this, endeavoured to deliver him out of their hands, and said:

text.NRSV: Reuben said to them, “Shed no blood; throw him into this pit here in the wilderness, but lay no hand on him”—that he might rescue him out of their hand and restore him to his father.
text.Rotherham: And Reuben said unto them Do not shed blood! Cast him into this pit, which is in the wilderness, but put not forth, a hand, against him! that he might rescue him out of their hand, to restore him unto his father.
text.KJV: And Reuben saide vnto them, Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wildernesse, and lay no hand vpon him; that he might rid him out of their hands, to deliuer him to his father againe.
text.NABRE: Then Reuben said, “Do not shed blood! Throw him into this cistern in the wilderness; but do not lay a hand on him.” His purpose was to save him from their hands and restore him to his father.
text.DRB: Do not take away his life, neither shed ye blood: but cast him into this cistern that is in the wilderness, and keep your hands harmless: and he said this, desirous to deliver him out of their hands, and to restore him to his father.

text.NRSV: So when Joseph came to his brothers, they stripped him of his robe, the long robe with sleeves that he wore;
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass, when Joseph had come in unto his brethren, that they stript Joseph of his tunic, the long tunic which was upon him,
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe when Ioseph was come vnto his brethren, that they stript Ioseph out of his coate, his coat of many colours that was on him.
text.NABRE: So when Joseph came up to his brothers, they stripped him of his tunic, the long ornamented tunic he had on;
text.DRB: As soon therefore as he came unto his brethren, forthwith they stripped him out of his side coat, and of diverse colours,

text.NRSV: and they took him and threw him into a pit. The pit was empty; there was no water in it.
text.Rotherham: and took him, and cast him into the pit, but the pit, was empty, there was in it no water.
text.KJV: And they tooke him and cast him into a pit: and the pit was emptie, there was no water in it.
text.NABRE: then they took him and threw him into the cistern. The cistern was empty; there was no water in it.
text.DRB: and cast him into the old cistern, that had not water.

text.NRSV: Then they sat down to eat; and looking up they saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with their camels carrying gum, balm, and resin, on their way to carry it down to Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And when they had sat down to eat bread, they lifted up their eyes and looked, and lo! a caravan of Ishmaelites, coming in from Gilead,—and, their camels, were bearing tragacanth gum, and balsam and cistus-gum, they were going their way, to take them down to Egypt.
text.KJV: And they sate downe to eat bread: and they lift vp their eyes and looked, and behold, a company of Ishmeelites came from Gilead, with their camels, bearing spicery, & baulme, and myrrhe, going to cary it downe to Egypt.
text.NABRE: Then they sat down to eat. Looking up, they saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, their camels laden with gum, balm, and resin to be taken down to Egypt.
text.DRB: And sitting to eat bread, they saw Ismaelites wayfaring men coming from Galaad, and their camels carrying spices, and rosen, and myrrh into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then Judah said to his brothers, “What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?
text.Rotherham: So Judah said unto his brethren,—What profit that we slay our brother, and conceal his blood?
text.KJV: And Iudah saide vnto his brethren, What profit is it if we slay our brother, and conceale his blood?
text.NABRE: Judah said to his brothers: “What is to be gained by killing our brother and concealing his blood?
text.DRB: Judas therefore said to his brethren: What availeth it us if we kill our brother, and conceal his blood?

text.NRSV: Come, let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and not lay our hands on him, for he is our brother, our own flesh.” And his brothers agreed.
text.Rotherham: Come and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites; but let not, our own hand, be upon him, for our own brother, our own flesh, is he And his brethren hearkened.
text.KJV: Come, and let vs sell him to the Ishmeelites, and let not our hand bee vpon him: for he is our brother, and our flesh; and his brethren were content.
text.NABRE: Come, let us sell him to these Ishmaelites, instead of doing away with him ourselves. After all, he is our brother, our own flesh.” His brothers agreed.
text.DRB: It is better that he be sold to the Ismaelites, and that our hands be not polluted: for he is our brother and our flesh. His brethren assented to his words.

text.NRSV: When some Midianite traders passed by, they drew Joseph up, lifting him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver. And they took Joseph to Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And there passed by certain Midianites travelling merchants, so they drew forth and uplifted Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites, for twenty pieces of silver,—and they brought Joseph into Egypt.
text.KJV: Then there passed by Midianites merchant men, and they drew and lift vp Ioseph out of the pit, and sold Ioseph to the Ishmeelites for twentie pieces of siluer: and they brought Ioseph into Egypt.
text.NABRE: Midianite traders passed by, and they pulled Joseph up out of the cistern. They sold Joseph for twenty pieces of silver to the Ishmaelites, who took him to Egypt.
text.DRB: And when the Madianite merchants passed by, they drawing him out of the cistern, sold him to the Ismaelites, for twenty pieces of silver, who brought him into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: When Reuben returned to the pit and saw that Joseph was not in the pit, he tore his clothes.
text.Rotherham: And Reuben returned unto the pit, and lo Joseph was not in the pit, so he rent his clothes;
text.KJV: ¶ And Reuben returned vnto the pit, and behold, Ioseph was not in the pit: and he rent his clothes.
text.NABRE: When Reuben went back to the cistern and saw that Joseph was not in it, he tore his garments,
text.DRB: And Ruben returning to the cistern, findeth not the boy:

text.NRSV: He returned to his brothers, and said, “The boy is gone; and I, where can I turn?”
text.Rotherham: and returned unto his brethren, and said,—The, lad, is not! And, I, oh where can I, go?
text.KJV: And hee returned vnto his brethren and said, The childe is not, and I, whither shall I goe?
text.NABRE: and returning to his brothers, he exclaimed: “The boy is gone! And I—where can I turn?”
text.DRB: and renting his garments went to his brethren, and said: The boy doth not appear, and whither shall I go?

text.NRSV: Then they took Joseph’s robe, slaughtered a goat, and dipped the robe in the blood.
text.Rotherham: And they took Joseph’s tunic,—and slaughtered a buck of the goats, and dipped the tunic in the blood;
text.KJV: And they tooke Iosephs coat, and killed a kid of the goats, and dipped the coat in the blood.
text.NABRE: They took Joseph’s tunic, and after slaughtering a goat, dipped the tunic in its blood.
text.DRB: And they took his coat, and dipped it in the blood of a kid which they had killed,

text.NRSV: They had the long robe with sleeves taken to their father, and they said, “This we have found; see now whether it is your son’s robe or not.”
text.Rotherham: and sent the long tunic and brought it in unto their father, and said This, have we found! Examine, we pray thee, whether it is the tunic of thy son, or not!
text.KJV: And they sent the coat of many colours, and they brought it to their father, and said, This haue we found: know now whether it bee thy sonnes coat or no.
text.NABRE: Then they sent someone to bring the long ornamented tunic to their father, with the message: “We found this. See whether it is your son’s tunic or not.”
text.DRB: sending some that should carry it to their father, and should say: This we have found: see whether it be thy son's coat, or no.

text.NRSV: He recognized it, and said, “It is my son’s robe! A wild animal has devoured him; Joseph is without doubt torn to pieces.”
text.Rotherham: So he examined it, and said—The tunic of my son! A cruel beast hath devoured him,—torn in pieces—torn in pieces, is Joseph!
text.KJV: And he knew it, and said, It is my sonnes coat: an euil beast hath deuoured him; Ioseph is without doubt rent in pieces.
text.NABRE: He recognized it and exclaimed: “My son’s tunic! A wild beast has devoured him! Joseph has been torn to pieces!”
text.DRB: Which when the father acknowledged, he said: It is my son's coat, a naughty wild beast hath eaten him, a beast hath devoured Joseph.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob tore his garments, and put sackcloth on his loins, and mourned for his son many days.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob rent his garments, and put sackcloth upon his loins,—and mourned over his son many days.
text.KJV: And Iacob rent his clothes, and put sackcloth vpon his loines, & mourned for his sonne many dayes.
text.NABRE: Then Jacob tore his garments, put sackcloth on his loins, and mourned his son many days.
text.DRB: And tearing his garments, did on sackcloth, mourning his son a great time.

text.NRSV: All his sons and all his daughters sought to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted, and said, “No, I shall go down to Sheol to my son, mourning.” Thus his father bewailed him.
text.Rotherham: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to console him but he refused to be consoled, and said—Surely I will go down unto my son mourning to hades! And his father wept for him.
text.KJV: And all his sonnes, and all his daughters rose vp to comfort him: but he refused to be comforted: and he said, For I will goe downe into the graue vnto my sonne, mourning; thus his father wept for him.
text.NABRE: Though his sons and daughters tried to console him, he refused all consolation, saying, “No, I will go down mourning to my son in Sheol.” Thus did his father weep for him.
text.DRB: And all his children being gathered together to assuage their father's sorrow, he would not take comfort, but said: I will descend unto my son into hell, mourning. And whilst he persevered in weeping,

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold him in Egypt to Potiphar, one of Pharaoh’s officials, the captain of the guard.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Midianites, sold him into Egypt,—to Potiphar courtier of Pharaoh, chief of the royal executioners.
text.KJV: And the Medanites sold him into Egypt vnto Potiphar, an officer of Pharaohs, and captaine of the guard.
text.NABRE: The Midianites, meanwhile, sold Joseph in Egypt to Potiphar, an official of Pharaoh and his chief steward.
text.DRB: the Madianites sold Joseph in Ægypt to Putiphar an Eunuch of Pharao's, master of the soldiers.

text.NRSV: It happened at that time that Judah went down from his brothers and settled near a certain Adullamite whose name was Hirah.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at that time, that Judah went down away from his brethren,—and turned aside unto a certain Adullamite, whose, name, was Hirah.
text.KJV: And it came to passe at that time, that Iudah went downe from his brethren, and turned in to a certaine Adullamite, whose name was Hirah:
text.NABRE: About that time Judah went down, away from his brothers, and pitched his tent near a certain Adullamite named Hirah.
text.DRB: The same time Judas going down from his brethren, turned in to a man an Odolamite, named Hiras.

text.NRSV: There Judah saw the daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua; he married her and went in to her.
text.Rotherham: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite whose, name, was Shua,—and he took her, and went in unto her;
text.KJV: And Iudah saw there a daughter of a certaine Canaanite, whose name was Shuah: and he tooke her, and went in vnto her.
text.NABRE: There Judah saw the daughter of a Canaanite named Shua; he married her, and had intercourse with her.
text.DRB: And he saw there the daughter of a man of Chanaan, called Sue: and taking her to wife, he did company with her.

text.NRSV: She conceived and bore a son; and he named him Er.
text.Rotherham: and she conceived and bare a son,—and he called his name Er.
text.KJV: And she conceiued & bare a sonne, and he called his name Er.
text.NABRE: She conceived and bore a son, whom she named Er.
text.DRB: Who conceived, and bare a son, and called his name Her.

text.NRSV: Again she conceived and bore a son whom she named Onan.
text.Rotherham: And she conceived again and bare a son,—and she called his name Onan.
text.KJV: And shee conceiued againe, and bare a sonne, and shee called his name, Onan.
text.NABRE: Again she conceived and bore a son, whom she named Onan.
text.DRB: And conceiving a child again, she called her son after he was born, Onan.

text.NRSV: Yet again she bore a son, and she named him Shelah. She was in Chezib when she bore him.
text.Rotherham: And she, yet again, bare a son, and called his name Shelah,—and she, was at Chezib, when she bare him.
text.KJV: And she yet againe conceiued and bare a sonne, and called his name Shelah: and hee was at Chezib, when shee bare him.
text.NABRE: Then she bore still another son, whom she named Shelah. She was in Chezib when she bore him.
text.DRB: She bare also the third, whom she called Sela: after whose birth, she ceased to bear any more.

text.NRSV: Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn; her name was Tamar.
text.Rotherham: And Judah took a wife, for Er his firstborn,—and, her name, was Tamar.
text.KJV: And Iudah tooke a wife for Er his first borne, whose name was Tamar.
text.NABRE: Judah got a wife named Tamar for his firstborn, Er.
text.DRB: And Judas gave a wife to Her his first-begotten, named Thamar.

text.NRSV: But Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the Lord, and the Lord put him to death.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that Er, Judah’s firstborn was displeasing in the eyes of Yahweh,—so Yahweh put him to death,
text.KJV: And Er, Iudahs first borne was wicked in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD slew him.
text.NABRE: But Er, Judah’s firstborn, greatly offended the Lord; so the Lord took his life.
text.DRB: Also Her the first-begotten of Judas, was wicked in the sight of our Lord, and was slain of him.

text.NRSV: Then Judah said to Onan, “Go in to your brother’s wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her; raise up offspring for your brother.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Judah to Onan, Go in unto thy brother’s wife, and fulfil the duty of a brother-in-law unto her,—and raise up seed, to thy brother.
text.KJV: And Iudah said vnto Onan, Goe in vnto thy brothers wife, and marrie her, and raise vp seed to thy brother.
text.NABRE: Then Judah said to Onan, “Have intercourse with your brother’s wife, in fulfillment of your duty as brother-in-law, and thus preserve your brother’s line.”
text.DRB: Judas therefore said to Onan his son: Company with thy brother's wife, and be joined to her, that thou mayest raise seed to thy brother.

text.NRSV: But since Onan knew that the offspring would not be his, he spilled his semen on the ground whenever he went in to his brother’s wife, so that he would not give offspring to his brother.
text.Rotherham: And Onan knew that the seed would not be his,—and so although he did go in unto his brother’s wife, yet made he waste upon the ground, so as not to give seed to his brother.
text.KJV: And Onan knew that the seed should not be his; and it came to passe when hee went in vnto his brothers wife, that hee spilled it on the ground, least that hee should giue seed to his brother.
text.NABRE: Onan, however, knew that the offspring would not be his; so whenever he had intercourse with his brother’s wife, he wasted his seed on the ground, to avoid giving offspring to his brother.
text.DRB: He knowing that the children should not be born to himself, companying with his brother's wife, shed his seed upon the ground, lest children might be born in his brother's name.

text.NRSV: What he did was displeasing in the sight of the Lord, and he put him to death also.
text.Rotherham: And it was displeasing, in the eyes of Yahweh what he had done,—so he put, him also, to death,
text.KJV: And the thing which he did, displeased the LORD: wherefore hee slew him also.
text.NABRE: What he did greatly offended the Lord, and the Lord took his life too.
text.DRB: And therefore our Lord struck him, because he did a detestable thing.

text.NRSV: Then Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar, “Remain a widow in your father’s house until my son Shelah grows up”—for he feared that he too would die, like his brothers. So Tamar went to live in her father’s house.
text.Rotherham: Then said Judah to Tamar his daughter-in-law—Remain a widow in the house of thy father until Shelah my son grow up. For he said, Lest, he also, die, like his brethren. So Tamar went her way, and remained in the house of her father.
text.KJV: Then said Iudah to Tamar his daughter in law, Remaine a widow at thy fathers house, til Shelah my sonne be growen: (for he said, Lest peraduenture he die also as his brethren did) and Tamar went and dwelt in her fathers house.
text.NABRE: Then Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar, “Remain a widow in your father’s house until my son Shelah grows up”—for he feared that Shelah also might die like his brothers. So Tamar went to live in her father’s house.
text.DRB: For the which cause Judas said to Thamar his daughter in law: Be a widow in thy father's house, till Sela my son grow up: for he feared lest he also might die, as his brethren. Who went her way and dwelt in her father's house.

text.NRSV: In course of time the wife of Judah, Shua’s daughter, died; when Judah’s time of mourning was over, he went up to Timnah to his sheepshearers, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
text.Rotherham: And when the days were multiplied, then died Shua’s daughter, Judah’s wife,—and Judah consoled himself and went up unto his sheep shearers, he and Hirah his friend the Adullamite, towards Timnah.
text.KJV: ¶ And in processe of time, the daughter of Shuah Iudahs wife died: and Iudah was comforted, and went vp vnto his sheepe-shearers to Timnath, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
text.NABRE: Time passed, and the daughter of Shua, Judah’s wife, died. After Judah completed the period of mourning, he went up to Timnah, to those who were shearing his sheep, in company with his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
text.DRB: And after many days were come and gone, the daughter of Sue the wife of Judas died. Who after his mourning having received consolation, went up to the shearers of his sheep, himself and Hiras his shepherd of his flock, the Odolamite, into Thamnas.

text.NRSV: When Tamar was told, “Your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep,”
text.Rotherham: And it was told Tamar saying,—Lo! thy father-in-law is going up towards Timnah, to the shearing of his sheep.
text.KJV: And it was told Lamar, saying, Behold, thy father in law goeth vp to Timnath to sheare his sheepe.
text.NABRE: Then Tamar was told, “Your father-in-law is on his way up to Timnah to shear his sheep.”
text.DRB: And it was told Thamar that her father in law came up into Thamnas to shear his sheep.

text.NRSV: she put off her widow’s garments, put on a veil, wrapped herself up, and sat down at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah. She saw that Shelah was grown up, yet she had not been given to him in marriage.
text.Rotherham: So she put off from her the garments of her widowhood and covered herself with a veil and wrapped herself up, and sat down in the entrance of, Enaim, which is by the way towards Timnah,—for she saw that Shelah had grown up, and she, had not been given him to wife.
text.KJV: And shee put her widowes garments off from her, and couered her with a vaile, and wrapped her selfe, and sate in an open place, which is by the way to Timnath: for shee sawe that Shelah was growen, and she was not giuen vnto him to wife.
text.NABRE: So she took off her widow’s garments, covered herself with a shawl, and having wrapped herself sat down at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the way to Timnah; for she was aware that, although Shelah was now grown up, she had not been given to him in marriage.
text.DRB: Who putting off the garments of her widowhood, took a veil: and changing her habit, sat in the cross way that leadeth to Thamnas: because Sela was grown, and she had not taken him to her husband.

text.NRSV: When Judah saw her, he thought her to be a prostitute, for she had covered her face.
text.Rotherham: And Judah, seeing her, reckoned her to be an unchaste woman,—for she had covered her face.
text.KJV: When Iudah saw her, he thought her to be an harlot: because she had couered her face.
text.NABRE: When Judah saw her, he thought she was a harlot, since she had covered her face.
text.DRB: Whom when Judas had seen, he supposed her to be an harlot: for she had covered her face, lest she should be known.

text.NRSV: He went over to her at the road side, and said, “Come, let me come in to you,” for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said, “What will you give me, that you may come in to me?”
text.Rotherham: So he turned aside unto her. by the way, and said Grant it! I pray thee, let me come in unto thee! For he knew not that she was his daughter-in-law. And she said, What wilt thou give me, if thou shouldst come in unto me?
text.KJV: And hee turned vnto her by the way, and said, Goe to, I pray thee, let me come in vnto thee: (for he knew not that she was his daughter in law) and she said, what wilt thou giue mee, that thou mayest come in vnto me?
text.NABRE: So he went over to her at the roadside and said, “Come, let me have intercourse with you,” for he did not realize that she was his daughter-in-law. She replied, “What will you pay me for letting you have intercourse with me?”
text.DRB: And going unto her, he said: Suffer me to lie with thee: for he knew her not to be his daughter in law. Who answering: What wilt thou give me that thou mayest enjoy my company?

text.NRSV: He answered, “I will send you a kid from the flock.” And she said, “Only if you give me a pledge, until you send it.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, I, will send a kid of the goats from the flock. And she said, Wilt thou give a pledge until thou send it?
text.KJV: And hee said, I will send thee a kid from the flocke: and shee saide, Wilt thou giue mee a pledge, till thou send it?
text.NABRE: He answered, “I will send you a young goat from the flock.” “Very well,” she said, “provided you leave me a pledge until you send it.”
text.DRB: He said: I will send thee a kid out of the flocks. And when she said again: I will suffer that thou wilt, if thou give me a pledge, till thou send that which thou dost promise;

text.NRSV: He said, “What pledge shall I give you?” She replied, “Your signet and your cord, and the staff that is in your hand.” So he gave them to her, and went in to her, and she conceived by him.
text.Rotherham: And he said—What is the pledge that I shall give thee? And She said—Thy signet-ring, and thy guard, and thy staff that is in thy hand. So he gave them to her and came in unto her and she conceived by him.
text.KJV: And he said, What pledge shall I giue thee? And she said, Thy signet, and thy bracelets, and thy staffe, that is in thine hand: and he gaue it her, & came in vnto her, and she conceived by him.
text.NABRE: Judah asked, “What pledge should I leave you?” She answered, “Your seal and cord, and the staff in your hand.” So he gave them to her and had intercourse with her, and she conceived by him.
text.DRB: Judas said: What wilt thou to be given thee for a pledge? She answered: Thy ring, and bracelet, and staff which thou holdest in thy hand. The woman therefore by once companying conceived,

text.NRSV: Then she got up and went away, and taking off her veil she put on the garments of her widowhood.
text.Rotherham: Then she arose and went her way, and put off her veil from her,—and put on the garments of her widowhood.
text.KJV: And shee arose and went away, and laid by her vaile from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.
text.NABRE: After she got up and went away, she took off her shawl and put on her widow’s garments again.
text.DRB: and rising she went her way: and putting off the apparel which she had taken, put on the garments of her widowhood.

text.NRSV: When Judah sent the kid by his friend the Adullamite, to recover the pledge from the woman, he could not find her.
text.Rotherham: And Judah sent the kid of the goats by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive the pledge at the hand of the woman,—but he found her not.
text.KJV: And Iudah sent the kidde by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive his pledge from the womans hand: but he found her not.
text.NABRE: Judah sent the young goat by his friend the Adullamite to recover the pledge from the woman; but he did not find her.
text.DRB: And Judas sent a kid by his shepherd the Odolamite, that he might receive the pledge again, which he had given to the woman: who when he had not found her,

text.NRSV: He asked the townspeople, “Where is the temple prostitute who was at Enaim by the wayside?” But they said, “No prostitute has been here.”
text.Rotherham: So he asked the men of her place saying. Where is the devotee, she that was in Enaim, by the way? And they said,—There hath been here no devotee.
text.KJV: Then hee asked the men of that place, saying, where is the harlot, that was openly by the way side? And they said, There was no harlot in this place.
text.NABRE: So he asked the men of that place, “Where is the prostitute, the one by the roadside in Enaim?” But they answered, “No prostitute has been here.”
text.DRB: he asked the men of that place: Where is the woman that sat in the cross way? All making answer: There was no harlot in this place,

text.NRSV: So he returned to Judah, and said, “I have not found her; moreover the townspeople said, ‘No prostitute has been here.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Then returned he unto Judah, and said—I found her not,—moreover also, the men of the place said, There hath been here no devotee.
text.KJV: And he returned to Iudah, and said, I cannot finde her: and also the men of the place said, That there was no harlot in this place.
text.NABRE: He went back to Judah and told him, “I did not find her; and besides, the men of the place said, ‘No prostitute has been here.’”
text.DRB: he returned to Judas, and said to him: I have not found her: yea the men also of that place said unto me, that there never sat harlot there.

text.NRSV: Judah replied, “Let her keep the things as her own, otherwise we will be laughed at; you see, I sent this kid, and you could not find her.”
text.Rotherham: And Judah said: Thou must take it to her, lest we become a contempt,—lo! I sent this kid, and, thou, hast not found her.
text.KJV: And Iudah said, Let her take it to her, lest we bee shamed: behold, I sent this kidde, and thou hast not found her.
text.NABRE: “Let her keep the things,” Judah replied; “otherwise we will become a laughingstock. After all, I did send her this young goat, but you did not find her.”
text.DRB: Judas said: Let her take it to her, surely she cannot charge us with a lie, I sent the kid which I promised: and thou didst not find her.

text.NRSV: About three months later Judah was told, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has played the whore; moreover she is pregnant as a result of whoredom.” And Judah said, “Bring her out, and let her be burned.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that about three months after, it was told Judah, saying—Tamar thy daughter-in-law hath been guilty of unchastity, moreover also lo! she hath conceived by unchastity. Then said Judah, Bring her forth and let her be burnt.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe about three moneths after, that it was tolde Iudah, saying, Tamar thy daughter in law hath played the harlot, and also behold, she is with child by whoredom: and Iudah said, Bring her foorth, and let her be burnt.
text.NABRE: About three months later, Judah was told, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has acted like a harlot and now she is pregnant from her harlotry.” Judah said, “Bring her out; let her be burned.”
text.DRB: And behold after three months they told Judas, saying: Thamar thy daughter in law hath played the harlot, and her belly seemeth to swell. And Judas said: Bring her forth that she may be burnt.

text.NRSV: As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law, “It was the owner of these who made me pregnant.” And she said, “Take note, please, whose these are, the signet and the cord and the staff.”
text.Rotherham: When, she, was about to be brought forth, then, she herself, sent unto her father-in-law saying, By the man to whom these belong, have I, conceived! And she said—Examine, I pray thee, to whom belong the signet-ring, and the guard, and the staff—these!
text.KJV: When she was brought forth, she sent to her father in law, saying, By the man whose these are, am I with child: and shee said, Discerne, I pray thee, whose are these, the signet, and bracelets, and staffe.
text.NABRE: But as she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law, “It is by the man to whom these things belong that I am pregnant.” Then she said, “See whose seal and cord and staff these are.”
text.DRB: Who when she was led to execution, she sent to her father in law, saying: By that man, whose these things are, have I conceived: look whose the ring is, and the bracelet, and the staff.

text.NRSV: Then Judah acknowledged them and said, “She is more in the right than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah.” And he did not lie with her again.
text.Rotherham: So Judah examined them and said—More righteous than I! forasmuch, as I had not given her to Shelah my son. And he knew her again no more.
text.KJV: And Iudah acknowledged them, and said, She hath bin more righteous then I: because that I gaue her not to Shelah my sonne: and he knew her againe no more.
text.NABRE: Judah recognized them and said, “She is in the right rather than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah.” He had no further sexual relations with her.
text.DRB: Who acknowledging the gifts, said: She is juster than I: because I did not give her to Sela my son. But he knew her no more.

text.NRSV: When the time of her delivery came, there were twins in her womb.
text.Rotherham: And, it came to pass at the time of her travail, that lo! twins, were in her womb.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe in the time of her trauaile, that beholde, twinnes were in her wombe.
text.NABRE: When the time of her delivery came, there were twins in her womb.
text.DRB: And when she was ready to be brought to bed, there appeared twins in her belly: and in the very delivery of the infants, one put forth the hand, wherein the midwife tied a scarlet string, saying:

text.NRSV: While she was in labor, one put out a hand; and the midwife took and bound on his hand a crimson thread, saying, “This one came out first.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when she was in travail, that one put forth a hand,—so the midwife took and bound upon his hand a crimson thread, saying—This came first!
text.KJV: And it came to passe when shee trauailed, that the one put out his hand, and the midwife tooke and bound vpon his hand a skarlet threed, saying, This came out first.
text.NABRE: While she was giving birth, one put out his hand; and the midwife took and tied a crimson thread on his hand, noting, “This one came out first.”
text.DRB: This shall come forth the former.

text.NRSV: But just then he drew back his hand, and out came his brother; and she said, “What a breach you have made for yourself!” Therefore he was named Perez.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, no sooner had he withdrawn his hand, than lo! his brother had come. And she said, Wherefore hast thou made for thyself a breach? So his name was called Perez.
text.KJV: And it came to passe as he drewe backe his hand, that behold, his brother came out: and she said, how hast thou broken foorth? this breach bee vpon thee: Therefore his name was called Pharez.
text.NABRE: But as he withdrew his hand, his brother came out; and she said, “What a breach you have made for yourself!” So he was called Perez.
text.DRB: But he drawing back his hand, the other came forth: and the woman said: Why is the partition divided for thee? And for this cause called his name Phares.

text.NRSV: Afterward his brother came out with the crimson thread on his hand; and he was named Zerah.
text.Rotherham: And afterwards, came his brother, on whose hand was the crimson thread,—so his name was called, Zerah.
text.KJV: And afterward came out his brother that had the skarlet threed vpon his hand, and his name was called Zarah.
text.NABRE: Afterward his brother, who had the crimson thread on his hand, came out; he was called Zerah.
text.DRB: Afterward his brother came forth, in whose hand was the scarlet string: whom she called Zara.

text.NRSV: Now Joseph was taken down to Egypt, and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
text.Rotherham: Now, Joseph, was taken down to Egypt,—and Potiphar, courtier of Pharaoh, chief of the royal executioners, an Egyptian bought him, at the hand of the Ishmaelites, who had brought him down thither.
text.KJV: And Ioseph was brought downe to Egypt, and Potiphar an Officer of Pharaoh, captaine of þ guard, an Egyptian, bought him of the hand of the Ishmeelites, which had brought him downe thither.
text.NABRE: When Joseph was taken down to Egypt, an Egyptian, Potiphar, an official of Pharaoh and his chief steward, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him there.
text.DRB: Therefore Joseph was brought into Ægypt, and Putiphar an Eunuch of Pharao, Prince of his army, a man of Ægypt bought him, at the hand of the Ismaelites, by whom he was brought.

text.NRSV: The Lord was with Joseph, and he became a successful man; he was in the house of his Egyptian master.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that Yahweh, was with Joseph, so that he became a prosperous man,—and was in the house of his lord the Egyptian.
text.KJV: And the LORD was with Ioseph, and hee was a prosperous man, and hee was in the house of his master the Egyptian.
text.NABRE: The Lord was with Joseph and he enjoyed great success and was assigned to the household of his Egyptian master.
text.DRB: And our Lord was with him, and he was a man that in all things did prosperously: and he dwelt in his master's house,

text.NRSV: His master saw that the Lord was with him, and that the Lord caused all that he did to prosper in his hands.
text.Rotherham: And his lord saw, that, Yahweh, was with him—and that whatsoever, he was doing, Yahweh, was causing to prosper in his hand.
text.KJV: And his master sawe that the LORD was with him, and that the LORD made all that he did, to prosper in his hand.
text.NABRE: When his master saw that the Lord was with him and brought him success in whatever he did,
text.DRB: who knew very well that our Lord was with him, and that all things which he did were directed by him in his hand.

text.NRSV: So Joseph found favor in his sight and attended him; he made him overseer of his house and put him in charge of all that he had.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph found favour in his eyes and waited upon him,—and he made him overseer over his house, and all that he had, gave he into his hand.
text.KJV: And Ioseph found grace in his sight, and he serued him; and hee made him ouerseer ouer his house, and all that he had he put into his hand.
text.NABRE: he favored Joseph and made him his personal attendant; he put him in charge of his household and entrusted to him all his possessions.
text.DRB: And Joseph found grace before his master, and ministered to him: by whom being made ruler over all his things, he governed the house committed to him, and all things that were delivered unto him:

text.NRSV: From the time that he made him overseer in his house and over all that he had, the Lord blessed the Egyptian’s house for Joseph’s sake; the blessing of the Lord was on all that he had, in house and field.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass from the time that he made him overseer in his house and over all that he had, that Yahweh blessed the house of the Egyptian, for Joseph’s sake,—yea it came to pass, that the blessing of Yahweh, was with all that he had, in the house and in the field;
text.KJV: And it came to passe from the time that hee had made him overseer in his house, and ouer all that he had, that the LORD blessed the Egyptians house for Iosephs sake: and the blessing of the LORD was vpon all that he had in the house, and in the field.
text.NABRE: From the moment that he put him in charge of his household and all his possessions, the Lord blessed the Egyptian’s house for Joseph’s sake; the Lord’s blessing was on everything he owned, both inside the house and out.
text.DRB: and our Lord blessed the house of the Ægyptian for Joseph's sake, and multiplied as well in houses, as in lands all his substance.

text.NRSV: So he left all that he had in Joseph’s charge; and, with him there, he had no concern for anything but the food that he ate. Now Joseph was handsome and good-looking.
text.Rotherham: so that he left all that he had in the hand of Joseph, and took note with him of nothing, save only the bread which, he himself, was eating. And so it was that Joseph was comely in form and comely in countenance.
text.KJV: And he left all that he had, in Iosephs hand: and he knew not ought he had, saue the bread which he did eate: and Ioseph was a goodly person, and well fauoured.
text.NABRE: Having left everything he owned in Joseph’s charge, he gave no thought, with Joseph there, to anything but the food he ate. Now Joseph was well-built and handsome.
text.DRB: Neither knew he any other thing, but the bread which he did eat. And Joseph was of beautiful countenance, and comely favoured to behold.

text.NRSV: And after a time his master’s wife cast her eyes on Joseph and said, “Lie with me.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that his lord’s wife lifted up her eyes unto Joseph,—and she said—Come! lie with me.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe after these things, that his masters wife cast her eyes vpon Ioseph, and shee said, Lie with me.
text.NABRE: After a time, his master’s wife looked at him with longing and said, “Lie with me.”
text.DRB: After many days therefore his Mistress cast her eyes on Joseph, and said: Sleep with me.

text.NRSV: But he refused and said to his master’s wife, “Look, with me here, my master has no concern about anything in the house, and he has put everything that he has in my hand.
text.Rotherham: And he refused and said unto his lord’s wife, Lo! my lord, taketh no note with me as to what is in the house,—but, all that pertaineth to him, hath he delivered into my hand:
text.KJV: But he refused, and said vnto his masters wife, Behold, my master wotteth not what is with mee in the house, and he hath committed all that he hath, to my hand.
text.NABRE: But he refused and said to his master’s wife, “Look, as long as I am here, my master does not give a thought to anything in the house, but has entrusted to me all he owns.
text.DRB: Who in nowise assenting to that wicked act, said to her: Behold, my master having delivered all things unto me, knoweth not what he hath in his own house:

text.NRSV: He is not greater in this house than I am, nor has he kept back anything from me except yourself, because you are his wife. How then could I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?”
text.Rotherham: There is no one greater in this house than I, neither hath he withheld from me anything, save only thyself in that thou, art his wife,—how, then could I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?
text.KJV: There is none greater in this house then I: neither hath hee kept backe any thing from me, but thee, because thou art his wife: how then can I doe this great wickednesse, and sinne against God?
text.NABRE: He has no more authority in this house than I do. He has withheld from me nothing but you, since you are his wife. How, then, could I do this great wrong and sin against God?”
text.DRB: Neither is there any thing which is not in my power, or that he hath not delivered to me, beside thee, that art his wife: how therefore can I do this wicked thing, and sin against my God?

text.NRSV: And although she spoke to Joseph day after day, he would not consent to lie beside her or to be with her.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that although she Spake unto Joseph day after day, yet hearkened he not unto her to lie beside her to be with her.
text.KJV: And it came to passe as she spake to Ioseph day by day, that hee hearkened not vnto her, to lie by her, or to bee with her.
text.NABRE: Although she spoke to him day after day, he would not agree to lie with her, or even be near her.
text.DRB: With these kind of words day by day both the woman was importune upon the young man, and he refused the adultery.

text.NRSV: One day, however, when he went into the house to do his work, and while no one else was in the house,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when, on a certain day, he went into the house to attend to his business,—and there was no man of the household, there in the house,
text.KJV: And it came to passe about this time, that Ioseph went in to the house, to doe his busines, and there was none of the men of the house there within.
text.NABRE: One such day, when Joseph came into the house to do his work, and none of the household servants were then in the house,
text.DRB: And it chanced on a certain day, that Joseph went into the house, and did some business without any man with him:

text.NRSV: she caught hold of his garment, saying, “Lie with me!” But he left his garment in her hand, and fled and ran outside.
text.Rotherham: that she caught him by his garment, saying—Come! lie with me. Then left he his garment in her hand, and fled and gat forth outside,
text.KJV: And shee caught him by his garment, saying, Lie with me: and he left his garment in her hand, and fled, and got him out.
text.NABRE: she laid hold of him by his cloak, saying, “Lie with me!” But leaving the cloak in her hand, he escaped and ran outside.
text.DRB: and she catching the skirt of his garment, said: Sleep with me. Who leaving the cloak in her hand, fled, and went forth abroad.

text.NRSV: When she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and had fled outside,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand,—and fled forth outside,
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when she saw that hee had left his garment in her hand, and was fled forth;
text.NABRE: When she saw that he had left his cloak in her hand as he escaped outside,
text.DRB: And when the woman saw the garment in her hands, and herself to be contemned,

text.NRSV: she called out to the members of her household and said to them, “See, my husband has brought among us a Hebrew to insult us! He came in to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice;
text.Rotherham: that she cried out to the men of her house and spake to them, saying, Look ye! he hath brought in to us a Hebrew man to insult us,—He came in unto me, to lie with me, so I cried out with a loud voice.
text.KJV: That she called vnto the men of her house, and spake vnto them, saying, See, he hath brought in an Hebrew vnto vs, to mocke vs: he came in vnto me to lie with me, and I cried with a loud voice.
text.NABRE: she cried out to her household servants and told them, “Look! My husband has brought us a Hebrew man to mock us! He came in here to lie with me, but I cried out loudly.
text.DRB: she called to her the men of her house, and said to them: See he hath brought in an Hebrew to delude us: he came upon me for to lie with me; and when I had cried out,

text.NRSV: and when he heard me raise my voice and cry out, he left his garment beside me, and fled outside.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he heard that I lifted high my voice, and cried out, then left he his garment beside me, and fled and gat forth outside.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when hee heard that I lifted vp my voice, and cried, that he left his garment with mee, and fled, and got him out.
text.NABRE: When he heard me scream, he left his cloak beside me and escaped and ran outside.”
text.DRB: and he heard my voice, he left the cloak that I held, and fled forth.

text.NRSV: Then she kept his garment by her until his master came home,
text.Rotherham: So she laid by his garment beside her,—until his lord should come into his house,
text.KJV: And she laid vp his garment by her, vntill her lord came home.
text.NABRE: She kept the cloak with her until his master came home.
text.DRB: For an argument therefore of her credit she reserved the cloak, & shewed it to her husband returning home,

text.NRSV: and she told him the same story, saying, “The Hebrew servant, whom you have brought among us, came in to me to insult me;
text.Rotherham: Then spake she unto him, according to these words, saying,—He hath been in unto me—the Hebrew servant whom thou didst bring in to us—to insult me.
text.KJV: And she spake vnto him, according to these words, saying, The Hebrew seruant which thou hast brought vnto vs, came in vnto me to mocke me.
text.NABRE: Then she told him the same story: “The Hebrew slave whom you brought us came to me to amuse himself at my expense.
text.DRB: and said: There came unto me the Hebrew servant, whom thou didst bring hither, for to delude me:

text.NRSV: but as soon as I raised my voice and cried out, he left his garment beside me, and fled outside.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when I lifted high my voice and cried out, then left he his garment beside me, and fled outside.
text.KJV: And it came to passe as I lift vp my voice, and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled out.
text.NABRE: But when I screamed, he left his cloak beside me and escaped outside.”
text.DRB: and when he heard me cry, he left the cloak which I held, and fled forth.

text.NRSV: When his master heard the words that his wife spoke to him, saying, “This is the way your servant treated me,” he became enraged.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when his lord heard the words of his wife which she spake unto him saying, According to these words, hath thy servant done to me, Then was kindled his anger.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when his master heard the words of his wife, which she spake vnto him, saying, After this maner did thy seruant to me, that his wrath was kindled.
text.NABRE: When the master heard his wife’s story in which she reported, “Thus and so your servant did to me,” he became enraged.
text.DRB: His master hearing these things, and giving over light credit to his wife's words, was very wrath:

text.NRSV: And Joseph’s master took him and put him into the prison, the place where the king’s prisoners were confined; he remained there in prison.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph’s lord took him and delivered him up to the prison,: the place where, the prisoners of the king, were imprisoned,—so he was there, in the prison.
text.KJV: And Iosephs master tooke him, and put him into the prison, a place, where þ kings prisoners were bound: and he was there in the prison.
text.NABRE: Joseph’s master seized him and put him into the jail where the king’s prisoners were confined. And there he sat, in jail.
text.DRB: and delivered Joseph into prison, where the king's prisoners were kept, and he was there shut up.

text.NRSV: But the Lord was with Joseph and showed him steadfast love; he gave him favor in the sight of the chief jailer.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Yahweh, was with Joseph, and extended unto him lovingkindness,—and gave him his favour in the eyes of the chief of the prison.
text.KJV: ¶ But the LORD was with Ioseph, and shewed him mercie, and gaue him fauour in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
text.NABRE: But the Lord was with Joseph, and showed him kindness by making the chief jailer well-disposed toward him.
