text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when, they, were emptying their, sacks then lo! each man’s bundle of silver, was in his sack,—and when they beheld their bundles of silver—they and their father, they were afraid,
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe as they emptied their sacks, that behold, euery mans bundle of money was in his sacke: and when both they and their father saw the bundels of money, they were afraid.
text.NABRE: When they were emptying their sacks, there in each one’s sack was his moneybag! At the sight of their moneybags, they and their father were afraid.
text.DRB: This being said when they poured out their corn, every man found his money tied in the mouth of the sacks: and all being astonished together,

text.NRSV: And their father Jacob said to them, “I am the one you have bereaved of children: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin. All this has happened to me!”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob their father said unto them, Me, have ye bereaved,—Joseph, is not, and, Simeon, is not, and Benjamin, ye would take! Against me, have happened all these things.
text.KJV: And Iacob their father said vnto them, We haue ye bereaued of my children: Ioseph is not, and Simeon is not, and ye wil take Beniamin away: all these things are against me.
text.NABRE: Their father Jacob said to them: “Must you make me childless? Joseph is no more, Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin away! All these things have happened to me!”
text.DRB: their father Jacob said: You have made me to be without children, Joseph is not living, Simeon is kept in bonds, and Benjamin you will take away: all these evils, are fallen upon me.

text.NRSV: Then Reuben said to his father, “You may kill my two sons if I do not bring him back to you. Put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you.”
text.Rotherham: Then spake Reuben unto his father saying, My two sons, shalt thou put to death, if I bring him not unto thee,—Come give him up upon my hand, and I, will restore him unto thee.
text.KJV: And Reuben spake vnto his father, saying; Slay my two sonnes, if I bring him not to thee: deliuer him into my hand, and I will bring him to thee againe.
text.NABRE: Then Reuben told his father: “You may kill my own two sons if I do not return him to you! Put him in my care, and I will bring him back to you.”
text.DRB: To whom Ruben answered: Kill my two sons, if I bring him not again to thee: deliver him into my hand, and I will restore him unto thee.

text.NRSV: But he said, “My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he alone is left. If harm should come to him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, My son shall not go down with you,—For, his brother, is dead and, he alone, is left, and as surely as there befall him any mischief by the way wherein ye go, so surely shall ye bring down my grey hairs with sorrow unto hades.
text.KJV: And he said, My sonne shall not goe downe with you, for his brother is dead, and he is left alone: if mischiefe befall him by the way in the which yee goe, then shall ye bring downe my gray haires with sorrow to the graue.
text.NABRE: But Jacob replied: “My son shall not go down with you. Now that his brother is dead, he is the only one left. If some disaster should befall him on the journey you must make, you would send my white head down to Sheol in grief.”
text.DRB: But he said: My son shall not go down with you: his brother is dead, and he alone is remaining: if any adversity shall chance to him in the land to the which you go, you shall bring down my hoar hairs with sorrow unto hell.

text.NRSV: Now the famine was severe in the land.
text.Rotherham: But the famine, was severe in the land.
text.KJV: And the famine was sore in the land.
text.NABRE: Now the famine in the land grew severe.
text.DRB: In the mean time the famine did oppress all the land very sore.

text.NRSV: And when they had eaten up the grain that they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go again, buy us a little more food.”
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when they had made an end of eating the corn which they had brought in out of Egypt, that their father said unto them,—Return buy for us a little food.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when they had eaten vp the corne, which they had brought out of Egypt, their father said vnto them, Goe againe, buy vs a little foode.
text.NABRE: So when they had used up all the grain they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go back and buy us a little more food.”
text.DRB: And the provision being spent, which they had brought out of Ægypt, Jacob said to his sons: Go again, and buy us a little victual.

text.NRSV: But Judah said to him, “The man solemnly warned us, saying, ‘You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.’
text.Rotherham: Then spake Judah unto him saying,—The man, did protest, to us saying—Ye shall not see my face, except, your brother, is with you.
text.KJV: And Iudah spake vnto him, saying, The man did solemnly protest vnto vs, saying, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
text.NABRE: But Judah replied: “The man strictly warned us, ‘You shall not see me unless your brother is with you.’
text.DRB: Judas answered: That Same man denounced unto us under attestation of an oath, saying: You shall not see my face, unless you bring your youngest brother with you.

text.NRSV: If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food;
text.Rotherham: If thou art sending our brother with us, we will certainly go down, and buy for thee food;
text.KJV: If thou wilt send our brother with vs, we will goe downe and buy thee food.
text.NABRE: If you are willing to let our brother go with us, we will go down to buy food for you.
text.DRB: If therefore thou wilt send him with us, we will go forward together, and will buy necessaries for thee:

text.NRSV: but if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’ ”
text.Rotherham: but, if thou art not sending him, we will not go down, for the man, said unto us Ye shall not see my face, except, your brother, is with you.
text.KJV: But if thou wilt not send him, we will not goe downe: for the man saide vnto vs, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
text.NABRE: But if you are not willing, we will not go down, because the man told us, ‘You shall not see me unless your brother is with you.’”
text.DRB: but if thou wilt not, we will not go: for the man, as we have often said, denounced unto us saying: You shall not see my face without your youngest brother.

text.NRSV: Israel said, “Why did you treat me so badly as to tell the man that you had another brother?”
text.Rotherham: And Israel said, Wherefore did ye ill to me,—to tell the man, there yet remained to you a brother?
text.KJV: And Israel said, Wherefore dealt ye so ill with me, as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?
text.NABRE: Israel demanded, “Why did you bring this trouble on me by telling the man that you had another brother?”
text.DRB: Israel said to them: You have done this to my misery, in that you told him that you had another brother also.

text.NRSV: They replied, “The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred, saying, ‘Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?’ What we told him was in answer to these questions. Could we in any way know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother down’?”
text.Rotherham: And they said, The man, did ask, concerning ourselves and concerning our kindred saying—Is your father yet alive? Have ye a brother? So we told him according to the tenor of these words. Could we, at all know, that he would say, Bring down your brother?
text.KJV: And they said, The man asked vs straitly of our state, and of our kindred, saying, Is your father yet aliue? haue yee another brother? and we tolde him according to the tenour of these words: Could we certainely knowe that he would say, Bring your brother downe?
text.NABRE: They answered: “The man kept asking about us and our family: ‘Is your father still living? Do you have another brother?’ We answered him accordingly. How could we know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother down here’?”
text.DRB: But they answered: The man asked us in order our progeny: if our father lived; if we had a brother: and we answered him consequently to that which he demanded: could we know that he would say: Bring hither your brother with you?

text.NRSV: Then Judah said to his father Israel, “Send the boy with me, and let us be on our way, so that we may live and not die—you and we and also our little ones.
text.Rotherham: Then said Judah unto Israel his father—Come! send thou down the young man with me that we may arise and go our way,—and live and not die, both we and thou and our little ones.
text.KJV: And Iudah said vnto Israel his father, Send the lad with me, and wee will arise and go, that we may liue, and not die, both we, and thou, and also our little ones.
text.NABRE: Then Judah urged his father Israel: “Let the boy go with me, that we may be off and on our way if you and we and our children are to keep from starving to death.
text.DRB: Judas also said to his father: Send the boy with me, that we may set forward, and may live: lest we and our little ones die.

text.NRSV: I myself will be surety for him; you can hold me accountable for him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever.
text.Rotherham: I, will be surety for him, at my hand, shalt thou require him,—as surely as I bring him not in unto thee, and set him down before thee, so surely will I be counted a sinner against thee all the days,
text.KJV: I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him: if I bring him not vnto thee, and set him before thee, then let me beare the blame for euer.
text.NABRE: I myself will serve as a guarantee for him. You can hold me responsible for him. If I fail to bring him back and set him before you, I will bear the blame before you forever.
text.DRB: Itake upon me the boy: require him of my hand, unless I bring him again, and restore him to thee, I will be guilty of sin against thee for ever.

text.NRSV: If we had not delayed, we would now have returned twice.”
text.Rotherham: For, if we had not lingered, surely now, had we come back here a second time.
text.KJV: For except we had lingred, surely now wee had returned this second time.
text.NABRE: Had we not delayed, we could have been there and back twice by now!”
text.DRB: If delay had not been made, we had been come now the second time.

text.NRSV: Then their father Israel said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry them down as a present to the man—a little balm and a little honey, gum, resin, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text.Rotherham: Then Israel their father said unto them—If so, then do this,—Take of the song of the land in your vessels, and carry down to the man a present,—A little balsam and a little honey, tragacanth gum and cistus gum, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text.KJV: And their father Israel said vnto them, If it must bee so now, doe this: take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels, and carie downe the man a Present, a litle balme, and a litle honie, spices, and myrrhe, nuts, and almonds.
text.NABRE: Israel their father then told them: “If it must be so, then do this: Put some of the land’s best products in your baggage and take them down to the man as gifts: some balm and honey, gum and resin, and pistachios and almonds.
text.DRB: Therefore Israel their father said to them: If it must needs be so, do that you will: take of the best fruits of the land in your vessels, and carry to the man for presents, a courtesy of rosen, & of honey, & of incense, of myrrh, of terebinth, and of almonds.

text.NRSV: Take double the money with you. Carry back with you the money that was returned in the top of your sacks; perhaps it was an oversight.
text.Rotherham: And double silver, take in your hand,—also the silver that was put back in the mouth of your sacks, carry ye back in your hand, peradventure it was an error,
text.KJV: And take double money in your hand, and the money that was brought againe in the mouth of your sackes: carie it againe in your hand, peraduenture it was an ouersight.
text.NABRE: Also take double the money along, for you must return the amount that was put back in the mouths of your bags; it may have been a mistake.
text.DRB: Double money also carry with you: and recarry that you found in your sacks, lest perhaps it was done by an error:

text.NRSV: Take your brother also, and be on your way again to the man;
text.Rotherham: Your brother also, take ye,—and arise go again unto the man.
text.KJV: Take also your brother, and arise, goe againe vnto the man.
text.NABRE: Take your brother, too, and be off on your way back to the man.
text.DRB: but take also your brother, and go to the man.

text.NRSV: may God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, so that he may send back your other brother and Benjamin. As for me, if I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.”
text.Rotherham: And, God Almighty, give you compassion before the man, so shall he send with you, your other brother, and Benjamin. But, as for me, when I am bereaved, I am bereaved!
text.KJV: And God Almightie giue you mercie before the man, that he may send away your other brother, and Beniamin: If I be bereaued of my children, I am bereaued.
text.NABRE: May God Almighty grant you mercy in the presence of the man, so that he may let your other brother go, as well as Benjamin. As for me, if I am to suffer bereavement, I shall suffer it.”
text.DRB: And my God almighty make him favourable unto you; and send back with you your brother, whom he keepeth, and this Benjamin: as for me I shall be desolate without children.

text.NRSV: So the men took the present, and they took double the money with them, as well as Benjamin. Then they went on their way down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.
text.Rotherham: So the men took this present, double silver also, took they in their hand and Benjamin,—and arose and went down into Egypt, and stood before Joseph.
text.KJV: ¶ And the men tooke that Present, and they tooke double money in their hand, and Beniamin, and rose vp, and went downe to Egypt, and stood before Ioseph.
text.NABRE: So the men took those gifts and double the money and Benjamin. They made their way down to Egypt and presented themselves before Joseph.
text.DRB: The men therefore took the presents, & the double money, & Benjamin; & went down into Ægypt, & stood before Joseph.

text.NRSV: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, “Bring the men into the house, and slaughter an animal and make ready, for the men are to dine with me at noon.”
text.Rotherham: And Joseph saw—with them—Benjamin, so he said to him that was over his house Bring the men into the house,—and slay meat and make ready, for with me, shall the men eat at noon.
text.KJV: And when Ioseph sawe Beniamin with them, hee said to the ruler of his house, Bring these men home, and slay, and make ready: for these men shall dine with me at noone.
text.NABRE: When Joseph saw them and Benjamin, he told his steward, “Take the men into the house, and have an animal slaughtered and prepared, for they are to dine with me at noon.”
text.DRB: Whom when he had seen & Benjamin withal, he commanded the steward of his house, saying: Bring in the men into the house, and kill victims, and prepare a feast; because they shall eat with me at noon.

text.NRSV: The man did as Joseph said, and brought the men to Joseph’s house.
text.Rotherham: So the men did as Joseph had said, and the man brought the men into Joseph’s house,
text.KJV: And the man did as Ioseph hade: and the man brought the men into Iosephs house.
text.NABRE: Doing as Joseph had ordered, the steward conducted the men to Joseph’s house.
text.DRB: He did that which was commanded him, and brought the men into the house.

text.NRSV: Now the men were afraid because they were brought to Joseph’s house, and they said, “It is because of the money, replaced in our sacks the first time, that we have been brought in, so that he may have an opportunity to fall upon us, to make slaves of us and take our donkeys.”
text.Rotherham: Then were the men afraid because they had been brought into the house of Joseph, and they said—For the matter of the silver that came back in our sacks at the beginning, have, we, been brought in,—that he may turn round upon us and fall upon us, and take us for servants and our asses.
text.KJV: And the men were afraid, because they were brought into Iosephs house, and they said, Because of the money that was returned in our sackes at the first time are we brought in, that hee may seeke occasion against vs, and fall vpon vs, and take vs for bondmen, and our asses.
text.NABRE: But they became apprehensive when they were led to his house. “It must be,” they thought, “on account of the money put back in our bags the first time, that we are taken inside—in order to attack us and take our donkeys and seize us as slaves.”
text.DRB: And there being sore afraid, they said one to another: because of the money which we carried back the first time in our sacks, we are brought in: that he may turn upon us a false accusation, and forcibly bring both us and our asses into bondage.

text.NRSV: So they went up to the steward of Joseph’s house and spoke with him at the entrance to the house.
text.Rotherham: So they drew near unto the man that was over Joseph’s house,—and spake unto him at the entrance of the house;
text.KJV: And they came neere to the steward of Iosephs house, and they communed with him at the doore of the house,
text.NABRE: So they went up to Joseph’s steward and talked to him at the entrance of the house.
text.DRB: Wherefore in the very door stepping to the steward of the house,

text.NRSV: They said, “Oh, my lord, we came down the first time to buy food;
text.Rotherham: and said, May it please my lord! We, did indeed come down, at the beginning to buy food.
text.KJV: And said, O Sir, we came indeed downe at the first time to buy food.
text.NABRE: “If you please, sir,” they said, “we came down here once before to buy food.
text.DRB: They spake: Sir, we desire thee to hear us. Now once before we came down to buy provision:

text.NRSV: and when we came to the lodging place we opened our sacks, and there was each one’s money in the top of his sack, our money in full weight. So we have brought it back with us.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when we had entered into the inn, that we opened our sacks, and lo! the silver of each man, in the mouth of his sack,—our silver in its full weight,—so we have bought it back in our hand!
text.KJV: And it came to passe when wee came to the Inne, that wee opened our sackes, and behold, euery mans money was in the mouth of his sacke, our money in ful weight: and we haue brought it againe in our hand.
text.NABRE: But when we arrived at a night’s encampment and opened our bags, there was each man’s money in the mouth of his bag—our money in the full amount! We have now brought it back.
text.DRB: Which being bought, when we were come to the Inn, we opened our sacks, and found our money in the mouths of the sacks: which we have now brought again in the same weight.

text.NRSV: Moreover we have brought down with us additional money to buy food. We do not know who put our money in our sacks.”
text.Rotherham: And, more silver, have we brought down in our hand, to buy food,—we know not who put our silver in our sacks.
text.KJV: And other money haue wee brought downe in our handes to buy food: we cannot tell who put our money in our sackes.
text.NABRE: We have brought other money to buy food. We do not know who put our money in our bags.”
text.DRB: But we have brought other money besides, to buy the things that are necessary for us: our conscience is not privy, who put it in our bags.

text.NRSV: He replied, “Rest assured, do not be afraid; your God and the God of your father must have put treasure in your sacks for you; I received your money.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Peace to you do not fear! Your God, and the God of your father, hath given you secret treasure, in your sacks, as for your silver, it came in unto me. And he brought forth unto them Simeon.
text.KJV: And he said, Peace be to you, feare not: your God, and the God of your father, hath giuen you treasure in your sackes: I had your money. And hee brought Simeon out vnto them.
text.NABRE: He replied, “Calm down! Do not fear! Your God and the God of your father must have put treasure in your bags for you. As for your money, I received it.” With that, he led Simeon out to them.
text.DRB: But he answered: Peace be with you, fear you not: your God, and the God of your father hath given you treasures in your sacks. For the money, which you gave me, I have for good. And he brought forth Simeon unto them.

text.NRSV: When the steward had brought the men into Joseph’s house, and given them water, and they had washed their feet, and when he had given their donkeys fodder,
text.Rotherham: So the man brought the men into Joseph’s house,—and gave them water and they bathed their feet, and he gave them provender for their asses.
text.KJV: And the man brought the men into Iosephs house, and gaue them water, and they washed their feete, and he gaue their asses prouender.
text.NABRE: The steward then brought the men inside Joseph’s house. He gave them water to wash their feet, and gave fodder to their donkeys.
text.DRB: And being brought into the house, he fetched water, and they washed their feet, and he gave provender to their asses.

text.NRSV: they made the present ready for Joseph’s coming at noon, for they had heard that they would dine there.
text.Rotherham: And they made ready the present, against the coming in of Joseph at noon,—for they heard that it was there they should eat bread.
text.KJV: And they made ready the Present against Ioseph came at noone: for they heard that they should eate bread there.
text.NABRE: Then they set out their gifts to await Joseph’s arrival at noon, for they had heard that they were to dine there.
text.DRB: But they made ready the presents, till Joseph should come in at noon: for they had heard that they should eat bread there.

text.NRSV: When Joseph came home, they brought him the present that they had carried into the house, and bowed to the ground before him.
text.Rotherham: So when Joseph came into the house, they brought in to him the present which was in their hand into the house,—and bowed themselves down to him to the earth.
text.KJV: ¶ And when Ioseph came home, they brought him the Present which was in their hand, into the house, and bowed themselues to him to the earth.
text.NABRE: When Joseph came home, they presented him with the gifts they had brought inside, while they bowed down before him to the ground.
text.DRB: Therefore Joseph came in to his house, and they offered him presents holding them in their hands, and they adored prostrate to the ground.

text.NRSV: He inquired about their welfare, and said, “Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?”
text.Rotherham: Then asked he after their welfare, and said—Is it well with your aged father, of whom ye spake? Is he yet alive?
text.KJV: And he asked them of their welfare, and said, Is your father well, the old man of whom ye spake? Is he yet aliue?
text.NABRE: After inquiring how they were, he asked them, “And how is your aged father, of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?”
text.DRB: But he courteously resaluting them, asked them saying: Is the old man your father in health, of whom you told me? Is he yet living.

text.NRSV: They said, “Your servant our father is well; he is still alive.” And they bowed their heads and did obeisance.
text.Rotherham: And they said—It is well with thy servant our father, he is yet alive. And they bent their heads and bowed them-selves down.
text.KJV: And they answered, Thy seruant our father is in good health, hee is yet aliue: & they bowed downe their heads, and made obeisance.
text.NABRE: “Your servant our father is still alive and doing well,” they said, as they knelt and bowed down.
text.DRB: Who answered: Thy servant our father is in health, he is yet living. And bowing themselves they adored him.

text.NRSV: Then he looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother’s son, and said, “Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me? God be gracious to you, my son!”
text.Rotherham: Then lifted he up his eyes, and saw Benjamin his brother son of his mother, and said—Is, this, your youngest brother, of whom ye spake unto me? And he said, God, grant thee favour my son!
text.KJV: And he lift vp his eyes, and sawe his brother Beniamin, his mothers sonne, and said, Is this your yonger brother, of whom yee spake vnto mee? and he said, God be gracious vnto thee, my sonne.
text.NABRE: Then Joseph looked up and saw Benjamin, his brother, the son of his mother. He asked, “Is this your youngest brother, of whom you told me?” Then he said to him, “May God be gracious to you, my son!”
text.DRB: And Joseph lifting up his eyes, saw Benjamin his brother of the same mother, and said: is this your young brother, of whom you told me? And again: God, saith he, be merciful unto thee my son.

text.NRSV: With that, Joseph hurried out, because he was overcome with affection for his brother, and he was about to weep. So he went into a private room and wept there.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph made haste for his compassions towards his brother were pent up, and he sought to weep,—so he went into the inner chamber and wept there,
text.KJV: And Ioseph made haste: for his bowels did yerne vpon his brother: and he sought where to weepe, and hee entred into his chamber, & wept there.
text.NABRE: With that, Joseph hurried out, for he was so overcome with affection for his brother that he was on the verge of tears. So he went into a private room and wept there.
text.DRB: And he made haste because his heart was moved upon his brother, and tears brake forth: and entering into his chamber he wept.

text.NRSV: Then he washed his face and came out; and controlling himself he said, “Serve the meal.”
text.Rotherham: Then bathed he his face and came forth,—and restrained himself, and said Set on bread!
text.KJV: And he washed his face, and went out, and refrained himselfe, and saide, Set on bread.
text.NABRE: After washing his face, he reappeared and, now having collected himself, gave the order, “Serve the meal.”
text.DRB: And when he had washed his face, coming forth again, he refrained himself, and said: Set bread on the table.

text.NRSV: They served him by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians could not eat with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.
text.Rotherham: So they set on for him—by himself, and for them—by themselves,—and for the Egyptians that were eating with him—by themselves, for the Egyptians might not eat, bread, with the Hebrews for an abomination, had that been to Egyptians.
text.KJV: And they set on for him by himselfe, and for them by themselues, and for the Egyptians which did eate with him, by themselues: because the Egyptians might not eate bread with the Hebrewes: for that is an abomination vnto the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: It was served separately to him, to the brothers, and to the Egyptians who partook of his board. Egyptians may not eat with Hebrews; that is abhorrent to them.
text.DRB: Which being set down, to Joseph apart, and to his brethren apart, to the Ægyptians also that did eat with him apart (for it is unlawful for the Ægyptians to eat with the Hebrews, and they think such a feast profane)

text.NRSV: When they were seated before him, the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth, the men looked at one another in amazement.
text.Rotherham: So they took their seats before him, the firstborn, according to his birthright and the youngest, according to his youth,—and the men looked with amazement, each one at his companion.
text.KJV: And they sate before him, the first borne according to his birthright, and the yongest according to his youth: and the men marueiled one at another.
text.NABRE: When they were seated before him according to their age, from the oldest to the youngest, they looked at one another in amazement;
text.DRB: they sat before him, the first begotten according to his first-birth, and the youngest according to his age. And they marveled out of measure,

text.NRSV: Portions were taken to them from Joseph’s table, but Benjamin’s portion was five times as much as any of theirs. So they drank and were merry with him.
text.Rotherham: And he sent portions from before him unto them, and the portion of Benjamin exceeded the portions of them all, five-fold. And they drank and made merry with him.
text.KJV: And hee tooke and sent measses vnto them from before him: but Beniamins measse was fiue times so much as any of theirs: and they drunke, and were merry with him.
text.NABRE: and as portions were brought to them from Joseph’s table, Benjamin’s portion was five times as large as anyone else’s. So they drank freely and made merry with him.
text.DRB: Taking the portions that they received of him: and the greater portion came to Benjamin, so that it exceeded by five parts. And they drank and were inebriated with him.

text.NRSV: Then he commanded the steward of his house, “Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man’s money in the top of his sack.
text.Rotherham: Then commanded he him who was over his house saying—Fill the sacks of the men, with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man’s silver in the mouth of his sack;
text.KJV: And hee commaunded the steward of his house, saying, Fill the mens sackes with food, as much as they can carie, and put euery mans money in his sacks mouth.
text.NABRE: Then Joseph commanded his steward: “Fill the men’s bags with as much food as they can carry, and put each man’s money in the mouth of his bag.
text.DRB: And Joseph commanded the steward of his house, saying: Fill their sacks with corn, as much as they can hold: and put the money of every one in the top of his sack.

text.NRSV: Put my cup, the silver cup, in the top of the sack of the youngest, with his money for the grain.” And he did as Joseph told him.
text.Rotherham: and my cup—the cup of silver, shalt thou put in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, with his corn-silver. So he did, according to the word of Joseph, which he had spoken.
text.KJV: And put my cup, the siluer cup, in the sackes mouth of the youngest, and his corne money: and he did according to the word that Ioseph had spoken.
text.NABRE: In the mouth of the youngest one’s bag put also my silver goblet, together with the money for his grain.” The steward did as Joseph said.
text.DRB: And in the sack's mouth of the younger put my silver cup, and the price which he gave for the wheat. And it was so done.

text.NRSV: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away with their donkeys.
text.Rotherham: By the time, the morning, was light, the men, had been sent away, they, and their asses.
text.KJV: Assoone as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they, and their asses.
text.NABRE: At daybreak the men and their donkeys were sent off.
text.DRB: And when the morning rose, they were dismissed with their asses.

text.NRSV: When they had gone only a short distance from the city, Joseph said to his steward, “Go, follow after the men; and when you overtake them, say to them, ‘Why have you returned evil for good? Why have you stolen my silver cup?
text.Rotherham: They themselves, had gone out of the city—they had not gone far, when Joseph said to him who was over his house, Rise chase after the men,—so shalt thou overtake them and shalt say unto them, Wherefore have ye requited evil for good?
text.KJV: And when they were gone out of the citie, and not yet farre off, Ioseph said vnto his steward, Up, follow after the men; and when thou doest ouertake them, say vnto them, Wherefore haue ye rewarded euill for good?
text.NABRE: They had not gone far out of the city when Joseph said to his steward: “Go at once after the men! When you overtake them, say to them, ‘Why did you repay good with evil? Why did you steal my silver goblet?
text.DRB: And they were now departed out of the city, and had gone forward a little way: then Joseph sending for the steward of his house, said: Arise, quote he, and pursue the men: and overtaking them say to them: Why have you rendered evil for good?

text.NRSV: Is it not from this that my lord drinks? Does he not indeed use it for divination? You have done wrong in doing this.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Is not this that in which my lord drinketh, and whereby, he himself, doth divine? Ye have done evil in what ye have done.
text.KJV: Is not this it, in which my lord drinketh? and whereby indeed he diuineth? ye haue done euill in so doing.
text.NABRE: Is it not the very one from which my master drinks and which he uses for divination? What you have done is wrong.’”
text.DRB: The cup which you have stolen is that wherein my lord doth drink, and wherein he is wont to divine: you have done a very evil thing.

text.NRSV: When he overtook them, he repeated these words to them.
text.Rotherham: So he overtook them,—and spake unto them these words.
text.KJV: ¶ And he ouertooke them, and he spake vnto them these same words.
text.NABRE: When the steward overtook them and repeated these words to them,
text.DRB: He did as he had commanded. And having overtaken them, he spake to them in the same order.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants that they should do such a thing!
text.Rotherham: And they said unto him, Wherefore should my lord speak such words as these? Far be thy servants, from doing such a thing as this!
text.KJV: And they said vnto him, Wherefore saith my lord these words? God forbid that thy seruants should doe according to this thing.
text.NABRE: they said to him: “Why does my lord say such things? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing!
text.DRB: Who answered: Why doth our lord speak so, as though thy servants had committed so heinous a fact?

text.NRSV: Look, the money that we found at the top of our sacks, we brought back to you from the land of Canaan; why then would we steal silver or gold from your lord’s house?
text.Rotherham: Lo! the silver that we found in the mouth of our sacks, we brought back unto thee out of the land of Canaan,—how then could we steal out of the house of thy lord, silver or gold?
text.KJV: Behold, the money which wee found in our sackes mouthes, wee brought againe vnto thee, out of the land of Canaan: how then should wee steale out of thy lords house, siluer or golde?
text.NABRE: We even brought back to you from the land of Canaan the money that we found in the mouths of our bags. How could we steal silver or gold from your master’s house?
text.DRB: The money that we found in the top of our sacks, we recarried to thee from the land of Chanaan: & how followeth it, that we have stolen out of thy lord's house gold or silver?

text.NRSV: Should it be found with any one of your servants, let him die; moreover the rest of us will become my lord’s slaves.”
text.Rotherham: With whomsoever of thy servants it can be found, he shall die,—and, we also, will become my lord’s servants.
text.KJV: With whom soeuer of thy seruants it be found, both let him die, and we also will be my lords bondmen.
text.NABRE: If any of your servants is found to have the goblet, he shall die, and as for the rest of us, we shall become my lord’s slaves.”
text.DRB: With whom soever of thy servants that shall be found which thou seekest, let him die, and we will be the bondmen of our lord.

text.NRSV: He said, “Even so; in accordance with your words, let it be: he with whom it is found shall become my slave, but the rest of you shall go free.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Yea, now, according to your words, so, let it be,—he with whom it is found, shall be my servant, Ye, however, shall be clear.
text.KJV: And he said, Now also let it be according vnto your wordes: hee with whom it is found, shall be my seruant: and ye shall be blamelesse.
text.NABRE: But he replied, “Now what you propose is fair enough, but only the one who is found to have it shall become my slave, and the rest of you can go free.”
text.DRB: Who said to them: Be it done according to your sentence: with whom soever it shall be found, be he my bondman, and you shall be guiltless.

text.NRSV: Then each one quickly lowered his sack to the ground, and each opened his sack.
text.Rotherham: So they hastened and took down each man his sack to the ground,—and opened each man his sack.
text.KJV: Then they speedily tooke downe euery man his sacke to the ground, and opened euery man his sacke.
text.NABRE: Then each of them quickly lowered his bag to the ground and opened it;
text.DRB: Therefore in haste taking down their sacks upon the ground, every man opened.

text.NRSV: He searched, beginning with the eldest and ending with the youngest; and the cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.
text.Rotherham: Then made he thorough search, with the eldest, he began, and with the youngest, he ended,—and the cup was found in the sack of Benjamin!
text.KJV: And he searched, and began at the eldest, and left at the yongest: and the cup was found in Beniamins sacke.
text.NABRE: and when a search was made, starting with the oldest and ending with the youngest, the goblet turned up in Benjamin’s bag.
text.DRB: Which when he had searched, beginning from the elder even to the youngest, he found the cup in Benjamin's sack.

text.NRSV: At this they tore their clothes. Then each one loaded his donkey, and they returned to the city.
text.Rotherham: And they rent their mantles,—and laded each man his ass, and returned to the city.
text.KJV: Then they rent their clothes, and laded euery man his asse, and returned to the citie.
text.NABRE: At this, they tore their garments. Then, when each man had loaded his donkey again, they returned to the city.
text.DRB: But they renting their garments, and loading their asses again, returned into the town.

text.NRSV: Judah and his brothers came to Joseph’s house while he was still there; and they fell to the ground before him.
text.Rotherham: And Judah and his brethren came into the house of Joseph, he, yet being there,—and fell down before him to the earth.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iudah and his brethren came to Iosephs house: (for he was yet there) and they fell before him on the ground.
text.NABRE: When Judah and his brothers entered Joseph’s house, he was still there; so they flung themselves on the ground before him.
text.DRB: And Judas foremost with his brethren entered in to Joseph (for he was not yet gone out of the place) and they fell together before him on the ground.

text.NRSV: Joseph said to them, “What deed is this that you have done? Do you not know that one such as I can practice divination?”
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said to them, What is this deed which ye have done? Know ye not that such a man as I, can even divine?
text.KJV: And Ioseph said vnto them, What deed is this that ye haue done? wote ye not, that such a man as I can certainely diuine?
text.NABRE: “How could you do such a thing?” Joseph asked them. “Did you not know that such a man as I could discern by divination what happened?”
text.DRB: To whom he said: Why would you do so? Know you not that there is not the like to me in the science of divining.

text.NRSV: And Judah said, “What can we say to my lord? What can we speak? How can we clear ourselves? God has found out the guilt of your servants; here we are then, my lord’s slaves, both we and also the one in whose possession the cup has been found.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Judah—What shall we say to my lord, how shall we speak and how shall we justify ourselves,—when, God himself, hath found out the iniquity of thy servants? Behold us! my lord’s servants, both we, and he in whose hand the cup hath been found.
text.KJV: And Iudah said, What shall wee say vnto my lord? what shal we speake? or how shall we cleare our selues? God hath found out the iniquitie of thy seruants: beholde, wee are my lords seruants, both we, and he also with whom the cup is found.
text.NABRE: Judah replied: “What can we say to my lord? How can we plead or how try to prove our innocence? God has uncovered your servants’ guilt. Here we are, then, the slaves of my lord—the rest of us no less than the one in whose possession the goblet was found.”
text.DRB: To whom Judas said: What shall we answer, my lord? Or what shall we speak, or be able justly to pretend? God hath found the iniquity of thy servants: lo we are all bondmen to my Lord, both we, & he with whom the cup was found.

text.NRSV: But he said, “Far be it from me that I should do so! Only the one in whose possession the cup was found shall be my slave; but as for you, go up in peace to your father.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Far be it from me to do this thing! The man in whose hand the cup hath been found, he, shall be my servant, but as for you, go ye up in peace unto your father.
text.KJV: And he said, God forbid that I should doe so: but the man in whose hand the cup is found, he shal be my seruant; and as for you, get you vp in peace vnto your father.
text.NABRE: Joseph said, “Far be it from me to act thus! Only the one in whose possession the goblet was found shall become my slave; the rest of you may go back unharmed to your father.”
text.DRB: Joseph answered: God forbid that I should so do: he that stole the cup, the same be my bondman: and go you free unto your father.

text.NRSV: Then Judah stepped up to him and said, “O my lord, let your servant please speak a word in my lord’s ears, and do not be angry with your servant; for you are like Pharaoh himself.
text.Rotherham: Then Judah drew near unto him, and said—Pardon my lord! Pray let thy servant speak a word in the ears of my lord, and do not let thine anger be kindled against thy servant,—for, thus, art, thou as Pharaoh!
text.KJV: ¶ Then Iudah came neere vnto him, and said, Oh my lord, let thy seruant, I pray thee, speake a word in my lords eares, & let not thine anger burne against thy seruant: for thou art euen as Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: Judah then stepped up to him and said: “I beg you, my lord, let your servant appeal to my lord, and do not become angry with your servant, for you are the equal of Pharaoh.
text.DRB: And Judas approaching nearer said boldly: I beseech thee my lord, let thy servant speak a word in thine ears, and be not angry with thy servant: for after Pharao thou art,

text.NRSV: My lord asked his servants, saying, ‘Have you a father or a brother?’
text.Rotherham: It was, my lord, who asked his servants, saying,—Have ye a father, or a brother?
text.KJV: My lord asked his seruants, saying; Haue ye a father, or a brother?
text.NABRE: My lord asked his servants, ‘Have you a father, or another brother?’
text.DRB: my Lord. Thou didst ask thy servants the first time: Have you a father or a brother?

text.NRSV: And we said to my lord, ‘We have a father, an old man, and a young brother, the child of his old age. His brother is dead; he alone is left of his mother’s children, and his father loves him.’
text.Rotherham: So we said unto my lord, We have an aged father, and the child of his old age a youth,—his brother, being dead, he alone, is left of his mother and, his father, loveth him.
text.KJV: And we said vnto my lord, Wee haue a father, an olde man, and a childe of his old age, a little one: and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loueth him.
text.NABRE: So we said to my lord, ‘We have an aged father, and a younger brother, the child of his old age. This one’s full brother is dead, and since he is the only one by his mother who is left, his father is devoted to him.’
text.DRB: and we answered thee my lord: We have a father an old man, and a little boy, that was born in his old age; whose brother by the mother is dead: and his mother hath him only, and his father loveth him tenderly.

text.NRSV: Then you said to your servants, ‘Bring him down to me, so that I may set my eyes on him.’
text.Rotherham: So then thou saidst unto thy servants, Bring him down unto me,—that I may set mine eyes upon him.
text.KJV: And thou saidst vnto thy seruants, Bring him downe vnto mee, that I may set mine eyes vpon him.
text.NABRE: Then you told your servants, ‘Bring him down to me that I might see him.’
text.DRB: And thou saidst to thy servants: Bring him hither to me, and I will set mine eyes on him.

text.NRSV: We said to my lord, ‘The boy cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.’
text.Rotherham: And we said unto my lord, The lad cannot leave his father,—as surely as he leaveth his father, so surely will he, die.
text.KJV: And we said vnto my lord, The lad cannot leaue his father: for if hee should leaue his father, his father would die.
text.NABRE: We replied to my lord, ‘The boy cannot leave his father; his father would die if he left him.’
text.DRB: We suggested to my lord: The boy cannot leave his father: for if he leave him, he will die.

text.NRSV: Then you said to your servants, ‘Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall see my face no more.’
text.Rotherham: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Except your youngest brother do come down with you, ye shall not again behold my face.
text.KJV: And thou saidst vnto thy seruants, Except your yongest brother come downe with you, you shall see my face no more.
text.NABRE: But you told your servants, ‘Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall not see me again.’
text.DRB: And thou saidst to thy servants: Unless your youngest brother come with you, you shall no more see my face.

text.NRSV: When we went back to your servant my father we told him the words of my lord.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when we had gone up unto thy servant, my father, that we told him the words of my lord.
text.KJV: And it came to passe when wee came vp vnto thy seruant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
text.NABRE: When we returned to your servant my father, we reported to him the words of my lord.
text.DRB: Therefore when we were gone up to thy servant our father, we told him all things that my lord did speak.

text.NRSV: And when our father said, ‘Go again, buy us a little food,’
text.Rotherham: Then said our father,—Go again buy us a little food,
text.KJV: And our father said, Goe againe, and buy vs a little food.
text.NABRE: “Later, our father said, ‘Go back and buy some food for us.’
text.DRB: And our father said: Go again, and buy us a little wheat.

text.NRSV: we said, ‘We cannot go down. Only if our youngest brother goes with us, will we go down; for we cannot see the man’s face unless our youngest brother is with us.’
text.Rotherham: And we said, We cannot go down,—If our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down, for we cannot see the face of the man if, our youngest brother, is not with us.
text.KJV: And we saide, Wee cannot goe downe: if our yongest brother be with vs, then will we goe downe: for wee may not see the mans face, except our yongest brother be with vs.
text.NABRE: So we reminded him, ‘We cannot go down there; only if our youngest brother is with us can we go, for we may not see the man if our youngest brother is not with us.’
text.DRB: To whom we said: We cannot go: if our youngest brother shall go down with us, we will set forward together: otherwise he being absent, we dare not see the man's face.

text.NRSV: Then your servant my father said to us, ‘You know that my wife bore me two sons;
text.Rotherham: Then said thy servant my father, unto us,—Ye yourselves, know, that, two, did my wife bear to me,
text.KJV: And thy seruant my father said vnto vs, Ye know that my wife bare me two sonnes.
text.NABRE: Then your servant my father said to us, ‘As you know, my wife bore me two sons.
text.DRB: Whereunto he answered: You know that my wife bare me two.

text.NRSV: one left me, and I said, Surely he has been torn to pieces; and I have never seen him since.
text.Rotherham: and the one went forth from me, and I said—Certainly he hath been, torn in pieces,—And I have never seen him since.
text.KJV: And the one went out from me, and I said, Surely he is torne in pieces: and I saw him not since.
text.NABRE: One of them, however, has gone away from me, and I said, “He must have been torn to pieces by wild beasts!” I have not seen him since.
text.DRB: One went forth, and you said: A beast did devour him: and hitherto he appeareth not.

text.NRSV: If you take this one also from me, and harm comes to him, you will bring down my gray hairs in sorrow to Sheol.’
text.Rotherham: As surely as ye take, this one also, away from before my face and there befall him any mischief, so surely shall ye bring down my grey hairs, with misfortune to hades.
text.KJV: And if ye take this also from me, and mischiefe befall him, ye shall bring downe my gray haires with sorrow to the graue.
text.NABRE: If you take this one away from me too, and a disaster befalls him, you will send my white head down to Sheol in grief.’
text.DRB: If you take this also, and ought befall him in the way, you shall bring down my hoar hairs with sorrow unto hell.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, when I come to your servant my father and the boy is not with us, then, as his life is bound up in the boy’s life,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, as surely as I go in unto thy servant my father, and, the lad, is not with us,—seeing that his soul is bound up with the lad’s soul,
text.KJV: Now therefore when I come to thy seruant my father, and the lad bee not with vs; (seeing that his life is bound vp in the lads life.)
text.NABRE: “So now, if the boy is not with us when I go back to your servant my father, whose very life is bound up with his, he will die as soon as he sees that the boy is missing;
text.DRB: Therefore if I shall enter to thy servant our father, and the boy be wanting (whereas his life dependeth upon the life of him)

text.NRSV: when he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die; and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.
text.Rotherham: so surely shall it come to pass that when he seeth that the lad is not with us, then will he die. So shall thy servants bring down the grey hairs of thy servant our father, with sorrow to hades.
text.KJV: It shall come to passe, when he seeth that the lad is not with vs, that he will die, and thy seruants shall bring downe the gray haires of thy seruant our father with sorrow to the graue.
text.NABRE: and your servants will thus send the white head of your servant our father down to Sheol in grief.
text.DRB: and he shall see that he is not with us, he will die, and thy servants shall bring down his hoar hairs with sorrow unto hell.

text.NRSV: For your servant became surety for the boy to my father, saying, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, then I will bear the blame in the sight of my father all my life.’
text.Rotherham: For thy servant, became surety for the lad, while away from my father saying,—If I bring him not unto thee, then will I count myself a sinner against my father all the days.
text.KJV: For thy seruant became surety for the lad vnto my father, saying, If I bring him not vnto thee, then I shall beare the blame to my father, for euer.
text.NABRE: Besides, I, your servant, have guaranteed the boy’s safety for my father by saying, ‘If I fail to bring him back to you, father, I will bear the blame before you forever.’
text.DRB: Let me be thy proper servant that did take him into my protection and promised, saying: Unless I bring him again I will be guilty of sin against my father for ever.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, please let your servant remain as a slave to my lord in place of the boy; and let the boy go back with his brothers.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I pray thee let thy servant remain instead of the lad, as servant to my lord,—but as for the lad, let him go up with his brethren.
text.KJV: Now therefore, I pray thee, let thy seruant abide in stead of the lad, a bondman to my lord, and let the lad goe vp with his brethren.
text.NABRE: So now let me, your servant, remain in place of the boy as the slave of my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers.
text.DRB: Itherefore thy servant will tarry instead of the child in the service of my lord, and let the child go up with his brethren.

text.NRSV: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the suffering that would come upon my father.”
text.Rotherham: For how can I go up unto my father, should the lad, not be with me? lest I look on the calamity, that shall find out my father,
text.KJV: For how shall I goe vp to my father, and the lad be not with mee, lest peraduenture I see the euill that shall come on my father?
text.NABRE: How could I go back to my father if the boy were not with me? I could not bear to see the anguish that would overcome my father.”
text.DRB: For I cannot return to my father, the child being absent; lest I stand by a witness of the calamity that shall oppress my father.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph could no longer control himself before all those who stood by him, and he cried out, “Send everyone away from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph could not restrain himself before all who were stationed near him, so he cried out—Have forth every man from me! And there stood no man with him, when Joseph made himself known unto his brethren,
text.KJV: Then Ioseph could not refraine himselfe before all them that stood by him: and he cried, Cause euery man to goe out from me; and there stood no man with him, while Ioseph made himselfe knowen vnto his brethren.
text.NABRE: Joseph could no longer restrain himself in the presence of all his attendants, so he cried out, “Have everyone withdraw from me!” So no one attended him when he made himself known to his brothers.
text.DRB: Joseph could no longer refrain many standing by in presence: whereupon he commanded that all should go forth, and no stranger should be present at their acknowledging one of another.

text.NRSV: And he wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.
text.Rotherham: Then gave he forth his voice in weeping,—and the Egyptians heard, and the house of Pharaoh heard.
text.KJV: And he wept aloud: and the Egyptians, and the house of Pharaoh heard.
text.NABRE: But his sobs were so loud that the Egyptians heard him, and so the news reached Pharaoh’s house.
text.DRB: And he lifted up his voice with weeping, which the Ægyptians heard, and all the house of Pharao.

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph. Is my father still alive?” But his brothers could not answer him, so dismayed were they at his presence.
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph unto his brethren: I, am Joseph, is my father yet alive? But his brethren could not answer him, for they were terrified because of him.
text.KJV: And Ioseph said vnto his brethren, I am Ioseph; Doeth my father yet liue? and his brethren could not answere him: for they were troubled at his presence.
text.NABRE: “I am Joseph,” he said to his brothers. “Is my father still alive?” But his brothers could give him no answer, so dumbfounded were they at him.
text.DRB: And he said to his Brethren: I am Joseph: is my father yet living? His brethren could not answer him being terribly astonished out of measure.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph said to his brothers, “Come closer to me.” And they came closer. He said, “I am your brother, Joseph, whom you sold into Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph unto his brethren—Draw near I pray you, unto me. And they drew near. And he said—I, am Joseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.
text.KJV: And Ioseph said vnto his brethren, Come neere to me, I pray you: and they came neere; and he said, I am Ioseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.
text.NABRE: “Come closer to me,” Joseph told his brothers. When they had done so, he said: “I am your brother Joseph, whom you sold into Egypt.
text.DRB: To whom gently he said: Come hither to me. And when they were come near him, I am, quoth he, Joseph your brother, whom you sold into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: And now do not be distressed, or angry with yourselves, because you sold me here; for God sent me before you to preserve life.
text.Rotherham: But, now, do not grieve, neither let it be vexing in your eyes, that ye sold me hither,—for, to save life, did God send me before you.
text.KJV: Now therefore bee not grieued, nor angry with your selues, that yee sold me hither: for God did send me before you, to preserue life.
text.NABRE: But now do not be distressed, and do not be angry with yourselves for having sold me here. It was really for the sake of saving lives that God sent me here ahead of you.
text.DRB: Be not afraid, neither let it seem to you a hard case that you did sell me into these countries: for God sent me before you into Ægypt for your preservation.

text.NRSV: For the famine has been in the land these two years; and there are five more years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvest.
text.Rotherham: For these two years, hath the famine been in the midst of the land,—and, yet five years, are there, in which there shall be neither ploughing, nor harvest.
text.KJV: For these two yeeres hath the famine bene in the land: and yet there are fiue yeeres, in the which there shall neither be earing nor haruest.
text.NABRE: The famine has been in the land for two years now, and for five more years cultivation will yield no harvest.
text.DRB: For it is two years since the famine began to be upon the earth, and yet five years remain, wherein there can be neither earing nor reaping.

text.NRSV: God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors.
text.Rotherham: So then God sent me before you, to plant for you a remainder in the earth,—and to save you alive, by a great deliverance,
text.KJV: And God sent me before you, to preserue you a posteritie in the earth, and to saue your liues by a great deliuerance.
text.NABRE: God, therefore, sent me on ahead of you to ensure for you a remnant on earth and to save your lives in an extraordinary deliverance.
text.DRB: And God sent me before, that you may be preserved upon the earth, and may have victuals to live.

text.NRSV: So it was not you who sent me here, but God; he has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore it was not ye, who sent me hither, but, God, himself,—who also appointed me to be a father to Pharaoh, and a lord to all his house, and a ruler over all the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: So now it was not you that sent me hither, but God: and he hath made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house, and a ruler throughout all the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: So it was not really you but God who had me come here; and he has made me a father to Pharaoh, lord of all his household, and ruler over the whole land of Egypt.
text.DRB: Not by your counsel, but by the will of God was I sent hither: who hath made me as it were a father to Pharao, and lord of his whole house, and Prince in all the land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Hurry and go up to my father and say to him, ‘Thus says your son Joseph, God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not delay.
text.Rotherham: Haste ye and go up unto my father, then shall ye say unto him—Thus saith thy son Joseph, God hath appointed me lord to all Egypt,—come down unto me do not delay;
text.KJV: Haste you, and goe vp to my father, and say vnto him, Thus saith thy sonne Ioseph; God hath made me lord of all Egypt; come downe vnto me, tary not.
text.NABRE: “Hurry back, then, to my father and tell him: ‘Thus says your son Joseph: God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me without delay.
text.DRB: Make haste and get ye up to my father, and you shall say to him: Thy son Joseph willeth thus: God hath made me lord of the whole land of Ægypt: come down to me, tarry not.

text.NRSV: You shall settle in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children’s children, as well as your flocks, your herds, and all that you have.
text.Rotherham: so shalt thou dwell in the land of Goshen, and shalt be near unto me, thou, and thy sons, and thy sons’ sons,—and thy flocks and thy herds and all that is thine;
text.KJV: And thou shalt dwell in the land of Goshen, and thou shalt be neere vnto me, thou, and thy children, and thy childrens children, and thy flockes, and thy heards, and all that thou hast.
text.NABRE: You can settle in the region of Goshen, where you will be near me—you and your children and children’s children, your flocks and herds, and everything that you own.
text.DRB: And thou shalt dwell in the land of Gessen: and thou shalt be near me thou and thy sons, and thy son's children, thy sheep, and thy herds, and all things that thou dost possess.

text.NRSV: I will provide for you there—since there are five more years of famine to come—so that you and your household, and all that you have, will not come to poverty.’
text.Rotherham: so will I sustain thee, there, for there are yet five years of famine,—lest thou come to poverty—thou and thy house and all who are thine.
text.KJV: And there wil I nourish thee, (for yet there are fiue yeeres of famine) lest thou and thy houshold, and all that thou hast, come to pouertie.
text.NABRE: I will provide for you there in the five years of famine that lie ahead, so that you and your household and all that are yours will not suffer want.’
text.DRB: And there I will feed thee (for yet there are five years of famine remaining) lest both thou perish and thy house, and all things that thou dost possess.

text.NRSV: And now your eyes and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see that it is my own mouth that speaks to you.
text.Rotherham: Now lo! your own eyes do see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin,—that it is my own mouth, that doth speak unto you.
text.KJV: And behold, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Beniamin, that it is my mouth that speaketh vnto you.
text.NABRE: Surely, you can see for yourselves, and Benjamin can see for himself, that it is I who am speaking to you.
text.DRB: Behold, your eyes, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin do see, that it is my mouth that speaketh unto you.

text.NRSV: You must tell my father how greatly I am honored in Egypt, and all that you have seen. Hurry and bring my father down here.”
text.Rotherham: As soon as ye have told my father all my honour in Egypt, and all that ye have seen, so soon shall ye hasten and bring down my father hither.
text.KJV: And you shall tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that you haue seene, and ye shall haste, and bring downe my father hither.
text.NABRE: Tell my father all about my high position in Egypt and all that you have seen. But hurry and bring my father down here.”
text.DRB: Report to my father my whole glory & all things that you have seen in Ægypt: make haste, and bring him to me.

text.NRSV: Then he fell upon his brother Benjamin’s neck and wept, while Benjamin wept upon his neck.
text.Rotherham: Then fell he upon the neck of Benjamin his brother, and wept,—and Benjamin wept on his neck.
text.KJV: And he fel vpon his brother Beniamins necke, and wept: and Beniamin wept vpon his necke.
text.NABRE: Then he threw his arms around his brother Benjamin and wept on his shoulder.
text.DRB: And falling upon the neck of his brother Benjamin, embracing him he wept: he also in like manner weeping upon his neck.

text.NRSV: And he kissed all his brothers and wept upon them; and after that his brothers talked with him.
text.Rotherham: Then kissed he all his brethren, and wept upon them,—and, thereafter, his brethren spake with him.
text.KJV: Moreouer hee kissed all his brethren, and wept vpon them: and after that, his brethren talked with him.
text.NABRE: Joseph then kissed all his brothers and wept over them; and only then were his brothers able to talk with him.
text.DRB: And Joseph kissed all his brethren, and wept upon every one: after which things they were bold to speak unto him.

text.NRSV: When the report was heard in Pharaoh’s house, “Joseph’s brothers have come,” Pharaoh and his servants were pleased.
text.Rotherham: Now, the report, was heard by the house of Pharaoh, saying, The brethren of Joseph have come in, And it was good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his servants.
text.KJV: ¶ And the fame thereof was heard in Pharaohs house, saying, Iosephs brethren are come: and it pleased Pharaoh well, and his seruants.
text.NABRE: The news reached Pharaoh’s house: “Joseph’s brothers have come.” Pharaoh and his officials were pleased.
text.DRB: And it was heard of, and very famously reported abroad in the king's court: The brethren of Joseph are come: and Pharao was glad, and all his family.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Say to your brothers, ‘Do this: load your animals and go back to the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: So then Pharaoh said unto Joseph—Say unto thy brethren This, do,—lade your asses, and go enter into the land of Canaan;
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said vnto Ioseph, Say vnto thy brethren, This doe yee, lade your beasts and goe, get you vnto the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: So Pharaoh told Joseph: “Say to your brothers: ‘This is what you shall do: Load up your animals and go without delay to the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: And he spake to Joseph that he should command his brethren, saying: Loading your beasts go into the Land of Chanaan,

text.NRSV: Take your father and your households and come to me, so that I may give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you may enjoy the fat of the land.’
text.Rotherham: and fetch your father and your households and come in unto me,—that I may give you the best of the land of Egypt, and eat ye the fat of the land.
text.KJV: And take your father, and your housholds, and come vnto mee: and I wil giue you the good of the land of Egypt, and ye shall eat the fat of the land.
text.NABRE: There get your father and your households, and then come to me; I will assign you the best land in Egypt, where you will live off the fat of the land.’
text.DRB: and take thence your father and kin, and come to me: and I will give you all the good things of Ægypt, that you may eat the marrow of the land.

text.NRSV: You are further charged to say, ‘Do this: take wagons from the land of Egypt for your little ones and for your wives, and bring your father, and come.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore command them, This, do ye,—Take you out of the land of Egypt, waggons for your little ones and for your wives, so shall ye bring your father and come in.
text.KJV: Now thou art commanded, this doe yee; Take you wagons out of the land of Egypt for your little ones, and for your wiues, and bring your father, and come.
text.NABRE: Instruct them further: ‘Do this. Take wagons from the land of Egypt for your children and your wives and bring your father back here.
text.DRB: Give commandment also that they take wains out of the land of Ægypt, for the carriage of their little ones and wives: and say: Take up your father, and make haste to come with all speed.

text.NRSV: Give no thought to your possessions, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Your eye, moreover, let it not look with pity upon your goods,—for, the best of all the land of Egypt, is, yours.
text.KJV: Also regard not your stuffe: for the good of all the land of Egypt is yours.
text.NABRE: Do not be concerned about your belongings, for the best in the whole land of Egypt shall be yours.’”
text.DRB: Neither do you leave any thing of your household stuff: for all the riches of Ægypt shall be yours.

text.NRSV: The sons of Israel did so. Joseph gave them wagons according to the instruction of Pharaoh, and he gave them provisions for the journey.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel did so, and Joseph gave them waggons, at the bidding of Pharaoh,—and gave them provisions for the way:
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did so: and Ioseph gaue them wagons, according to the commandement of Pharaoh, and gaue them prouision for the way.
text.NABRE: The sons of Israel acted accordingly. Joseph gave them the wagons, as Pharaoh had ordered, and he supplied them with provisions for the journey.
text.DRB: And the sons of Israel did as it was commanded them. To whom Joseph gave wains according to Pharao's commandment, and victuals for the way.

text.NRSV: To each one of them he gave a set of garments; but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five sets of garments.
text.Rotherham: to all of them, gave he to each man changes of raiment,—but, to Benjamin, he gave three hundred pieces of silver, and five changes of raiment.
text.KJV: To all of them he gaue each man changes of raiment: but to Beniamin hee gaue three hundred pieces of siluer, and fiue changes of raiment.
text.NABRE: He also gave to each of them a set of clothes, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred shekels of silver and five sets of clothes.
text.DRB: He bade also to be brought up for every one two robes: but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver with five robes of the best:

text.NRSV: To his father he sent the following: ten donkeys loaded with the good things of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and provision for his father on the journey.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, to his father, sent he as followeth—ten he-asses, laden from the best of Egypt,—and ten she-asses laden with corn and bread and nourishing food for his father for the way.
text.KJV: And to his father hee sent after this maner: ten asses laden with the good things of Egypt, and ten shee asses laden with corne, and bread and meat for his father by the way.
text.NABRE: Moreover, what he sent to his father was ten donkeys loaded with the finest products of Egypt and another ten loaded with grain and bread and provisions for his father’s journey.
text.DRB: sending to his father as much money & raiment, adding besides them he asses that should carry of all the riches of Ægypt, and as many she asses, carrying wheat for the journey and bread.

text.NRSV: Then he sent his brothers on their way, and as they were leaving he said to them, “Do not quarrel along the way.”
text.Rotherham: Thus sent he forth his brethren and they departed,—and he said unto them, Do not fall out by the way.
text.KJV: So he sent his brethren away, and they departed: and hee said vnto them, See that yee fall not out by the way.
text.NABRE: As he sent his brothers on their way, he told them, “Do not quarrel on the way.”
text.DRB: Therefore he dismissed his brethren, and when they were departing he said to them: Be not angry in the way.

text.NRSV: So they went up out of Egypt and came to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And they went up out of Egypt,—and came into the land of Canaan, unto Jacob their father.
text.KJV: ¶ And they went vp out of Egypt, and came into the land of Canaan vnto Iacob their father,
text.NABRE: So they went up from Egypt and came to the land of Canaan, to their father Jacob.
text.DRB: Who going up out of Ægypt, came into the land of Chanaan to their father Jacob.

text.NRSV: And they told him, “Joseph is still alive! He is even ruler over all the land of Egypt.” He was stunned; he could not believe them.
text.Rotherham: And they told him saying—Joseph: is, yet alive, yea, indeed, he, is ruler in all the land of Egypt. And his heart became faint, for he believed them not.
text.KJV: And told him, saying, Ioseph is yet aliue, and he is gouernour ouer all the land of Egypt. And Iacobs heart fainted, for he beleeued them not.
text.NABRE: When they told him, “Joseph is still alive—in fact, it is he who is governing all the land of Egypt,” he was unmoved, for he did not believe them.
text.DRB: And they told him saying: Joseph thy son is living: and he ruleth in all the Land of Ægypt. Which when Jacob heard, awaking as it were out of a heavy sleep, notwithstanding did not believe them.

text.NRSV: But when they told him all the words of Joseph that he had said to them, and when he saw the wagons that Joseph had sent to carry him, the spirit of their father Jacob revived.
text.Rotherham: So they spake unto him all the words of Joseph which he had spoken unto them h then saw he the waggons, which Joseph had sent to carry him,—and the spirit of Jacob their father revived.
text.KJV: And they told him all the words of Ioseph, which hee had saide vnto them: and when hee saw the wagons which Ioseph had sent to carie him, the spirit of Iacob their father reuiued.
text.NABRE: But when they recounted to him all that Joseph had told them, and when he saw the wagons that Joseph had sent to transport him, the spirit of their father Jacob came to life.
text.DRB: They on the contrary side reported the whole order of the thing. And when he saw the wains and all things that he had sent, his spirit revived,

text.NRSV: Israel said, “Enough! My son Joseph is still alive. I must go and see him before I die.”
text.Rotherham: And Israel said, Enough! Joseph my son is, yet alive, I must go and see him before I die!
text.KJV: And Israel said, It is enough; Ioseph my sonne is yet aliue: I will goe and see him before I die.
text.NABRE: “Enough,” said Israel. “My son Joseph is still alive! I must go and see him before I die.”
text.DRB: and he said: It sufficeth me if Joseph my son be living yet: I will go, and see him before I die.

text.NRSV: When Israel set out on his journey with all that he had and came to Beer-sheba, he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
text.Rotherham: So Israel brake up, with all that he had, and came in to Beer-sheba,—and offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
text.KJV: And Israel tooke his iourney with all that hee had, and came to Beersheba, and offered sacrifices vnto the God of his father Isaac.
text.NABRE: Israel set out with all that was his. When he arrived at Beer-sheba, he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
text.DRB: And Israel taking his journey with all things that he had came to the well of the oath, and killing there victims to the God of his father Isaac,

text.NRSV: God spoke to Israel in visions of the night, and said, “Jacob, Jacob.” And he said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: And God said to Israel in the visions of the night, and he said—Jacob! Jacob! And he said—Behold me!
text.KJV: And God spake vnto Israel in the visions of the night, and said, Iacob, Iacob. And he said, Here am I.
text.NABRE: There God, speaking to Israel in a vision by night, called: Jacob! Jacob! He answered, “Here I am.”
text.DRB: he heard him by a vision of the night calling him, and saying unto him: Jacob, Jacob. To whom he answered: Lo here I am.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “I am God, the God of your father; do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for I will make of you a great nation there.
text.Rotherham: And he said: I, am GOD himself, the God of thy father,—Be not afraid of going down to Egypt, seeing that for a great nation, will I plant thee there.
text.KJV: And he said, I am God, the God of thy father, feare not to goe downe into Egypt: for I will there make of thee a great nation.
text.NABRE: Then he said: I am God, the God of your father. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you a great nation.
text.DRB: God said to him: I am the most mighty God of thy father: fear not, go down into Ægypt, for into a great nation will I make thee there.

text.NRSV: I myself will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also bring you up again; and Joseph’s own hand shall close your eyes.”
text.Rotherham: I will go down with thee to Egypt, and I will bring thee up, yea wholly up,—and, Joseph, shall lay his own hand upon thine eyes.
text.KJV: I will goe downe with thee into Egypt; and I will also surely bring thee vp againe: and Ioseph shall put his hand vpon thine eyes.
text.NABRE: I will go down to Egypt with you and I will also bring you back here, after Joseph has closed your eyes.
text.DRB: I will go down with thee thither, and thence will I bring thee returning: Joseph also shall put his hands upon thine eyes.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob set out from Beer-sheba; and the sons of Israel carried their father Jacob, their little ones, and their wives, in the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob rose up from Beer-sheba,—and the sons of Israel brought Jacob their father, and their little ones, and their wives, in the waggons which Pharaoh had sent to bring him;
text.KJV: And Iacob rose vp from Beersheba: and the sonnes of Israel caried Iacob their father, and their litle ones, and their wiues, in the wagons which Pharaoh had sent to cary him.
text.NABRE: So Jacob departed from Beer-sheba, and the sons of Israel put their father and their wives and children on the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to transport him.
text.DRB: And Jacob rose up from the well of the oath: and his sons took him up, with their little ones and wives in the wains, which Pharao had sent to carry the old man,

text.NRSV: They also took their livestock and the goods that they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and they came into Egypt, Jacob and all his offspring with him,
text.Rotherham: and they took their cattle and the gains which they had gained in the land of Canaan, and came into Egypt,—Jacob and all his seed with him:
text.KJV: And they tooke their cattell, and their goods which they had gotten in the land of Canaan, and came into Egypt, Iacob, and all his seed with him:
text.NABRE: They took with them their livestock and the possessions they had acquired in the land of Canaan. So Jacob and all his descendants came to Egypt.
text.DRB: and all that he had possessed in the Land of Chanaan: and he came into Ægypt with all his seed,

text.NRSV: his sons, and his sons’ sons with him, his daughters, and his sons’ daughters; all his offspring he brought with him into Egypt.
text.Rotherham: his sons, and his sons’ sons with him, his daughters, and his sons’ daughters, and all his seed, brought he with him into Egypt.
text.KJV: His sonnes, and his sonnes sonnes with him, his daughters, and his sonnes daughters, and all his seed brought he with him into Egypt.
text.NABRE: His sons and his grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters—all his descendants—he took with him to Egypt.
text.DRB: His sons, and nephews, daughters, and all his progeny together.

text.NRSV: Now these are the names of the Israelites, Jacob and his offspring, who came to Egypt. Reuben, Jacob’s firstborn,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the names of the sons of Israel who came into Egypt—Jacob and his sons,—the firstborn of Jacob, Reuben:
text.KJV: ¶ And these are the names of the children of Israel, which came into Egypt, Iacob and his sonnes: Reuben Iacobs first borne;
text.NABRE: These are the names of the Israelites, Jacob and his children, who came to Egypt. Reuben, Jacob’s firstborn,
