text.DRB: And these are the names of the children of Israel, that entered into Ægypt, himself with his children. His first-begotten Ruben.

text.NRSV: and the children of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Reuben,—Hanoch and Pallu, and Hezron and Carmi;
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Reuben, Hanoch, and Phallu, and Hezron, and Carmi.
text.NABRE: and the sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text.DRB: The sons of Ruben: Henoch and Phallu and Hesron and Charmi.

text.NRSV: The children of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Simeon,—Jemuel and Jamin and Ohad and Jachin and Zohar,—and Shaul, son of the Canaanitess;
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Simeon: Iemuel, and Iamin, and Ohad, and Iachin, and Zohar, and Shaul the sonne of a Canaanitish woman.
text.NABRE: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, son of a Canaanite woman.
text.DRB: The sons of Simeon: Iamuel and Jamin and Ahod, and Jachin and Sohar, and Saul the Son of Chananitess.

text.NRSV: The children of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Levi,—Gershon, Kohath and Merari;
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Leui: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.NABRE: The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.DRB: The sons of Levi: Gerson and Caath and Merari.

text.NRSV: The children of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah (but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan); and the children of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Judah,—Er and Onan and Shelah, and Perez and Zerah,—but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan, and the sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul;
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Iudah: Er, and Onan, and Shelah, and Pharez, and Zerah: But Er & Onan died in the land of Canaan. And the sonnes of Pharez, were Hezron, and Hamul.
text.NABRE: The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah—but Er and Onan had died in the land of Canaan; and the sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
text.DRB: The sons of Juda: Her & Onan and Sela and Phares and Zara. And Her and Onan died in the land of Chanaan. And there were sons born to Phares: Hesron and Hamul.

text.NRSV: The children of Issachar: Tola, Puvah, Jashub, and Shimron.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Issachar,—Tola and Puvah, and Iob and Shimron;
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Issachar: Tola, and Phuuah, and Iob, and Shimron.
text.NABRE: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron.
text.DRB: The sons of Issachar: Thola and Phua and Job and Semron.

text.NRSV: The children of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Zebulon,—Sered and Elon, and Jahleel.
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Zebulun: Sered, and Elon, and Iahleel.
text.NABRE: The sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
text.DRB: The sons of Zabulon: Sared and Elon and Iahelel.

text.NRSV: (these are the sons of Leah, whom she bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram, together with his daughter Dinah; in all his sons and his daughters numbered thirty-three).
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Leah whom she bare to Jacob in Padan-aram, with Dinah also his daughter,—All the souls of his sons and of his daughters, were thirty-three.
text.KJV: These bee the sonnes of Leah, which she bare vnto Iacob in Padan-Aram, with his daughter Dinah: all the soules of his sonnes and his daughters, were thirtie and three.
text.NABRE: These were the sons whom Leah bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram, along with his daughter Dinah—thirty-three persons in all, sons and daughters.
text.DRB: These are the sons of Lia, which she bare in Mesopotamia of Syria with Dina his daughter. All the souls of his sons and daughters are thirty three.

text.NRSV: The children of Gad: Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Gad,—Zaphon and Haggi, Shuni and Ezbon,—Eri and Arodi and Areli;
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Gad: Ziphion, and Haggi, Shuni, and Ezbon, Eri, and Arodi, and Areli.
text.NABRE: The sons of Gad: Zephon, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arod, and Areli.
text.DRB: The sons of Gad: Sephion and Haggi and Siuni & Esebon and Heri and Arodi and Areli.

text.NRSV: The children of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah. The children of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Asher,—Imnah and Ishvah and Ishvi and Beriah, and Serah their sister,—and the sons of Beriah, Heber and Malchiel.
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Asher: Iimnah, and Ishuah, and Isui, and Beriah, and Serah their sister: And the sonnes of Beriah: Heber, and Malchiel.
text.NABRE: The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, and Beriah, with their sister Serah; and the sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel.
text.DRB: The sons of Aser: Jamne and Jesua and Jessui and Beria, Sara also their sister. The sons of Beria: Heber and Melchiel,

text.NRSV: (these are the children of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Leah; and these she bore to Jacob—sixteen persons).
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter,—so she bare these to Jacob,—sixteen souls.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Zilpah, whome Laban gaue to Leah his daughter: and these she bare vnto Iacob, euen sixteene soules.
text.NABRE: These are the children of Zilpah, whom Laban had given to his daughter Leah; these she bore to Jacob—sixteen persons in all.
text.DRB: these be the sons of Zelpha, whom Laban gave to Lia his daughter, and these she bare to Jacob sixteen souls.

text.NRSV: The children of Jacob’s wife Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Rachel, Jacob’s wife,—Joseph and Benjamin;
text.KJV: The sonnes of Rachel Iacobs wife: Ioseph, and Beniamin.
text.NABRE: The sons of Jacob’s wife Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
text.DRB: The sons of Rachel Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.

text.NRSV: To Joseph in the land of Egypt were born Manasseh and Ephraim, whom Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, bore to him.
text.Rotherham: And there were born to Joseph in the land of Egypt, whom Asenath daughter of Poti-phera, priest of On, bare to him,: Manasseh and Ephraim;
text.KJV: ¶ And vnto Ioseph in the lande of Egypt, were borne Manasseh and Ephraim, which Asenath the daughter of Poti-pherah Priest of On bare vnto him.
text.NABRE: In the land of Egypt Joseph became the father of Manasseh and Ephraim, whom Asenath, daughter of Potiphera, priest of Heliopolis, bore to him.
text.DRB: And there were sons born to Joseph in the Land of Ægypt, which Aseneth the daughter of Putiphar Priest of Heliopolis bare to him: Manasses and Ephraim.

text.NRSV: The children of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Benjamin—Bela and Becher, and Ashbel, Gera and Naaman, Ehi and Rosh,—Muppim and Huppim, and Ard.
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Beniamin were Belah, and Becher, and Ashbel, Gera, and Naaman, Ehi and Rosh, Muppim, and Huppim, and Ard.
text.NABRE: The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ahiram, Shupham, Hupham, and Ard.
text.DRB: The sons of Benjamin: Bela and Bechor and Asbel and Gera and Naaman and Echi and Ros and Mophim and Ophim and Ared.

text.NRSV: (these are the children of Rachel, who were born to Jacob—fourteen persons in all).
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Rachel, who were born to Jacob,—all the souls, were fourteen.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Rachel which were borne to Iacob: all the soules were fourteene.
text.NABRE: These are the sons whom Rachel bore to Jacob—fourteen persons in all.
text.DRB: These be the sons of Rachel, which she bare to Jacob: all the souls, fourteen.

text.NRSV: The children of Dan: Hashum.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Dan,—Hushim;
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Dan: Hushim.
text.NABRE: The sons of Dan: Hushim.
text.DRB: The sons of Dan: Husim.

text.NRSV: The children of Naphtali: Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Naphtali,—Jahzeel and Guni, and Jezer and Shillem.
text.KJV: ¶ And the sonnes of Naphtali: Iahzeel, and Guni, and Iezer, and Shillem.
text.NABRE: The sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
text.DRB: The sons of Nephthali: Jaziel and Guni and Ieser and Sallem.

text.NRSV: (these are the children of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Rachel, and these she bore to Jacob—seven persons in all).
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter,—so she bare these to Jacob,—all the souls, were seven.
text.KJV: These are the sonnes of Bilhah, which Laban gaue vnto Rachel his daughter, and she bare these vnto Iacob: all the soules were seuen.
text.NABRE: These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban had given to his daughter Rachel; these she bore to Jacob—seven persons in all.
text.DRB: These be the sons of Bala, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter: and these she bare to Jacob; all the souls, seven.

text.NRSV: All the persons belonging to Jacob who came into Egypt, who were his own offspring, not including the wives of his sons, were sixty-six persons in all.
text.Rotherham: All the souls that came in with Jacob to Egypt that had come forth of his loins, besides Jacob’s sons’ wives,—all the souls, were sixty-six;
text.KJV: All the soules that came with Iacob into Egypt, which came out of his loines, besides Iacobs sonnes wiues, all the soules were threescore and sixe.
text.NABRE: Jacob’s people who came to Egypt—his direct descendants, not counting the wives of Jacob’s sons—numbered sixty-six persons in all.
text.DRB: All the souls, that entered with Jacob into Ægypt, and that came out of his thigh, besides his son's wives, sixty six.

text.NRSV: The children of Joseph, who were born to him in Egypt, were two; all the persons of the house of Jacob who came into Egypt were seventy.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Joseph who were born to him in Egypt, were two souls: All the souls of the house of Jacob that came into Egypt, were seventy.
text.KJV: And the sonnes of Ioseph, which were borne him in Egypt, were two soules: all the soules of the house of Iacob, which came into Egypt, were threescore and ten.
text.NABRE: Together with Joseph’s sons who were born to him in Egypt—two persons—all the people comprising the household of Jacob who had come to Egypt amounted to seventy persons in all.
text.DRB: And the sons of Joseph, that were born to him in the land of Ægypt, two souls, All the souls of the house of Jacob, that entered into Ægypt, were seventy.

text.NRSV: Israel sent Judah ahead to Joseph to lead the way before him into Goshen. When they came to the land of Goshen,
text.Rotherham: And he sent, Judah, before him unto Joseph, to direct his face to Goshen,—so they came in towards the land of Goshen.
text.KJV: ¶ And he sent Iudah before him vnto Ioseph, to direct his face vnto Goshen, and they came into the lande of Goshen.
text.NABRE: Israel had sent Judah ahead to Joseph, so that he might meet him in Goshen. On his arrival in the region of Goshen,
text.DRB: And he sent Judas before him to Joseph, that he should tell him, and he should come into Gessen to meet him.

text.NRSV: Joseph made ready his chariot and went up to meet his father Israel in Goshen. He presented himself to him, fell on his neck, and wept on his neck a good while.
text.Rotherham: Then yoked Joseph his chariot, and went up to meet Israel his father towards Goshen,—and he appeared unto him, and fell upon his neck, and wept upon his neck a good while.
text.KJV: And Ioseph made ready his charet, and went vp to meet Israel his father, to Goshen, and presented himselfe vnto him: and he fell on his necke, and wept on his necke a good while.
text.NABRE: Joseph prepared his chariot and went up to meet his father Israel in Goshen. As soon as Israel made his appearance, Joseph threw his arms around him and wept a long time on his shoulder.
text.DRB: Wither when he was come, Joseph addressing his chariot went up to meet his father, unto the same place: and seeing him fell upon his neck, and as they embraced he wept.

text.NRSV: Israel said to Joseph, “I can die now, having seen for myself that you are still alive.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Israel unto Joseph I am ready to die at last,—now that I have seen thy face, in that thou art, yet alive.
text.KJV: And Israel saide vnto Ioseph, Now let me die, since I haue seene thy face, because thou art yet aliue.
text.NABRE: And Israel said to Joseph, “At last I can die, now that I have seen for myself that you are still alive.”
text.DRB: And his father said to Joseph: Now will I die with joy, because I have seen thy face, and do leave thee alive.

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his brothers and to his father’s household, “I will go up and tell Pharaoh, and will say to him, ‘My brothers and my father’s household, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph unto his brethren and unto the house of his father, I must go up, and must tell Pharaoh—and must say unto him, My brethren, and the house of my father, who were in the land of Canaan, have come in unto me.
text.KJV: And Ioseph said vnto his brethren, and vnto his fathers house, I will goe vp, and shew Pharaoh, and say vnto him, My brethren, & my fathers house, which were in the land of Canaan, are come vnto me.
text.NABRE: Joseph then said to his brothers and his father’s household: “I will go up and inform Pharaoh, telling him: ‘My brothers and my father’s household, whose home is in the land of Canaan, have come to me.
text.DRB: But he spake to his brethren, and to all his father's house: I will go up, & will tell Pharao, and will say to him: My brethren, and my father's house that were in the Land of Chanaan, are come to me:

text.NRSV: The men are shepherds, for they have been keepers of livestock; and they have brought their flocks, and their herds, and all that they have.’
text.Rotherham: Now, the men, are feeders of flocks, for men of cattle, they are,—and their flocks and their herds and all that they have, have they brought in.
text.KJV: And the men are sheapheards, for their trade hath bene to feed cattell: and they haue brought their flocks, and their heards, and all that they haue.
text.NABRE: The men are shepherds, having been owners of livestock; and they have brought with them their flocks and herds, as well as everything else they own.’
text.DRB: and the men are pastors of sheep, & their trade is to feed flocks: their cattle, & herds, and all that they could have, they have brought with them.

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh calls you, and says, ‘What is your occupation?’
text.Rotherham: So shall it come to pass, when Pharaoh shall call for you,—and shall say—What is your occupation?
text.KJV: And it shall come to passe when Pharaoh shall call you, and shall say, What is your occupation?
text.NABRE: So when Pharaoh summons you and asks what your occupation is,
text.DRB: And when he shall call you & shall say: What is your trade?

text.NRSV: you shall say, ‘Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our ancestors’—in order that you may settle in the land of Goshen, because all shepherds are abhorrent to the Egyptians.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye say—Men of cattle, have thy servants been from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,—To the end ye may dwell in the land of Goshen, for an abomination to Egyptians, is every feeder of a flock,
text.KJV: That ye shall say, Thy seruants trade hath bene about cattell, from our youth euen vntill now, both we, and also our fathers: that ye may dwell in the land of Goshen; for euery shepheard is an abomination vnto the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: you must answer, ‘We your servants, like our ancestors, have been owners of livestock from our youth until now,’ in order that you may stay in the region of Goshen, since all shepherds are abhorrent to the Egyptians.”
text.DRB: You shall answer: We thy servants are pastors, from our infancy until this present, both we and our fathers. And this you shall say, that you may dwell in the Land of Gessen, because the Ægyptians detest all pastors of sheep.

text.NRSV: So Joseph went and told Pharaoh, “My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they possess, have come from the land of Canaan; they are now in the land of Goshen.”
text.Rotherham: So then Joseph went in, and told Pharaoh, and said—My father and my brethren, and their flocks and their herds and all that they have, are come in from the land of Canaan,—and, here they are, in the land of Goshen.
text.KJV: Then Ioseph came and tolde Pharaoh, and saide, My father and my brethren, and their flockes, and their heards, and all that they haue, are come out of the land of Canaan: and behold, they are in the land of Goshen.
text.NABRE: Joseph went and told Pharaoh, “My father and my brothers have come from the land of Canaan, with their flocks and herds and everything else they own; and they are now in the region of Goshen.”
text.DRB: Joseph therefore going in told Pharao, saying: My father and brethren, their sheep and herds, and all things that they possess, are come out of the Land of Chanaan; and behold they stay in the Land of Gessen.

text.NRSV: From among his brothers he took five men and presented them to Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: Moreover out of the whole number of his brethren, he took with him five men,—and set them before Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And hee tooke some of his brethren, euen fiue men, & presented them vnto Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: He then presented to Pharaoh five of his brothers whom he had selected from their full number.
text.DRB: The utmost also of his brethren five persons he presented before the king:

text.NRSV: Pharaoh said to his brothers, “What is your occupation?” And they said to Pharaoh, “Your servants are shepherds, as our ancestors were.”
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said unto the brethren of Joseph—What is your occupation? And they said unto Pharaoh—Feeders of flocks, are thy servants, both we and our fathers.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said vnto his brethren, What is your occupation? And they said vnto Pharaoh, Thy seruants are shepheards, both wee and also our fathers.
text.NABRE: When Pharaoh asked them, “What is your occupation?” they answered, “We, your servants, like our ancestors, are shepherds.
text.DRB: whom he asked: What trade have you? They answered: We thy servants are pastors of sheep, both we, and our fathers.

text.NRSV: They said to Pharaoh, “We have come to reside as aliens in the land; for there is no pasture for your servants’ flocks because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now, we ask you, let your servants settle in the land of Goshen.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto Pharaoh—To sojourn in the land, are we come in, for there is no pasture for the flocks which pertain to thy servants, for, severe, is the famine in the land of Canaan. Now, therefore, let thy servants dwell we pray thee in the land of Goshen.
text.KJV: They said moreouer vnto Pharaoh, For to soiourne in the land are we come: for thy seruants haue no pasture for their flockes, for the famine is sore in the land of Canaan: now therefore we pray thee, let thy seruants dwel in the land of Goshen.
text.NABRE: We have come,” they continued, “in order to sojourn in this land, for there is no pasture for your servants’ flocks, because the famine has been severe in the land of Canaan. So now please let your servants settle in the region of Goshen.”
text.DRB: We are come to sojourn in the land, because there is no grass for thy servant's flocks, the famine being very sore in the land of Chanaan: and we desire thee to command that we thy servants may be in the Land of Gessen.

text.NRSV: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Your father and your brothers have come to you.
text.Rotherham: Then spake Pharaoh unto Joseph saying,—Thy father and thy brethren, are come in unto thee.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh spake vnto Ioseph, saying, Thy father and thy brethren are come vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Now that your father and your brothers have come to you,
text.DRB: And the king therefore said to Joseph: Thy father and thy brethren are come to thee.

text.NRSV: The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and your brothers in the best part of the land; let them live in the land of Goshen; and if you know that there are capable men among them, put them in charge of my livestock.”
text.Rotherham: The land of Egypt, is, before thee, in the best of the land, cause thou thy father and thy brethren to dwell,—let them dwell in the land of Goshen, And if thou knowest that there are among them men of ability, then shalt thou set them as chieftains of cattle over mine.
text.KJV: The land of Egypt is before thee: in the best of the land make thy father and brethren to dwell, in the lande of Goshen let them dwell: and if thou knowest any man of actiuitie amongst them, then make them rulers ouer my cattell.
text.NABRE: the land of Egypt is at your disposal; settle your father and brothers in the pick of the land. Let them settle in the region of Goshen. And if you know of capable men among them, put them in charge of my livestock.”
text.DRB: The Land of Ægypt is in thy sight: make them to dwell in the best place, & deliver them the Land of Gessen. And if so be thou know that there are industrious men among them, appoint them masters of my cattle.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph brought in his father Jacob, and presented him before Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph brought in Jacob his father, and caused him to stand before Pharaoh,—and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And Ioseph brought in Iacob his father, and set him before Pharaoh: and Iacob blessed Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: Then Joseph brought his father Jacob and presented him to Pharaoh. And Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
text.DRB: After this Joseph brought in his father to the king, and set him before him: who blessed him,

text.NRSV: Pharaoh said to Jacob, “How many are the years of your life?”
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said unto Jacob: About how many, have been the days of the years of thy life?
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said vnto Iacob, How old art thou?
text.NABRE: Then Pharaoh asked Jacob, “How many years have you lived?”
text.DRB: and being asked of him: How many be the days of the years of thy life?

text.NRSV: Jacob said to Pharaoh, “The years of my earthly sojourn are one hundred thirty; few and hard have been the years of my life. They do not compare with the years of the life of my ancestors during their long sojourn.”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said unto Pharaoh, The days of the years of my sojournings, have been a hundred and thirty years,—Few and evil, have been the days of the years of my life, neither have they attained unto the days of the years of the lives of my fathers, in the days of their sojournings.
text.KJV: And Iacob said vnto Pharaoh, The dayes of the yeeres of my pilgrimage are an hundred & thirtie yeres: few and euill haue the dayes of the yeeres of my life bene, and haue not attained vnto the dayes of the yeeres of the life of my fathers, in the dayes of their pilgrimage.
text.NABRE: Jacob replied: “The years I have lived as a wayfarer amount to a hundred and thirty. Few and hard have been these years of my life, and they do not compare with the years that my ancestors lived as wayfarers.”
text.DRB: He answered: The days of the pilgrimage of my life are an hundred thirty years, few and evil, and they are not come to the days of my fathers, in which they were pilgrims.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from the presence of Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob blessed Pharaoh,—and came forth from the presence of Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And Iacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from before Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh and withdrew from his presence.
text.DRB: And blessing the king, he went forth.

text.NRSV: Joseph settled his father and his brothers, and granted them a holding in the land of Egypt, in the best part of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had instructed.
text.Rotherham: So then Joseph fixed the dwelling of his father and his brethren, and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt in the best of the land. in the land of Rameses,—as Pharaoh had commanded.
text.KJV: ¶ And Ioseph placed his father, and his brethren, and gaue them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
text.NABRE: Joseph settled his father and brothers and gave them a holding in Egypt on the pick of the land, in the region of Rameses, as Pharaoh had ordered.
text.DRB: But Joseph gave possession to his father and his brethren in Ægypt, in the best place of the land, in Rhamesses, as Pharao had commanded.

text.NRSV: And Joseph provided his father, his brothers, and all his father’s household with food, according to the number of their dependents.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph nourished his father and his brethren, and all his father’s house,—with bread, according to the need of their little ones.
text.KJV: And Ioseph nourished his father and his brethren, and all his fathers houshold with bread, according to their families.
text.NABRE: And Joseph provided food for his father and brothers and his father’s whole household, down to the youngest.
text.DRB: And he nourished them, and all his father's house, allowing victuals to every one.

text.NRSV: Now there was no food in all the land, for the famine was very severe. The land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished because of the famine.
text.Rotherham: But, bread, was there none in all the land,—for the famine was, very severe,—so that the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan fainted by reason of the famine,
text.KJV: ¶ And there was no bread in all the land: for the famine was very sore, so that the land of Egypt and all the land of Canaan fainted by reason of the famine.
text.NABRE: Since there was no food in all the land because of the extreme severity of the famine, and the lands of Egypt and Canaan were languishing from hunger,
text.DRB: For in the whole world there wanted bread. & famine oppressed the land, especially of Ægypt and Chanaan.

text.NRSV: Joseph collected all the money to be found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, in exchange for the grain that they bought; and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh’s house.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph gathered up all the silver that was found in the land of Egypt. and in the land of Canaan, for the corn which, they, were buying,—and Joseph brought the silver into the house of Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And Ioseph gathered vp all the money that was found in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, for the corne which they bought: and Ioseph brought the money into Pharaohs house.
text.NABRE: Joseph gathered in, as payment for the grain that they were buying, all the money that was to be found in Egypt and Canaan, and he put it in Pharaoh’s house.
text.DRB: Out of which he gathered together all the money for the selling of corn, & brought it in unto the king's treasure.

text.NRSV: When the money from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan was spent, all the Egyptians came to Joseph, and said, “Give us food! Why should we die before your eyes? For our money is gone.”
text.Rotherham: And the silver was spent out of the land of Egypt and out of the land of Canaan, so all the Egyptians came in unto Joseph, saying—Do give us bread, for why should we die before thee, although, silver, hath failed?
text.KJV: And when money failed in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came vnto Ioseph, and said, Giue vs bread: for why should we die in thy presence? for the money faileth.
text.NABRE: When all the money in Egypt and Canaan was spent, all the Egyptians came to Joseph, pleading, “Give us food! Why should we perish in front of you? For our money is gone.”
text.DRB: And when the buyers wanted money, all Ægypt came to Joseph, saying: Give us bread: why die we before thee, our money failing?

text.NRSV: And Joseph answered, “Give me your livestock, and I will give you food in exchange for your livestock, if your money is gone.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph: Give your cattle, and I will give you for your cattle,—if silver hath failed.
text.KJV: And Ioseph said, Giue your cattell: and I will giue you for your catell, if money faile.
text.NABRE: “Give me your livestock if your money is gone,” replied Joseph. “I will give you food in return for your livestock.”
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Bring your cattle, and for them I will give you victuals, if you have not to pay.

text.NRSV: So they brought their livestock to Joseph; and Joseph gave them food in exchange for the horses, the flocks, the herds, and the donkeys. That year he supplied them with food in exchange for all their livestock.
text.Rotherham: So they brought in their cattle unto Joseph, and Joseph gave them bread in exchange for the horses and for the cattle of the flock and for the cattle of the herd, and for the asses,—and he provided them with bread for all their cattle, throughout that year.
text.KJV: And they brought their cattel vnto Ioseph: and Ioseph gaue them bread in exchange for horses, and for the flockes, and for the cattell of the heards, and for the asses, and he fed them with bread, for all their cattel, for that yeere.
text.NABRE: So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and he gave them food in exchange for their horses, their flocks of sheep and herds of cattle, and their donkeys. Thus he supplied them with food in exchange for all their livestock in that year.
text.DRB: Which when they had brought, he gave them sustenance for horses, and sheep, and oxen, and asses: and he sustained them that year for the exchange of the cattle.

text.NRSV: When that year was ended, they came to him the following year, and said to him, “We can not hide from my lord that our money is all spent; and the herds of cattle are my lord’s. There is nothing left in the sight of my lord but our bodies and our lands.
text.Rotherham: When that year was ended, then came they unto him in the second year and said to him—We will not hide from my lord, how that the silver and the herds of beasts for my lord are come to an end,—there is nothing left before my lord, save only our bodies and our ground:
text.KJV: When that yeere was ended, they came vnto him the second yeere, and said vnto him, We will not hide it from my lord, how that our money is spent, my lord also had our heards of cattell: there is not ought left in the sight of my lord, but our bodies, and our lands.
text.NABRE: That year ended, and they came to him in the next one and said: “We cannot hide from my lord that, with our money spent and our livestock made over to my lord, there is nothing left to put at my lord’s disposal except our bodies and our land.
text.DRB: And they came the second year, and said to him: We will not conceal from our lord, that our money failing, our cattle withal have failed: neither art thou ignorant, that we have nothing besides our bodies and land.

text.NRSV: Shall we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land in exchange for food. We with our land will become slaves to Pharaoh; just give us seed, so that we may live and not die, and that the land may not become desolate.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore should we die before thine eyes both we, and our ground? Buy thou us and our ground, for bread,—and we and our ground will become servants to Pharaoh, and give seed, that we may live, and not die, and the ground, not lie waste.
text.KJV: Wherfore shall we die before thine eyes, both we, and our land? buy vs and our land for bread, and we and our land will be seruants vnto Pharaoh: and giue vs seede that we may liue and not die, that the land be not desolate.
text.NABRE: Why should we and our land perish before your very eyes? Take us and our land in exchange for food, and we will become Pharaoh’s slaves and our land his property; only give us seed, that we may survive and not perish, and that our land may not turn into a waste.”
text.DRB: Why therefore shall we die in thy sight? Both we and our land will be thine: buy us to be the king's bondmen, & give us seed, lest for default of tillers the land be turned into a wilderness.

text.NRSV: So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh. All the Egyptians sold their fields, because the famine was severe upon them; and the land became Pharaoh’s.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph bought all the ground of the Egyptians for Pharaoh, for the Egyptians sold every man his field, because the famine had laid fast hold upon them,—so the land became Pharaoh’s.
text.KJV: And Ioseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh: for the Egyptians sold euery man his field, because the famine preuailed ouer them: so the land became Pharaohs.
text.NABRE: So Joseph acquired all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh. Each of the Egyptians sold his field, since the famine weighed heavily upon them. Thus the land passed over to Pharaoh,
text.DRB: Joseph therefore bought all the Land of Ægypt, every man selling his possessions for the greatness of the famine. And he brought it under Pharao's hands,

text.NRSV: As for the people, he made slaves of them from one end of Egypt to the other.
text.Rotherham: And as for the people, he brought them into bondage as servants,—from one end of the boundary of Egypt unto the other end thereof.
text.KJV: And as for the people, he remoued them to cities from one end of the borders of Egypt, euen to the other ende thereof.
text.NABRE: and the people were reduced to slavery, from one end of Egypt’s territory to the other.
text.DRB: and all the people thereof from the farthest ends of Ægypt, even to the uttermost coasts thereof,

text.NRSV: Only the land of the priests he did not buy; for the priests had a fixed allowance from Pharaoh, and lived on the allowance that Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their land.
text.Rotherham: Only, the ground of the priests, bought he not,—for the priests had a statutory-portion, from Pharaoh and they had been eating their statutory-portion, which Pharaoh had given them, for which cause, they had not sold their ground.
text.KJV: Onely the land of the Priests bought he not: for the priests had a portion assigned them of Pharaoh, and did eate their portion which Pharaoh gaue them: wherefore they solde not their lands.
text.NABRE: Only the priests’ lands Joseph did not acquire. Since the priests had a fixed allowance from Pharaoh and lived off the allowance Pharaoh had granted them, they did not have to sell their land.
text.DRB: saving the land of the Priests, which the king had delivered them: to whom also a certain allowance of victuals was given out of the common barns, and therefore they were not driven to sell their possessions.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph said to the people, “Now that I have this day bought you and your land for Pharaoh, here is seed for you; sow the land.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto the people, Lo! I have bought you this day, and your ground for Pharaoh,—see, here is seed for you, so shall ye sow the ground;
text.KJV: Then Ioseph said vnto the people, Behold, I haue bought you this day, and your land for Pharaoh: Loe, here is seed for you, and ye shall sow the land.
text.NABRE: Joseph told the people: “Now that I have acquired you and your land for Pharaoh, here is your seed for sowing the land.
text.DRB: Joseph therefore said to the people: Behold as you see, Pharao possesseth both you and your land: take seed, and sow the fields,

text.NRSV: And at the harvests you shall give one-fifth to Pharaoh, and four-fifths shall be your own, as seed for the field and as food for yourselves and your households, and as food for your little ones.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall come to pass that, of the yield, ye shall give a fifth to Pharaoh,—but the four parts, shall be your own—for seed for the field, and for your food and for them who are in your households and for food for your little ones.
text.KJV: And it shall come to passe in the increase, that you shall giue the fift part vnto Pharaoh, and foure parts shall be your owne, for seed of the field, and for your food, and for them of your households, and for food for your litle ones.
text.NABRE: But when the harvest is in, you must give a fifth of it to Pharaoh, while you keep four-fifths as seed for your fields and as food for yourselves and your households and as food for your children.”
text.DRB: that you may have corn. The fifth part you shall give to the king: the other four Iam content you shall have for seed, & for food to your families & your children.

text.NRSV: They said, “You have saved our lives; may it please my lord, we will be slaves to Pharaoh.”
text.Rotherham: And they said—Thou hast saved our lives! let us find favour in the eyes of my lord, so will we become Pharaoh’s servants.
text.KJV: And they said, Thou hast saued our liues: let vs find grace in the sight of my lord, and we will be Pharaohs seruants.
text.NABRE: “You have saved our lives!” they answered. “We have found favor with my lord; now we will be Pharaoh’s slaves.”
text.DRB: Who answered: Our life is in thy hand: only let our lord have a respect unto us, and we will gladly serve the king.

text.NRSV: So Joseph made it a statute concerning the land of Egypt, and it stands to this day, that Pharaoh should have the fifth. The land of the priests alone did not become Pharaoh’s.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph appointed it for a statute—until this day, regarding the ground of Egypt, that Pharaoh, should take a fifth,—only, the ground of the priests, was, theirs alone, it had not become Pharaoh’s.
text.KJV: And Ioseph made it a law ouer the land of Egypt vnto this day, that Pharaoh should haue the fift part: except the land of the priests onely, which became not Pharaohs.
text.NABRE: Thus Joseph made it a statute for the land of Egypt, which is still in force, that a fifth of its produce should go to Pharaoh. Only the land of the priests did not pass over to Pharaoh.
text.DRB: From that time until this present day in the whole land of Ægypt, the fifth part is payed to the kings, and it became as it were a law, saving the land of the Priests, which was free from this condition.

text.NRSV: Thus Israel settled in the land of Egypt, in the region of Goshen; and they gained possessions in it, and were fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.
text.Rotherham: Thus Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt in the land of Goshen,—and gained possessions therein, and became fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.
text.KJV: ¶ And Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt in the countrey of Goshen, and they had possessions therein, and grew, and multiplied exceedingly.
text.NABRE: Thus Israel settled in the land of Egypt, in the region of Goshen. There they acquired holdings, were fertile, and multiplied greatly.
text.DRB: Israel therefore dwelt in Ægypt, that is, in the Land of Gessen, and possessed it: and was increased, & multiplied exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; so the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were one hundred forty-seven years.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years,—so it came to pass that the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were a hundred and forty-seven years.
text.KJV: And Iacob liued in the land of Egypt seuenteene yeres: so the whole age of Iacob was an hundred fourtie and seuen yeeres.
text.NABRE: Jacob lived in the land of Egypt for seventeen years; the span of his life came to a hundred and forty-seven years.
text.DRB: And he lived in it seventeen years: and all the days of his life came to an hundred forty seven years.

text.NRSV: When the time of Israel’s death drew near, he called his son Joseph and said to him, “If I have found favor with you, put your hand under my thigh and promise to deal loyally and truly with me. Do not bury me in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And the days of Israel drew near that he must die, so he called for his son for Joseph and said to him—If, I pray thee I have found favour in thine eyes, place, I pray thee thy hand under my thigh,—so shalt thou deal with me in lovingkindness and faithfulness—Do not I pray thee, bury me in Egypt.
text.KJV: And the time drew nigh that Israel must die, and he called his sonne Ioseph, and said vnto him, If now I haue found grace in thy sight, put, I pray thee, thy hand vnder my thigh, and deale kindly and truely with mee, bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt.
text.NABRE: When the time approached for Israel to die, he called his son Joseph and said to him: “If it pleases you, put your hand under my thigh as a sign of your enduring fidelity to me; do not bury me in Egypt.
text.DRB: And when he saw that the day of his death approached, he called his son Joseph, and said to him: If I have found grace in thy sight, put thy hand under my thigh: and thou shalt do me this mercy and truth. Not to bury me in Ægypt:

text.NRSV: When I lie down with my ancestors, carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place.” He answered, “I will do as you have said.”
text.Rotherham: When I shall lie down with my fathers, then shalt thou carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their buryingplace. And he said: I, will do according to thy word.
text.KJV: But I will lie with my fathers, and thou shalt carie mee out of Egypt, and bury me in their burying place: and he said, I will doe as thou hast said.
text.NABRE: When I lie down with my ancestors, take me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place.” “I will do as you say,” he replied.
text.DRB: but I will sleep with my fathers, and take me away out of this land, & bury me in the sepulchre of my ancestors. To whom Joseph answered: I will do that thou hast commanded.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Swear to me”; and he swore to him. Then Israel bowed himself on the head of his bed.
text.Rotherham: Then he said: Come, swear to me! And he sware to him. Then did Israel bow himself down on the head of the couch.
text.KJV: And he said, Sweare vnto mee: and he sware vnto him. And Israel bowed himselfe vpon the beds head.
text.NABRE: But his father demanded, “Swear it to me!” So Joseph swore to him. Then Israel bowed at the head of the bed.
text.DRB: And he said: Swear then to me. Who swearing, Israel adored God, turning to the bed's head.

text.NRSV: After this Joseph was told, “Your father is ill.” So he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that one said to Joseph, Lo! thy father, is sick. So he took his two sons with him, Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.KJV: And it came to passe after these things, that one told Ioseph, Behold, thy father is sicke: and he tooke with him his two sonnes, Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.NABRE: Some time afterward, Joseph was informed, “Your father is failing.” So he took along with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.DRB: These things being so done, it was told Joseph that his father was sick: who, taking his two sons Manasses and Ephraim, went forward.

text.NRSV: When Jacob was told, “Your son Joseph has come to you,” he summoned his strength and sat up in bed.
text.Rotherham: And one told Jacob, and said—Lo! thy son Joseph, coming in unto thee. So Israel strengthened himself, and sat up on the couch.
text.KJV: And one told Iacob, and said, Behold, thy sonne Ioseph commeth vnto thee: and Israel strengthened himselfe, and sate vpon the bed.
text.NABRE: When Jacob was told, “Your son Joseph has come to you,” Israel rallied his strength and sat up in bed.
text.DRB: And it was told the old man: Behold thy son Joseph cometh to thee. Who being strengthened sat on his bed.

text.NRSV: And Jacob said to Joseph, “God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and he blessed me,
text.Rotherham: Then said Jacob unto Joseph, GOD Almighty, appeared unto me in Luz in the land of Canaan,—and blessed me;
text.KJV: And Iacob saide vnto Ioseph, God Almightie appeared vnto mee at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessed mee,
text.NABRE: Jacob then said to Joseph: “God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessing me,
text.DRB: And Joseph being entered in to him, he said: God Almighty appeared to me in Luza, which is in the Land of Chanaan: and he blessed me,

text.NRSV: and said to me, ‘I am going to make you fruitful and increase your numbers; I will make of you a company of peoples, and will give this land to your offspring after you for a perpetual holding.’
text.Rotherham: and said unto me—Behold me! about to make thee fruitful, so will I multiply thee, and give thee to become a multitude of peoples,—so will I give this land to thy seed after thee, as an age-abiding possession.
text.KJV: And said vnto me, Behold, I wil make thee fruitfull, and multiplie thee, and I will make of thee a multitude of people, and will giue this land to thy seede after thee, for an euerlasting possession.
text.NABRE: he said, ‘I will make you fertile and multiply you and make you into an assembly of peoples, and I will give this land to your descendants after you as a permanent possession.’
text.DRB: and said: I will increase, and multiply thee, and make thee into multitudes of peoples: and I will give thee this land, and to thy seed after thee for an everlasting possession.

text.NRSV: Therefore your two sons, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are now mine; Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, just as Reuben and Simeon are.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore thy two sons who were born to thee in the land of Egypt, before I came in unto thee in Egypt, are mine! Ephraim and Manasseh, like Reuben and Simeon, shall be mine!
text.KJV: ¶ And now thy two sonnes, Ephraim and Manasseh, which were borne vnto thee in the land of Egypt, before I came vnto thee into Egypt, are mine: as Reuben and Simeon, they shalbe mine.
text.NABRE: So now your two sons who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I joined you here, shall be mine; Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine as much as Reuben and Simeon are mine.
text.DRB: Thy two sons therefore which were born to thee in the Land of Ægypt before I came hither to thee, shall be mine: Ephraim and Manasses, as Ruben and Simeon shall be reputed to me.

text.NRSV: As for the offspring born to you after them, they shall be yours. They shall be recorded under the names of their brothers with regard to their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: But thine offspring which thou hast begotten after them, thine own shall they be,—after the name of their brethren, shall they be called in their inheritance.
text.KJV: And thy issue which thou begettest after them, shall be thine, and shall be called after the name of their brethren in their inheritance.
text.NABRE: Progeny born to you after them shall remain yours; but their heritage shall be recorded in the names of their brothers.
text.DRB: But the rest begotten of thee after them, shall be thine, and shall be called by the name of their brethren in their possessions.

text.NRSV: For when I came from Paddan, Rachel, alas, died in the land of Canaan on the way, while there was still some distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath” (that is, Bethlehem).
text.Rotherham: But, as for me, when I came in from Padan, Rachel died by me, in the land of Canaan, in the way, while yet there was a stretch of country to come into Ephrath,—so I buried her there in the way to Ephrath, the same, is Bethlehem.
text.KJV: And as for me, when I came from Padan, Rachel died by me in the land of Canaan, in the way, when yet there was but a little way to come vnto Ephrath: and I buried her there in the way of Ephrath, the same is Bethlehem.
text.NABRE: I do this because, when I was returning from Paddan, your mother Rachel died, to my sorrow, during the journey in Canaan, while we were still a short distance from Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath [now Bethlehem].”
text.DRB: For unto me, when I came out of Mesopotamia, Rachel died in the land of Chanaan in the very journey, and it was spring time: and I entered into Ephrata, and buried her by the way side to Ephrata, which by another name is called Bethleem.

text.NRSV: When Israel saw Joseph’s sons, he said, “Who are these?”
text.Rotherham: Then Israel saw Joseph’s sons,—and said—Who are these?
text.KJV: And Israel behelde Iosephs sonnes, and said, Who are these?
text.NABRE: When Israel saw Joseph’s sons, he asked, “Who are these?”
text.DRB: And seeing his sons he said to him: Who are these?

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his father, “They are my sons, whom God has given me here.” And he said, “Bring them to me, please, that I may bless them.”
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto his father, My sons, they are, whom God hath given me, in this place. And he said—Bring them, I pray thee, unto me, that I may bless them.
text.KJV: And Ioseph said vnto his father, They are my sonnes, whom God hath giuen me in this place: and he said, Bring them, I pray thee, vnto me, and I will blesse them.
text.NABRE: “They are my sons,” Joseph answered his father, “whom God has given me here.” “Bring them to me,” said his father, “that I may bless them.”
text.DRB: He answered: They are my sons, whom God hath given me in this place. Bring them, quoth he, to me, that I may bless them.

text.NRSV: Now the eyes of Israel were dim with age, and he could not see well. So Joseph brought them near him; and he kissed them and embraced them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the eyes of Israel, had become dim from old age,—he could not see,—so he drew them near unto him, and kissed them, and embraced them.
text.KJV: (Now the eyes of Israel were dimme for age, so that he could not see,) and hee brought them neere vnto him, and he kissed them, and imbraced them.
text.NABRE: Now Israel’s eyes were dim from age; he could not see well. When Joseph brought his sons close to him, he kissed and embraced them.
text.DRB: For Israel's eyes were dim by reason of very great age, and he could not see clearly. And when they were set beside him, kissing and embracing them,

text.NRSV: Israel said to Joseph, “I did not expect to see your face; and here God has let me see your children also.”
text.Rotherham: And Israel said unto Joseph, To see thine own face, had I not thought,—and lo! God hath caused me to see even thy seed!
text.KJV: And Israel said vnto Ioseph, I had not thought to see thy face: and loe, God hath shewed me also thy seed.
text.NABRE: Then Israel said to Joseph, “I never expected to see your face again, and now God has allowed me to see your descendants as well!”
text.DRB: he said to his son: I am not defrauded of thy sight, moreover God hath shewed me thy seed.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph removed them from his father’s knees, and he bowed himself with his face to the earth.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph brought them out from beside his knees, and bowed himself down with his face towards the earth.
text.KJV: And Ioseph brought them out from betweene his knees, and hee bowed himselfe with his face to the earth.
text.NABRE: Joseph removed them from his father’s knees and bowed down before him with his face to the ground.
text.DRB: And when Joseph had taken them from his father's lap, he adored prostrate unto the ground.

text.NRSV: Joseph took them both, Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel’s left, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel’s right, and brought them near him.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph took them both,—Ephraim in his right hand on Israel’s left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand, on Israel’s right hand, and drew them near unto him.
text.KJV: And Ioseph tooke them both, Ephraim in his right hand, toward Israels left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand towards Israels right hand, and brought them neere vnto him.
text.NABRE: Then Joseph took the two, Ephraim with his right hand, to Israel’s left, and Manasseh with his left hand, to Israel’s right, and brought them up to him.
text.DRB: And he set Ephraim on his right hand, that is, on the left hand of Israel: but Manasses on his own left hand, to wit, on his father's right hand, and put them near to him.

text.NRSV: But Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on the head of Ephraim, who was the younger, and his left hand on the head of Manasseh, crossing his hands, for Manasseh was the firstborn.
text.Rotherham: Then Israel put forth his right hand and laid it upon the head of Ephraim, though, he, was the younger, and his left hand upon the head of Manasseh,—crossing his hands, although, Manasseh, was the first-born.
text.KJV: And Israel stretched out his right hand, and layd it vpon Ephraims head who was the yonger; and his left hand vpon Manassehs head, guiding his hands wittingly: for Manasseh was the first borne.
text.NABRE: But Israel, crossing his hands, put out his right hand and laid it on the head of Ephraim, although he was the younger, and his left hand on the head of Manasseh, although he was the firstborn.
text.DRB: Who stretching forth his right hand, put it upon the head of Ephraim the younger brother: and the left upon the head of Manasses, that was the elder, changing hands.

text.NRSV: He blessed Joseph, and said, “The God before whom my ancestors Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has been my shepherd all my life to this day,
text.Rotherham: Thus blessed he Joseph, and said,—God himself—before whom walked my fathers. Abraham and Isaac, God himself—who hath been my shepherd, since I came into being until this day;—
text.KJV: ¶ And he blessed Ioseph and said, God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac did walke, the God which fedde mee all my life long vnto this day,
text.NABRE: Then he blessed them with these words: “May the God in whose presence my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, The God who has been my shepherd from my birth to this day,
text.DRB: And Jacob blessed the sons of Joseph, and said: God, in whose sight my fathers Abraham & Isaac have walked, God that feedeth me from my youth until this present day:

text.NRSV: the angel who has redeemed me from all harm, bless the boys; and in them let my name be perpetuated, and the name of my ancestors Abraham and Isaac; and let them grow into a multitude on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: The Messenger—who hath been my redeemer from all evil—bless the lads! And may there be called upon them, my name, and the name of my fathers. Abraham and Isaac; And may they swarm into a multitude, in the midst of the earth!
text.KJV: The Angel which redeemed mee from all euill, blesse the laddes, and let my name be named on them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac, and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.
text.NABRE: The angel who has delivered me from all harm, bless these boys That in them my name be recalled, and the names of my fathers, Abraham and Isaac, And they may become teeming multitudes upon the earth!”
text.DRB: The Angel that delivereth me from all evils, bless these children: and be my name called upon them, the names also of my fathers Abraham, and Isaac, and grow they into a multitude upon the earth.

text.NRSV: When Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim, it displeased him; so he took his father’s hand, to remove it from Ephraim’s head to Manasseh’s head.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph saw that his father had laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim and it was displeasing in his eyes,—so he took hold of his father’s hand to remove it from off the head of Ephraim, on to the head of Manasseh.
text.KJV: And when Ioseph saw that his father laide his right hand vpon the head of Ephraim, it displeased him: and he held vp his fathers hand, to remoue it from Ephraims head, vnto Manassehs head.
text.NABRE: When Joseph saw that his father had laid his right hand on Ephraim’s head, this seemed wrong to him; so he took hold of his father’s hand, to remove it from Ephraim’s head to Manasseh’s,
text.DRB: And Joseph seeing that his father had put his right hand upon the head of Ephraim, took it heavily: and taking his father's hand he went about to lift it from Ephraim's head & to remove it upon the head of Manasses.

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his father, “Not so, my father! Since this one is the firstborn, put your right hand on his head.”
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto his father—Not so, my father! for, this, is the firstborn, lay thy right hand upon his head.
text.KJV: And Ioseph saide vnto his father, Not so my father: for this is the first borne; put thy right hand vpon his head.
text.NABRE: saying, “That is not right, father; the other one is the firstborn; lay your right hand on his head!”
text.DRB: And he said to his father: It is not convenient father so to be: because this is the first-begotten put thy right hand upon his head.

text.NRSV: But his father refused, and said, “I know, my son, I know; he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great. Nevertheless his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations.”
text.Rotherham: And his father refused, and said—I know—my son, I know! He also, shall become a people, and he also, shall become great,—but nevertheless, his younger brother, shall become greater than he, and his seed, shall become a filling up of the nations.
text.KJV: And his father refused, and said, I know it, my sonne, I know it: he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great: but truely his yonger brother shall be greater then he; and his seede shall become a multitude of nations.
text.NABRE: But his father refused. “I know it, son,” he said, “I know. That one too shall become a people, and he too shall be great. Nevertheless, his younger brother shall surpass him, and his descendants shall become a multitude of nations.”
text.DRB: Who refusing, said: I know my son, I know: and this same indeed shall be into peoples, and shall be multiplied: but his younger brother shall be greater than he: and his seed shall grow into nations.

text.NRSV: So he blessed them that day, saying, “By you Israel will invoke blessings, saying, ‘God make you like Ephraim and like Manasseh.’ ” So he put Ephraim ahead of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: And he blessed them on that day, saying—In thee, let Israel bless himself saying, God set thee as Ephraim, and as Manasseh! So he set Ephraim before Manasseh.
text.KJV: And he blessed them that day, saying, In thee shall Israel blesse, saying, God make thee as Ephraim, and as Manasseh: and he set Ephraim before Manasseh.
text.NABRE: So he blessed them that day and said, “By you shall the people of Israel pronounce blessings, saying, ‘God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh.’” Thus he placed Ephraim before Manasseh.
text.DRB: And he blessed them at that time, saying: In thee shall Israel be blessed, and it shall be said: God do unto thee as unto Ephraim, & as unto Manasses. And he set Ephraim before Manasses.

text.NRSV: Then Israel said to Joseph, “I am about to die, but God will be with you and will bring you again to the land of your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: And Israel said unto Joseph: Lo! I, am about to die,—but it shall come to pass that, God will be with you, and will take you back into the land of your fathers.
text.KJV: And Israel saide vnto Ioseph, Behold, I die: but God shall be with you, and bring you againe vnto the land of your fathers.
text.NABRE: Then Israel said to Joseph: “I am about to die. But God will be with you and will restore you to the land of your ancestors.
text.DRB: And he said to Joseph his son: Behold I die, & God will be with you, and will bring you back into the land of your fathers.

text.NRSV: I now give to you one portion more than to your brothers, the portion that I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow.”
text.Rotherham: But, I, have given to thee one mountain-track above thy brethren,—which I took out of the hand of the Amorite, with my sword and with my bow.
text.KJV: Moreouer I haue giuen to thee one portion aboue thy brethren, which I tooke out of the hand of the Amorite with my sword, and with my bow.
text.NABRE: As for me, I give to you, as to the one above his brothers, Shechem, which I captured from the Amorites with my sword and bow.”
text.DRB: I do give thee one portion above thy brethren, which I took out of the hand of the Amorrhean with my sword and bow.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob called his sons, and said: “Gather around, that I may tell you what will happen to you in days to come.
text.Rotherham: Then called Jacob unto his sons,—and said—Gather yourselves together, and let me tell you, that which shall befall you in the afterpart of the days.
text.KJV: And Iacob called vnto his sonnes, and said, Gather your selues together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the last dayes.
text.NABRE: Jacob called his sons and said: “Gather around, that I may tell you what is to happen to you in days to come.
text.DRB: And Jacob called his sons, and said to them: Come together, that I may shew you the things that shall come to you in the last days.

text.NRSV: Assemble and hear, O sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.
text.Rotherham: Assemble yourselves and hear ye sons of Jacob,—And hearken unto Israel your father!
text.KJV: Gather your selues together, and heare ye sonnes of Iacob, and hearken vnto Israel your father.
text.NABRE: “Assemble and listen, sons of Jacob, listen to Israel, your father.
text.DRB: Come together, and hear you sons of Jacob, hear ye Israel your father.

text.NRSV: Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might and the first fruits of my vigor, excelling in rank and excelling in power.
text.Rotherham: Reuben, my first-born, thou, My vigour, and the first-fruit of my strength,—Pre-eminence of elevation and pre-eminence of power:
text.KJV: ¶ Reuben, thou art my first borne, my might, and the beginning of my strength, the excellencie of dignitie, and the excellencie of power:
text.NABRE: “You, Reuben, my firstborn, my strength and the first fruit of my vigor, excelling in rank and excelling in power!
text.DRB: Ruben my first-begotten, thou art my strength, & the beginning of my sorrow: former in gifts, greater in Empire.

text.NRSV: Unstable as water, you shall no longer excel because you went up onto your father’s bed; then you defiled it—you went up onto my couch!
text.Rotherham: Boiling over like water, thou mayest not have pre-eminence, Because thou didst mount the bed of thy father,—Then, wast thou profane—My marriage-bed, he mounted!
text.KJV: Unstable as water, thou shalt not excell, because thou wentest vp to thy fathers bed: then defiledst thou it. He went vp to my couche.
text.NABRE: Turbulent as water, you shall no longer excel, for you climbed into your father’s bed and defiled my couch to my sorrow.
text.DRB: Thou art poured out as water, increase thou not: because thou didst ascend thy father's bed, and didst defile his couch.

text.NRSV: Simeon and Levi are brothers; weapons of violence are their swords.
text.Rotherham: Simeon and Levi, are brethren,—Instruments of violence, are their agreements:
text.KJV: ¶ Simeon and Leui are brethren, instruments of crueltie are in their habitations.
text.NABRE: “Simeon and Levi, brothers indeed, weapons of violence are their knives.
text.DRB: Simeon and Levi brethren: vessels of iniquity warring.

text.NRSV: May I never come into their council; may I not be joined to their company— for in their anger they killed men, and at their whim they hamstrung oxen.
text.Rotherham: Into their circle, do not thou enter my soul! In their convocation, do not thou join mine honour! For in their anger, they slew brave men, And in their wantonness, they ham-strung oxen.
text.KJV: O my soule, come not thou into their secret: vnto their assembly mine honour be not thou vnited: for in their anger they slew a man, and in their selfe will they digged downe a wall.
text.NABRE: Let not my person enter their council, or my honor be joined with their company; For in their fury they killed men, at their whim they maimed oxen.
text.DRB: Into their counsel come not my soul, and in their congregation be not my glory: because in their fury they slew a man, and in their willfulness they undermined a wall.

text.NRSV: Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce, and their wrath, for it is cruel! I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Accursed, be their anger, because fierce, And their wrath because cruel,—I divide them in Jacob, And I disperse them in Israel.
text.KJV: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce; and their wrath, for it was cruell: I will diuide them in Iacob, and scatter them in Israel.
text.NABRE: Cursed be their fury so fierce, and their rage so cruel! I will scatter them in Jacob, disperse them throughout Israel.
text.DRB: Cursed be their fury, because it is stubborn; and their indignation, because it is hard: I will divide them in Jacob, and will disperse them in Israel.

text.NRSV: Judah, your brothers shall praise you; your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; your father’s sons shall bow down before you.
text.Rotherham: Judah, as for thee, thy brethren shall praise thee when thy hand is on the neck of thy foes,—The sons of thy father, shall bow them-selves down to thee:
text.KJV: ¶ Iudah, thou art he whom thy brethren shall praise: thy hand shall be in the necke of thine enemies, thy fathers children shall bow downe before thee.
text.NABRE: “You, Judah, shall your brothers praise —your hand on the neck of your enemies; the sons of your father shall bow down to you.
text.DRB: Judas, thee thy brethren shall praise: thy hand shall be in the neck of thine enemies: thy father's children shall adore thee.

text.NRSV: Judah is a lion’s whelp; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He crouches down, he stretches out like a lion, like a lioness—who dares rouse him up?
text.Rotherham: A lion’s whelp, is Judah, From the prey, my son hast thou come up! He hath stooped—hath crouched As a lion—or as a lioness, Who shall rouse him?
text.KJV: Iudah is a Lyons whelpe: from the pray my sonne thou art gone vp: he stouped downe, hee couched as a Lyon, and as an old Lyon: who shall rouse him vp?
text.NABRE: Judah is a lion’s cub, you have grown up on prey, my son. He crouches, lies down like a lion, like a lioness—who would dare rouse him?
text.DRB: Alion's whelp Judas: to the prey my son thou didst ascend: taking thy rest thou didst lie as a lion, and as it were a lioness, who shall raise him up?

text.NRSV: The scepter shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, until tribute comes to him; and the obedience of the peoples is his.
text.Rotherham: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, Nor the commander’s staff from between his feet,—Until that he come in as a Shiloh, And, his, be the obedience of the peoples:
text.KJV: The scepter shall not depart from Iudah, nor a Law-giuer from betweene his feete, vntill Shiloh come: and vnto him shall the gathering of the people be:
text.NABRE: The scepter shall never depart from Judah, or the mace from between his feet, Until tribute comes to him, and he receives the people’s obedience.
text.DRB: The Sceptre Shall Not be Taken Away From Judas, And A duke Out of His Thigh, Till he do Come That is to be Sent, And The Same Shall be The Expectation of The Gentiles.

text.NRSV: Binding his foal to the vine and his donkey’s colt to the choice vine, he washes his garments in wine and his robe in the blood of grapes;
text.Rotherham: Binding, to the vine, his ass, And to the choice vine his ass’s colt, He hath washed in wine his raiment, And in the blood of grapes, his mantle:
text.KJV: Binding his foale vnto the vine, and his asses colt vnto the choice vine; he washed his garments in wine, and his clothes in the blood of grapes.
text.NABRE: He tethers his donkey to the vine, his donkey’s foal to the choicest stem. In wine he washes his garments, his robe in the blood of grapes.
text.DRB: Tying to the vineyard his colt, and to the vine, o my son, his she ass. He shall wash his stole in wine, and in the blood of the grape his cloak.

text.NRSV: his eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
text.Rotherham: Darker—his eyes, than wine,—Whiter—his teeth than milk!
text.KJV: His eyes shall be red with wine, and his teeth white with milke.
text.NABRE: His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth are whiter than milk.
text.DRB: His eyes are more beautiful than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.

text.NRSV: Zebulun shall settle at the shore of the sea; he shall be a haven for ships, and his border shall be at Sidon.
text.Rotherham: Zebulon, by a haven of seas, shall he settle down,—Even he, by a haven of ships, With his utmost part upon Zidon.
text.KJV: ¶ Zebulun shall dwell at the hauen of the sea, and hee shall be for an Hauen of ships: and his border shall be vnto Zidon.
text.NABRE: “Zebulun shall dwell by the seashore; he will be a haven for ships, and his flank shall rest on Sidon.
text.DRB: Zabulon shall dwell in the shore of the sea, and in the road of ships reaching as far as Sidon.

text.NRSV: Issachar is a strong donkey, lying down between the sheepfolds;
text.Rotherham: Issachar, an ass of strength,—couching between the pens;
text.KJV: ¶ Issachar is a strong asse, couching downe betweene two burdens.
text.NABRE: “Issachar is a rawboned donkey, crouching between the saddlebags.
text.DRB: Issachar a strong ass lying at rest between the borders.

text.NRSV: he saw that a resting place was good, and that the land was pleasant; so he bowed his shoulder to the burden, and became a slave at forced labor.
text.Rotherham: Then beholdeth he rest, that it is good, And the ground that it is pleasant,—So he bendeth his shoulder to bear, And becometh a tributary servant.
text.KJV: And he saw that rest was good, and the land that it was pleasant: and bowed his shoulder to beare, and became a seruant vnto tribute.
text.NABRE: When he saw how good a settled life was, and how pleasant the land, He bent his shoulder to the burden and became a toiling serf.
text.DRB: He saw rest that it was good; and the earth that it was very good: and he put under his shoulder to carry, and became serving under tributes.

text.NRSV: Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Dan, shall vindicate his people,—as one of the tribes of Israel;
text.KJV: ¶ Dan shall iudge his people, as one of the tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: “Dan shall achieve justice for his people as one of the tribes of Israel.
text.DRB: Dan shall judge his own people, as also another tribe in Israel.

text.NRSV: Dan shall be a snake by the roadside, a viper along the path, that bites the horse’s heels so that its rider falls backward.
text.Rotherham: It cometh to pass that, Dan, is a serpent upon the way, A horned viper, upon the path,—That biteth the heels of the horse, And his rider falleth backwards:—
text.KJV: Dan shalbe a serpent by the way, an adder in the path, that biteth the horse heeles, so that his rider shall fall backward.
text.NABRE: Let Dan be a serpent by the roadside, a horned viper by the path, That bites the horse’s heel, so that the rider tumbles backward.
text.DRB: Be Dan a snake in the way, a serpent in the path, biting the hoofs of the horse, that his rider may fall backward.

text.NRSV: I wait for your salvation, O Lord.
text.Rotherham: For thy salvation, have I waited O Yahweh!
text.KJV: I haue waited for thy saluation, O LORD.
text.NABRE: “I long for your deliverance, O Lord!
text.DRB: I will expect Thy Salvation o Lord.

text.NRSV: Gad shall be raided by raiders, but he shall raid at their heels.
text.Rotherham: Gad! a troop shall troop on him,—But, he, shall troop on the rear.
text.KJV: ¶ Gad, a troupe shall ouercome him: but he shall ouercome at the last.
text.NABRE: “Gad shall be raided by raiders, but he shall raid at their heels.
text.DRB: Gad, the girded shall fight before him: and himself shall be girded backward.

text.NRSV: Asher’s food shall be rich, and he shall provide royal delicacies.
text.Rotherham: Out of Asher, fatness, is his food,—So, he, setteth forth dainties for a king.
text.KJV: ¶ Out of Asher his bread shall be fat, and he shall yeeld royall dainties.
text.NABRE: “Asher’s produce is rich, and he shall furnish delicacies for kings.
text.DRB: Aser, his bread is fat, and he shall give dainties to kings.

text.NRSV: Naphtali is a doe let loose that bears lovely fawns.
text.Rotherham: Naphtali, is a slender hind,—That putteth forth antlers of beauty.
text.KJV: ¶ Naphtali is a hinde let loose: He giueth goodly words.
text.NABRE: “Naphtali is a hind let loose, which brings forth lovely fawns.
text.DRB: Nephthali, a hart let forth, and giving speeches of beauty.

text.NRSV: Joseph is a fruitful bough, a fruitful bough by a spring; his branches run over the wall.
text.Rotherham: A fruitful bough, is Joseph, A fruitful bough over a fountain,—Branches creeping over a wall.
text.KJV: ¶ Ioseph is a fruitfull bough, euen a fruitfull bough by a well, whose branches runne ouer the wall.
text.NABRE: “Joseph is a wild colt, a wild colt by a spring, wild colts on a hillside.
text.DRB: Joseph a child increasing, increasing and comely to behold: the daughters coursed to and fro upon the wall.

text.NRSV: The archers fiercely attacked him; they shot at him and pressed him hard.
text.Rotherham: So they attack him and have shot,—So they enclose him, do the masters of arrows.
text.KJV: The archers haue sorely grieued him, and shot at him, and hated him.
text.NABRE: Harrying him and shooting, the archers opposed him;
text.DRB: But the dart men did exasperate him, and brawled, and envied him.

text.NRSV: Yet his bow remained taut, and his arms were made agile by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, by the name of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel,
text.Rotherham: But abideth, as an enduring one, his bow, And supple are the arms of his hands,—From the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, From thence, is the Shepherd the Stone of Israel:
text.KJV: But his bow abode in strength, and the armes of his hands were made strong, by the hands of the mighty God of Iacob: from thence is the Sheapheard, the stone of Israel,
text.NABRE: But his bow remained taut, and his arms were nimble, By the power of the Mighty One of Jacob, because of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel,
text.DRB: His bow sat upon the strong, and the bands of his arms and his hands were dissolved, by the hands of the Mighty of Jacob: thence came forth a pastor, the son of Israel.

text.NRSV: by the God of your father, who will help you, by the Almighty who will bless you with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that lies beneath, blessings of the breasts and of the womb.
text.Rotherham: From the GOD of thy father who doth help thee And GOD Almighty who doth bless thee, Blessings of the heavens, above, Blessings of the abyss, couching beneath,—Blessings of breasts and womb:
text.KJV: Euen by the God of thy father who shall helpe thee, and by the Almightie, who shall blesse thee with blessings of heauen aboue, blessings of the deepe that lyeth vnder, blessings of the breasts and of the wombe.
text.NABRE: The God of your father, who helps you, God Almighty, who blesses you, With the blessings of the heavens above, the blessings of the abyss that crouches below, The blessings of breasts and womb,
text.DRB: The God of thy father shall be thy helper, and the Almighty shall bless thee with the blessings of heaven from above, with the blessings of the depth that lieth beneath, with the blessings of the paps and of the womb.

text.NRSV: The blessings of your father are stronger than the blessings of the eternal mountains, the bounties of the everlasting hills; may they be on the head of Joseph, on the brow of him who was set apart from his brothers.
text.Rotherham: The blessings of thy father, have prevailed, Over the blessings of the perpetual mountains, The charm of the age-abiding hills,—They turn out to be, for the head of Joseph, And for the crown of the head of him who was set apart from his brethren.
text.KJV: The blessings of thy father haue preuailed aboue the blessings of my progenitors: vnto the vtmost bound of the euerlasting hils, they shall bee on the head of Ioseph, and on the crowne of the head of him that was separate from his brethren.
text.NABRE: the blessings of fresh grain and blossoms, the blessings of the everlasting mountains, the delights of the eternal hills. May they rest on the head of Joseph, on the brow of the prince among his brothers.
text.DRB: The blessings of thy father were strengthened with the blessings of his fathers: until the desire of the eternal hills came: be they upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the Nazarite among his brethren.

text.NRSV: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf, in the morning devouring the prey, and at evening dividing the spoil.”
text.Rotherham: Benjamin a wolf that teareth in pieces, In the morning, he eateth prey, And at eventide, he divideth spoil.
text.KJV: ¶ Beniamin shall rauine as a wolfe: In the morning hee shall deuoure the pray, and at night he shall diuide the spoile.
text.NABRE: “Benjamin is a ravenous wolf; mornings he devours the prey, and evenings he distributes the spoils.”
text.DRB: Benjamin a ravening wolf, in the morning shall eat the prey, and in the evening shall divide the spoil.

text.NRSV: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said to them when he blessed them, blessing each one of them with a suitable blessing.
text.Rotherham: All these, are the twelve tribes of Israel,—And this, is that which their father spake to them when he blessed them, Each man severally according to the blessing wherewith he blessed them.
text.KJV: ¶ All these are the twelue tribes of Israel, and this is it that their father spake vnto them, and blessed them: euery one according to his blessing he blessed them.
text.NABRE: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said about them, as he blessed them. To each he gave a suitable blessing.
text.DRB: All these in the tribes of Israel twelve: these things spake their father to them, and he blessed every one, with their proper blessings.

text.NRSV: Then he charged them, saying to them, “I am about to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my ancestors—in the cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
text.Rotherham: Then commanded he them and said unto them—I, am about to be gathered unto my people, Bury me among my fathers,—within the cave, that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite:
text.KJV: And hee charged them and said vnto them, I am to bee gathered vnto my people: burie me with my fathers, in the caue that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
text.NABRE: Then he gave them this charge: “Since I am about to be gathered to my people, bury me with my ancestors in the cave that lies in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
text.DRB: And he commanded them, saying: I am gathered unto my people: bury you me with my fathers in the double cave, which is in the field of Ephron the Hethite,

text.NRSV: in the cave in the field at Machpelah, near Mamre, in the land of Canaan, in the field that Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite as a burial site.
text.Rotherham: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah which is over against Mamre, in the land of Canaan,—which Abraham bought, along with the field, from Ephron the Hittite for a possession of a buryingplace:—
text.KJV: In the caue that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field of Ephron the Hittite, for a possession of a burying place.
