text.NABRE: the cave in the field of Machpelah, facing on Mamre, in the land of Canaan, the field that Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite for a burial ground.
text.DRB: Against Mambre in the Land of Chanaan, which Abraham bought with the field of Ephron the Hethite for a possession to bury in.

text.NRSV: There Abraham and his wife Sarah were buried; there Isaac and his wife Rebekah were buried; and there I buried Leah—
text.Rotherham: There, buried they Abraham, and Sarah his wife, There, buried they Isaac, and Rebekah his wife,—And there, buried I Leah:—
text.KJV: (There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife, there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife, and there I buried Leah.)
text.NABRE: There Abraham and his wife Sarah are buried, and so are Isaac and his wife Rebekah, and there, too, I buried Leah—
text.DRB: There they buried him, and Sara his wife: there was Isaac buried with Rebecca his wife: there also Lia doth lie buried.

text.NRSV: the field and the cave that is in it were purchased from the Hittites.”
text.Rotherham: buying the field and the cave that is therein, from the sons of Heth.
text.KJV: The purchase of the field and of the caue that is therein, was from the children of Heth.
text.NABRE: the field and the cave in it that had been purchased from the Hittites.”
text.DRB: And when he had finished the precepts wherewith he instructed his sons, he plucked up his feet upon the bed, and died; and he was put unto his people.

text.NRSV: When Jacob ended his charge to his sons, he drew up his feet into the bed, breathed his last, and was gathered to his people.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob made an end of commanding his sons, and gathered up his feet into the couch,—and ceased to breathe and was gathered unto his people.
text.KJV: And when Iacob had made an end of commanding his sonnes, he gathered vp his feete into the bed, and yeelded vp the ghost, and was gathered vnto his people.
text.NABRE: When Jacob had finished giving these instructions to his sons, he drew his feet into the bed, breathed his last, and was gathered to his people.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph threw himself on his father’s face and wept over him and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph fell upon the face of his father, and wept upon him and kissed him.
text.KJV: And Ioseph fell vpon his fathers face, and wept vpon him, and kissed him.
text.NABRE: Joseph flung himself upon his father and wept over him as he kissed him.
text.DRB: Which Joseph seeing fell upon his father's face weeping and kissing him.

text.NRSV: Joseph commanded the physicians in his service to embalm his father. So the physicians embalmed Israel;
text.Rotherham: Then Joseph commanded his servants the physicians, to embalm his father,—so the physicians embalmed Israel.
text.KJV: And Ioseph commanded his seruants the physicians to imbalme his father: and the physicians imbalmed Israel.
text.NABRE: Then Joseph ordered the physicians in his service to embalm his father. When the physicians embalmed Israel,
text.DRB: And he commanded his servants the physicians, that they should embalm his father with spices.

text.NRSV: they spent forty days in doing this, for that is the time required for embalming. And the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.
text.Rotherham: And they fulfilled for him forty days, for so, are they wont to fulfil the days of the embalmed,—and the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.
text.KJV: And fortie dayes were fulfilled for him, (for so are fulfilled the dayes of those which are imbalmed) and the Egyptians mourned for him threescore and ten dayes.
text.NABRE: they spent forty days at it, for that is the full period of embalming; and the Egyptians mourned him for seventy days.
text.DRB: Who fulfilling his commandments, there passed forty days; for this was the manner of corpses embalmed, and Ægypt mourned him seventy days.

text.NRSV: When the days of weeping for him were past, Joseph addressed the household of Pharaoh, “If now I have found favor with you, please speak to Pharaoh as follows:
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of weeping for him were passed, Joseph spake unto the house of Pharaoh saying,—If I pray you, I have found favour in your eyes, speak ye I pray you in the ears of Pharaoh saying:
text.KJV: And when the dayes of his mourning were past, Ioseph spake vnto the house of Pharaoh, saying, If now I haue found grace in your eyes, speake, I pray you, in the eares of Pharaoh, saying,
text.NABRE: When the period of mourning was over, Joseph spoke to Pharaoh’s household. “If you please, appeal to Pharaoh, saying:
text.DRB: And the mourning time being expired, Joseph spake to the family of Pharao: If I have found grace in your sight, speak in the ears of Pharao:

text.NRSV: My father made me swear an oath; he said, ‘I am about to die. In the tomb that I hewed out for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me.’ Now therefore let me go up, so that I may bury my father; then I will return.”
text.Rotherham: My father, made me swear saying,—Lo! I, am about to die,—in my grave which I digged for myself in the land of Canaan, there, shalt thou bury me. Now, therefore, let me go up, I pray thee and bury my father, and return.
text.KJV: My father made me sweare, saying, Loe, I die: in my graue which I haue digged for me, in the land of Canaan, there shalt thou bury me. Now therfore let me goe vp, I pray thee, and bury my father, and I will come againe.
text.NABRE: My father made me swear: ‘I am dying. Bury me in my grave that I have prepared for myself in the land of Canaan.’ So now let me go up to bury my father. Then I will come back.”
text.DRB: for so much as my father did adjure me, saying: Behold I die, in my sepulchre which I digged for myself in the land of Chanaan, thou shalt bury me. I will go up therefore, and bury my father, and return.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh answered, “Go up, and bury your father, as he made you swear to do.”
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said,—Go up., and bury thy father according as he made thee swear.
text.KJV: And Pharaoh said, Goe vp, and bury thy father, according as he made thee sweare.
text.NABRE: Pharaoh replied, “Go and bury your father, as he made you promise on oath.”
text.DRB: And Pharao said to him: Go up and bury thy father as thou wast adjured.

text.NRSV: So Joseph went up to bury his father. With him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his household, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,
text.Rotherham: So Joseph went up. to bury his father,—and there went up with him all the servants of Pharaoh the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt;
text.KJV: ¶ And Ioseph went vp to bury his father: and with him went vp all the seruants of Pharaoh, the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,
text.NABRE: So Joseph went up to bury his father; and with him went all of Pharaoh’s officials who were senior members of his household and all the other elders of the land of Egypt,
text.DRB: Who going up, there went with him all the ancients of Pharao's house, and all the elders of the Land of Ægypt:

text.NRSV: as well as all the household of Joseph, his brothers, and his father’s household. Only their children, their flocks, and their herds were left in the land of Goshen.
text.Rotherham: and all the house of Joseph, and his brethren, and the house of his father,—only their little ones and their flocks and their herds, left they. in the land of Goshen.
text.KJV: And all the house of Ioseph, and his brethren, and his fathers house: onely their litle ones, and their flockes, and their heards, they left in the land of Goshen.
text.NABRE: as well as Joseph’s whole household, his brothers, and his father’s household; only their children and their flocks and herds were left in the region of Goshen.
text.DRB: the house of Joseph with his brethren, saving their little ones, and the flocks and herds, which they had left in the Land of Gessen.

text.NRSV: Both chariots and charioteers went up with him. It was a very great company.
text.Rotherham: And there went up with him, both chariots and horsemen,—so it came to pass that, the company, was exceeding great,
text.KJV: And there went vp with him both charets and horsemen: and it was a very great company.
text.NABRE: Chariots, too, and horsemen went up with him; it was a very imposing retinue.
text.DRB: He had also in his train chariots and horsemen: and it became no small multitude.

text.NRSV: When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they held there a very great and sorrowful lamentation; and he observed a time of mourning for his father seven days.
text.Rotherham: And they came in, as far as the threshing-floor of the Buckthorn, which is beyond the Jordan, then wailed they there—an exceeding great and grievous wailing,—and he made for his father a mourning, of seven days.
text.KJV: And they came to the threshing floore of Atad, which is beyond Iordan, and there they mourned with a great and very sore lamentation: and he made a mourning for his father seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: When they arrived at Goren-ha-atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they held there a very great and solemn memorial service; and Joseph observed seven days of mourning for his father.
text.DRB: And they came to the floor of Atad, which is situate beyond Jordan: where celebrating the exequies with great and vehement mourning, they spent full seven days.

text.NRSV: When the Canaanite inhabitants of the land saw the mourning on the threshing floor of Atad, they said, “This is a grievous mourning on the part of the Egyptians.” Therefore the place was named Abel-mizraim; it is beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And when the dwellers in the land—the Canaanites—saw the mourning in the threshing-floor of the Buckthorn, they said,—A grievous mourning, this! for the Egyptians. For this cause, was the name thereof called Abel Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.
text.KJV: And when the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites sawe the mourning in the floore of Atad, they saide, This is a grieuous mourning to the Egyptians: wherfore the name of it was called, Abel Mizraim, which is beyond Iordan.
text.NABRE: When the Canaanites who inhabited the land saw the mourning at Goren-ha-atad, they said, “This is a solemn funeral on the part of the Egyptians!” That is why the place was named Abel-mizraim. It is beyond the Jordan.
text.DRB: Which when the Inhabiters of the Land of Chanaan had seen, they said: This is a great mourning unto the Ægyptians. And therefore the name of that place was called, The mourning of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Thus his sons did for him as he had instructed them.
text.Rotherham: And his sons did for him thus as he had commanded them;
text.KJV: And his sonnes did vnto him according as he commanded them.
text.NABRE: Thus Jacob’s sons did for him as he had instructed them.
text.DRB: Therefore the sons of Jacob did as he commanded them:

text.NRSV: They carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field at Machpelah, the field near Mamre, which Abraham bought as a burial site from Ephron the Hittite.
text.Rotherham: so his sons carried him to the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah,—which Abraham bought—with the field—for a possession of a buryingplace from Ephron the Hittite over against Mamre.
text.KJV: For his sonnes caried him into the land of Canaan, and buried him in the caue of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham bought with the field for a possession of a burying place, of Ephron the Hittite, before Mamre.
text.NABRE: They carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave in the field of Machpelah, facing on Mamre, the field that Abraham had bought for a burial ground from Ephron the Hittite.
text.DRB: and carrying him into the Land of Chanaan, they buried him in the double cave, which Abraham had bought with the field for a possession to bury in of Ephron the Hethite against Mambre.

text.NRSV: After he had buried his father, Joseph returned to Egypt with his brothers and all who had gone up with him to bury his father.
text.Rotherham: Then Joseph returned to Egypt he and his brethren and all who had been up with him to bury his father,—after he had buried his father.
text.KJV: ¶ And Ioseph returned into Egypt, he and his brethren, and all that went vp with him, to bury his father, after he had buried his father.
text.NABRE: After Joseph had buried his father he returned to Egypt, together with his brothers and all who had gone up with him for the burial of his father.
text.DRB: And Joseph returned into Ægypt with his brethren, and with all the train, his father being buried.

text.NRSV: Realizing that their father was dead, Joseph’s brothers said, “What if Joseph still bears a grudge against us and pays us back in full for all the wrong that we did to him?”
text.Rotherham: Now when the brethren of Joseph saw that their father was dead, they said—Oh! if Joseph should lie in wait for us,—and should return, to us, all the evil wherewith we requited him!
text.KJV: ¶ And when Iosephs brethren saw that their father was dead, they said, Ioseph will peraduenture hate vs, and will certainely requite vs all the euill which we did vnto him.
text.NABRE: Now that their father was dead, Joseph’s brothers became fearful and thought, “Suppose Joseph has been nursing a grudge against us and now most certainly will pay us back in full for all the wrong we did him!”
text.DRB: After whose death his brethren fearing, & talking one with another: Lest perhaps he be mindful of the injury which he suffered, and requite us all the evil that we have done;

text.NRSV: So they approached Joseph, saying, “Your father gave this instruction before he died,
text.Rotherham: So they sent in charge unto Joseph saying,—Thy father, gave command before he died saying:
text.KJV: And they sent a messenger vnto Ioseph, saying, Thy father did command before he died, saying,
text.NABRE: So they sent to Joseph and said: “Before your father died, he gave us these instructions:
text.DRB: they advertised him saying: Thy father commanded us before he died,

text.NRSV: ‘Say to Joseph: I beg you, forgive the crime of your brothers and the wrong they did in harming you.’ Now therefore please forgive the crime of the servants of the God of your father.” Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall ye say to Joseph—Ah now! do forgive we pray thee the transgression of thy brethren and their sin in that with evil, they requited thee. Now, therefore grant forgiveness we pray thee, for the transgression of the servants of the God of thy father! And Joseph wept when they spake unto him.
text.KJV: So shall ye say vnto Ioseph, Forgiue, I pray thee now, the trespasse of thy brethren, and their sinne: for they did vnto thee euill: And now wee pray thee, forgiue the trespasse of the seruants of the God of thy father. And Ioseph wept, when they spake vnto him.
text.NABRE: ‘Thus you shall say to Joseph: Please forgive the criminal wrongdoing of your brothers, who treated you harmfully.’ So now please forgive the crime that we, the servants of the God of your father, committed.” When they said this to him, Joseph broke into tears.
text.DRB: that we should say thus much to thee in his words: I beseech that thou forget the wicked fact of thy brethren, and the sin and malice which they have exercised against thee: we also desire thee, that to the servants of the God of thy father thou remit this iniquity. Whom when Joseph had heard he wept.

text.NRSV: Then his brothers also wept, fell down before him, and said, “We are here as your slaves.”
text.Rotherham: Then went his brethren also, and fell down before him,—and said, Behold us! thine for servants.
text.KJV: And his brethren also went and fell downe before his face, and they said, Behold, we be thy seruants.
text.NABRE: Then his brothers also proceeded to fling themselves down before him and said, “We are your slaves!”
text.DRB: And his brethren came to him; and adoring prostrate on the ground they said: We are thy servants.

text.NRSV: But Joseph said to them, “Do not be afraid! Am I in the place of God?
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto them—Do not fear,—although in place of God, I am.
text.KJV: And Ioseph saide vnto them, Feare not: for am I in the place of God?
text.NABRE: But Joseph replied to them: “Do not fear. Can I take the place of God?
text.DRB: To whom he answered: Fear not; can we resist the will of God?

text.NRSV: Even though you intended to do harm to me, God intended it for good, in order to preserve a numerous people, as he is doing today.
text.Rotherham: Ye, indeed, planned against me, evil,—God, planned it, for good, for the sake of doing, as at this day, to save alive much people.
text.KJV: But as for you, yee thought euill against me, but God meant it vnto good, to bring to passe, as it is this day, to saue much people aliue.
text.NABRE: Even though you meant harm to me, God meant it for good, to achieve this present end, the survival of many people.
text.DRB: You thought evil against me, but God turned that into good, that he might exalt me, as presently you see, and might save many peoples.

text.NRSV: So have no fear; I myself will provide for you and your little ones.” In this way he reassured them, speaking kindly to them.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore do not fear! I will nourish you and your little ones. Thus he consoled them, and spake unto their heart.
text.KJV: Now therefore feare yee not: I will nourish you, and your litle ones. And hee comforted them, and spake kindly vnto them.
text.NABRE: So now, do not fear. I will provide for you and for your children.” By thus speaking kindly to them, he reassured them.
text.DRB: Fear not: I will feed you and your little ones, and he comforted them, and spake gently & mildly.

text.NRSV: So Joseph remained in Egypt, he and his father’s household; and Joseph lived one hundred ten years.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph abode in Egypt, he, and his father’s house,—and Joseph lived a hundred and ten years.
text.KJV: ¶ And Ioseph dwelt in Egypt, he, and his fathers house: and Ioseph liued an hundred and ten yeeres.
text.NABRE: Joseph remained in Egypt, together with his father’s household. He lived a hundred and ten years.
text.DRB: And he dwelt in Ægypt, with all his father's house: and lived an hundred and ten years. And he saw the children of Ephraim unto the third generation. Also the children of Machir the son of Manasses were born in Joseph's knee.

text.NRSV: Joseph saw Ephraim’s children of the third generation; the children of Machir son of Manasseh were also born on Joseph’s knees.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph saw Ephraim’s, sons of three generations—moreover the sons of Machir, son of Manasseh, were born upon Joseph’s knees.
text.KJV: And Ioseph sawe Ephraims children, of the third generation: the children also of Machir, the sonne of Manasseh were brought vp vpon Iosephs knees.
text.NABRE: He saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation, and the children of Manasseh’s son Machir were also born on Joseph’s knees.
text.DRB: Which things being done he spake to his brethren: After my death God will visit you, and will make you go up out of this land, to the land which he sware to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph said to his brothers, “I am about to die; but God will surely come to you, and bring you up out of this land to the land that he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph unto his brethren, I, am about to die,—but God will surely concern, himself for you, so will he take you up out of this land, into the land which he sware to Abraham to Isaac and to Jacob.
text.KJV: And Ioseph saide vnto his brethren, I die: and God will surely visit you, and bring you out of this land, vnto the land which hee sware to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Iacob.
text.NABRE: Joseph said to his brothers: “I am about to die. God will surely take care of you and lead you up from this land to the land that he promised on oath to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.”
text.DRB: And when he had adjured them and said. God will visit you carry my bones with you out of this place:

text.NRSV: So Joseph made the Israelites swear, saying, “When God comes to you, you shall carry up my bones from here.”
text.Rotherham: Then Joseph made the sons of Israel swear saying,—God will, surely concern, himself for you, So shall ye carry up my bones, from hence.
text.KJV: And Ioseph tooke an othe of the children of Israel, saying, God will surely visite you, and ye shal carie vp my bones from hence.
text.NABRE: Then, putting the sons of Israel under oath, he continued, “When God thus takes care of you, you must bring my bones up from this place.”
text.DRB: he died, being an hundred and ten years old. And being embalmed with spices, was put in a coffin in Ægypt.

text.NRSV: And Joseph died, being one hundred ten years old; he was embalmed and placed in a coffin in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph died, being a hundred and ten years old,—and they embalmed him, and put him in a coffin in Egypt.
text.KJV: So Ioseph died, being an hundred and ten yeeres old: and they imbalmed him, and he was put in a coffin, in Egypt.
text.NABRE: Joseph died at the age of a hundred and ten. He was embalmed and laid to rest in a coffin in Egypt.

31.55: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them; then he set out on his journey back home.
